project_goal.value: Multi-zone vegetable farm management platform tracking field inputs, irrigation schedules, and harvest yields with role-based data access for agronomists, field workers, and farm owners
project_goal.rationale: Core operational need replacing spreadsheet-based record-keeping

target_users.value: Farm owners, agronomists, and field workers at mid-size commercial vegetable operations
target_users.rationale: Three distinct roles with different data access and entry needs

project_class.rationale: Requires React dashboard, FastAPI backend, and persistent farm data storage

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, analytics, admin_panel
capabilities.rationale: Farm analytics and owner dashboards require analytics and admin capabilities

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-zone data model, RBAC, EPA record-keeping compliance, and predictive yield analytics add significant complexity

risk_level.rationale: Confidential agronomic data and regulatory compliance elevate risk above low

data_sensitivity.rationale: Proprietary yield data, pesticide usage records, and supplier contracts are commercially sensitive

external_exposure.rationale: All access requires authentication; no public endpoints

access_model.value: RBAC with three roles: owner (full access), agronomist (zone-scoped read/write), field_worker (task-scoped read-only plus input logging)
access_model.rationale: Different operational responsibilities require distinct permission boundaries

feature_scope.value: Growing zone management, field input logging (fertilizer/pesticide per zone), irrigation schedule builder, harvest yield recording, agronomist assignment, re-entry interval alerts, yield vs prediction reconciliation, admin user management
feature_scope.rationale: Full operational workflow from planting to harvest

mvp_scope.value: Growing zone CRUD, field input logging with re-entry interval alerts, irrigation schedule display, harvest yield entry, basic owner analytics dashboard
mvp_scope.rationale: Core workflow needed before planting season

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 in transit, AES-256 at rest, Cognito with MFA for owners and agronomists, JWT short-lived tokens, RBAC enforced at API layer
security_baseline.rationale: Confidential data and regulatory context require strong baseline controls

frontend_stack.value: React 18 with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, React Query, Recharts for analytics
frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise; Recharts for zone yield visualizations

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2, Pydantic v2, Celery for background jobs
backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI async performance; Celery for re-entry interval alert scheduling

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 (RDS Multi-AZ), Redis 7 (ElastiCache) for task queues and session cache
data_platform.rationale: Relational model suits zone-input-harvest relationships; Redis for Celery broker

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate, RDS Multi-AZ us-west-2, CloudFront for static assets
hosting_target.rationale: Managed containers; Multi-AZ for operational uptime during growing season

privacy_retention_policy.value: Pesticide application records retained 2 years per EPA 40 CFR Part 171; yield data retained 5 years; session logs 90 days
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: EPA record-keeping and state agricultural reporting requirements

future_scope.value: Integration with precision irrigation hardware sensors, mobile field worker app, crop rotation planning wizard, supplier contract management
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 roadmap pending MVP validation

constraints.value: Must go live before spring planting season (8-week window), AWS us-west-2 only, budget under $800/month infrastructure
constraints.rationale: Seasonal deployment constraint is hard deadline

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM on ECS tasks, structured JSON logs with zone_id and worker_id correlation, PagerDuty for re-entry alert delivery failures
observability_baseline.rationale: Alert delivery failures for pesticide re-entry are safety-critical

execution_preference.value: Two-week sprints, feature flags for analytics rollout, agronomist beta group for testing
execution_preference.rationale: Iterative delivery with controlled rollout to trusted users first

compliance_context.value: EPA 40 CFR Part 171 pesticide applicator record-keeping; state agricultural reporting via USDA Farm Service Agency forms; re-entry interval (REI) compliance per EPA label requirements
compliance_context.rationale: Confidential data sensitivity and medium risk level require explicit compliance documentation

project.goal: Track field inputs, irrigation, and harvest yields across growing zones with role-scoped access
project.target_users: Farm owners, agronomists, field workers

frontend.state: React Query for server state, Zustand for zone selection UI state
frontend.charts: Recharts for yield and input analytics

backend.tasks: Celery with Redis broker for REI alert scheduling

security.auth: AWS Cognito with MFA for owners and agronomists
security.rbac: owner / agronomist / field_worker roles enforced at route dependencies

devops.environments: dev, staging, prod

reasoner_reviews.product: The re-entry interval alert workflow is the highest-value safety feature; field workers must receive timely notifications before entering treated zones. The harvest yield reconciliation against predicted models adds meaningful agronomic value but can be deferred to Phase 2 if timeline pressure mounts.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: FastAPI with Celery on Fargate is the right architecture for REI alert delivery. Recommend a dedicated Celery beat scheduler container separate from the API to avoid interference during traffic spikes. Zone-scoped RBAC filtering must be applied at the SQLAlchemy query layer, not just the route layer, to prevent data leakage across agronomist assignments.
reasoner_reviews.security: The previous plan lacked row-level security enforcement in the data layer. Agronomists scoped to specific growing zones must not be able to query inputs from zones outside their assignment table. Add a ZonePermissionDep dependency to all zone-data endpoints and enforce it via SQLAlchemy filter predicates, not post-fetch filtering.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Budget of $800/month is tight for Multi-AZ RDS plus Fargate. Recommend t3.medium RDS instance for dev/staging and db.r6g.large only for prod. Celery workers can scale down to zero in off-season via ECS service desired count scheduling.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The first-round plan omitted a clear data retention and deletion schedule aligned with EPA 2-year record-keeping requirement. The compliance_context field specifies 40 CFR Part 171 but the phased_implementation did not include a retention automation task in any phase.

backend.service_design: FastAPI app split into routers: /zones, /inputs, /irrigation, /harvests, /alerts, /analytics. Zone ownership enforced via ZonePermissionDep injected into all zone-data routes. Celery beat scheduler runs REI expiry checks every 15 minutes, querying field_inputs where rei_expires_at <= now() + 2h.
backend.api_patterns: RESTful with versioned prefix /api/v1. OpenAPI 3.1 spec auto-generated. Idempotency keys on POST /inputs to prevent duplicate field input records from mobile retries.
backend.background_jobs: Celery beat: rei_alert_check (every 15 min), yield_reconciliation_trigger (nightly at 2am), epa_report_export (monthly first Sunday). Worker containers: 2 replicas in prod, scale-to-zero in staging.
backend.failure_handling: REI alert delivery failures write to alert_delivery_log with retry_count. After 3 failures, escalate to PagerDuty via webhook. FastAPI global exception handler returns RFC 7807 problem+json responses.

frontend.app_structure: React SPA with lazy-loaded route modules: /dashboard (owner), /zones/:id (agronomist), /inputs/log (field worker), /irrigation, /admin. Zone selector persists to Zustand store; React Query prefetches zone data on hover.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Owner Dashboard: yield-vs-prediction Recharts bar chart per zone, input cost totals, REI active alert banner. Zone Detail: input log table with pesticide product, application rate, REI expiry. Field Worker Input Form: zone picker (limited to assigned zones), product name, application date, REI lookup.
frontend.state_management: React Query for all server data with staleTime 2 minutes. Zustand for selectedZoneId and activeRole context. Optimistic updates on input log submissions.

security.auth_design: Cognito user pool with MFA enforced for owner and agronomist groups. Field workers use password-only (MFA optional). Cognito groups map directly to RBAC roles injected into JWT claims.
security.authorization_model: ZonePermissionDep FastAPI dependency validates that the requesting agronomist has an active record in agronomist_assignments for the requested zone_id. Owner role bypasses zone check. Field worker write access restricted to field_inputs table only.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Structured audit log emitted on every field_input CREATE, UPDATE, DELETE with user_id, zone_id, product_name, and timestamp. Retained in audit_events table for 2 years to satisfy EPA record-keeping. Exported monthly to S3 for compliance archiving.

data.entities: growing_zones (id, farm_id, zone_name, hectares, crop_type, planting_date, status). field_inputs (id, zone_id, input_type ENUM fertilizer/pesticide/amendment, product_name, application_rate, applied_by, applied_at, rei_hours, rei_expires_at). irrigation_schedules (id, zone_id, schedule_type, duration_minutes, frequency_days, next_run_at). harvest_records (id, zone_id, harvest_date, yield_kg, yield_quality ENUM A/B/C). agronomist_assignments (agronomist_id, zone_id, assigned_at, revoked_at). crop_rotations (id, zone_id, previous_crop, current_crop, rotation_year).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 RDS db.r6g.large Multi-AZ in prod. Row-level access enforced via SQLAlchemy query filters in ZonePermissionDep. S3 for monthly EPA compliance exports. Redis for Celery task queue and 5-min session cache.
data.retention_and_deletion: field_inputs retained 2 years per EPA 40 CFR Part 171. harvest_records 5 years. audit_events 2 years with monthly S3 archival. Automated pg_cron job deletes expired records after archive confirmation.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate cluster with 3 services: api (2-4 tasks), celery-worker (2 tasks), celery-beat (1 task). ALB routes /api/* to api service. CloudFront serves React build from S3. RDS Multi-AZ in private subnet. Redis in private subnet.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: ruff lint, mypy, pytest (85% coverage gate), Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling update with health check on /api/v1/health. Staging deploy on main merge; prod deploy on tag push.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM sidecar. Structured JSON logs with zone_id, worker_id, action correlation fields. Custom metrics: rei_alerts_sent, rei_alert_failures, harvest_entries_per_day. PagerDuty P1 on rei_alert_failures > 0.
devops.rollback_strategy: ECS rolling update with minimum healthy 50%. On failed deployment health check, automatic rollback to previous task definition revision.

ISS-001.title: Zone-scoped RBAC not enforced at data layer
ISS-001.detail: The round 1 plan described RBAC at the route level only. Agronomist queries to /zones/:id/inputs did not include SQLAlchemy filter predicates validating agronomist_assignments membership, allowing potential cross-zone data reads.

history.note: Identified by security reviewer: no data-layer enforcement shown in data_model or api_design sections

ISS-002.title: EPA retention schedule absent from phased implementation
ISS-002.detail: compliance_context specifies 40 CFR Part 171 2-year retention for pesticide records but the phase plan included no task for automated retention enforcement or S3 archival pipeline.

history.note: Critic reviewer flagged missing retention automation in all three phases

focus_issues.title: Zone-scoped RBAC not enforced at data layer
focus_issues.detail: Agronomist routes must validate zone ownership via agronomist_assignments at the SQLAlchemy query layer, not only at route middleware.

focus_issues.title: EPA retention schedule absent from phased implementation
focus_issues.detail: 2-year pesticide record retention with S3 archival must appear in phase plan and deployment_and_operations.

latest_recommendations: Add ZonePermissionDep FastAPI dependency that filters agronomist_assignments at query time
Include EPA record retention pg_cron job and S3 archival pipeline in Phase 1 or Phase 2

convergence_state.reason: High severity RBAC data-layer gap and missing compliance retention task block convergence

previous_audits.summary: Solid domain-specific plan with well-structured growing zone model and REI alert workflow, but fails on data-layer RBAC enforcement and omits EPA retention automation entirely.
strengths: Re-entry interval alert Celery architecture is well-specified with 15-min polling and PagerDuty escalation
Growing zone and field_inputs data model is domain-accurate with correct REI fields
React zone-selector UI flow matches field worker and agronomist role workflows
concerns: Agronomist cross-zone data access not prevented at SQLAlchemy query layer
No S3 archival pipeline or pg_cron deletion schedule for EPA record retention
phased_implementation makes no mention of compliance automation in any phase
blocking_issues: ISS-001: Data-layer RBAC gap allows agronomists to query zones outside their assignments
ISS-002: EPA 40 CFR Part 171 retention requirement has no implementation plan
recommendations: Add ZonePermissionDep that injects agronomist_assignments filter predicate into all zone-data queries
Add Phase 1 task: implement pg_cron retention job and monthly S3 audit export for field_inputs
Document S3 Glacier archival lifecycle in deployment_and_operations

issue_updates.title: Zone-scoped RBAC not enforced at data layer
issue_updates.detail: api_design section showed /zones/:id/inputs without data-layer guard; security section mentioned route-level only

issue_updates.title: EPA retention schedule absent from phased implementation

previous_plan.title: FarmTrack Zone Management Platform v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: A fullstack farm management platform built with React 18 and FastAPI on AWS ECS Fargate, tracking field inputs, irrigation schedules, and harvest yields across growing zones with Cognito RBAC and Celery-powered re-entry interval alerts.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: React SPA via CloudFront to FastAPI on ECS Fargate, PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ for zone and input records, Redis for Celery REI alert scheduler, Cognito for RBAC authentication.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Cognito MFA for owners and agronomists, RBAC enforced at FastAPI route layer, TLS 1.3, AES-256 at rest. EPA compliance_context acknowledged but no retention automation specified.
previous_plan.phased_implementation: Phase 1: zone CRUD, field input logging, Cognito auth, basic dashboard. Phase 2: irrigation scheduler, yield recording, Recharts analytics. Phase 3: mobile app, sensor integration.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 must close ISS-001 by demonstrating ZonePermissionDep data-layer filtering in api_design and security_and_compliance, and close ISS-002 by adding pg_cron retention job and S3 archival to Phase 1 and deployment_and_operations. The farm management domain requires special attention to EPA record-keeping specifics -- 2-year retention for pesticide application records under 40 CFR Part 171. The core architecture is sound; this round focuses on security and compliance gaps surfaced by the auditor.

fix_report.action_taken: Added ZonePermissionDep FastAPI dependency to api_design section with explicit SQLAlchemy filter predicate example. Updated security_and_compliance to describe data-layer enforcement pattern for agronomist zone scoping.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Agronomists can only read and write field_inputs, irrigation_schedules, and harvest_records for zones where an active agronomist_assignments row exists for their user_id.

fix_report.action_taken: Added pg_cron retention job and monthly S3 export task to Phase 1 scope. Updated deployment_and_operations to describe S3 Glacier lifecycle for field_inputs older than 2 years. Added audit_events archival note to data_model section.
fix_report.expected_outcome: EPA 40 CFR Part 171 2-year pesticide record retention is automated with monthly S3 archival and Glacier transition at 24 months, satisfying compliance_context requirement.

target_output.title: FarmTrack Zone Management Platform
target_output.executive_summary: A RBAC-enforced fullstack farm management platform for mid-size commercial vegetable operations, built with React 18 TypeScript and FastAPI on AWS ECS Fargate. Tracks fertilizer and pesticide field inputs per growing zone with automated re-entry interval alerts, irrigation scheduling, and harvest yield recording. Zone-scoped data access enforced at the SQLAlchemy query layer. Pesticide application records retained 2 years with monthly S3 archival per EPA 40 CFR Part 171.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-tier architecture: React 18 SPA served from S3 via CloudFront, communicating over HTTPS to a FastAPI REST API on ECS Fargate behind an ALB. API connects to PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ for zone, input, irrigation, and harvest records, with Redis 7 via ElastiCache as the Celery broker for REI alert scheduling. AWS Cognito handles authentication with MFA enforced for owner and agronomist groups. A dedicated Celery beat container polls every 15 minutes for expiring re-entry intervals and dispatches field worker alerts. ZonePermissionDep, a FastAPI dependency, injects agronomist_assignments filter predicates into all zone-data queries, preventing cross-zone data reads at the ORM layer.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, React Query (staleTime 2min), Zustand (zone selection state), Recharts (yield and input analytics). Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2 (async), Pydantic v2, Celery 5 with Redis broker. Database: PostgreSQL 16 RDS db.r6g.large Multi-AZ (prod), db.t3.medium (dev/staging). Cache/Queue: ElastiCache Redis 7. Infra: ECS Fargate (api, celery-worker, celery-beat services), ALB, CloudFront, S3, Cognito, RDS, ACM, Secrets Manager.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Growing Zone Registry: zone CRUD with hectares, crop type, planting date, status. Field Input Logger: record fertilizer/pesticide/amendment applications per zone with product name, application rate, REI hours; auto-compute rei_expires_at. Re-Entry Interval Alert Engine: Celery beat checks rei_expires_at thresholds and notifies assigned field workers via SES/SNS 2 hours before zone re-entry is permitted. Irrigation Schedule Builder: define drip/sprinkler/flood schedules per zone with frequency and duration. Harvest Yield Recorder: log kg per zone per harvest date with quality grade. Owner Analytics Dashboard: Recharts yield-vs-prediction bar chart per zone, input cost aggregation, active REI alert count. Admin Panel: user management, agronomist zone assignment, EPA export trigger.
target_output.system_components: CloudFront distribution serving React build from S3. ALB with WAF managed rules routing /api/v1/* to ECS api service. ECS Fargate cluster: api service (2-4 tasks, CPU 512, Mem 1024), celery-worker service (2 tasks), celery-beat service (1 task singleton). RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ db.r6g.large in private subnet. ElastiCache Redis 7 in private subnet. S3 bucket for monthly EPA audit exports with Glacier transition at 24 months. Cognito user pool with owner/agronomist/field_worker groups. Secrets Manager for DB credentials and Cognito client secret.
target_output.workflows: Field Input Logging and REI Alert: Agronomist or field worker POSTs to /api/v1/zones/:zone_id/inputs with product_name, application_rate, rei_hours. ZonePermissionDep validates agronomist_assignments for requesting user and zone_id. API computes rei_expires_at = applied_at + rei_hours. Celery beat (every 15 min) queries field_inputs WHERE rei_expires_at BETWEEN now() AND now()+2h AND alert_sent=false. Worker sends SES email to field workers assigned to the zone, sets alert_sent=true, logs to alert_delivery_log. On delivery failure after 3 retries, PagerDuty P1 webhook fires. Harvest Yield Reconciliation: Nightly Celery task aggregates harvest_records per zone per season, computes actual_yield_kg vs predicted_yield_kg from crop_rotations baseline, writes delta to zone_analytics table, surfaces zones with >15% shortfall in owner dashboard as underperforming blocks.
target_output.data_model: growing_zones (id UUID PK, farm_id UUID FK, zone_name VARCHAR, hectares DECIMAL, crop_type VARCHAR, planting_date DATE, status ENUM active/fallow/harvested). field_inputs (id UUID PK, zone_id UUID FK, input_type ENUM fertilizer/pesticide/amendment, product_name, epa_registration_number, application_rate DECIMAL, application_unit, applied_by UUID FK, applied_at TIMESTAMPTZ, rei_hours INT, rei_expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ, alert_sent BOOL DEFAULT false). irrigation_schedules (id UUID PK, zone_id UUID FK, schedule_type ENUM drip/sprinkler/flood, duration_minutes INT, frequency_days INT, next_run_at TIMESTAMPTZ). harvest_records (id UUID PK, zone_id UUID FK, harvest_date DATE, yield_kg DECIMAL, yield_quality ENUM A/B/C, harvested_by UUID FK, notes TEXT). agronomist_assignments (agronomist_id UUID FK, zone_id UUID FK, assigned_at TIMESTAMPTZ, revoked_at TIMESTAMPTZ). audit_events (id UUID PK, user_id UUID FK, action, resource_type, resource_id UUID, zone_id UUID, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ) -- retained 2 years, monthly S3 export. crop_rotations (id UUID PK, zone_id UUID FK, previous_crop, current_crop, rotation_year INT).
target_output.api_design: RESTful API at /api/v1. GET /zones (owner: all; agronomist: filtered by agronomist_assignments via ZonePermissionDep). POST /zones, GET /zones/:id, PATCH /zones/:id. POST /zones/:zone_id/inputs -- ZonePermissionDep executes SELECT 1 FROM agronomist_assignments WHERE agronomist_id=current_user.id AND zone_id=:zone_id AND revoked_at IS NULL; returns 403 on miss. GET /zones/:zone_id/inputs?input_type=pesticide&from=2026-01-01. POST /zones/:zone_id/harvests, GET /zones/:zone_id/harvests. GET /analytics/yield-summary?season=2026 (owner role only). POST /admin/zone-assignments, DELETE /admin/zone-assignments/:id.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Cognito user pool: MFA enforced for owner and agronomist groups (TOTP); field_worker group MFA optional. JWT claims include custom:role and custom:user_id injected at Cognito post-authentication Lambda trigger. FastAPI Depends(get_current_user) decodes Cognito JWT and populates CurrentUser context. ZonePermissionDep: for agronomist role, executes SELECT 1 FROM agronomist_assignments WHERE agronomist_id=$user_id AND zone_id=$zone_id AND revoked_at IS NULL; returns HTTP 403 if no row found. Owner role bypasses zone check. Field worker write access restricted to field_inputs table only via route-level permission check. TLS 1.3 everywhere; RDS encrypted with AWS-managed KMS key. WAF on ALB blocks OWASP Top 10 managed rules. EPA compliance: field_inputs retained 2 years, audit_events retained 2 years, monthly pg_cron export to S3 epa-audit-exports bucket, Glacier transition at 24 months.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI pipeline: ruff lint, mypy type check, pytest with 85% coverage gate, Docker build and ECR push, ECS rolling update (min healthy 50%, health check /api/v1/health). Environments: dev (single-AZ, t3.medium RDS, 1 Fargate task each service), staging (multi-AZ mirror of prod config), prod (multi-AZ us-west-2a/2b, db.r6g.large RDS, 2-4 api tasks autoscale on CPU 60%). Celery beat runs as singleton ECS service; ECS service scheduler sets desired count 0 for celery-worker November through February (off-season). Migrations: Alembic run as ECS one-off task pre-deploy. Secrets: Secrets Manager with 30-day rotation for DB password and Cognito client secret. EPA retention automation: pg_cron job runs first Sunday of each month, exports field_inputs older than 23 months to CSV, uploads to S3 epa-audit-exports/YYYY-MM/, deletes records older than 24 months after S3 confirmation.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM sidecar on each ECS task with trace context propagated via correlation headers. Structured JSON logs: every request includes zone_id, user_id, role, action fields. Custom Datadog metrics: rei_alerts_sent_total (counter), rei_alert_delivery_failures (counter, PagerDuty P1 trigger at >0), harvest_entries_per_zone_per_day, active_rei_zones_count, epa_export_last_run_at (gauge). Datadog dashboard: zone input activity heatmap, REI alert pipeline health, harvest yield trend per zone.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $680/month for prod: RDS db.r6g.large Multi-AZ ~$280, ECS Fargate 4 tasks ~$180, ElastiCache cache.t3.micro ~$25, CloudFront ~$15, S3 ~$5, Secrets Manager/ACM/Route53 ~$20, Datadog APM ~$155. Under $800/month budget. ECS autoscaling: api service scales out at CPU 60%, scales in at CPU 30% with 5-min cooldown. Celery workers scale to zero November-February for off-season cost reduction.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 - Core Platform (8 weeks, targets spring planting deadline): Growing zone CRUD, field input logging with REI computation, Cognito auth with RBAC (Cognito groups plus ZonePermissionDep data-layer filtering), re-entry interval alert Celery pipeline with PagerDuty escalation, owner basic dashboard, EPA retention automation (pg_cron monthly export plus S3 archival pipeline configured at launch), CI/CD GitHub Actions pipeline. Phase 2 - Operational Depth (4 weeks post-launch): Irrigation schedule builder with next_run_at scheduling, harvest yield recording with quality grading, agronomist assignment admin panel, yield-vs-prediction Recharts analytics with underperforming zone flagging. Phase 3 - Extensibility (Q4): Mobile React Native field worker app, precision irrigation sensor webhook integration, supplier contract management module, USDA Farm Service Agency report export.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python: ruff linter plus mypy strict mode in CI. SQLAlchemy queries must go through ZonePermissionDep for all zone-scoped resources -- no raw SQL bypassing the ORM filter. FastAPI route dependencies must declare explicit role requirements via Depends(require_role(['owner','agronomist'])). React: ESLint plus Prettier, React Query for all data fetching. 85% pytest coverage for API routes; Playwright E2E for REI alert end-to-end flow. ADR required for any change to zone permission model.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Celery beat singleton failure stops all REI alerts -- mitigated by ECS service restart policy and PagerDuty dead-man-switch alert if rei_alerts_sent_total flatlines for 30 min during growing season. Risk: Agronomist assignment revocation latency -- revoked_at check in ZonePermissionDep is synchronous per-request; acceptable given low revocation frequency. Tradeoff: ORM-level zone filtering over PostgreSQL row-level security (RLS) -- chose ORM approach for developer familiarity and debuggability; RLS remains an option if multi-tenant isolation requirements escalate. Risk: off-season scale-to-zero for Celery workers may miss shoulder-season inputs -- mitigated by keeping celery-beat running at 1 task year-round.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Celery over AWS EventBridge Scheduler for REI alerts -- Celery provides sub-minute polling needed for tight REI windows; EventBridge minimum interval is 1 minute. Confirmed ZonePermissionDep ORM pattern over PostgreSQL RLS for agronomist isolation -- ORM approach is testable and visible in FastAPI dependency graph. Confirmed monthly pg_cron export over streaming CDC for EPA archival -- batch export is sufficient for compliance and CDC adds operational complexity not justified by requirements.

metadata.notes: Round 2 farm management platform: closes data-layer RBAC gap (ISS-001) and EPA retention automation gap (ISS-002).

project_goal.value: Migrate on-premise grain elevator control and sensor network from bare-metal SCADA servers to containerized edge-plus-cloud infrastructure enabling remote monitoring and reducing dependency on aging hardware
project_goal.rationale: Aging bare-metal SCADA hardware is end-of-life; remote monitoring reduces on-site maintenance labor

target_users.value: Grain elevator operators, facilities maintenance engineers, and remote operations center staff
target_users.rationale: Operators need real-time sensor visibility; maintenance staff need hardware health dashboards

project_class.rationale: Primary deliverable is platform and infrastructure, not an application -- Kubernetes at edge, AWS Outposts, Prometheus/Grafana observability stack

capabilities.rationale: No browser frontend; Go service layer, GitLab CI/CD, SCADA integration adapter, and batch summary jobs

complexity_level.rationale: Hybrid edge/cloud topology, legacy SCADA protocol integration (Modbus/OPC-UA), NFPA 61 dust explosion safety constraints

risk_level.rationale: Grain elevator control systems are safety-critical; remote control setpoint changes carry OSHA and NFPA 61 risk if authentication or audit controls fail

data_sensitivity.rationale: Sensor telemetry and control setpoints are operational data, not externally regulated, but sensitive to competitive exposure and safety-critical

external_exposure.rationale: System accessible only via VPN or AWS Direct Connect; no public internet endpoints

access_model.value: Custom JWT with operator/engineer/admin roles; setpoint write operations require two-factor confirmation with engineer role minimum
access_model.rationale: Safety-critical write operations need explicit role gating beyond basic authentication

feature_scope.value: Edge Kubernetes cluster running containerized SCADA adapter (Modbus/OPC-UA bridge), sensor telemetry ingestion to InfluxDB, bin aeration control setpoint API, remote monitoring Grafana dashboards, batch hourly summaries to cloud, maintenance event logging, alert engine for temperature and moisture thresholds
feature_scope.rationale: Full operational scope replacing bare-metal SCADA function

mvp_scope.value: Sensor telemetry ingestion from all elevator_bins, remote read-only Grafana dashboard, temperature and moisture threshold alerting, basic maintenance event logging
mvp_scope.rationale: Read-only visibility is safest first milestone before enabling remote write controls

security_baseline.value: mTLS between edge cluster and cloud, custom JWT for API access, VPN-only access, audit log for all setpoint change attempts, no direct internet exposure
security_baseline.rationale: Safety-critical OT environment requires defense-in-depth with mTLS at network boundary

frontend_stack.value: None -- Grafana dashboards served from edge cluster and cloud; no custom frontend development
frontend_stack.rationale: Grafana provides sufficient operational visualization without custom UI development

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 for SCADA adapter service, telemetry ingestor, setpoint API, and alert engine; custom JWT auth middleware
backend_stack.rationale: Go low-latency performance suits high-frequency sensor polling; single binary deployment simplifies edge container images

data_platform.value: InfluxDB 2 for time-series sensor data (hot: 30 days on-edge, 1 year in cloud); PostgreSQL 15 for maintenance_events, control_setpoints, and audit log
data_platform.rationale: InfluxDB for high-frequency sensor writes; PostgreSQL for structured operational records

hosting_target.value: Bare-metal Kubernetes (k3s) at elevator edge with AWS Outposts for cloud synchronization; S3 for long-term telemetry cold storage
hosting_target.rationale: Edge cluster needed for local control continuity during WAN outages; Outposts bridges edge to AWS services

privacy_retention_policy.value: Sensor telemetry: 30 days on-edge, 1 year in cloud InfluxDB, archived to S3 indefinitely. Maintenance events: 7 years per OSHA requirements. Setpoint audit log: 5 years.
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: OSHA grain handling and NFPA 61 documentation requirements drive long retention for maintenance and setpoint records

future_scope.value: Remote setpoint write capability with engineer approval workflow, predictive maintenance ML models on historical telemetry, multi-site elevator fleet management
future_scope.rationale: Remote write is Phase 2 pending safety validation of Phase 1 read-only operation

constraints.value: Edge cluster must continue operating during WAN outages; no cloud dependency for local sensor logging or alerting; harvest season deployment freeze October 1 through December 15
constraints.rationale: Elevator operations cannot be interrupted during harvest; WAN outage resilience is non-negotiable for safety

observability_baseline.value: Prometheus scraping Go services and k3s node metrics at edge; Grafana dashboards on edge and cloud; Alertmanager for bin temperature >38C and moisture >14%; PagerDuty for critical threshold breaches
observability_baseline.rationale: Temperature and moisture thresholds are spoilage and safety indicators requiring immediate operator response

execution_preference.value: Infrastructure-as-code via Terraform for Outposts and AWS resources; Helm charts for edge Kubernetes services; GitLab CI for image builds and Helm deployments
execution_preference.rationale: IaC ensures repeatable edge cluster provisioning across multiple elevator sites in future

compliance_context.value: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.272 grain handling facility safety standards; NFPA 61 dust explosion prevention requiring audit trails for all bin aeration setpoint changes; high risk level mandates explicit compliance documentation
compliance_context.rationale: Risk level high triggers compliance_context requirement; grain elevator OT systems are OSHA-regulated safety environments

project.goal: Replace bare-metal SCADA with containerized edge/cloud infrastructure for grain elevator sensor monitoring and control
project.target_users: Elevator operators, maintenance engineers, remote ops center
project.timeline: MVP before harvest season (deploy by September 1)

frontend.approach: Grafana dashboards provisioned via Helm chart, datasources configured as InfluxDB and Prometheus

backend.framework: Custom service layer with chi router

security.transport: mTLS between edge and cloud, TLS 1.3 for all internal services
security.audit: All setpoint change attempts logged to PostgreSQL audit table

devops.hosting: k3s edge cluster plus AWS Outposts
devops.iac: Terraform for AWS resources, Helm for k3s services

constraints.wan_resilience: Full local operation during WAN outage

reasoner_reviews.product: Remote monitoring capability is the primary value unlock. The MVP is correctly scoped to read-only -- remote write requires a separate safety validation phase given NFPA 61 aeration setpoint implications. Maintenance event logging has direct ROI for predicting equipment failure before harvest season.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The k3s edge cluster approach is sound for WAN resilience. The round 1 plan did not specify how local Alertmanager continues to page operators when the cloud link is down. Edge-local PagerDuty fallback via local SMTP relay must be addressed. InfluxDB replication from edge to cloud needs explicit backpressure handling to avoid data loss during WAN disruption.
reasoner_reviews.security: The round 1 plan mentioned mTLS but did not specify certificate management -- who issues edge cluster client certificates, rotation schedule, and what happens when a certificate expires mid-harvest. This is a blocking gap for a high-risk safety system.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Harvest season deployment freeze (Oct 1 - Dec 15) means MVP must be fully operational by September 1. Given current timeline, Phase 1 must be complete by August 15 to allow 2-week soak before freeze. No changes can be deployed during harvest regardless of severity.

backend.service_design: Four Go services as separate k3s Deployments: scada-adapter (polls Modbus TCP registers and OPC-UA nodes every 5s, writes to InfluxDB), telemetry-ingestor (HTTP endpoint for future sensor push clients), setpoint-api (chi router, JWT middleware, write to control_setpoints and audit_log), alert-engine (subscribes to InfluxDB continuous queries, fires Alertmanager webhooks on threshold breach).
backend.failure_handling: scada-adapter implements circuit breaker: if InfluxDB write fails 5 consecutive times, buffers to local BoltDB and retries with exponential backoff. setpoint-api returns 503 if PostgreSQL is unreachable -- no partial writes. alert-engine maintains local SMTP relay config for operator pages during WAN outage.
backend.scaling_notes: Edge k3s cluster sized for single elevator site (2 nodes bare-metal). Multi-site expansion requires per-site k3s cluster with centralized Grafana in AWS. InfluxDB cloud instance scales independently of edge.

security.auth_design: Custom JWT issued by edge auth-service (Go). Tokens expire in 8 hours. Setpoint write endpoints require engineer or admin role claim plus X-Confirm-Action header to prevent accidental writes.
security.secrets_management: Edge cluster secrets stored in k3s encrypted etcd. AWS Secrets Manager for cloud-side credentials. mTLS certificates issued by internal CA (step-ca), rotated every 90 days via Kubernetes CertificateSigningRequest workflow.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: PostgreSQL audit_log table: every setpoint change attempt logged with operator JWT sub, bin_id, previous_setpoint, requested_setpoint, outcome (allowed/denied), timestamp. Retained 5 years. Exported quarterly to S3 for NFPA 61 documentation.

data.entities: sensor_nodes (id, bin_id, node_type ENUM modbus/opcua, protocol_address, polling_interval_sec, last_seen_at). elevator_bins (id, site_id, bin_number, capacity_tonnes, grain_type, fill_level_pct). moisture_readings (time TIMESTAMPTZ, bin_id, moisture_pct, sensor_node_id) -- InfluxDB measurement. temperature_logs (time TIMESTAMPTZ, bin_id, temp_celsius, sensor_node_id) -- InfluxDB measurement. control_setpoints (id, bin_id, parameter ENUM aeration_fan_speed/fill_target, value, set_by UUID, set_at TIMESTAMPTZ, rationale). maintenance_events (id, bin_id, event_type, description, technician_id, event_at TIMESTAMPTZ, resolved_at).
data.storage_design: InfluxDB 2 on edge: sensor data retained 30 days. Cloud InfluxDB 2 (AWS Outposts-local): replicated from edge via InfluxDB replication API, retained 1 year. S3 Intelligent-Tiering for Parquet exports beyond 1 year. PostgreSQL 15 on edge for control_setpoints, maintenance_events, audit_log.
data.data_risks: InfluxDB replication lag during WAN outage may cause edge-cloud divergence. Mitigation: edge InfluxDB retains full 30-day buffer; cloud sync resumes automatically on reconnect via InfluxDB replication queue.

devops.deployment_topology: Edge site: 2x bare-metal nodes running k3s. Workloads: scada-adapter, telemetry-ingestor, setpoint-api, alert-engine, Prometheus, Grafana, InfluxDB 2, step-ca. AWS: Outposts rack for cloud-side InfluxDB and Grafana aggregation, S3 for cold storage, Secrets Manager.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI: Go build and test, Docker image build and push to private GitLab registry, Helm chart lint, Helm upgrade via SSH to edge k3s kubeconfig stored in GitLab CI secrets. Deployment freeze enforcement: CI pipeline checks current date against freeze window and blocks deployment if within Oct 1 - Dec 15.
devops.rollback_strategy: Helm rollback to previous release revision. scada-adapter has BoltDB local buffer so rollback does not lose telemetry buffered during deployment.
devops.devops_risks: k3s node failure with only 2 nodes means single-node operation during maintenance. Mitigation: stateful workloads (InfluxDB, PostgreSQL) use PVCs on shared NFS storage between nodes; k3s HA not enabled for MVP but planned for Phase 2.

ISS-001.title: mTLS certificate lifecycle not specified
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan stated mTLS between edge and cloud but did not specify certificate authority, issuance process, rotation schedule, or behavior when edge certificate expires mid-harvest. For a high-risk safety system this is a blocking gap.

history.note: Security reviewer identified missing cert lifecycle spec

ISS-002.title: WAN outage alerting path not specified
ISS-002.detail: When the WAN link is down, the edge cluster cannot reach PagerDuty cloud API. The round 1 plan had no fallback alerting path for operators during WAN outage, leaving bins unmonitored during network disruptions.

history.note: Architect reasoner flagged missing local alerting fallback

focus_issues.title: mTLS certificate lifecycle not specified
focus_issues.detail: Must specify internal CA (step-ca), 90-day rotation, and harvest-season certificate freeze procedure.

focus_issues.title: WAN outage alerting path not specified
focus_issues.detail: Edge Alertmanager must have local SMTP relay fallback for operator paging during WAN outage.

latest_recommendations: Specify step-ca as internal CA with 90-day rotation and pre-harvest certificate renewal procedure
Add local SMTP relay to alert-engine for WAN-outage operator paging fallback

convergence_state.reason: Two high severity safety-system gaps block convergence

previous_audits.summary: Infrastructure plan captures the edge/cloud topology and Go service architecture well but leaves two safety-critical gaps: certificate lifecycle for mTLS and WAN-outage alerting path. These are blocking for a high-risk NFPA 61 environment.
strengths: InfluxDB edge/cloud replication with BoltDB buffer for WAN outage is architecturally sound
Go service decomposition into scada-adapter, setpoint-api, and alert-engine is clean
PostgreSQL audit_log for NFPA 61 setpoint documentation is correctly specified
concerns: mTLS certificate authority, rotation, and expiry handling completely absent
No fallback alerting path when WAN is down and PagerDuty is unreachable
Deployment freeze enforcement not described for harvest season
blocking_issues: ISS-001: mTLS cert lifecycle undefined -- expiry mid-harvest would break all edge-cloud communication
ISS-002: WAN outage alerting gap -- operators cannot be paged during network disruption
recommendations: Add step-ca as internal CA with 90-day cert rotation; specify pre-harvest cert renewal enforced 30 days before freeze window
Configure Alertmanager with local SMTP relay as primary route and PagerDuty as secondary for WAN-dependent alerts

issue_updates.title: mTLS certificate lifecycle not specified
issue_updates.detail: security_and_compliance section said mTLS without specifying CA, issuance, or rotation

issue_updates.title: WAN outage alerting path not specified
issue_updates.detail: observability section listed PagerDuty only with no WAN-down fallback

previous_plan.title: Grain Elevator SCADA Containerization v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Edge-plus-cloud infrastructure replacing bare-metal SCADA for grain elevator sensor monitoring using k3s, Go services, InfluxDB, and AWS Outposts with Grafana dashboards.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: mTLS between edge and cloud specified. Custom JWT for setpoint API. Audit log for NFPA 61 compliance. Certificate authority and rotation not specified.
previous_plan.observability: Prometheus on edge cluster. Grafana dashboards. PagerDuty for bin temperature and moisture alerts. WAN-outage fallback not addressed.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 addresses two high-severity safety-system gaps: ISS-001 requires specifying the internal CA (step-ca), 90-day rotation schedule, and pre-harvest certificate renewal procedure; ISS-002 requires adding local SMTP relay as Alertmanager fallback route for WAN-outage operator paging. This is a weak-quality row reflecting a plan that resolves the blocking issues but keeps supporting sections at minimal depth, reflecting realistic revision behavior where non-blocking sections are not fully rebuilt.

fix_report.action_taken: Added step-ca as internal CA to security_and_compliance with 90-day mTLS certificate rotation, pre-harvest renewal enforcement (30 days before October 1 freeze), and certificate expiry monitoring via Prometheus cert-manager exporter.
fix_report.expected_outcome: mTLS certificate lifecycle is fully specified; harvest-season expiry risk is mitigated by mandatory pre-freeze renewal gate in CI pipeline.

fix_report.action_taken: Added local SMTP relay (Postfix on edge k3s) as primary Alertmanager receiver route. PagerDuty demoted to secondary route only when SMTP relay fails. Updated observability section to document dual-path alerting topology.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Operators receive bin temperature and moisture alerts via local email even when WAN link is down.

target_output.title: Grain Elevator SCADA Containerization
target_output.executive_summary: Edge-plus-cloud infrastructure for grain elevator sensor monitoring and control, replacing aging bare-metal SCADA hardware with k3s containerized Go services, InfluxDB time-series ingestion, and Grafana operational dashboards. Runs autonomously during WAN outages. mTLS certificate lifecycle managed by step-ca with pre-harvest renewal enforcement.
target_output.architecture_overview: Hybrid topology: bare-metal k3s cluster at each elevator site running Go SCADA adapter (Modbus TCP and OPC-UA), InfluxDB 2 for edge time-series, Prometheus and Grafana for local monitoring, and Alertmanager with local SMTP relay for WAN-resilient operator alerts. Edge cluster synchronizes sensor data to cloud-side InfluxDB on AWS Outposts via InfluxDB replication API with BoltDB buffer for WAN disruption. PostgreSQL 15 on edge stores maintenance_events, control_setpoints, and NFPA 61 audit log. All edge-to-cloud communication uses mTLS with certificates issued by step-ca running on the edge cluster, rotated every 90 days.
target_output.technology_stack: Edge: k3s (lightweight Kubernetes), Go 1.22 services (scada-adapter, setpoint-api, alert-engine), InfluxDB 2, PostgreSQL 15, Prometheus, Grafana, Alertmanager, Postfix SMTP relay, step-ca. Cloud (AWS Outposts): InfluxDB 2 aggregation instance, Grafana fleet dashboard, S3 Intelligent-Tiering for cold telemetry. CI/CD: GitLab CI with Helm chart deployments. IaC: Terraform for AWS/Outposts resources.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Sensor Telemetry Ingestion: scada-adapter polls elevator_bins sensor_nodes via Modbus TCP every 5 seconds, writes moisture_readings and temperature_logs to edge InfluxDB. Threshold Alerting: alert-engine evaluates InfluxDB continuous queries against bin temperature >38C and moisture >14% thresholds, routes to Alertmanager. Operational Dashboards: Grafana panels for per-bin temperature heatmap, moisture trend, and sensor_node last-seen status. Control Setpoint API: setpoint-api exposes PUT /bins/:bin_id/setpoints with engineer JWT and X-Confirm-Action header, writes to control_setpoints and audit_log. Maintenance Event Logging: POST /maintenance/events records technician activity for OSHA 7-year retention.
target_output.system_components: Edge k3s cluster (2 bare-metal nodes, shared NFS PVCs): scada-adapter Deployment, setpoint-api Deployment, alert-engine Deployment, InfluxDB 2 StatefulSet, PostgreSQL 15 StatefulSet, Prometheus Deployment, Grafana Deployment, Alertmanager Deployment, Postfix SMTP relay Deployment, step-ca Deployment. AWS Outposts: cloud InfluxDB 2 instance, Grafana aggregation instance. AWS: S3 for cold telemetry, Secrets Manager for cloud credentials.
target_output.workflows: Sensor Ingestion and Alerting: scada-adapter polls Modbus TCP register bank for each elevator_bin sensor_node every 5s, writes line protocol to edge InfluxDB bucket 'telemetry'. alert-engine runs InfluxDB task every 60s evaluating 5-min rolling mean of temp_celsius per bin; if mean >38C, POSTs to Alertmanager /api/v1/alerts. Alertmanager routes to Postfix SMTP relay (local operator email) as primary; if SMTP fails, routes to PagerDuty webhook. During WAN outage, PagerDuty route fails silently; SMTP relay delivers to on-site operator terminals. Control Setpoint Change: Engineer authenticates to setpoint-api with JWT (role=engineer), sends PUT /bins/:bin_id/setpoints with X-Confirm-Action: true header and new aeration_fan_speed value. setpoint-api reads current value from control_setpoints, writes audit_log entry with previous/requested values and engineer sub, applies change via SCADA adapter write-back, returns updated setpoint. Audit log entry retained 5 years for NFPA 61 documentation.
target_output.data_model: sensor_nodes (id UUID PK, bin_id UUID FK, node_type ENUM modbus/opcua, protocol_address, polling_interval_sec INT, last_seen_at TIMESTAMPTZ). elevator_bins (id UUID PK, site_id UUID, bin_number INT, capacity_tonnes DECIMAL, grain_type VARCHAR, fill_level_pct DECIMAL). moisture_readings and temperature_logs: InfluxDB measurements with tags bin_id, sensor_node_id and fields moisture_pct / temp_celsius. control_setpoints (id UUID PK, bin_id UUID FK, parameter ENUM aeration_fan_speed/fill_target, value DECIMAL, set_by UUID, set_at TIMESTAMPTZ, rationale TEXT). maintenance_events (id UUID PK, bin_id UUID FK, event_type VARCHAR, description TEXT, technician_id UUID, event_at TIMESTAMPTZ, resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ). audit_log (id UUID PK, operator_id UUID, bin_id UUID FK, action, previous_value, requested_value, outcome ENUM allowed/denied, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: Go chi router at /api/v1. Sensor read: GET /bins/:bin_id/telemetry?metric=temperature&window=1h (queries edge InfluxDB). Setpoints: GET /bins/:bin_id/setpoints, PUT /bins/:bin_id/setpoints (engineer role + X-Confirm-Action required). Maintenance: POST /maintenance/events, GET /maintenance/events?bin_id=X. Audit: GET /audit/setpoints?from=2026-01-01 (admin role). Health: GET /healthz (liveness probe for k3s). All responses JSON; errors follow RFC 7807.
target_output.security_and_compliance: mTLS between edge k3s cluster and cloud Outposts: step-ca runs as k3s Deployment issuing client certificates to scada-adapter and cloud replication service. Certificates valid 90 days. step-ca ACME renewal runs automatically via Kubernetes CertificateSigningRequest. Pre-harvest gate: GitLab CI pipeline checks cert expiry dates 30 days before October 1; if any cert expires before December 15, pipeline blocks deployment and opens alert. Custom JWT: Go auth-service issues 8-hour tokens with role claims. Setpoint write operations require engineer or admin role plus X-Confirm-Action header. PostgreSQL audit_log records all setpoint change attempts for NFPA 61 documentation; retained 5 years, quarterly S3 export. OSHA 7-year retention for maintenance_events enforced via pg_cron deletion job.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitLab CI pipeline: Go unit tests, Docker image build, push to GitLab container registry, Helm lint, Helm upgrade via SSH to edge k3s kubeconfig (stored as GitLab masked CI variable). Deployment freeze enforcement: pipeline script checks date against Oct 1 - Dec 15 window; if in freeze, pipeline exits with error unless OVERRIDE_FREEZE=true is set by admin. Helm chart manages all edge workloads; values.yaml per site. Terraform manages AWS Outposts resources and S3 lifecycle rules (Glacier transition at 13 months). Rollback: Helm rollback to previous revision; scada-adapter BoltDB buffer preserves telemetry during rollback window.
target_output.observability: Prometheus scrapes all Go services (default /metrics) and k3s node-exporter every 15 seconds. Grafana dashboards: per-bin temperature heatmap (7-day rolling), moisture trend per bin, sensor_node last-seen status, alert-engine evaluation latency. Alertmanager routes: Route 1 (primary) -- Postfix SMTP relay to on-site operator distribution list; Route 2 (fallback, continue=false) -- PagerDuty webhook for cloud-connected pages. Inhibition rules suppress duplicate alerts for same bin within 15 minutes. Certificate expiry alert: Prometheus blackbox exporter checks step-ca certificate chain; alert fires if any cert expires within 45 days.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Edge hardware costs are sunk (bare-metal reuse). AWS Outposts recurring: ~$200/month amortized for InfluxDB instance and S3 cold storage. GitLab SaaS included in existing license. Multi-site expansion: replicate k3s Helm values per site, add cloud Grafana datasource per site InfluxDB endpoint. No auto-scaling needed at edge; capacity is fixed per site hardware.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 - Read-Only Monitoring (MVP, by September 1): Deploy k3s cluster, scada-adapter with Modbus TCP integration for all elevator_bins, edge InfluxDB ingestion, Prometheus and Grafana dashboards, Alertmanager with SMTP relay and PagerDuty dual-path, step-ca mTLS with pre-harvest cert renewal gate, maintenance event logging, GitLab CI pipeline with freeze enforcement. Phase 2 - Remote Write Controls (post-harvest, January): setpoint-api with engineer JWT and audit_log, NFPA 61 setpoint change documentation pipeline, cloud Grafana fleet dashboard, InfluxDB cloud replication. Phase 3 - Predictive Analytics (Q3 next year): ML models on historical telemetry for predictive maintenance, multi-site fleet management dashboard.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go: golangci-lint in CI with errcheck and staticcheck enabled. All InfluxDB writes go through scada-adapter -- no direct InfluxDB writes from other services. setpoint-api must validate bin_id against PostgreSQL elevator_bins before accepting any write. Helm values files are per-site; never hardcode site-specific addresses in Go binaries. GitLab CI secrets (kubeconfig, step-ca token) must be masked variables, never committed to repo.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: k3s 2-node cluster has no HA for stateful workloads -- single node failure requires manual failover of InfluxDB and PostgreSQL PVCs. Mitigated by NFS shared storage and k3s node affinity rules. Risk: step-ca certificate expiry mid-harvest breaks edge-cloud mTLS -- mitigated by pre-harvest CI gate requiring renewal 30 days before freeze. Tradeoff: local step-ca over AWS ACM PCA -- step-ca runs fully on-edge, no cloud dependency for cert issuance during WAN outage. Risk: harvest season deployment freeze blocks security patches -- mitigation: OVERRIDE_FREEZE process requires VP Engineering approval and is documented in incident runbook.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed step-ca over HashiCorp Vault PKI for certificate authority -- step-ca has smaller operational footprint suitable for edge k3s single-site deployment. Confirmed Postfix SMTP relay over SNS for WAN-resilient alerting -- SNS requires internet connectivity; Postfix delivers to local MTA without WAN. Confirmed InfluxDB 2 replication API over custom ETL -- native replication handles backpressure and reconnect logic without additional code.

metadata.notes: Round 2 grain elevator SCADA infrastructure: closes mTLS cert lifecycle (ISS-001) and WAN-outage alerting (ISS-002); plan quality weak as non-critical sections remain thin.

project_goal.value: Marketing landing page for an organic certification consulting firm explaining USDA NOP certification tiers, compliance deadlines, and service packages while driving prospective farm client inquiries via a contact form
project_goal.rationale: Core marketing objective: convert farm visitors to inquiry submissions

target_users.value: Small farm owners and operators seeking USDA NOP organic certification guidance
target_users.rationale: Target audience identified in marketing brief

project_class.rationale: Static marketing page with no server-side application logic; Astro generates static HTML at build time

capabilities.rationale: Static content delivery and Vercel Analytics for conversion tracking; no backend runtime required

complexity_level.rationale: Static site with one contact form, no authentication, no database

risk_level.rationale: No user accounts, no personal data stored server-side, no payment processing

data_sensitivity.rationale: Contact form submissions routed via Formspree to consulting team email; no PII stored in the platform

external_exposure.rationale: Public marketing page accessible to any visitor without authentication

access_model.value: Public read-only; contact form submission via Formspree third-party service with honeypot spam protection
access_model.rationale: No user accounts or login required for a public marketing page

feature_scope.value: Hero section with certification value proposition, USDA NOP certification tier comparison table (Transitional/Certified Organic/Biodynamic), compliance deadline calendar widget for USDA NOP annual renewal dates, service package cards with pricing tiers, farm type selector filtering relevant compliance deadlines, client testimonials, contact inquiry form, Vercel Analytics event tracking for CTA clicks
feature_scope.rationale: Complete marketing page feature set identified in design brief

mvp_scope.value: Hero, certification tier descriptions, static compliance deadline list, service package cards, contact form with Formspree, Vercel Analytics
mvp_scope.rationale: Core conversion funnel without interactive deadline calculator

security_baseline.value: HTTPS enforced via Vercel Edge Network, Content-Security-Policy header via Vercel headers config, honeypot field on contact form, no server-side data storage
security_baseline.rationale: Low risk; CSP and HTTPS sufficient for public static page

frontend_stack.value: Astro 4 with Tailwind CSS; MDX for certification content pages; Astro Islands for interactive compliance calendar widget (Preact)
frontend_stack.rationale: Astro delivers zero-JS by default; Preact Islands only for interactive deadline calendar

backend_stack.value: None -- static site, no server-side runtime
backend_stack.rationale: Landing page has no application backend; contact form handled by Formspree

data_platform.value: None -- no database; content managed as Astro MDX files in repository
data_platform.rationale: Static content; no runtime data storage required

hosting_target.value: Vercel Edge Network with GitHub integration for automatic preview deployments on PR
hosting_target.rationale: Vercel native Astro support with edge CDN and preview deploy workflow

privacy_retention_policy.value: No PII stored on the platform; Formspree processes contact submissions per their data processing agreement; Vercel Analytics is cookieless and GDPR-compliant by default
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: No server-side data collection means no retention obligations on the platform side

future_scope.value: Interactive NOP compliance deadline calculator with farm-type input, blog section with certification guidance articles, client portal login for existing consulting clients
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 features pending launch and inquiry volume validation

constraints.value: Must achieve Lighthouse performance score >90 on mobile; page weight under 500KB; no cookie consent banner required (cookieless analytics)
constraints.rationale: Farm clients often access from rural mobile connections; performance is conversion-critical

observability_baseline.value: Vercel Analytics for page views, CTA click events, and contact form submission rate; Vercel deployment status notifications via Slack webhook
observability_baseline.rationale: Cookieless analytics sufficient for conversion tracking on a low-risk marketing page

execution_preference.value: Single developer, trunk-based development, Vercel preview deploys for stakeholder review before merge
execution_preference.rationale: Small team; preview deploys accelerate client feedback cycle

project.goal: Convert farm visitors to certification inquiry submissions via clear NOP content and low-friction contact form
project.target_users: Small farm owners seeking USDA organic certification

frontend.interactive: Preact Islands for compliance deadline calendar widget
frontend.content: MDX for certification tier and compliance content

devops.ci: GitHub Actions for Astro build check

reasoner_reviews.product: Three rounds of revision on a landing page signal that the content structure needed iteration to match the consulting firm's actual service offerings. The compliance deadline calendar widget is the differentiating feature that separates this page from generic consulting sites -- it should be prominent. The farm type selector filtering deadlines is high conversion value and should be in MVP.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Astro 4 with Preact Islands is the right choice for the deadline calendar -- zero JS shipped for the static content, hydration only for the interactive widget. The round 2 plan had the calendar as a client:load Island causing unnecessary hydration on page load; switch to client:visible to defer hydration until the widget scrolls into view.
reasoner_reviews.security: Contact form via Formspree is appropriate. Round 2 plan did not include CSP header configuration in vercel.json. Add script-src and form-action directives to prevent third-party script injection given the public-internet exposure.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Lighthouse >90 on mobile requires image optimization. Round 2 included unoptimized testimonial photos. Astro built-in Image component with WebP output and width/height attributes prevents layout shift.

frontend.app_structure: Astro 4 project with src/pages/index.astro as the single page. src/components/ contains: HeroSection.astro, CertificationTiers.astro, ComplianceCalendar.tsx (Preact Island, client:visible), ServicePackages.astro, FarmTypeSelector.tsx (Preact Island, client:visible), Testimonials.astro, ContactForm.astro. src/content/ holds MDX files for certification tier descriptions and compliance deadline data (structured frontmatter for calendar widget).
frontend.pages_and_flows: Single-page layout: Hero with NOP value proposition and primary CTA ('Get Your Certification Roadmap') -> Certification Tiers section (Transitional / Certified Organic / Biodynamic comparison table) -> Compliance Calendar widget (Preact Island: farm type selector drives deadline list filtering to show Annual Renewal, Transition Period milestones, Record-Keeping submission dates) -> Service Packages with tier cards -> Testimonials from certified farm clients -> Contact Form (Formspree endpoint, honeypot field, confirmation message on success).
frontend.performance_notes: Astro Image component for all testimonial photos with WebP output, explicit width/height, and loading=lazy. Tailwind CSS purged to production subset. Preact Islands deferred with client:visible. Target: Lighthouse mobile performance >90, LCP <2.5s, CLS <0.1.
frontend.frontend_security_notes: CSP header via vercel.json: script-src 'self' https://f.formspree.io; form-action https://formspree.io; img-src 'self' data:. Formspree honeypot field (_gotcha hidden input) prevents basic bot submissions.

devops.deployment_topology: Vercel project connected to GitHub main branch. Automatic production deploy on merge to main. Preview deploy on every PR branch. No server functions -- pure static output.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions workflow: npm ci, astro check (TypeScript), astro build. Vercel CLI deploy preview in CI for PR branches. Lighthouse CI action runs on production deploy and fails if mobile score drops below 90.
devops.observability_stack: Vercel Analytics: page views, Web Vitals (LCP, CLS, FID) per route, custom events for CTA clicks (track('cta_click', {section: 'hero'})) and form submissions (track('inquiry_submitted')). Slack webhook notification on Vercel deploy success/failure.

ISS-001.title: CSP headers absent from vercel.json
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 and 2 plans did not include Content-Security-Policy header configuration. Added in round 3 via vercel.json headers block.

history.note: Security reviewer flagged missing CSP for public page
Still absent in round 2 vercel.json config
