target_output.title: NOP Certification Consulting Landing Page
target_output.executive_summary: A public marketing landing page built with Astro 4 and Tailwind CSS on Vercel for an organic certification consulting firm targeting small farm operators. Features a USDA NOP certification tier comparison, an interactive compliance deadline calendar (Preact Island, client:visible), service package pricing, and a Formspree contact form. Vercel Analytics tracks CTA clicks and inquiry submissions without cookies. Lighthouse mobile score 92.
target_output.architecture_overview: Fully static Astro 4 site built at CI time and deployed to Vercel Edge Network CDN. No server runtime, no database, no authentication. Interactive elements (compliance deadline calendar and farm type selector) implemented as Preact Islands with client:visible directive, deferring Preact JS hydration until the component enters the viewport. Contact form submissions POST to Formspree external service endpoint -- no server-side form handling required. Vercel Analytics injected via Astro Vercel adapter for cookieless Web Vitals and custom event tracking. vercel.json configures CSP, caching, and redirect headers at the edge.
target_output.technology_stack: Astro 4, Tailwind CSS, Preact 10 (Islands only), MDX for certification content, Astro Image component (WebP output). Hosting: Vercel Edge Network. Contact form: Formspree. Analytics: Vercel Analytics (cookieless). CI: GitHub Actions with Lighthouse CI. Content: MDX files in src/content/ with structured YAML frontmatter for certification tiers and deadline data.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Hero Section: NOP value proposition headline, primary CTA button ('Get Your Certification Roadmap') tracked as cta_click event in Vercel Analytics. Certification Tier Comparison: three-column table (Transitional / Certified Organic / Biodynamic) with USDA NOP requirements, typical timeline, and key record-keeping obligations per tier. Compliance Deadline Calendar: Preact Island (client:visible) reading deadline MDX frontmatter; farm type selector (Vegetables / Livestock / Processed Products) filters displayed deadlines to show Annual OSP Renewal, Transition Period completion, and Record-Keeping submission dates. Service Packages: three card tiers (Starter Audit / Full Certification Guidance / Ongoing Compliance Retainer) with scope and pricing. Testimonials: five certified farm client quotes with Astro Image WebP headshots. Contact Form: Formspree POST with farm name, contact name, email, farm type, and message fields; honeypot _gotcha field; success confirmation message rendered client-side.
target_output.system_components: Vercel Edge Network: serves pre-built Astro static output globally with edge caching (Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable for hashed assets; max-age=0 for index.astro). vercel.json headers block: CSP, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options. GitHub repository: Astro source, MDX content files, Preact Island components, Tailwind config. Formspree: external contact form endpoint (no data stored on platform). Vercel Analytics: cookieless, injected by Astro Vercel adapter.
target_output.workflows: Farm Visitor Inquiry Flow: Visitor lands on page -> reads certification tier comparison -> selects farm type in FarmTypeSelector Preact Island -> ComplianceCalendar filters to show USDA NOP deadlines relevant to their farm type (e.g., Vegetables: Annual Renewal by October 1, Transitional Start documentation by March 1) -> reads matching service package -> fills ContactForm (farm name, type, question) -> Formspree validates and emails submission to consulting team -> visitor sees 'Thank you, we will contact you within 1 business day' confirmation. Deployment and Preview Flow: Developer pushes branch -> GitHub Actions runs astro check and astro build -> Vercel creates preview deployment with unique URL -> Stakeholder reviews NOP content on preview -> PR merged to main -> Vercel production deploy -> Lighthouse CI runs and asserts mobile score >=90.
target_output.data_model: No runtime database. Content stored as MDX files: certification_tiers (src/content/tiers/*.mdx with frontmatter: tier_name, requirements[], typical_timeline, record_keeping_obligations[]). compliance_deadlines (src/content/deadlines/*.mdx with frontmatter: deadline_name, farm_types[], date_pattern, usda_citation, description). service_packages (src/content/packages/*.mdx with frontmatter: tier_name, scope[], price_range, cta_label). testimonials (src/content/testimonials/*.mdx with frontmatter: farm_name, farm_type, state, quote, photo_src).
target_output.api_design: No custom API. External integrations: Formspree POST https://formspree.io/f/{form_id} with fields farm_name, contact_name, email, farm_type, message, _gotcha (honeypot). Vercel Analytics track() calls: track('cta_click', {section: 'hero'}), track('inquiry_submitted', {farm_type: selectedType}), track('calendar_interaction', {farm_type: selectedType}). No backend endpoints.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced by Vercel Edge Network (HSTS header). vercel.json headers block: Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' https://f.formspree.io; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; form-action https://formspree.io; img-src 'self' data:; frame-ancestors 'none'. X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff. X-Frame-Options: DENY. Contact form: Formspree honeypot (_gotcha hidden input) blocks basic bot submissions. No PII stored on platform; Formspree processes submissions per their DPA. Vercel Analytics is cookieless; no cookie consent banner required. USDA NOP marketing guidelines: no false certification claims; all tier descriptions sourced from NOP Program Handbook citations.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Vercel GitHub integration: automatic production deploy on merge to main branch; preview deployment on every PR with unique vercel.app URL for stakeholder review. GitHub Actions CI workflow: checkout, npm ci, astro check (TypeScript and Astro component validation), astro build (fails on build error). Lighthouse CI action runs against production URL post-deploy: asserts mobile performance >=90, accessibility >=90, best-practices >=90. If Lighthouse gate fails, Slack notification sent to #web-deploys channel. vercel.json: caching headers (immutable for hashed assets), CSP headers block, redirect from /certification to /#tiers for any legacy links.
target_output.observability: Vercel Analytics dashboard: page views, bounce rate, session duration, Web Vitals (LCP, CLS, FID) tracked per Astro route. Custom events: cta_click (section dimension), inquiry_submitted (farm_type dimension), calendar_interaction (farm_type dimension). Lighthouse CI badge in README shows current mobile score. Vercel deploy notifications posted to Slack #web-deploys via webhook. No server-side logs required (no server runtime).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Vercel Hobby tier ($0/month) sufficient for expected traffic (<10K monthly visitors). Formspree free tier (50 submissions/month) adequate for initial inquiry volume; upgrade to Formspree Gold ($8/month) if submissions exceed 50/month. No infrastructure scaling required; Vercel Edge Network handles traffic spikes globally via CDN.
target_output.phased_implementation: Single launch phase (2 weeks): Hero with CTA tracking, NOP certification tier comparison from MDX content, compliance deadline calendar Preact Island (client:visible) with farm type filtering, service packages, Testimonials with Astro Image WebP, ContactForm with Formspree and honeypot, vercel.json CSP headers, Vercel Analytics custom events, Lighthouse CI gate. Post-launch (Phase 2): interactive NOP compliance deadline calculator with custom date inputs, blog section using Astro content collections for certification guidance articles.
target_output.development_guidelines: astro check must pass in CI before any merge. All images must use Astro Image component with format='webp', explicit width and height, and loading='lazy' -- no raw img tags. Preact Islands must use client:visible unless interaction is required above the fold. MDX frontmatter schemas defined with Zod in src/content/config.ts; content shape changes require schema update. Vercel Analytics track() calls must include at minimum one dimension property for segmentation.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Formspree service outage prevents inquiry submissions -- mitigated by displaying consulting team email address as fallback below the form. Tradeoff: MDX content files over a headless CMS (Contentful) -- MDX in repository is simpler for a single-developer project and avoids CMS dependency and cost; acceptable until content editors who lack Git access need to update certification tier descriptions. Risk: USDA NOP regulation changes require content updates -- mitigated by MDX sourcing from USDA NOP Program Handbook citations, making outdated content easy to identify during annual review.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Formspree over Netlify Forms -- project is deployed to Vercel which does not have a built-in form service; Formspree integrates via simple POST action. Confirmed Vercel Analytics over Plausible or Fathom -- Vercel Analytics is free on Hobby plan and natively integrated via Astro Vercel adapter with zero configuration. Confirmed single-page layout over multi-page Astro site -- farm visitors arriving from agricultural trade publication ads should see full pitch in one scroll without navigation friction.

metadata.notes: Round 3 NOP certification landing page: resolves all three open issues (CSP headers, image optimization, Island hydration). Moderate quality appropriate for simple landing_page class.

profile.domain: AI Crop Disease Detection for Smallholder Farmers

project_goal.value: AI-powered crop disease detection system for smallholder farmers that analyzes smartphone photos of plant leaves and returns real-time diagnoses with treatment recommendations, designed for low-bandwidth rural environments with optional offline inference on-device
project_goal.rationale: Addresses critical agronomic advisory gap for smallholders lacking access to extension officers

target_users.value: Smallholder farmers in low-bandwidth rural areas, agricultural extension officers monitoring regional disease outbreaks
target_users.rationale: Two distinct user types with different interaction patterns: farmers as primary submitters, extension officers as alert recipients

project_class.rationale: Core value is ML inference pipeline for plant disease classification; mobile app is the delivery vector

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, ai_llm, data, integrations, analytics
capabilities.rationale: React Native mobile app, FastAPI inference backend, LLM for treatment recommendation text generation, PostgreSQL for diagnosis history, Firebase push for extension officer alerts

complexity_level.rationale: On-device TFLite model for offline inference, cloud vision model as fallback, LLM treatment text generation, regional disease aggregation pipeline, and low-bandwidth image compression requirements

risk_level.rationale: Misdiagnosis could lead to incorrect treatment and crop loss; model confidence scoring and human-review pathway mitigate but cannot eliminate this risk

data_sensitivity.rationale: Farmer geolocation and crop data is operationally sensitive but not regulated; no health or financial data

external_exposure.rationale: Farmers access via public internet on mobile networks; no VPN requirement feasible for rural smallholder population

access_model.value: Firebase Auth with anonymous auth for offline-first farmers; optional account creation for diagnosis history sync; extension officers have named accounts with push notification permissions
access_model.rationale: Anonymous auth lowers barrier to entry for rural farmers; named accounts needed for extension officer alert routing

feature_scope.value: React Native mobile app: leaf photo capture, on-device TFLite inference (offline), cloud inference fallback (online), ranked disease diagnosis with confidence scores, LLM-generated treatment recommendation text, diagnosis history per farmer, offline queue for submissions during connectivity loss. Extension officer dashboard: regional disease prevalence heatmap, emerging outbreak alerts, area-level submission counts. Data pipeline: regional aggregation of confirmed diagnoses for outbreak detection.
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature scope including offline-first mobile and extension officer alerting

mvp_scope.value: Mobile app with on-device TFLite inference for top 10 crop diseases, FastAPI cloud inference endpoint, LLM treatment text generation, Firebase Auth anonymous login, diagnosis saved locally, basic extension officer web dashboard showing regional submission heatmap
mvp_scope.rationale: Core farmer workflow and extension officer visibility achievable in MVP timeframe

security_baseline.value: Firebase Auth (anonymous and named), HTTPS for all cloud API calls, image uploads to GCS signed URLs, API rate limiting (30 requests/hour per device), model confidence threshold (minimum 60% confidence before returning diagnosis)
security_baseline.rationale: Rate limiting prevents abuse of inference endpoints; confidence threshold prevents low-quality diagnoses from being surfaced

frontend_stack.value: React Native 0.74 with Expo 51, TypeScript, Expo Camera for leaf photo capture, TFLite React Native for on-device inference, React Query for cloud API calls
frontend_stack.rationale: Expo managed workflow simplifies OTA updates which are critical for model updates without full app store release

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI on GCP Cloud Run; Google Cloud Vision API as cloud inference option; Gemini 1.5 Flash for treatment recommendation generation; Vertex AI for model hosting
backend_stack.rationale: Cloud Run autoscales to zero for cost efficiency; Gemini Flash is cost-optimized for treatment text generation

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 (Cloud SQL) for farmer_profiles, crop_images, disease_diagnoses, treatment_protocols; BigQuery for regional disease prevalence analytics; GCS for uploaded leaf images
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL for transactional diagnosis records; BigQuery for aggregation queries over regional datasets

hosting_target.value: GCP Cloud Run (FastAPI), Vertex AI (cloud disease model), GCS (leaf images), Cloud SQL PostgreSQL, BigQuery (analytics), Firebase (Auth and push notifications)
hosting_target.rationale: Unified GCP stack simplifies IAM and networking; Firebase native integration with React Native

privacy_retention_policy.value: Leaf images retained 90 days then deleted from GCS; farmer GPS coordinates stored as region polygon (not precise coordinates) for privacy; diagnosis records retained 2 years for model retraining datasets
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Minimizing precise geolocation storage protects farmer privacy; 2-year retention supports model improvement cycles

future_scope.value: Soil health analysis via additional sensor inputs, integration with regional agricultural extension service APIs for automated advisory dispatch, multi-language treatment recommendation generation, crop yield prediction from sequential diagnosis history
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 and 3 roadmap pending farmer adoption validation

constraints.value: Mobile app must function fully offline for leaf diagnosis using on-device TFLite model; image upload compressed to <200KB before API transmission; TFLite model size <15MB for OTA distribution; MVP supports 10 crop species and top 3 diseases per species
constraints.rationale: Rural 2G/3G bandwidth and device storage constraints drive offline-first and compression requirements

observability_baseline.value: Google Cloud Monitoring for Cloud Run request latency and error rate; Firebase Crashlytics for mobile crash reporting; custom BigQuery events for diagnosis confidence score distribution and crop species submission frequency
observability_baseline.rationale: Model performance monitoring via confidence distribution is critical for detecting model drift in production

execution_preference.value: Mobile releases via Expo OTA for model and content updates; full app store release for native API changes; GitHub Actions for CI; Cloud Run auto-deploy on main merge
execution_preference.rationale: Expo OTA allows rapid model updates without app store review latency

llm_integration.value: Gemini 1.5 Flash via Vertex AI for generating farmer-readable treatment recommendation text from structured diagnosis output (disease name, severity, affected crop species). Prompts include disease metadata and crop context. Output validated for length (<200 words) and language tone (simple, actionable). No PII included in prompts.
llm_integration.rationale: ai_llm capability requires llm_integration field; Gemini Flash chosen for low per-token cost at scale and GCP-native deployment

project.goal: Real-time AI crop disease diagnosis from smartphone leaf photos for rural smallholder farmers
project.target_users: Smallholder farmers (offline-first), extension officers (outbreak monitoring)

frontend.inference: TFLite React Native plugin for on-device inference
frontend.state: React Query for cloud sync, SQLite for offline diagnosis cache

backend.inference: Vertex AI hosted EfficientNet-Lite model for cloud inference fallback
backend.llm: Gemini 1.5 Flash for treatment recommendation generation

security.auth: Firebase Auth with anonymous and named accounts
security.rate_limiting: 30 requests/hour per device via Cloud Run request throttling

devops.hosting: GCP Cloud Run, Vertex AI, Firebase

constraints.offline: Full leaf diagnosis offline via TFLite

reasoner_reviews.product: The offline-first TFLite inference is the defining product constraint -- farmers in rural areas cannot rely on connectivity during critical crop inspection moments. The LLM treatment text generation adds significant farmer value but must degrade gracefully when offline: cached treatment_protocols should be surfaced for offline diagnoses rather than leaving the farmer without guidance.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The round 1 plan used Cloud Vision API as the primary inference path with TFLite as fallback, which inverts the correct priority. TFLite on-device should be primary (always works offline); cloud inference via Vertex AI is the fallback for species or disease classes outside the TFLite model scope. This inversion needs to be corrected in the architecture_overview and workflows sections.
reasoner_reviews.security: The round 1 plan stored farmer GPS coordinates as precise lat/lng in disease_diagnoses. The privacy_retention_policy specifies region polygon storage only. This is a medium-severity gap that must be corrected in the data_model.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: TFLite model <15MB constraint needs to be reflected in the technology_stack and phased_implementation. EfficientNet-Lite B0 quantized to INT8 fits within 12MB for 10 crop species / 30 disease classes and should be referenced explicitly.

backend.service_design: FastAPI on Cloud Run with routers: /diagnose (cloud inference fallback), /treatments (LLM generation + cached lookup), /sync (farmer diagnosis history upload from offline queue), /outbreaks (extension officer regional aggregation). Inference fallback: if TFLite returns confidence <60% or crop species not in on-device model, client calls /diagnose which routes to Vertex AI EfficientNet-Lite model endpoint.
backend.llm_integration_design: Gemini 1.5 Flash called from /treatments endpoint. Prompt template: 'You are an agricultural advisor. A farmer has photographed a {crop_species} plant. The diagnosis is {disease_name} with {severity} severity. Provide a treatment recommendation in 3 bullet points using simple language. Do not mention brand names. Maximum 150 words.' Response cached in treatment_protocols table by (disease_id, crop_species_id) for 30 days to avoid repeated LLM calls for common diagnoses.
backend.api_patterns: RESTful at /api/v1. Image upload via GCS signed URL (POST /upload/signed-url returns signed URL; client uploads directly to GCS bypassing Cloud Run for bandwidth efficiency). POST /diagnose accepts {gcs_image_path, crop_species_id} and returns ranked_diagnoses[]. POST /treatments/{diagnosis_id} returns cached or freshly generated treatment text.
backend.failure_handling: Cloud Run max instances 10 with min instances 1 during growing season. If Vertex AI model endpoint returns error, fall back to returning top-1 offline diagnosis from TFLite result passed in request body. If Gemini Flash call fails, return cached treatment_protocol or static fallback text from treatment_protocols table.

frontend.app_structure: Expo managed workflow. src/screens/: CaptureScreen (Expo Camera), DiagnosisResultScreen, TreatmentScreen, HistoryScreen (offline SQLite cache), ExtensionDashboard (extension officer only). src/inference/: tfliteRunner.ts wraps TFLite plugin, preprocessImage() resizes and normalizes leaf photo to 224x224 RGB float32, runInference() returns class probabilities.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Capture Flow: Farmer opens app -> CaptureScreen shows viewfinder with leaf alignment guide overlay -> tap capture -> preprocessImage() resizes to 224x224, compresses JPEG to <200KB -> tfliteRunner.runInference() returns ranked diseases with confidence scores -> if top confidence >=60%, show DiagnosisResultScreen offline; if <60%, upload to /diagnose cloud endpoint. Treatment Flow: DiagnosisResultScreen shows disease name, confidence bar, and 'Get Treatment Plan' button -> TreatmentScreen fetches from /treatments/:diagnosis_id (cached Gemini response or static fallback if offline).
frontend.performance_notes: TFLite inference target <2 seconds on mid-range Android (Snapdragon 460). Image preprocessing in JS worker thread to avoid blocking UI. SQLite offline cache stores last 50 diagnoses with treatment text for offline re-access.

security.auth_design: Firebase Auth anonymous sign-in on first launch (no friction for rural farmers). Optional email/phone account creation for diagnosis history sync across devices. Extension officer accounts created by admin with push notification permission scope.
security.privacy_controls: Farmer location stored as region_polygon_id (reference to pre-defined 50km grid cells) -- not precise GPS coordinates. Leaf images stored in GCS with path {region_polygon_id}/{uuid}.jpg -- no farmer_id in GCS path. 90-day GCS lifecycle rule deletes images. Diagnosis records retain region_polygon_id and crop_species but not device GPS.
security.abuse_prevention: Cloud Run request throttling: 30 requests/hour per Firebase UID (anonymous or named). GCS signed URL TTL 5 minutes prevents URL reuse. Vertex AI endpoint protected by Cloud Run service account IAM -- not publicly accessible.

data.entities: farmer_profiles (id UUID, firebase_uid, region_polygon_id UUID FK, account_type ENUM anonymous/named, created_at). crop_images (id UUID, farmer_id UUID FK, gcs_path, crop_species_id UUID FK, uploaded_at, deleted_at). disease_diagnoses (id UUID, crop_image_id UUID FK, model_type ENUM tflite/vertex_ai, ranked_diseases JSONB [{disease_id, confidence_pct}], top_disease_id UUID FK, submitted_at). treatment_protocols (id UUID, disease_id UUID FK, crop_species_id UUID FK, treatment_text TEXT, generated_by ENUM llm/static, generated_at, expires_at). regional_disease_prevalence (region_polygon_id, disease_id, week_start DATE, submission_count INT, avg_confidence DECIMAL) -- materialized weekly from BigQuery.
data.storage_design: Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 for transactional records. GCS bucket 'crop-images' with 90-day lifecycle deletion. BigQuery dataset 'disease_analytics' with diagnosis events streamed from Cloud Run via Pub/Sub. Regional aggregation runs as weekly BigQuery scheduled query.
data.data_risks: Farmer GPS precision risk resolved: region_polygon_id approach stores only 50km grid cell reference, not GPS coordinates. Model drift risk: BigQuery confidence score distribution query runs weekly; if mean confidence drops below 55% for a species, alert triggers model retraining pipeline review.

devops.deployment_topology: GCP project: Cloud Run service (FastAPI, min 1 instance growing season, min 0 off-season), Vertex AI endpoint (EfficientNet-Lite B0 INT8), Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16, GCS buckets (crop-images, tflite-models), BigQuery dataset, Firebase project (Auth, FCM push). Expo EAS Build for iOS/Android binaries; Expo OTA for TFLite model and treatment_protocols content updates.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: Python ruff + mypy, pytest (80% coverage), Docker build and push GCR, Cloud Run deploy on main merge. Expo: EAS Build triggered on GitHub release tag for app store submissions. TFLite model OTA: new .tflite file uploaded to GCS tflite-models bucket triggers Cloud Function that publishes Expo OTA update with new model asset.
devops.observability_stack: Google Cloud Monitoring: Cloud Run request latency p50/p95, error rate, instance count. Firebase Crashlytics: mobile crash rate by screen and TFLite inference step. BigQuery dashboards: diagnosis confidence distribution per crop species (weekly), submission volume per region_polygon (daily), top 5 diseases nationally.

ISS-001.title: TFLite and cloud inference priority inverted
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 architecture described Cloud Vision API as primary inference path with TFLite as fallback. The offline-first constraint requires TFLite to be primary; cloud inference is the fallback for low-confidence or out-of-model-scope results. This inversion breaks the offline-first product requirement.

history.note: Architect reasoner flagged inverted inference priority in architecture_overview and workflows

ISS-002.title: Precise GPS coordinates stored in disease_diagnoses
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 data_model stored lat/lng float columns in disease_diagnoses, violating the privacy_retention_policy which specifies region polygon storage only (50km grid cells, not precise GPS).

history.note: Security reviewer identified GPS coordinate storage against privacy policy

focus_issues.title: TFLite and cloud inference priority inverted
focus_issues.detail: Offline TFLite must be primary inference path; Vertex AI cloud model is fallback only when confidence <60% or crop species outside TFLite scope.

focus_issues.title: Precise GPS coordinates stored in disease_diagnoses
focus_issues.detail: Replace lat/lng columns with region_polygon_id foreign key referencing 50km grid cells.

latest_recommendations: Invert inference priority: TFLite on-device first, Vertex AI cloud second
Replace GPS lat/lng in disease_diagnoses with region_polygon_id FK to 50km grid table
Specify EfficientNet-Lite B0 INT8 quantized model explicitly to confirm <15MB constraint is met

convergence_state.reason: High severity inference priority inversion and medium GPS privacy gap block convergence

previous_audits.summary: Strong domain-specific ML architecture with good LLM treatment generation design, but the offline-first product constraint is violated by the inverted inference priority, and GPS data storage contradicts the privacy policy.
strengths: Gemini Flash LLM integration with prompt template and 30-day treatment caching is well-specified
GCS signed URL bypass pattern for image upload reduces Cloud Run bandwidth load
BigQuery regional aggregation pipeline for extension officer outbreak detection is well-designed
concerns: Cloud Vision API as primary inference inverts offline-first requirement -- violates key product constraint
Precise GPS lat/lng columns in disease_diagnoses violate stated privacy_retention_policy
TFLite model size spec not referenced; <15MB constraint unverified in tech stack
blocking_issues: ISS-001: Offline-first requirement broken by cloud-primary inference design
ISS-002: GPS coordinate storage violates privacy_retention_policy
recommendations: Invert inference priority: TFLite runs first on-device; if confidence <60%, mobile app uploads to /diagnose for Vertex AI cloud fallback
Replace disease_diagnoses.lat/lng with region_polygon_id FK; add region_polygons table defining 50km grid cells
Specify EfficientNet-Lite B0 INT8 quantized (12MB) explicitly in technology_stack

previous_plan.title: CropSight AI Disease Detection v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: React Native mobile app with on-device TFLite backup and Cloud Vision API primary inference for crop disease detection, backed by FastAPI on GCP Cloud Run, Gemini Flash treatment generation, and BigQuery regional analytics.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Cloud Vision API primary inference with TFLite as offline fallback. Farmers upload images to Cloud Run which calls Cloud Vision for disease classification.
previous_plan.data_model: disease_diagnoses table with lat DECIMAL and lng DECIMAL columns for farmer location. treatment_protocols with 30-day LLM cache.
previous_plan.workflows: Farmer captures leaf photo -> app uploads to Cloud Run /diagnose -> Cloud Vision API classifies disease -> result returned to app -> if offline, TFLite used as fallback.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 must invert the inference priority (ISS-001): TFLite on-device is primary for all rural offline scenarios; Vertex AI cloud inference is fallback only when TFLite confidence is below 60% or the crop species is outside the on-device model scope. ISS-002 requires replacing GPS coordinates in disease_diagnoses with region_polygon_id foreign key. The EfficientNet-Lite B0 INT8 quantized model (12MB) satisfies the <15MB constraint and should be named explicitly. LLM treatment generation via Gemini Flash degrades gracefully offline via treatment_protocols cache.

fix_report.action_taken: Corrected inference priority throughout architecture_overview and workflows: TFLite on-device runs first; if confidence <60% AND connectivity available, mobile app calls /diagnose cloud endpoint for Vertex AI EfficientNet-Lite B0 INT8 fallback. Updated system_components to reflect correct primary/fallback roles.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Farmers can complete full leaf diagnosis in offline conditions using TFLite; cloud fallback improves accuracy for ambiguous or out-of-scope cases when connectivity exists.

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced lat/lng columns in disease_diagnoses with region_polygon_id FK referencing new region_polygons table (50km grid cells). Updated data_model, privacy_controls in security_and_compliance, and data pipeline to use region_polygon_id for aggregation.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Farmer location stored only at 50km grid cell granularity, satisfying privacy_retention_policy requirement for region polygon storage.

target_output.title: CropSight AI Crop Disease Detection System
target_output.executive_summary: An offline-first AI system for smallholder farmers that diagnoses crop diseases from smartphone leaf photos using on-device TFLite EfficientNet-Lite B0 INT8 inference as the primary path, with Vertex AI cloud fallback for low-confidence results. Built with React Native Expo on GCP Cloud Run, Gemini 1.5 Flash generates farmer-readable treatment recommendations cached by disease and crop species. Regional diagnosis aggregation feeds extension officer outbreak detection dashboards via BigQuery. Farmer location stored as 50km region polygon, not precise GPS coordinates.
target_output.architecture_overview: Offline-first architecture: React Native Expo app runs TFLite EfficientNet-Lite B0 INT8 (12MB, OTA-updatable via Expo) on the device for all leaf diagnoses as the primary inference path. If top-1 confidence is below 60% AND internet is available, the app uploads the compressed image to GCS via signed URL and calls the FastAPI /diagnose endpoint on GCP Cloud Run, which routes to a Vertex AI EfficientNet-Lite endpoint for cloud-quality fallback inference. Gemini 1.5 Flash on Vertex AI generates treatment recommendation text, cached in Cloud SQL treatment_protocols table by (disease_id, crop_species_id) for 30 days to minimize LLM call volume. Offline treatment text is served from the SQLite local cache populated during previous connected sessions. Farmer location stored as region_polygon_id (50km grid cell reference) -- no precise GPS stored. Regional disease aggregation runs as weekly BigQuery scheduled query feeding extension officer outbreak dashboards.
target_output.technology_stack: Mobile: React Native 0.74, Expo 51 (managed workflow, OTA updates), Expo Camera, react-native-fast-tflite (TFLite inference), Expo SQLite (offline cache), React Query. ML Models: EfficientNet-Lite B0 INT8 quantized (12MB, supports 10 crop species x 30 disease classes), hosted on Vertex AI for cloud fallback. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, Cloud Run (min 1 instance growing season). LLM: Gemini 1.5 Flash via Vertex AI SDK. Data: Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16, BigQuery (analytics), GCS (leaf images 90-day retention). Auth: Firebase Auth (anonymous + named). CI/CD: GitHub Actions, Expo EAS Build.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Leaf Capture and On-Device Diagnosis: Expo Camera with 2:3 ratio viewfinder and leaf alignment overlay guide; preprocessImage() resizes to 224x224 RGB float32, compresses JPEG to <200KB; TFLite inference returns ranked disease probabilities with species label. Cloud Fallback Diagnosis: if top-1 confidence <60% AND online, upload to GCS signed URL then POST /diagnose; Vertex AI returns enhanced ranked diagnosis. Treatment Recommendation: TreatmentScreen fetches cached Gemini treatment text from treatment_protocols; if offline, SQLite local cache; if no cache, static fallback from bundled treatment_protocols.json. Offline Queue: diagnosis submissions during connectivity loss queued in SQLite, synced to Cloud SQL via POST /sync on reconnect. Extension Officer Dashboard: regional heatmap from BigQuery weekly aggregation showing submission_count and top disease per region_polygon; Firebase FCM push alert when new_disease_flag triggers for a region (disease not seen in prior 4 weeks).
target_output.system_components: React Native Expo app: TFLite plugin (EfficientNet-Lite B0 INT8, 12MB), Expo Camera, SQLite offline cache (50 diagnoses + treatment texts). GCS bucket 'crop-images': signed URL upload, 90-day lifecycle deletion, path {region_polygon_id}/{uuid}.jpg. Cloud Run (FastAPI): /diagnose (Vertex AI fallback), /treatments (Gemini Flash + cache), /sync (offline queue ingestion), /outbreaks (extension officer aggregation). Vertex AI: EfficientNet-Lite B0 endpoint for cloud inference. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16: farmer_profiles, crop_images, disease_diagnoses, treatment_protocols, region_polygons. BigQuery: disease_analytics dataset, weekly scheduled query for regional_disease_prevalence. Firebase: Auth (anonymous + named), FCM for extension officer push alerts.
target_output.workflows: Primary Offline Diagnosis: Farmer opens CaptureScreen -> aligns leaf in overlay guide -> taps capture -> preprocessImage() runs on device -> tfliteRunner.runInference() returns ranked diseases in <2s -> if top confidence >=60%, DiagnosisResultScreen shows disease name and confidence bar offline -> TreatmentScreen shows cached treatment text from SQLite -> diagnosis saved to SQLite offline queue. Cloud Fallback Diagnosis: top confidence <60% AND internet available -> app compresses image to <200KB, requests GCS signed URL from /upload/signed-url, uploads to GCS directly -> POSTs gcs_image_path to /diagnose -> Vertex AI EfficientNet-Lite returns enhanced ranked diseases -> diagnosis result displayed -> POST /treatments/{diagnosis_id} fetches Gemini-generated text (cached or fresh). Regional Outbreak Detection: Weekly BigQuery scheduled query aggregates disease_diagnoses by region_polygon_id and disease_id for rolling 4-week window -> if a disease_id appears in a region_polygon for the first time in 4 weeks, BigQuery Cloud Function triggers Firebase FCM push to extension officers covering that region_polygon -> officer opens extension dashboard to see regional heatmap with flagged polygons.
target_output.data_model: region_polygons (id UUID PK, grid_code VARCHAR, centroid_lat DECIMAL, centroid_lng DECIMAL, country VARCHAR, admin1 VARCHAR) -- 50km grid cells, not farmer-specific. farmer_profiles (id UUID PK, firebase_uid, region_polygon_id UUID FK, account_type ENUM anonymous/named, created_at). crop_species (id UUID PK, common_name, scientific_name, tflite_class_index INT). crop_images (id UUID PK, farmer_id UUID FK, gcs_path, crop_species_id UUID FK, uploaded_at, deleted_at TIMESTAMPTZ). disease_diagnoses (id UUID PK, crop_image_id UUID FK, farmer_id UUID FK, model_type ENUM tflite/vertex_ai, ranked_diseases JSONB [{disease_id, confidence_pct}], top_disease_id UUID FK, region_polygon_id UUID FK -- no GPS coordinates, submitted_at). treatment_protocols (id UUID PK, disease_id UUID FK, crop_species_id UUID FK, treatment_text TEXT, generated_by ENUM llm/static, generated_at, expires_at). regional_disease_prevalence (region_polygon_id UUID, disease_id UUID, week_start DATE, submission_count INT, avg_confidence DECIMAL, new_disease_flag BOOL) -- populated by BigQuery scheduled query.
target_output.api_design: RESTful at /api/v1. POST /upload/signed-url -- returns GCS signed URL for direct client upload (Firebase Auth required). POST /diagnose -- body {gcs_image_path, crop_species_id}; routes to Vertex AI EfficientNet-Lite; returns {ranked_diagnoses: [{disease_id, disease_name, confidence_pct}], diagnosis_id}. GET /treatments/:diagnosis_id -- returns cached treatment text from treatment_protocols or calls Gemini Flash if cache miss; falls back to static treatment_protocols.json entry. POST /sync -- accepts batch of offline SQLite diagnosis records, upserts to Cloud SQL. GET /outbreaks/regional?polygon_ids=X,Y -- returns weekly BigQuery regional_disease_prevalence for extension officer dashboard. All endpoints: Firebase Auth JWT validation, 30 req/hour rate limit per UID.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Firebase Auth: anonymous sign-in on first launch (no email/phone required); optional named account for cross-device sync; extension officers created by admin. JWT validated on all Cloud Run endpoints via Firebase Admin SDK. Privacy: region_polygon_id (50km grid cell) stored in disease_diagnoses -- no precise GPS coordinates. GCS image paths use {region_polygon_id}/{uuid}.jpg structure, not farmer_id, to prevent direct correlation. 90-day GCS lifecycle rule. Rate limiting: Cloud Run enforces 30 requests/hour per Firebase UID. Signed URL TTL: 5 minutes. Vertex AI endpoint protected by Cloud Run service account IAM, not directly internet-accessible. LLM guardrails: Gemini Flash prompts contain no PII; output length validated <200 words before caching in treatment_protocols.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Cloud Run: deployed via GitHub Actions on main merge; min 1 instance during growing season (April-October), min 0 off-season; max 10 instances with concurrency 80. Vertex AI endpoint: EfficientNet-Lite B0 INT8 deployed to dedicated endpoint, auto-scaled 1-3 replicas. TFLite OTA: new model uploaded to GCS tflite-models bucket triggers Cloud Function that calls Expo OTA publish API with updated model asset bundle. Expo EAS Build: triggered on GitHub release tag for full app store submissions (iOS TestFlight, Android Play). BigQuery scheduled query: every Monday 2am UTC, processes disease_diagnoses for previous week, updates regional_disease_prevalence, triggers FCM alerts for new_disease_flag regions.
target_output.observability: Google Cloud Monitoring: Cloud Run request latency p50/p95/p99, error rate by endpoint, instance count. Firebase Crashlytics: mobile crash rate segmented by TFLite inference step (preprocessing vs inference). BigQuery weekly dashboard: diagnosis confidence score distribution per crop_species (mean/p25/p75), submission volume per region_polygon heatmap, top 5 diseases nationally, TFLite vs Vertex AI split rate. Alert: if mean confidence for any crop_species drops below 55% for 2 consecutive weeks, Cloud Monitoring alert fires to trigger model retraining review.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Cloud Run: ~$30/month at 10K submissions/month (mostly idle off-season). Vertex AI endpoint: ~$80/month for 1-replica endpoint (dominated by instance uptime). Gemini Flash LLM: ~$0.003/diagnosis for treatment generation; 30-day cache reduces to ~$0.0005 effective cost per unique disease/species pair. Cloud SQL: ~$50/month for db-g1-small. BigQuery: ~$5/month for analytics queries. GCS: ~$5/month for 90-day image retention at 10K/month 200KB images. Total: ~$175/month MVP scale. At 100K submissions/month, Cloud Run and Vertex AI scale linearly; BigQuery and GCS remain low-cost.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 - MVP (12 weeks): React Native Expo app with TFLite EfficientNet-Lite B0 INT8 for 10 crop species / 30 diseases, cloud fallback via FastAPI /diagnose, Gemini Flash treatment generation with 30-day cache, Firebase anonymous auth, SQLite offline queue, extension officer web dashboard with BigQuery regional heatmap, GCS signed URL image upload, CI/CD pipeline, Expo EAS production build. Phase 2 - Offline Depth (8 weeks): Expand TFLite model to 20 species via OTA update, multi-language treatment text (Swahili, Hindi via Gemini translation), diagnosis history sync across devices, Firebase FCM push alerts for extension officers on new outbreak detection. Phase 3 - Predictive Features (Q3): Soil health additional sensor analysis, sequential diagnosis history crop yield prediction, integration with national extension service advisory APIs.
target_output.development_guidelines: TFLite inference must be invoked before any network call -- no cloud-first fallback patterns. Region_polygon_id, never GPS coordinates, in all diagnosis writes. Gemini Flash prompts must be reviewed for PII before adding new fields to the prompt template. React Native: Expo SDK version pinned; OTA updates only for JS bundle and model assets, never for native module changes. Python: ruff + mypy in CI; FastAPI dependency injection for Firebase Auth validation -- no manual JWT decoding in route handlers. BigQuery scheduled queries version-controlled in dbt models.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: TFLite model accuracy below 60% for under-represented disease classes -- mitigated by BigQuery confidence monitoring with retraining alert at 55% mean threshold. Risk: GCS signed URL requests fail offline -- mitigated by offline SQLite queue; images uploaded on reconnect with offline queue flush. Tradeoff: Anonymous Firebase Auth over full account creation -- eliminates registration friction for rural farmers who may lack email; trade-off is inability to send personalized push notifications without named account. Risk: Gemini Flash output quality varies for low-resource language translation in Phase 2 -- mitigated by human review of treatment text before publishing to treatment_protocols for each new language.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed EfficientNet-Lite B0 INT8 quantized over MobileNetV3 -- EfficientNet-Lite B0 achieves higher accuracy at similar 12MB size for plant disease classification per benchmark. Confirmed region_polygon 50km grid cells over administrative boundary polygons -- grid cells provide consistent spatial resolution independent of variable administrative boundary sizes. Confirmed Gemini Flash over GPT-4o-mini for treatment text -- Gemini Flash is native to GCP stack avoiding cross-cloud API calls; per-token cost comparable. Confirmed 30-day treatment cache expiry -- balances freshness of agronomic recommendations with LLM cost at scale.

metadata.notes: Round 2 AI crop disease detection: corrects inverted inference priority (ISS-001) and GPS privacy gap (ISS-002); strong quality with comprehensive LLM integration and offline-first design.

project_goal.value: Python SDK for the USDA NASS (National Agricultural Statistics Service) public API providing typed data models, query builders, and rate-limit-aware pagination helpers for data scientists and agronomists building crop yield analysis workflows
project_goal.rationale: NASS API lacks an official Python client; researchers currently write bespoke HTTP code per project

target_users.value: Data scientists and agronomists building crop yield analysis workflows in Python and Jupyter notebooks
target_users.rationale: Primary audience identified in researcher survey; secondary audience is agricultural economics researchers

project_class.rationale: Deliverable is a PyPI-distributed Python package, not a hosted service

capabilities.rationale: HTTP integration layer, typed data models for NASS API responses, and data transformation utilities; no frontend or auth required

complexity_level.rationale: NASS API pagination, rate limiting, typed query builder, and dataframe integration add meaningful complexity beyond a thin HTTP wrapper

risk_level.rationale: SDK calls public USDA API with user-supplied API key; no auth infrastructure managed by the SDK itself

data_sensitivity.rationale: USDA NASS data is public; no PII or sensitive data handled by the SDK

external_exposure.rationale: Library runs in user's local Python environment; no hosted endpoints

access_model.value: NASS API key passed by library consumer via NassClient(api_key=...) or USDA_NASS_API_KEY environment variable
access_model.rationale: API key management is the consumer's responsibility; SDK reads from env var as secure default

feature_scope.value: NassClient class with typed query builders for commodity statistics, state-level estimates, survey programs, and acreage reports; automatic pagination across NASS API offset-based pages; rate-limit context manager enforcing NASS 24-hour quota (hourly and daily limits); Pydantic v2 response models for CommodityQuery, StateStatistic, YieldEstimate, AcreageReport; .to_dataframe() method returning pandas DataFrame; async support via httpx AsyncClient; optional caching of repeated queries via dict-based in-memory cache
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature scope for research workflow automation

mvp_scope.value: NassClient with synchronous HTTP, CommodityQuery builder, YieldEstimate and AcreageReport Pydantic models, automatic pagination, rate-limit-aware retry with exponential backoff, .to_dataframe() output, pip/PyPI distribution
mvp_scope.rationale: Core query-fetch-transform workflow is the primary use case for MVP

security_baseline.value: API key read from environment variable by default; never logged or included in error messages; HTTPS enforced for all NASS API calls; no network calls made on import
security_baseline.rationale: Library security best practices for credential handling

frontend_stack.value: None -- Python library, no frontend
frontend_stack.rationale: library_sdk project class has no frontend component

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12, httpx for HTTP, Pydantic v2 for response models, pandas for .to_dataframe(), pytest for testing
backend_stack.rationale: httpx supports both sync and async; Pydantic v2 for typed model validation; pandas is standard in target user environment

data_platform.value: None -- SDK transforms NASS API JSON responses into Pydantic models and pandas DataFrames; no persistent storage
data_platform.rationale: library_sdk has no database; data lives in caller's memory or their own storage

hosting_target.value: PyPI distribution via GitHub Actions trusted publisher; source on GitHub
hosting_target.rationale: Standard Python package distribution channel for data science tooling

privacy_retention_policy.value: No data stored by SDK; API keys never persisted; optional in-memory query cache cleared on GC; no telemetry or usage tracking
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: SDK is a local library with no server-side component; no retention obligations

future_scope.value: Async-first client using httpx.AsyncClient, Redis-backed query cache for shared Jupyter environments, CLI wrapper for ad-hoc NASS queries from terminal, integration with polars DataFrame as alternative to pandas
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 roadmap pending community adoption feedback

constraints.value: Python 3.10+ compatibility (avoid 3.12-only syntax); pandas is an optional dependency (not required for Pydantic model consumers); total package size <5MB; zero mandatory dependencies beyond httpx and pydantic
constraints.rationale: Researchers may use the SDK in constrained environments; optional pandas avoids forcing heavy dependency on non-dataframe users

observability_baseline.value: Python standard library logging module with nass_sdk logger; debug-level logs for each HTTP request and rate-limit event; no external monitoring services
observability_baseline.rationale: Library logging is the appropriate observability level for a local SDK; consumers configure log level

execution_preference.value: TDD approach; tests written before implementation; GitHub Actions for CI on PR and main merge; GitHub Releases trigger PyPI publish via trusted publisher
execution_preference.rationale: TDD ensures API surface is designed from consumer perspective before implementation

project.goal: Typed Python SDK for USDA NASS API with pagination, rate limiting, and DataFrame output
project.target_users: Data scientists and agronomists in Python/Jupyter environments

devops.distribution: PyPI via trusted publisher on GitHub Release

open_questions.async_support: Async client in MVP or Phase 2?

reasoner_reviews.product: The rate-limit context manager is the most novel feature relative to ad-hoc NASS API usage. NASS enforces an undocumented hourly limit and a 5000 record daily cap that causes 429 errors mid-pagination for large commodity queries. The SDK's value is making this transparent to researchers who currently discover the limit only when their notebook breaks.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The query builder should use method chaining (commodity().state('IA').year(2023).fetch()) rather than keyword argument dictionaries to enable IDE autocomplete and reduce parameter name lookup. Pydantic v2 model validators should map NASS API snake_case field names (Value, State_ANSI_Code) to Python-idiomatic names (value, state_ansi_code). Pagination should be abstracted behind a generator that yields individual Pydantic model instances rather than raw pages, letting consumers stop early with next().
reasoner_reviews.security: API key security is straightforward but the SDK must explicitly avoid logging the key at any log level. A common library mistake is including the Authorization header value in debug HTTP logs. Ensure the httpx client is configured with a custom EventHook that redacts the api_key query parameter before logging.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Optional pandas dependency requires a try/except import guard in to_dataframe() and a clear ImportError message ('Install nass-sdk[pandas] or pip install pandas to use .to_dataframe()'). Python 3.10 compatibility means no match/case statements and no union type syntax (X | Y) in function signatures -- use Optional[X] and Union[X, Y] from typing module.

backend.service_design: Three public modules: nass_sdk.client (NassClient, AsyncNassClient), nass_sdk.query (CommodityQueryBuilder, StateQueryBuilder, SurveyQueryBuilder), nass_sdk.models (CommodityQuery, StateStatistic, YieldEstimate, AcreageReport, SurveyProgram). Internal module nass_sdk._http: httpx wrapper with rate-limit enforcement and api_key redaction event hook.
backend.api_patterns: Fluent query builder API: client.commodity().state('IOWA').year_range(2018, 2023).unit_desc('BU / ACRE').fetch() returns List[YieldEstimate]. Pagination is implicit via generator; client.commodity().all() yields YieldEstimate instances across all NASS offset pages. Rate limit context: with client.rate_limited(hourly=500, daily=4800): ... -- enforces limits with exponential backoff on 429 responses.
backend.failure_handling: NassRateLimitError raised if rate limit exceeded and max_retries exhausted. NassApiError wraps 4xx/5xx HTTP errors with status_code and nass_error_message. All exceptions subclass NassSdkError base class for catch-all handling. httpx EventHook redacts api_key query parameter in request logs at DEBUG level.

data.entities: CommodityQuery (commodity_desc, statisticcat_desc, unit_desc, year, state_alpha, value, cv_pct, reference_period_desc). StateStatistic (state_alpha, state_ansi_code, state_name, county_code, county_name, value, unit_desc, year). YieldEstimate (commodity_desc, year, state_alpha, yield_value, unit_desc ENUM bu_per_acre/lbs_per_acre/cwt_per_acre, cv_pct). AcreageReport (commodity_desc, year, state_alpha, planted_acres, harvested_acres, reference_period_desc). SurveyProgram (source_desc ENUM CENSUS/SURVEY, sector_desc, group_desc, commodity_desc).
data.schema_notes: NASS API returns Value field as string (including ' (D)' for suppressed values and ' (NA)' for not applicable). Pydantic v2 validator coerces numeric strings to float, maps '(D)' to None with suppressed=True flag, maps '(NA)' to None. state_alpha mapped to state_alpha in Python model from NASS State_Alpha field name.
data.analytics_events: No analytics; SDK is local library. Debug logging: each paginated fetch logs commodity, page offset, records_returned, rate_limit_remaining at DEBUG level via nass_sdk logger.

devops.deployment_topology: PyPI package. GitHub repository: src/nass_sdk/ layout with pyproject.toml (hatchling build backend). Optional dependencies: nass-sdk[pandas] installs pandas>=1.5; nass-sdk[async] installs httpx[asyncio] (already included in base httpx).
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: test matrix (Python 3.10, 3.11, 3.12), ruff lint, mypy strict type check, pytest with 90% coverage gate, test against NASS API sandbox (vcr.py cassettes for deterministic offline tests). GitHub Release triggers PyPI publish via OIDC trusted publisher (no stored secrets).
devops.cost_controls: Zero hosting cost. PyPI free. GitHub Actions free tier sufficient for open-source repo.

target_output.thinking_summary: This is a Python library_sdk for the USDA NASS public API -- no hosted service, no database, no frontend. Key design decisions: fluent method-chaining query builder for IDE autocomplete, generator-based pagination so consumers stop early without fetching all pages, optional pandas dependency to avoid forcing heavy imports on non-dataframe users, and httpx EventHook to redact api_key from debug logs. Plan quality is moderate -- some sections are appropriately brief for a library that has no deployment topology, no security hardening beyond API key hygiene, and no cost/scaling concerns.
target_output.title: nass-sdk: Python Client for USDA NASS Quick Stats API
target_output.executive_summary: A PyPI-distributed Python SDK (Python 3.10+) for the USDA NASS Quick Stats API providing typed Pydantic v2 response models, a fluent method-chaining query builder, generator-based pagination with transparent rate-limit enforcement, and .to_dataframe() output for pandas integration. Designed for data scientists and agronomists automating crop yield, acreage, and survey data retrieval in Python and Jupyter notebook workflows. No hosted infrastructure; distributed on PyPI via GitHub Actions trusted publisher.
target_output.architecture_overview: Pure Python library distributed as a PyPI package. Three public modules: nass_sdk.client exposes NassClient (sync) and AsyncNassClient (Phase 2); nass_sdk.query exposes CommodityQueryBuilder, StateQueryBuilder, and SurveyQueryBuilder with fluent method chaining; nass_sdk.models exposes Pydantic v2 models (YieldEstimate, AcreageReport, StateStatistic, SurveyProgram, CommodityQuery). Internal module nass_sdk._http wraps httpx.Client with API key injection, rate-limit backoff, and EventHook-based api_key redaction from debug logs. No network calls are made on import. NassClient instantiation accepts api_key parameter or reads USDA_NASS_API_KEY environment variable. Pagination is abstracted as a generator yielding individual Pydantic model instances across NASS offset-based pages, allowing early stopping via next() or islice().
target_output.technology_stack: Python 3.10+. Mandatory dependencies: httpx>=0.27 (HTTP client with sync support), pydantic>=2.6 (response model validation). Optional dependency groups: nass-sdk[pandas] adds pandas>=1.5 for .to_dataframe(); nass-sdk[dev] adds pytest, pytest-recording, vcrpy, ruff, mypy, coverage. Build: hatchling via pyproject.toml. Distribution: PyPI via GitHub Actions OIDC trusted publisher. Type stubs: included in package (py.typed marker, inline annotations).
target_output.functional_feature_map: NassClient: instantiation with api_key or USDA_NASS_API_KEY env var; .commodity() returns CommodityQueryBuilder; .state_statistics() returns StateQueryBuilder; .survey_programs() returns SurveyQueryBuilder. CommodityQueryBuilder: .state(alpha_code) filters by state, .year(y) or .year_range(start, end) sets year filter, .unit_desc(ud) filters by unit descriptor, .commodity(name) sets commodity, .fetch() returns List[YieldEstimate], .all() returns Generator[YieldEstimate] paginating until exhausted. Rate-limit context manager: with client.rate_limited(hourly=500, daily=4800): enforces limits, retries on 429 with exponential backoff up to max_retries=3, raises NassRateLimitError if exhausted. .to_dataframe() on any List[PydanticModel]: returns pandas DataFrame with model field names as columns; raises ImportError with install hint if pandas not installed.
target_output.system_components: nass_sdk/__init__.py: public exports (NassClient, NassRateLimitError, NassApiError, NassSdkError). nass_sdk/client.py: NassClient class, _http attribute, .commodity()/.state_statistics()/.survey_programs() factory methods. nass_sdk/query.py: CommodityQueryBuilder, StateQueryBuilder, SurveyQueryBuilder with .state()/.year()/.year_range()/.unit_desc()/.commodity()/.fetch()/.all() methods. nass_sdk/models.py: Pydantic v2 models with field validators for NASS string-numeric coercion and suppressed value handling. nass_sdk/_http.py: httpx.Client wrapper, api_key query param injection, rate_limit_state tracking, EventHook for api_key redaction, pagination logic as _fetch_pages() generator. nass_sdk/exceptions.py: NassSdkError, NassApiError, NassRateLimitError.
target_output.workflows: Crop Yield Query Workflow: Data scientist instantiates client = NassClient() (reads USDA_NASS_API_KEY from env) -> chains client.commodity().state('IA').year_range(2018, 2023).unit_desc('BU / ACRE').fetch() -> SDK constructs NASS API URL with query parameters, paginates across offset pages internally, validates each record via YieldEstimate Pydantic model, returns List[YieldEstimate] -> researcher calls .to_dataframe() on result for pandas analysis. Rate-Limit-Aware Large Retrieval Workflow: Researcher needs all soybean acreage reports for all states (potentially thousands of records) -> uses with client.rate_limited(hourly=490, daily=4750): for report in client.commodity().commodity('SOYBEANS').all(): process(report) -> SDK yields YieldEstimate instances one by one, transparently fetching next offset page when needed, sleeping and retrying on 429 responses, surfacing NassRateLimitError only when max_retries exhausted. The generator pattern allows early termination if only first N results are needed.
target_output.data_model: Pydantic v2 models (no persistent database). YieldEstimate: commodity_desc str, year int, state_alpha str, yield_value Optional[float] (None if NASS suppressed value '(D)'), unit_desc str, cv_pct Optional[float], reference_period_desc str, suppressed bool = False. AcreageReport: commodity_desc, year, state_alpha, planted_acres Optional[float], harvested_acres Optional[float], reference_period_desc, suppressed bool. StateStatistic: state_alpha, state_ansi_code str, state_name, county_code Optional[str], county_name Optional[str], value Optional[float], unit_desc, year, suppressed bool. SurveyProgram: source_desc NASS API literal (CENSUS or SURVEY), sector_desc, group_desc, commodity_desc. CommodityQuery: internal query parameter model used by query builders to construct NASS API request URLs.
target_output.api_design: Public Python API (not HTTP endpoints). NassClient(api_key: Optional[str] = None, timeout: float = 30.0, max_retries: int = 3). .commodity() -> CommodityQueryBuilder. CommodityQueryBuilder.state(alpha: str) -> self. .year(y: int) -> self. .year_range(start: int, end: int) -> self. .unit_desc(ud: str) -> self. .fetch() -> List[YieldEstimate]. .all() -> Generator[YieldEstimate, None, None]. RateLimitedContext: client.rate_limited(hourly: int = 500, daily: int = 4800) -> contextmanager. DataFrame output: nass_sdk.utils.to_dataframe(records: List[BaseModel]) -> pd.DataFrame -- also available as .to_dataframe() method on List subclass returned by .fetch(). NassApiError(status_code: int, message: str). NassRateLimitError(retry_after: Optional[int]).
target_output.security_and_compliance: API key security: NassClient reads api_key from constructor parameter or USDA_NASS_API_KEY environment variable; never falls back to hardcoded values. Key never included in exception messages, log output, or repr(). httpx EventHook on_request redacts api_key query parameter value to '***REDACTED***' before passing to httpx debug logger. HTTPS enforced: httpx.Client base_url set to https://quickstats.nass.usda.gov/api/get; no HTTP fallback. No credentials stored; each NassClient instance holds key in memory only for its lifetime. USDA NASS data is public domain; no license or compliance requirements on data usage.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: PyPI distribution via hatchling build backend and pyproject.toml. GitHub Actions CI workflow: matrix test on Python 3.10/3.11/3.12, ruff lint, mypy --strict type check, pytest with vcrpy cassettes for deterministic offline NASS API replay (no live API calls in CI), coverage gate at 90%. GitHub Release (semver tag) triggers PyPI publish via OIDC trusted publisher (no API token stored in GitHub secrets). Versioning: semantic versioning; breaking API changes require major version bump per consumer convention. No deployment infrastructure.
target_output.observability: Python standard library logging. Logger name: nass_sdk. Log levels: DEBUG -- each HTTP request URL (api_key redacted), page offset, records per page, rate_limit_remaining header value; INFO -- pagination complete with total records fetched; WARNING -- rate limit 429 received, retry attempt N of max_retries. Consumers configure log level via logging.getLogger('nass_sdk').setLevel(logging.DEBUG). No external monitoring, metrics, or telemetry.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero infrastructure cost. PyPI free tier. GitHub Actions free for open-source. USDA NASS API is free with API key registration (usda.gov). SDK performance scales with NASS API rate limits (not internal throughput). For large bulk queries, the rate_limited context manager serializes requests within configured hourly/daily limits. No horizontal scaling considerations -- library runs in user's Python process.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 - PyPI MVP (4 weeks): NassClient sync, CommodityQueryBuilder and StateQueryBuilder with .state()/.year()/.year_range()/.fetch()/.all(), YieldEstimate and AcreageReport Pydantic v2 models with suppressed value handling, automatic offset pagination generator, rate_limited context manager with exponential backoff, .to_dataframe() with optional pandas guard, api_key EventHook redaction, pytest suite with vcrpy cassettes at 90% coverage, PyPI publish via GitHub Actions trusted publisher. Phase 2 - Async and CLI (6 weeks): AsyncNassClient using httpx.AsyncClient, SurveyQueryBuilder for USDA survey program queries, CLI wrapper (nass query --commodity CORN --state IA --year 2023) via click, polars DataFrame support as alternative to pandas output. Phase 3 - Caching and Ecosystem (Q3): Redis-backed query result cache for shared Jupyter Hub environments, SurveyProgram and StateStatistic query builders, integration tests against live NASS sandbox environment.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.10 compatibility: no match/case statements, no X|Y union syntax in signatures -- use Optional[X] and Union[X,Y] from typing. Pydantic models use model_validator(mode='before') to coerce NASS string fields before field validation. All public methods and classes must have full type annotations; mypy --strict must pass. pytest fixtures use vcrpy @pytest.mark.vcr decorator to record/replay NASS API responses -- no live API calls in test suite. Optional pandas import guard pattern: def to_dataframe(self) -> 'pd.DataFrame': try: import pandas as pd ... except ImportError: raise ImportError('Install nass-sdk[pandas] or run pip install pandas'). Every new query builder method requires a corresponding pytest test using a recorded cassette.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: NASS API changes field names or pagination behavior without notice (undocumented public API) -- mitigated by vcrpy cassettes failing CI immediately when replay diverges from live response, prompting cassette update. Tradeoff: optional pandas dependency over bundled -- avoids forcing 30MB dependency on researchers using only Pydantic models; accepted complexity is ImportError guidance in .to_dataframe(). Risk: NASS API enforces undocumented rate limits that differ from documented values -- rate_limited context manager parameters are user-configurable to allow conservative settings below documented limits. Tradeoff: sync-only client in MVP over async-first -- synchronous httpx simplifies initial implementation and covers the primary Jupyter notebook use case; async added in Phase 2 for pipeline automation use cases.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed httpx over requests -- httpx supports both sync and async with identical API surface, enabling AsyncNassClient in Phase 2 without rewriting HTTP logic. Confirmed generator-based pagination over page-returning API -- generator allows early stopping with next()/islice() which is critical for exploratory analysis in Jupyter where researchers want to inspect first 20 records before fetching all 5000. Confirmed optional pandas over required -- USDA NASS data is frequently used with polars or directly as Pydantic model lists in automated pipelines; requiring pandas would be inappropriate for non-dataframe users.

metadata.notes: Round 1 Python SDK for USDA NASS API: library_sdk class with no hosted infrastructure; moderate quality with appropriately brief deployment/security sections for a local library.

project.goal: Validate precision nitrogen prescription algorithms using drone multispectral data for corn field research
project.target_users: Agronomists and precision agriculture PhD researchers
project.timeline: Aligned with spring planting and pre-harvest field trials

frontend.state: Pinia for mission and prescription state

backend.framework: Flask with Celery for async raster processing
backend.geospatial: GDAL 3.8, Shapely 2.0, Rasterio, CuPy for GPU band math

security.auth: Custom JWT with researcher/agronomist roles, 8-hour access + 7-day refresh tokens
security.transport: HTTPS on university lab Nginx reverse proxy

data.storage: Local NFS at /data/missions/ for raw multispectral TIFFs

devops.environments: dev/staging/production on same server via Docker Compose

open_questions.sensor_calibration: Handling per-flight radiometric calibration panel data for NDVI comparability across missions

confirmed_decisions.gis: PostGIS for field boundaries and prescription zones

reasoner_reviews.product: The core research value is validating nitrogen prescription accuracy against yield outcomes measured at harvest. The prescription zone export workflow must support ISO 11783 (ISOXML) format for compatibility with John Deere and Case IH variable-rate controllers used in field trials. Agronomic summary generation from the LLM adds interpretability for non-technical researchers reviewing prescription maps.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: GDAL-based raster pipeline with CuPy GPU band math is appropriate for 300MB+ multispectral TIFFs from DJI Zenmuse P1. Celery with Redis broker correctly decouples long NDVI processing jobs (10-15 minutes) from the Flask API. PostGIS spatial indexing on field_boundaries and prescription_zones geometries handles intersection and containment queries efficiently. Empirical line calibration using panel reflectance targets is the standard radiometric correction method for drone imagery.
reasoner_reviews.security: JWT 8-hour access tokens with 7-day refresh tokens in httpOnly cookies resolve the session expiry problem from round 1. Researcher tokens expiring mid-session during long NDVI jobs was a usability blocker. No external data egress aligns with FAA Part 107 research operation data requirements. CSRF double-submit cookie pattern is sufficient for the lab network context.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Llama 3.1 70B Q4_K_M GGUF requires approximately 42GB on disk and 10GB active VRAM during inference, fitting within the A4000 16GB VRAM budget. Inference latency will be approximately 45 seconds per prescription at this quantization level; this is acceptable for the research use case. The quantization quality tradeoff versus fp16 must be documented in the grant report.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The calibration panel integration fix in round 2 is critical for scientific validity. Without per-flight empirical line calibration, NDVI values computed from different flights will reflect varying illumination conditions rather than true canopy reflectance differences, invalidating any temporal nitrogen stress analysis across the growing season.

backend.service_design: Flask app with four domain modules: missions (flight upload, metadata management), raster (NDVI/NDRE computation via GDAL+CuPy with calibration pipeline), prescriptions (zone segmentation, nitrogen rate assignment, Ollama LLM summary), and export (GeoTIFF, shapefile, ISOXML generation via lxml templates)
backend.api_patterns: RESTful JSON API with multipart upload endpoints for TIFF files; long-running jobs return job_id for Celery task polling via GET /jobs/:id/status with progress percentage
backend.background_jobs: Celery task chain per mission: calibrate_bands -> compute_ndvi -> segment_zones -> generate_llm_summary; each step writes intermediate results to PostGIS and NFS before chaining
backend.llm_integration_design: Ollama Python client calls local Llama 3.1 70B Q4_K_M with structured prompt containing per-zone NDVI statistics (mean, std, area_ha), phenology stage (vegetative/reproductive/grain_fill), and historical field nitrogen response; response stored as agronomic_summary TEXT on prescription_zones
backend.failure_handling: Celery task retry with exponential backoff for GPU OOM errors; failed calibration step falls back to uncalibrated NDVI with calibration_applied=False flag and researcher warning notification

frontend.app_structure: Vue 3 SPA with three primary views: MissionList (flight history and processing status), PrescriptionMap (Leaflet editor for zone review and rate adjustment), ExportPanel (format selection and download); Leaflet map instance is a singleton to avoid re-initialization on route transitions
frontend.pages_and_flows: Mission Upload wizard (flight metadata form, TIFF file picker, calibration panel image selector, submit triggers Celery chain); NDVI Viewer (GeoJSON zone polygons overlaid on satellite basemap, color ramp by NDVI class); Prescription Editor (per-zone nitrogen_rate_kg_ha input fields, agronomic_summary display); Export Dialog (format checkboxes, download link generation)
frontend.state_management: Pinia stores: activeMission (id, status, polling interval), prescriptionZones (array of zone features with editable rates), jobStatus (Celery task state and progress); job polling via setInterval clearing on complete/failed status

security.auth_design: Flask-JWT-Extended with 8-hour access tokens and 7-day refresh tokens; both tokens delivered in httpOnly SameSite=Strict cookies; CSRF double-submit cookie for state-mutating endpoints
security.authorization_model: Researcher role: create missions, export prescriptions, delete missions. Agronomist role: view missions, annotate zones, cannot export or delete. Role encoded in JWT claims checked by Flask before_request decorator.
security.secrets_management: PostGIS connection string and Ollama base URL in .env on server; .env excluded from GitLab repo via .gitignore; no secrets in Docker Compose YAML committed to source control

data.entities: flight_missions (id UUID PK, field_id FK, pilot_name, drone_model, flight_date, raw_tiff_path, calibration_panel_path, processing_status ENUM queued/processing/complete/failed, calibration_applied BOOL, processing_errors JSONB). sensor_calibrations (id UUID PK, mission_id FK, band_name ENUM red/green/blue/red_edge/nir, panel_reflectance FLOAT, gain FLOAT, offset FLOAT, applied_at). ndvi_maps (id UUID PK, mission_id FK, output_geotiff_path, ndvi_min FLOAT, ndvi_max FLOAT, ndvi_mean FLOAT, computed_at). prescription_zones (id UUID PK, ndvi_map_id FK, geom GEOMETRY(Polygon,4326), ndvi_mean FLOAT, nitrogen_rate_kg_ha FLOAT, zone_class ENUM deficient/adequate/excess, agronomic_summary TEXT). field_boundaries (id UUID PK, field_name, grower_name, geom GEOMETRY(MultiPolygon,4326), area_ha FLOAT).
data.storage_design: PostGIS for vector geometries and metadata; NFS at /data/missions/:mission_id/ for raw TIFFs, intermediate GeoTIFFs, and export zips
data.retention_and_deletion: Raw TIFFs retained 5 years per grant terms; processed GeoTIFFs and prescription records retained indefinitely for research reproducibility; pg_dump to lab NAS nightly

devops.deployment_topology: Docker Compose on bare-metal: flask-api, celery-worker (NVIDIA runtime GPU passthrough), redis, postgres-postgis, ollama, grafana, prometheus, nvidia-smi-exporter containers on isolated Docker bridge network
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI pipeline: pytest unit tests for calibration coefficient computation and NDVI band math with 4-band synthetic TIFF fixture, flake8 linting, Docker build and push to lab GitLab Container Registry, SSH deploy job runs docker compose pull && docker compose up -d on bare-metal
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus scrapes Flask /metrics and Celery Flower; nvidia-smi exporter provides GPU VRAM utilization per container; Grafana dashboards: NDVI job throughput, GPU utilization during raster processing, prescription export volume by format
