fix_report.action_taken: Updated ClickHouse price_ticks table from MergeTree to ReplacingMergeTree with deduplication key (contract_id, tick_time, exchange) and documented OPTIMIZE TABLE DEDUPLICATE schedule and final deduplicated view creation in data_model and deployment_and_operations sections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: CME retransmission duplicate ticks deduplicated during scheduled OPTIMIZE runs; historical price data integrity maintained

fix_report.action_taken: Added Redis cache warm-up goroutine to feed adapter pod startup sequence: on init, goroutine queries ClickHouse for latest 100 ticks per assigned commodity group and populates Redis hot-cache before pod readiness probe passes.
fix_report.expected_outcome: New pod startup no longer causes cold-cache P99 latency spikes; readiness probe gate ensures cache is warm before traffic is routed to new pod

target_output.title: Agricultural Commodity Price API
target_output.executive_summary: Go 1.22 Gin REST API serving normalized CME commodity spot and futures price data to credentialed agricultural partner clients with P99 sub-50ms latency via Redis ElastiCache hot-cache. Feed adapter pods ingest CME FIX/FAST ticks via goroutine pools, normalize to CanonicalPriceTick schema, write to ClickHouse ReplacingMergeTree for deduplication-safe historical storage, and populate Redis hot-cache with 10s TTL. Partner authentication uses HMAC-SHA256 API keys with per-commodity access control for exchange license compliance. Round 2 adds CME certificate rotation procedure, ClickHouse deduplication key, and Redis cache warm-up on pod startup.
target_output.architecture_overview: Two independent Kubernetes deployments on EKS us-east-1: feed-adapter and api-handler. Feed-adapter pods maintain persistent mTLS connections to CME FIX/FAST endpoints via goroutine pools partitioned by commodity group (grains, oilseeds, softs). Each incoming tick is parsed, normalized to CanonicalPriceTick (contract_id, exchange, bid, ask, last, volume, tick_time), written asynchronously to ClickHouse price_ticks table and synchronously to Redis hot-cache HSET per contract. Api-handler pods run Gin with middleware chain: TLS termination at ALB, HMAC-SHA256 API key validation, commodity access control enforcement, Redis sliding window rate limit check, then route to Redis hot-cache read (spot/front-month) or ClickHouse query (historical). No cross-service synchronous calls between feed-adapter and api-handler; Redis is the shared data bus for real-time prices.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Go 1.22, Gin 1.9, custom CME FIX/FAST parser (goroutine pool per commodity group). Tick storage: ClickHouse 24.x ReplacingMergeTree on dedicated i4i.4xlarge EC2 (NVMe). Hot-cache: Redis 7 ElastiCache cluster mode r7g.xlarge 3 shards. Auth: HMAC-SHA256 API key middleware, Redis key registry, commodity ACL. Transport: TLS 1.3 for partner API via AWS ALB; mTLS via ACM Private CA for CME feed connections. Orchestration: AWS EKS with HPA (compute-optimized C6i for feed-adapter, memory-optimized R6i for api-handler). CI/CD: GitHub Actions with canary Helm chart. Observability: Datadog APM, custom Go expvar metrics.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Spot Price Endpoint: GET /v1/prices/spot/{commodity} returns best bid, ask, last trade, and timestamp from Redis hot-cache for the front-month contract. P99 target 15ms. Futures Chain Endpoint: GET /v1/prices/futures/{commodity}/{expiry} returns settlement price and open interest for a specific contract expiry from Redis or ClickHouse depending on recency. Historical Price Query: GET /v1/prices/history/{commodity} with from/to/resolution parameters queries ClickHouse deduplicated ReplacingMergeTree view; P99 target 500ms for 30-day range. API Key Management: POST /internal/admin/keys (issue key), DELETE /internal/admin/keys/{id} (revoke within 1s via Redis propagation), GET /internal/admin/keys/{id}/usage (rate limit consumption stats). Partner Commodity ACL: per-key metadata in Redis enforces allowed commodity list per exchange license tier.
target_output.system_components: CME FIX/FAST Feed Adapter: Go goroutine pool (configurable 4-16 goroutines per commodity group) maintaining mTLS connections to CME Market Data Platform. Parses FIX/FAST messages into CanonicalPriceTick structs, batches writes to ClickHouse via HTTP bulk insert, and writes to Redis HSET per contract. Redis Cache Warm-Up Goroutine: on feed-adapter pod startup, queries ClickHouse SELECT contract_id, last, bid, ask, volume FROM price_ticks_final ORDER BY tick_time DESC LIMIT 100 per commodity group, pre-populates Redis hot-cache, blocks readiness probe until complete. Gin API Handler: middleware stack (ALB TLS termination, HMAC-SHA256 key validation, commodity ACL check, Redis rate limit sliding window, request ID injection), routes to PriceHandler (Redis GET) and HistoryHandler (ClickHouse query). ClickHouse price_ticks_final view: FINAL modifier on ReplacingMergeTree deduplicates retransmitted ticks for query consistency. Datadog APM agent: sidecar on each pod emitting traces with exchange, commodity, and partner_key_id span tags.
target_output.workflows: Tick Ingestion Workflow: CME FIX/FAST message arrives on mTLS connection. Feed adapter goroutine parses message, extracts tick fields, creates CanonicalPriceTick. Goroutine writes to Redis HSET commodity:{contract_id} (synchronous, 10s TTL refresh) and appends to ClickHouse bulk insert buffer (flushed every 100ms or 1000 ticks, whichever first). If Redis write fails, retried up to 3 times with 10ms backoff before logging error and continuing. Feed lag metric (ingestion_time minus tick_time) emitted to Datadog. Partner Price Query Workflow: Partner client sends GET /v1/prices/spot/CORN with X-API-Key and X-Signature headers. ALB routes to api-handler pod. Middleware chain: (1) validates HMAC-SHA256 signature and timestamp freshness within 30s, (2) checks key commodity ACL allows CORN, (3) increments Redis sliding window counter for key_id and enforces RPM limit, (4) executes Redis HGET commodity:ZCN25 returning bid/ask/last/updated_at, (5) returns JSON response. Total path P99 target 50ms.
target_output.data_model: commodity_contracts: {contract_id STRING, exchange ENUM cme/ice, commodity ENUM corn/soybeans/wheat/cotton/sorghum, expiry_month STRING YYYY-MM, tick_size FLOAT, contract_size_bushels INT}. price_ticks ClickHouse ReplacingMergeTree: (contract_id STRING, exchange String, bid Float64, ask Float64, last Float64, volume UInt32, tick_time DateTime64(3), ingestion_time DateTime64(3)) ENGINE = ReplacingMergeTree(ingestion_time) PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(tick_time) ORDER BY (contract_id, tick_time, exchange). price_ticks_final view: SELECT * FROM price_ticks FINAL for deduplicated reads. OPTIMIZE TABLE price_ticks DEDUPLICATE BY (contract_id, tick_time, exchange) scheduled nightly 02:00 ET. spot_cache_entries Redis HSET: key=commodity:{contract_id}, fields bid/ask/last/volume/updated_at, TTL 10s. api_clients Redis HASH: key=apikey:{key_id}, fields org_name/commodities_allowed/rate_limit_rpm/license_tier/expires_at. Commodity trading financial entities: trade_transactions (id UUID PK, contract_id VARCHAR FK, trade_date DATE, settlement_date DATE, trade_type ENUM buy/sell/spread, quantity_bushels NUMERIC, price_per_bushel NUMERIC, notional_value_cents BIGINT, currency CHAR(3), account_id VARCHAR, counterparty_id VARCHAR), account_ledger (id UUID PK, account_id VARCHAR, trade_id FK, entry_type ENUM debit/credit, amount_cents BIGINT, currency CHAR(3), balance_after_cents BIGINT, margin_call_flag BOOLEAN, posted_at TIMESTAMPTZ), settlement_runs (id UUID PK, settlement_date DATE, exchange VARCHAR, total_transactions INT, total_settlement_cents BIGINT, currency CHAR(3), status ENUM pending/settled/failed, cleared_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: REST API v1 base path /v1. GET /v1/prices/spot/{commodity} — Redis hot-cache read, returns {contract_id, bid, ask, last, volume, updated_at, exchange}. GET /v1/prices/futures/{commodity}/{expiry_month} — Redis for current month, ClickHouse for historical expiries. GET /v1/prices/history/{commodity}?from=ISO&to=ISO&resolution=1m|5m|1h — ClickHouse aggregated OHLCV query against price_ticks_final view. GET /v1/status — liveness: feed_adapter_connected bool, cache_hit_ratio float, feed_lag_ms int. POST /internal/admin/keys — issue new partner API key with commodity ACL payload. DELETE /internal/admin/keys/{key_id} — immediate Redis revocation. All endpoints require X-API-Key and X-Signature headers except /internal/admin (VPN-only network policy).
target_output.security_and_compliance: mTLS for CME feed connections via ACM Private CA: 90-day certificate lifecycle. Automated renewal: cert-manager CertificateRequest 30 days before expiry generates new cert from ACM Private CA. Feed adapter connection pool implements dual-cert hot-swap: new cert loaded while old cert remains active; graceful drain of connections using old cert over 60s window; old cert removed from pool. Certificate private keys stored in AWS Secrets Manager; never written to pod filesystem. Partner API key auth: HMAC-SHA256(key_secret, HTTP_METHOD+URL_PATH+UNIX_TIMESTAMP) with 30s timestamp tolerance for replay prevention. Per-partner commodity ACL enforced at API handler layer before route execution; unauthorized commodity access returns 403 and logged to CloudWatch compliance stream. CFTC audit log: all partner API requests with key_id, commodity, endpoint, timestamp written to CloudWatch Logs group /commodity-api/cftc-audit with 1-year retention. Exchange license enforcement: api_clients.license_tier field maps to allowed commodities per exchange agreement; premium tier includes options chain data when available.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: EKS cluster us-east-1: feed-adapter node group (C6i.2xlarge, 2-10 nodes, HPA on custom Datadog metric cmc.tick_ingestion_rate), api-handler node group (R6i.xlarge, 3-30 nodes, HPA on RPS 5000/pod). ClickHouse on 2x i4i.4xlarge EC2 with keeper (ZooKeeper replacement) for replication. ElastiCache Redis cluster (r7g.xlarge, 3 shards, Multi-AZ). GitHub Actions CI: go vet, golangci-lint, go test -race, docker build, ECR push, Helm upgrade with canary weight 10%, Datadog SLO API check (P99 <50ms for 5 minutes), promote to 100% or rollback. ClickHouse deduplication maintenance: nightly CloudWatch Events rule triggers ECS task running OPTIMIZE TABLE price_ticks DEDUPLICATE BY (contract_id, tick_time, exchange) at 02:00 ET. Certificate rotation: cert-manager watches ACM Private CA cert expiry, triggers renewal at T-30 days, new cert loaded into feed-adapter connection pool via Kubernetes Secret update and pod rolling restart with 60s graceful drain. Pod startup sequence: init container populates Redis hot-cache from ClickHouse latest ticks; readiness probe passes only after cache warm-up complete.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM distributed traces: feed-adapter spans tagged with exchange/commodity/tick_latency_ms; api-handler spans tagged with endpoint/partner_key_id/commodity/cache_hit bool. Custom Go expvar metrics exported to Datadog: cmc.tick_ingestion_rate (ticks/sec per exchange), cmc.cache_hit_ratio (target >99%), cmc.feed_lag_ms (target <100ms), cmc.api_p99_ms (target <50ms), cmc.rate_limit_rejections_per_partner. PagerDuty critical alerts: feed_lag_ms >500ms (CME connection degraded), cache_hit_ratio <98% for 2 minutes, api_p99_ms >50ms for 5 minutes, any feed-adapter pod CrashLoopBackOff. CloudWatch CFTC audit log stream for compliance reporting. ClickHouse system.query_log monitored for slow historical queries via Datadog log pipeline.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $4,200/month: EKS compute $1,800 (feed-adapter C6i.2xlarge x3 baseline + api-handler R6i.xlarge x5 baseline), ClickHouse i4i.4xlarge x2 $1,400, ElastiCache r7g.xlarge x3 $600, data transfer and ALB $400. HPA scales feed-adapter to 10 pods during CME peak hours (09:00-14:30 ET market open). Spot instance pricing for non-critical api-handler pods reduces compute cost 40% with fallback to on-demand. ClickHouse monthly OPTIMIZE reduces storage growth from retransmission duplicates by estimated 8-12%.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (Complete, Round 1): CME FIX/FAST feed adapter for corn, soybeans, wheat, cotton; REST spot and front-month futures endpoints; Redis hot-cache; ClickHouse tick storage; HMAC-SHA256 API key auth; EKS deployment with HPA. Phase 2 (Current, Round 2 focus): ClickHouse ReplacingMergeTree deduplication, CME cert rotation procedure, Redis cache warm-up on pod startup, per-commodity ACL enforcement, CFTC audit log stream. Phase 3 (6 weeks): ICE feed adapter for sugar, coffee, cocoa; WebSocket price stream subscriptions via gorilla/websocket; historical bulk export API endpoint; partner self-service key management portal.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go 1.22 with strict golangci-lint ruleset (errcheck, gosec, staticcheck). All ClickHouse writes via asynchronous bulk insert buffer to prevent tick ingestion blocking on DB I/O. Redis operations use pipelining for batch cache warm-up on pod startup. HMAC-SHA256 key validation in constant-time comparison to prevent timing attacks. ClickHouse queries against historical data must use price_ticks_final view (FINAL deduplication) not raw price_ticks table. Exchange-specific error codes mapped to canonical ErrorType enum in feed adapter to prevent exchange-specific error handling from leaking into business logic. Integration tests run against ClickHouse and Redis in GitHub Actions via testcontainers-go.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: CME FIX/FAST protocol version upgrade requires feed adapter parser changes with potential data gap during migration. Mitigation: maintain parser for current and previous FIX/FAST version simultaneously with feature flag for cutover. Risk: ClickHouse OPTIMIZE TABLE DEDUPLICATE is a heavyweight operation that can cause read latency spikes on the deduplicated view during execution. Mitigation: schedule nightly at 02:00 ET (minimum CME trading volume); monitor ClickHouse system.merges during optimize. Tradeoff: ReplacingMergeTree deduplication requires querying price_ticks_final view (FINAL modifier) which is slower than raw table scan; acceptable for historical queries where P99 SLA is 500ms not 50ms. Risk: Redis ElastiCache cluster failover during CME market open could cause 3-10 second hot-cache unavailability. Mitigation: api-handler falls back to ClickHouse latest-tick query on Redis miss with 503 header warning of degraded latency.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: CME certificate renewal confirmed as ACM Private CA with cert-manager; dual-cert hot-swap procedure eliminates downtime risk. ClickHouse deduplication confirmed as ReplacingMergeTree with (contract_id, tick_time, exchange) dedup key; nightly OPTIMIZE scheduled for off-peak hours. Redis cache warm-up confirmed as init goroutine backfilling last 100 ticks per commodity from ClickHouse before readiness probe passes.

metadata.notes: High-scale commodity price API; Go/ClickHouse/Redis; Round 2 fixing mTLS cert rotation (ISS-001), ClickHouse deduplication (ISS-002), and Redis warm-up (ISS-003).

project_goal.value: Mobile app for livestock producers tracking individual animal health events, vaccination records, and weight measurements with offline-capable data entry and cloud sync for birth-to-market traceability
project_goal.rationale: Livestock producers need field-ready mobile tools that work without cellular coverage in remote paddocks

target_users.value: Livestock producers, ranch managers, and field workers conducting daily animal health observations and vaccination administration on cattle, swine, and sheep operations
target_users.rationale: Primary users work in remote paddocks with intermittent connectivity; data entry must be fast and offline-capable

project_class.rationale: Flutter mobile app with offline SQLite storage and cloud sync is the correct class; no primary desktop or web interface

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, analytics, integrations
capabilities.rationale: Mobile UI, Node.js REST backend, PostgreSQL, Firebase Auth, GCP analytics, RFID integration for ear tag scanning

complexity_level.rationale: Offline-first sync with conflict resolution, RFID ear tag scanning integration, and traceability report generation add significant complexity

risk_level.rationale: Animal health records have traceability compliance implications; offline sync conflicts could result in incorrect treatment records

data_sensitivity.rationale: Proprietary farm animal health and production data; not a regulated data category but commercially sensitive

external_exposure.rationale: App access restricted to authenticated farm operation users; no public-facing endpoints

access_model.value: Firebase Auth with operation-scoped tenancy; field workers see only animals in their assigned lots; managers and owners have full operation view
access_model.rationale: Multi-user farm operations require role-based lot assignment to prevent cross-operation data access

feature_scope.value: RFID ear tag scanning for animal identification, health event logging (illness, treatment, death), vaccination record entry with withdrawal period tracking, weight measurement logging, lot and pen assignment management, birth record entry, market shipment traceability reports
feature_scope.rationale: Full birth-to-market traceability feature set

mvp_scope.value: RFID ear tag scan for animal ID, health event and vaccination logging with offline SQLite queue, cloud sync to PostgreSQL on connectivity restore, basic animal detail view, Flutter iOS and Android
mvp_scope.rationale: Core logging workflow must work offline; traceability reports and weight tracking deferred to Phase 2

security_baseline.value: Firebase Auth JWT for API authentication, HTTPS TLS 1.3 for sync API, SQLite database encrypted with SQLCipher on device, operation-scoped data isolation in PostgreSQL
security_baseline.rationale: Animal health records are commercially sensitive; device encryption and operation isolation are baseline requirements

frontend_stack.value: Flutter 3 with Dart; Drift (SQLite ORM) for local offline storage; flutter_blue_plus for RFID reader BLE integration
frontend_stack.rationale: Flutter provides cross-platform iOS and Android from single codebase; Drift is the mature Flutter SQLite ORM with offline sync support

backend_stack.value: Node.js 20 with Express; REST API for sync endpoints; Bull queue for background traceability report generation
backend_stack.rationale: Existing team Node.js expertise; Express is sufficient for the sync API throughput at this scale

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for cloud storage; SQLite with SQLCipher on device for offline queue; Redis 7 for session cache and report job queue
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL for relational animal and health event data; SQLite offline queue for field data entry

hosting_target.value: GCP Cloud Run for Node.js API; Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16; Bitrise for mobile CI/CD builds
hosting_target.rationale: Cloud Run serverless scaling suits burst sync load during morning and evening check times

privacy_retention_policy.value: Animal health records retained 5 years per USDA APHIS traceability guidelines; device SQLite data purged on sign-out; operation data deleted 30 days after account cancellation
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Livestock traceability records support disease traceback investigations requiring multi-year retention

future_scope.value: Weight measurement logging with scale Bluetooth integration, market shipment traceability report generation, integration with packer buyer traceability portals, herd analytics dashboard
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 and 3 features expanding from core health logging to full production analytics

constraints.value: Must operate fully offline in paddocks with no cellular coverage; sync conflict resolution must never silently discard health event records; iOS 16+ and Android 12+ minimum versions
constraints.rationale: Remote paddock operation is the primary use case; health record completeness is non-negotiable for traceability

observability_baseline.value: Google Cloud Monitoring for Cloud Run metrics; Firebase Crashlytics for mobile crash reporting; sync success and conflict rate tracked as custom metrics
observability_baseline.rationale: Mobile crash visibility and sync health monitoring are critical for field reliability

execution_preference.value: Bi-weekly Bitrise builds for mobile; continuous Cloud Run deployment on merge to main; TestFlight and Play Store internal testing tracks
execution_preference.rationale: Mobile release cadence allows adequate field testing before production rollout

project.goal: Offline-first mobile app for livestock health event logging and birth-to-market traceability
project.target_users: Livestock producers and field workers on iOS and Android

frontend.offline_storage: Drift SQLite ORM with SQLCipher encryption
frontend.rfid: flutter_blue_plus for BLE RFID reader connection

backend.queue: Bull with Redis for report generation jobs
backend.sync_api: REST sync endpoint with last-write-wins conflict resolution

data.cache: Redis 7 for session and Bull queue

devops.ci: Bitrise for Flutter builds; GitHub Actions for Node.js API
devops.mobile_distribution: TestFlight and Play Store internal track

constraints.offline: Full functionality offline; sync on connectivity restore
constraints.conflict_policy: Never silently discard health event records

reasoner_reviews.product: Round 1 delivered the core offline logging architecture. The main weakness identified was the plan describing the tech stack as React Native despite the contract specifying Flutter 3. This inconsistency in the technology_stack and system_components sections is a contract violation that must be corrected in Round 2.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The sync conflict resolution strategy in Round 1 was described as last-write-wins at the health_event level, which is acceptable for non-medical records. However the plan did not specify how the offline sync queue handles network interruption during a partial batch upload — a data loss scenario for health records logged mid-sync. The offline queue must be append-only with atomic batch commit.
reasoner_reviews.security: Round 1 security section omitted SQLCipher as the device encryption mechanism despite it being specified in the contract. Instead it described standard SQLite without encryption. This is a contract violation and a blocking issue for the security rubric.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The conflict resolution policy 'never silently discard health event records' was not reflected in the Round 1 sync implementation description. The plan described last-write-wins which can silently discard concurrent edits to the same health event.

frontend.app_structure: Flutter 3 app with feature-based module structure: animals (list, detail, RFID scan), health_events (log entry, history, offline queue), vaccinations (record entry, withdrawal calendar), lots (pen and lot assignment). Drift database with SyncQueue table for pending uploads. Riverpod 2 providers for offline-first state with optimistic local writes.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Home screen with lot summary counts (animals per lot, pending health events). Animal List screen with RFID scan button activating flutter_blue_plus BLE reader. Animal Detail screen with health event timeline, vaccination history, and current lot/pen. Health Event Entry screen (offline-capable): event type selector (illness/treatment/vaccination/death/observation), symptom multi-select, treatment drug picker with withdrawal period auto-calculation. Sync status indicator in app bar showing pending upload count.
frontend.state_management: Riverpod 2: AnimalNotifier (local Drift query with cloud sync overlay), HealthEventNotifier (offline queue with sync status), SyncNotifier (connectivity watch, batch upload coordinator). Optimistic local write pattern: health event written to local SQLite immediately, queued for sync, sync status reflected in UI.
frontend.frontend_risks: RFID BLE reader pairing reliability varies by device model and Android BLE stack; test against target hardware (Allflex RS420 reader) early. SQLCipher key management must handle app upgrade migrations without database re-encryption overhead on large herds.

backend.service_design: Express REST API on Cloud Run with routes: POST /sync/health-events (batch upload with operation_id scoping), GET /animals/:id (cloud record fetch), POST /animals (create with RFID tag number). Bull queue for PDF traceability report generation jobs triggered by POST /reports/traceability.
backend.api_patterns: POST /sync/health-events: accepts array of HealthEventPayload with client_created_at timestamp and local_id. Server uses operation_id from JWT claim to scope inserts. Conflict handling: if server record for same animal_id + event_type + server_created_at within 60s window exists, keep both records with conflict_flag=true rather than discarding either. Returns array of {local_id, server_id, status} for client to update its local sync queue.
backend.failure_handling: Cloud Run request timeout 60s for sync batch; client retries with same batch idempotency key if timeout occurs. Bull queue jobs retried 3 times with exponential backoff on report generation failure.

security.auth_design: Firebase Auth JWT tokens validated in Express middleware. operation_id claim extracted from custom Firebase token claims and used for all database query scoping. Token refresh handled by Firebase SDK on mobile; server validates token expiry.
security.authorization_model: PostgreSQL row-level security policies: all tables include operation_id column; RLS policy enforces operation_id = current_setting('app.operation_id') for all queries. Field worker role restricted to animals and health events for their assigned lots only via lot_assignments table join.
security.security_risks: SQLite on-device must use SQLCipher with a device-derived key (using secure enclave on iOS, Android Keystore on Android) to protect animal health records if device is lost. Plain SQLite without encryption is a contract violation.

data.entities: animals (id UUID, operation_id UUID FK, rfid_tag_number STRING UNIQUE per operation, species ENUM cattle/swine/sheep, birth_date DATE, sex ENUM, dam_id UUID nullable, sire_id UUID nullable, current_lot_id UUID FK, status ENUM active/sold/dead), health_events (id UUID, animal_id UUID FK, operation_id UUID FK, event_type ENUM illness/treatment/vaccination/death/observation, event_date DATE, notes TEXT, recorded_by UUID FK users, conflict_flag BOOL DEFAULT false, client_created_at TIMESTAMP, server_created_at TIMESTAMP), vaccination_records (id UUID, health_event_id UUID FK, vaccine_product STRING, dose_ml FLOAT, route ENUM subcutaneous/intramuscular/oral, withdrawal_days INT, withdrawal_date DATE COMPUTED), lot_assignments (animal_id UUID FK, lot_id UUID FK, assigned_at TIMESTAMP, released_at TIMESTAMP nullable), market_shipments (id UUID, operation_id UUID FK, shipment_date DATE, destination_buyer STRING, lot_ids UUID[]).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 Cloud SQL with operation_id row-level security. Local SQLite via Drift with SyncQueue table (local_id, entity_type, payload_json, sync_status ENUM pending/uploading/synced/failed, created_at). SQLCipher encryption on local SQLite database with key derived from Firebase UID via platform secure storage.
data.retention_and_deletion: Cloud health records 5-year retention. Local SQLite synced records prunable after 90 days to manage device storage. Operation data deletion 30 days post-cancellation via Cloud Tasks scheduled job.

devops.deployment_topology: GCP Cloud Run service (Node.js, min 1 instance for cold start avoidance, max 20 for burst sync load). Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 in same VPC. Redis Memorystore for Bull queue and session cache. Bitrise workflows for Flutter build, test, and TestFlight/Play Store distribution.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions for Node.js: lint (ESLint), test (Jest with Cloud SQL emulator), docker build, Cloud Run deploy. Bitrise for Flutter: flutter analyze, flutter test, flutter build ios/android, upload to TestFlight and Play Store internal track on main merge. Bi-weekly manual promotion from internal to production track after field testing sign-off.
devops.devops_risks: Cloud Run cold starts add 2-4 second latency to first sync after idle period; min-instances=1 prevents cold start for the primary sync endpoint but adds baseline cost.

target_output.title: Livestock Health Tracking Mobile App
target_output.executive_summary: Flutter 3 offline-first mobile app for livestock producers logging individual animal health events, vaccinations, and RFID ear tag scans in remote paddocks without cellular connectivity. Local Drift/SQLite storage with SQLCipher encryption queues records for batch sync to a Node.js Express Cloud Run API backed by PostgreSQL 16. Firebase Auth with operation_id row-level security enforces multi-operation tenancy. Conflict resolution keeps both concurrent records with conflict_flag rather than silently discarding, honoring the traceability completeness constraint.
target_output.architecture_overview: Flutter 3 mobile app with Drift ORM managing local SQLite (SQLCipher-encrypted) for offline-first data entry. Riverpod 2 providers implement optimistic local write: health events, vaccination records, and RFID scan results written immediately to device SQLite SyncQueue, then batch-uploaded to Cloud Run Node.js Express API on connectivity restore. PostgreSQL 16 Cloud SQL on GCP stores the authoritative animal health record with operation_id row-level security for multi-operation tenancy. Sync conflict policy: server creates both records with conflict_flag=true when concurrent edits detected, never discarding either. Firebase Auth issues JWT with operation_id custom claim; Express middleware validates JWT and sets PostgreSQL session variable for RLS enforcement. Bull queue on Redis Memorystore handles background traceability PDF report generation.
target_output.technology_stack: Mobile: Flutter 3.22 with Dart 3, Drift 2.x SQLite ORM, SQLCipher 4.x for device database encryption, Riverpod 2 for state management, flutter_blue_plus for BLE RFID reader (Allflex RS420) pairing, Firebase Auth SDK for JWT token management. Backend: Node.js 20, Express 4, firebase-admin for JWT validation, Bull 4 with Redis for report job queue, pg driver for PostgreSQL. Cloud: GCP Cloud Run (min 1 instance), Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16, Redis Memorystore, Firebase Auth, Google Cloud Monitoring, Firebase Crashlytics. CI/CD: Bitrise for Flutter iOS/Android builds and TestFlight/Play Store distribution; GitHub Actions for Node.js API.
target_output.functional_feature_map: RFID Ear Tag Scan: flutter_blue_plus connects to Allflex RS420 BLE reader, receives tag number, looks up or creates animal record locally. Health Event Entry: offline-capable form with event_type selector (illness/treatment/vaccination/death/observation), symptom multi-select from species-specific symptom library, treatment drug picker with automatic withdrawal period calculation from drug database. Vaccination Record: records vaccine product, dose, route, and computes withdrawal_date from drug withdrawal_days. Lot and Pen Management: assign animals to lots, view lot summary with active animal count and pending health events. Sync Status: SyncNotifier watches connectivity, shows pending upload count in app bar, executes batch upload on connectivity restore. Market Shipment Traceability Report: links all animals in a lot to their complete health history and validates antibiotic withdrawal compliance before shipment (Phase 2).
target_output.system_components: Flutter App: Drift database (SQLCipher key from platform secure storage), SyncQueue table (local_id, entity_type, payload_json, sync_status, attempt_count), AnimalRepository (Drift queries + cloud sync overlay), HealthEventRepository (offline write + queue), SyncCoordinator (connectivity watch via connectivity_plus, batch upload to /sync endpoint). Node.js Express API: authMiddleware (Firebase JWT validation, operation_id extraction), syncRouter (POST /sync/health-events batch upsert with conflict detection), animalsRouter (CRUD with RLS), reportRouter (Bull job enqueue for PDF generation). PostgreSQL 16: animals, health_events (with conflict_flag), vaccination_records, lot_assignments, market_shipments tables with operation_id RLS policies. Redis Memorystore: Bull job queue for report generation, Firebase session cache. Firebase Crashlytics: mobile crash reports with device model and OS version tags.
target_output.workflows: Offline Health Event Logging Workflow: Field worker scans RFID tag with Allflex BLE reader via flutter_blue_plus. Flutter app looks up animal by rfid_tag_number in local Drift SQLite. Worker selects event type (e.g., vaccination), picks vaccine product from local drug list, enters dose and route. Drift writes health_event and vaccination_record to local SQLite with sync_status=pending. SyncNotifier detects no connectivity; event remains in SyncQueue. When paddock connectivity restores, SyncCoordinator batches pending events (up to 50 per request) and POSTs to /sync/health-events with idempotency key. Server inserts records scoped to operation_id, checks for concurrent edits within 60s window, creates conflict_flag=true duplicates if found. Returns local_id to server_id mapping; Drift updates sync_status to synced. Market Shipment Traceability Workflow (Phase 2): Ranch manager selects a lot for shipment, app generates traceability report request. Bull job fetches all animals in lot, joins health_event history and vaccination_records, validates withdrawal dates are cleared for all treatment records, generates PDF via PDFKit, uploads to Cloud Storage, pushes download link notification to manager.
target_output.data_model: animals: {id UUID PK, operation_id UUID FK, rfid_tag_number VARCHAR, species VARCHAR, birth_date DATE, sex CHAR(1), dam_id UUID nullable FK, sire_id UUID nullable FK, current_lot_id UUID FK, status VARCHAR}. health_events: {id UUID PK, animal_id UUID FK, operation_id UUID FK, event_type VARCHAR, event_date DATE, notes TEXT, recorded_by UUID FK, conflict_flag BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, client_created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, server_created_at TIMESTAMPTZ}. vaccination_records: {id UUID PK, health_event_id UUID FK, vaccine_product VARCHAR, dose_ml NUMERIC, route VARCHAR, withdrawal_days INT, withdrawal_date DATE}. lot_assignments: {animal_id UUID FK, lot_id UUID FK, assigned_at TIMESTAMPTZ, released_at TIMESTAMPTZ}. Local SQLite SyncQueue (Drift): {local_id TEXT PK, entity_type TEXT, payload_json TEXT, sync_status TEXT, attempt_count INT, created_at INTEGER}. NOTE: data_model uses generic VARCHAR types rather than proper ENUM constraints — this is an intentional quality flaw in this revision demonstrating a plan that fixes the blocking contract violations but still has a data model quality gap.
target_output.api_design: POST /sync/health-events — accepts {idempotency_key, events: [{local_id, animal_id, event_type, event_date, notes, client_created_at, vaccination_payload?}]}. Returns {synced: [{local_id, server_id}], conflicts: [{local_id, conflict_server_id}]}. GET /animals/:id — returns animal with latest health event summary. POST /animals — create animal with rfid_tag_number (unique per operation_id). GET /lots — list lots with animal counts for operation. POST /reports/traceability — enqueue PDF report Bull job for lot_id. All routes require Firebase JWT Authorization header; operation_id extracted from custom claim.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Firebase Auth JWT validated in Express authMiddleware; operation_id custom claim used to set PostgreSQL session variable app.operation_id before each query, enabling RLS enforcement. PostgreSQL RLS: all tables include operation_id column with policy USING (operation_id = current_setting('app.operation_id')::uuid). Device encryption: SQLCipher 4.x on local Drift SQLite database. Key derivation: iOS uses SecKeyCreateRandomKey in Keychain with kSecAttrTokenIDSecureEnclave binding; Android uses Android Keystore AES-256-GCM key. Key stored in platform secure storage via flutter_secure_storage; database re-keyed on password change. USDA APHIS traceability: operation_id isolation ensures farm operations cannot access other producers' animal records. Firebase Crashlytics configured with data redaction to exclude animal health event content from crash reports.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GCP Cloud Run Node.js service: min-instances=1 (prevent cold start on morning sync burst), max-instances=20, Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 via Cloud SQL Auth Proxy sidecar. Redis Memorystore for Bull queue. GitHub Actions CI: ESLint, Jest tests with Cloud SQL emulator, docker build, Cloud Run deploy on main merge. Bitrise Flutter CI: flutter analyze, flutter test, flutter build ios (--release), flutter build appbundle, upload to TestFlight (iOS) and Play Store internal track (Android) on main merge. Bi-weekly field testing cycle: internal track promoted to production track after QA sign-off on offline sync and RFID BLE pairing. Firebase Crashlytics integrated in Flutter app for production crash visibility. Google Cloud Monitoring alerts: Cloud Run error rate >1%, sync endpoint P99 latency >3s.
target_output.observability: Firebase Crashlytics: mobile crash reports with Flutter stack traces, device model, OS version, and Flutter version tags. Custom Crashlytics keys: sync_queue_depth (pending upload count at time of crash), last_sync_status. Google Cloud Monitoring: Cloud Run request count, error rate, P99 latency. Custom metrics logged via Cloud Logging: sync_batch_size (events per batch upload), sync_conflict_count (per-operation daily count), ble_connection_success_rate (RFID reader pairing success ratio). Alert: sync_conflict_count >10/day for any operation triggers investigation; high conflict rates indicate clock skew between worker devices.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: GCP estimated $380/month: Cloud Run min-instances=1 $45, burst scaling to 20 instances during morning/evening sync $80, Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 db-standard-2 $190, Redis Memorystore Basic 1GB $40, Firebase Auth free tier, Bitrise Team plan $25/month. Scales to 500 concurrent farm operations without architecture changes; Cloud Run auto-scales sync burst load; Cloud SQL read replica added when analytics queries degrade sync endpoint performance.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): Flutter iOS and Android app with RFID ear tag scan, health event and vaccination logging, offline SQLite SyncQueue with SQLCipher, Firebase Auth, Node.js sync API, PostgreSQL with RLS, Bitrise CI/CD to TestFlight and Play Store internal track. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Weight measurement logging with Bluetooth scale integration, lot management and pen assignment, market shipment traceability PDF report generation via Bull queue, antibiotic withdrawal compliance validation. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Herd analytics dashboard (death rate, treatment frequency by lot), packer buyer portal integration for electronic traceability record submission, bulk import from legacy paper records via CSV.
target_output.development_guidelines: Flutter: Dart null safety enforced, Riverpod 2 for all state (no setState in feature widgets), Drift migrations versioned and tested with in-memory SQLite in unit tests. SQLCipher key must never be logged or included in crash reports — Crashlytics redaction configured. Node.js: ESLint with no-console rule (use structured logging via pino), all PostgreSQL queries use parameterized statements (no string interpolation), operation_id extracted from JWT claim never from request body. Sync idempotency: SyncCoordinator includes idempotency_key (UUID v4 generated per batch) in POST /sync/health-events; server stores processed idempotency keys in Redis for 24 hours to handle retry deduplication.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Allflex RS420 BLE connection unreliable on certain Android device models due to manufacturer-specific BLE stack variations. Mitigation: manual RFID tag number entry fallback always available in UI; field test with target device hardware before GA release. Risk: SQLCipher key loss (device wipe without backup) results in unrecoverable local SQLite data. Mitigation: sync pending queue items before enabling device wipe; warn users if sync_queue_depth >0 when logout initiated. Tradeoff: keep-both-with-conflict-flag policy creates duplicate health event records requiring manual review by ranch manager for conflicting entries; accepted because data completeness for USDA APHIS traceability is more important than clean records. Risk: Cloud Run cold start (when min-instances not warm) causes 3-5s first-sync delay for early-morning field workers; mitigated by min-instances=1 baseline.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Conflict resolution confirmed as keep-both-with-conflict-flag (not last-write-wins) per explicit constraint. SQLCipher 4.x selected over Android-native Room encryption because Drift SQLite ORM has first-class SQLCipher support via sqlcipher_flutter_libs. RFID reader confirmed as Allflex RS420 BLE; flutter_blue_plus supports BLE central mode on both iOS and Android.

metadata.notes: Round 2 fixing ISS-001 (React Native -> Flutter) and ISS-002 (SQLCipher omitted) contract violations; data_model still uses VARCHAR instead of ENUM constraints — intentional residual flaw.

profile.domain: AI Yield Prediction Platform for Row Crop Operations

project_goal.value: Deliver field-level corn and soybean yield predictions from multi-year yield monitor data, satellite NDVI time series, SSURGO soil surveys, and NOAA weather history to support pre-harvest marketing decisions
project_goal.rationale: Core business value is reducing price risk uncertainty for large row-crop operators before harvest

target_users.value: Grain operation managers, farm agronomists, and grain merchandisers at large-scale row crop farms (5000+ acres)
target_users.rationale: Primary users are making pre-harvest marketing contract decisions using yield predictions

project_class.rationale: ML training pipelines and inference serving are the architectural center of this product

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, ai_llm, data, analytics, batch_jobs, integrations, public_api
capabilities.rationale: Requires model training pipelines, real-time inference, web dashboard, public API for merchandising system integration

complexity_level.rationale: Processes satellite tiles at field resolution across millions of acres with continuous model retraining

risk_level.rationale: Farm yield data is commercially sensitive but not regulated; model errors could affect marketing decisions

data_sensitivity.rationale: Farm yield records and field boundaries are proprietary and subject to contractual non-disclosure

external_exposure.rationale: Dashboard and public API require authentication; no anonymous access to farm data

access_model.value: JWT-based auth via Auth0 with organization-scoped tenancy; API key auth for public API consumers; field data isolated per farm organization
access_model.rationale: Multi-tenant architecture requires strict data isolation between farm organizations

feature_scope.value: Yield monitor data ingestion, satellite imagery processing pipeline, soil survey integration, weather data fusion, per-field ML model training on Vertex AI, yield prediction dashboard with confidence intervals, public REST API for prediction export
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set required for commercial launch

mvp_scope.value: Yield monitor upload and validation, SSURGO soil feature extraction, single-season prediction model per field, dashboard showing predictions with CI bands, API endpoint for prediction export
mvp_scope.rationale: MVP scoped to corn predictions for existing pilot customers before soybean model expansion

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 in transit, AES-256 at rest for farm data, Auth0 JWT with RS256, API key rotation, GCP CMEK for BigQuery datasets
security_baseline.rationale: Confidential farm data requires encryption and strong key management

frontend_stack.value: React 18 with TypeScript, Mapbox GL JS for field visualization, Recharts for prediction timelines
frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise; Mapbox GL JS handles high-resolution field polygon rendering at scale

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI for inference API and management services; Vertex AI for model training and serving
backend_stack.rationale: Python ecosystem for ML pipelines; FastAPI for async performance on high-throughput prediction requests

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for operational data, BigQuery for feature store and model training datasets, GCS for satellite imagery tiles and yield monitor raw files
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL for transactional records; BigQuery for petabyte-scale feature engineering

hosting_target.value: GKE Autopilot for API and web services; Vertex AI Pipelines for ML workflows; Cloud Build for CI/CD
hosting_target.rationale: GKE Autopilot reduces cluster management overhead; Vertex AI integrates natively with GCP data stack

privacy_retention_policy.value: Farm yield data retained for 7 years per contractual obligation; satellite imagery tiles retained 3 years; model artifacts retained for 5 years for audit reproducibility; data deleted on customer offboarding within 90 days
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Contractual data retention and reproducibility requirements for model audits

future_scope.value: Soybean and wheat model expansion, intra-season yield forecast updates as imagery accumulates, integration with CME futures pricing for automated hedge recommendations
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap contingent on MVP model accuracy validation

constraints.value: Satellite imagery licensing costs must stay under $0.02/acre/season; model retraining must complete within 48 hours of new imagery ingestion; predictions must reach customers before typical marketing window (October 1 for corn)
constraints.rationale: Commercial viability depends on per-acre unit economics and delivery timing

observability_baseline.value: Google Cloud Monitoring for infrastructure metrics, Vertex AI Model Monitoring for prediction drift, custom dashboards for batch pipeline completion rates and model accuracy degradation
observability_baseline.rationale: ML system requires model-specific observability beyond standard APM

execution_preference.value: Two-week sprints with dedicated ML engineering and backend tracks; model accuracy gate required before production promotion
execution_preference.rationale: ML and software engineering cycles need parallel but coordinated execution

compliance_context.value: Commercial satellite imagery licensing restricts redistribution and resale of raw tiles; contractual farm data confidentiality NDAs with each farm organization; no public regulatory compliance requirements for yield prediction
compliance_context.rationale: Data licensing and contractual obligations are the primary compliance constraints

llm_integration.value: Vertex AI Gemini Pro used for natural-language explanation of yield prediction factors; users can query 'why is field 14-N predicted 12% below farm average?' and receive agronomic explanations synthesized from feature importance scores
llm_integration.rationale: LLM-powered explanations reduce adoption barrier for non-technical farm managers

project.goal: Field-level yield predictions for corn and soybean to support pre-harvest marketing
project.target_users: Farm managers, agronomists, grain merchandisers
project.timeline: October 1 delivery for corn prediction window

frontend.mapping: Mapbox GL JS for field polygon visualization
frontend.charts: Recharts for yield prediction timelines and CI bands

backend.ml_platform: Vertex AI Pipelines and Model Registry

security.auth: Auth0 with RS256 JWT, org-scoped claims
security.api_auth: API key management with per-key rate limits
security.encryption: CMEK via Cloud KMS for BigQuery, GCS server-side encryption

data.object_store: GCS for satellite tiles, yield monitor files
data.cache: Redis 7 on Memorystore for API response caching

devops.ci: Cloud Build with automated model validation gates
devops.environments: dev, staging, production with model promotion workflow

reasoner_reviews.product: Pre-harvest marketing use case is tightly scoped and commercially motivated. The October 1 hard deadline for corn predictions must be a hard constraint in all pipeline scheduling. Intra-season update cadence needs clarification for user expectation setting -- customers may expect weekly updates as imagery accumulates through the growing season.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Vertex AI Pipelines is the right choice for ML workflow orchestration given the GCP commitment, but the 48-hour retraining SLA is aggressive for fields with large yield monitor history. Feature engineering in BigQuery via SQL transforms avoids Python runtime bottlenecks for the data prep stage. The prediction API should cache results in Redis to handle dashboard load during the pre-harvest period when all users are active simultaneously.
reasoner_reviews.security: Multi-tenant field data isolation must be enforced at the database row level using organization_id predicates, not just at the application layer. The public API must enforce per-organization quotas independently of Auth0 JWT claims, because API key consumers may bypass the web auth flow. Satellite imagery tiles stored in GCS must use object-level ACLs tied to organization buckets, not a shared bucket with path-based access.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The $0.02/acre imagery cost constraint will require selective imagery provider sourcing -- Planet Basemaps are typically priced above this for high-resolution products. The 48-hour retraining SLA assumes pre-computed soil feature vectors are available; the pipeline must cache SSURGO features per field to avoid re-querying the USDA API on every training run.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 2 addressed the missing multi-tenant isolation in the data model, but the public API rate limiting strategy still references a global counter rather than per-organization counters. The Gemini LLM integration also lacks a fallback when explanation generation fails -- the dashboard must degrade gracefully to showing raw feature importances if the LLM call times out.

backend.service_design: Three FastAPI services: InferenceService (sync predictions and async batch jobs), ManagementService (farm/field CRUD, user management), PublicAPIService (external API key auth, rate limiting). Vertex AI SDK calls wrapped in circuit breakers with 10s timeout.
backend.api_patterns: REST with OpenAPI 3.0. Internal services use gRPC for low-latency prediction requests from dashboard. External public API is REST only with versioned paths (/v1/predictions, /v1/fields).
backend.background_jobs: Vertex AI Pipelines DAGs for: (1) imagery ingestion and tile normalization, (2) feature engineering per field, (3) model retraining per organization, (4) prediction batch generation. Triggered by GCS event notifications when new data arrives.
backend.llm_integration_design: Gemini Pro called via Vertex AI SDK after prediction generation. Input: field_id, feature_importance dict (top 10 features), prediction value, farm-average prediction. Output: 3-sentence agronomic explanation. Timeout: 8s. Fallback: return null, UI renders feature importance chart instead.
backend.failure_handling: Prediction pipeline failures trigger PagerDuty alert if any field organization misses the October 1 SLA. DLQ on Pub/Sub for failed imagery ingestion events. Stale predictions flagged with last_updated timestamp in API responses.

frontend.app_structure: React SPA with code-split routes: /dashboard (field map + prediction overview), /fields/:id (per-field detail with yield history and prediction CI), /api-docs (public API reference), /admin (org management).
frontend.pages_and_flows: Field Map Dashboard: Mapbox GL JS layer showing all fields colored by predicted yield percentile. Click field to open prediction detail drawer. Prediction Detail: yield prediction with 80% CI band overlaid on historical yield chart, top-5 contributing features, LLM explanation panel. Marketing Integration: export predictions to clipboard or API key generation for merchandising system push.
frontend.state_management: React Query for all server data with 5-minute stale time. Zustand for map viewport and selected field state. Prediction export wizard uses local form state.
frontend.frontend_risks: Mapbox GL JS rendering 10,000+ field polygons simultaneously requires vector tile clustering or GeoJSON simplification for fields under 100 acres at zoom levels below 12. Without this, dashboard paint time will exceed 3 seconds on mid-range hardware.

security.auth_design: Auth0 with GCP OIDC federation. RS256 JWT claims include organization_id and role (manager/agronomist/viewer). Public API uses API key header (X-API-Key) validated against organization_api_keys table.
security.authorization_model: Row-level security on all PostgreSQL tables via organization_id. GCS buckets partitioned per organization (org-{uuid}-satellite-tiles). BigQuery dataset per organization for feature store isolation.
security.secrets_management: GCP Secret Manager for database credentials and API keys. Vertex AI service account uses Workload Identity. No secrets in container images or environment variables.
security.security_risks: LLM prompt injection risk if field names contain adversarial strings that alter the Gemini explanation prompt. Mitigation: sanitize all user-controlled strings before LLM context construction.

data.entities: yield_monitor_records (id, field_id, organization_id, harvest_year, geometry GEOMETRY, yield_value_bu_ac, harvest_date, equipment_id), satellite_imagery_tiles (id, field_id, acquisition_date, band_type ENUM ndvi/ndre/red_edge, tile_gcs_path, cloud_cover_pct), soil_survey_units (id, field_id, ssurgo_mukey, sand_pct, clay_pct, om_pct, aws_100cm, hydric_flag), field_predictions (id, field_id, model_version_id, prediction_bu_ac, ci_lower, ci_upper, prediction_date, feature_importance_json), model_versions (id, organization_id, crop_type, training_dataset_version, rmse_cv, r2_cv, promoted_at, deprecated_at)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL for operational records. BigQuery ML dataset per organization for feature engineering and model training materialized views. GCS buckets per organization for raw satellite tiles and yield monitor upload staging.
data.analytics_events: prediction_viewed, prediction_exported_to_api, llm_explanation_requested, model_accuracy_viewed, field_added, yield_monitor_uploaded
data.data_risks: SSURGO soil data is fetched from USDA WFS at field setup time; if the USDA API is unavailable, soil features will be missing and model accuracy degrades by ~15% based on ablation tests. Cache soil features in BigQuery after first fetch.

devops.deployment_topology: GKE Autopilot cluster in us-central1 (primary GCP region for Vertex AI proximity). Three namespaces: inference, management, public-api. Vertex AI Pipelines in same project for IAM simplicity.
devops.ci_cd_design: Cloud Build triggers on PR merge: unit tests, model validation against holdout dataset (RMSE < 15 bu/ac gate), Docker build, push to Artifact Registry, rolling deploy to GKE. Model promotion requires manual approval in Cloud Build for production.
devops.observability_stack: Google Cloud Monitoring for GKE node and pod metrics. Cloud Logging for structured API logs with field_id and organization_id correlation. Vertex AI Model Monitoring tracks prediction distribution drift vs. training baseline. Custom Cloud Monitoring dashboard for pipeline completion rates.
devops.devops_risks: GKE Autopilot node provisioning latency can delay batch pipeline starts during imagery ingestion peaks. Mitigation: pre-warm node pool with placeholder workloads during known harvest season peak periods (August-October).

ISS-001.title: Public API rate limiting uses global counter instead of per-organization counters
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 and Round 2 plans describe rate limiting as a global request counter per API key, but the requirement is per-organization isolation so one customer cannot starve others. Redis key schema must be rate_limit:{organization_id}:{api_key_id}:{minute_bucket}.

history.detail: Initial plan used a single global rate limit counter
Updated description still references global counter; Redis key schema not corrected

ISS-002.title: LLM explanation endpoint missing fallback when Gemini call fails
ISS-002.detail: If the Vertex AI Gemini call times out or returns an error, the current plan has no defined fallback behavior. The frontend must receive a degraded response (feature importance JSON) rather than a 500 error that collapses the prediction detail panel.

history.detail: Identified during round 2 audit; fallback path not specified in API design or frontend sections

ISS-003.title: SSURGO soil feature cache not addressed in data pipeline design
ISS-003.detail: Round 2 added BigQuery caching for SSURGO features after initial fetch, satisfying the constraint that per-field soil queries must not re-hit the USDA WFS API on every retraining run.

history.detail: BigQuery caching added to data section in round 2 plan

focus_issues.title: Public API rate limiting uses global counter instead of per-organization counters
focus_issues.detail: Redis key schema must use rate_limit:{organization_id}:{api_key_id}:{minute_bucket} to enforce per-organization isolation.

focus_issues.title: LLM explanation endpoint missing fallback when Gemini call fails
focus_issues.detail: Fallback must return feature importance JSON with a degraded flag so the frontend renders the chart instead of a 500 error.

previous_plan.title: Yield Prediction Platform Architecture v2
previous_plan.executive_summary: A GCP-native AI system for field-level corn and soybean yield predictions using multi-year yield monitor data, NDVI satellite time series, and SSURGO soil features. React 18 dashboard with Mapbox GL JS field visualization and FastAPI inference services on GKE Autopilot. Vertex AI Pipelines orchestrate seasonal retraining with a hard October 1 delivery gate.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: GCP-native three-tier architecture with React SPA on Cloud CDN, FastAPI microservices on GKE Autopilot, and Vertex AI for ML training and serving. Field data isolated per organization in PostgreSQL and partitioned GCS buckets.
previous_plan.api_design: POST /v1/predictions/batch (trigger batch prediction run), GET /v1/predictions/{field_id} (fetch latest prediction with CI), GET /v1/explanations/{field_id} (LLM agronomic explanation), POST /v1/fields (register new field with boundary GeoJSON), GET /v1/models/current (active model version metadata). Public API rate limited per API key (global counter -- unresolved ISS-001).
previous_plan.data_model: yield_monitor_records, satellite_imagery_tiles, soil_survey_units (with BigQuery cache added round 2), field_predictions, model_versions, organization_api_keys.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 focuses on two unresolved issues: ISS-001 (per-organization rate limiting Redis schema) and ISS-002 (LLM explanation fallback path). The API design section must explicitly show the rate_limit:{org_id}:{key_id}:{minute_bucket} Redis key structure and the quota enforcement middleware order. The LLM fallback must be specified in both the API design (response envelope with degraded flag) and frontend sections (conditional rendering logic). All other sections from round 2 are stable and can be carried forward with minor refinements.

fix_report.action_taken: Updated API design to document rate limiting middleware using per-organization Redis keys: rate_limit:{organization_id}:{api_key_id}:{minute_window}. Quota enforcement happens in PublicAPIService middleware before routing to handlers. Counters use Redis INCR with 60-second TTL. Each organization has an independent quota ceiling configured in the organization_api_keys table.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Per-organization rate limiting enforced independently; one organization exhausting its quota does not affect others; ISS-001 resolved

fix_report.action_taken: Added explicit LLM fallback specification: GET /v1/explanations/{field_id} returns {explanation: null, degraded: true, feature_importance: {...}} with HTTP 200 when Gemini call fails or times out after 8s. Frontend conditional: if degraded=true, renders FeatureImportanceChart instead of ExplanationPanel. Error is logged with field_id and organization_id for monitoring.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Dashboard never shows 500 errors from LLM failures; feature importance chart renders as fallback; ISS-002 resolved

target_output.title: Yield Prediction Platform Architecture v3
target_output.executive_summary: A GCP-native AI system for field-level corn and soybean yield predictions serving large row-crop operations (5000+ acres). React 18 and Mapbox GL JS dashboard provides field-polygon-level yield prediction visualization with confidence intervals and LLM-powered agronomic explanations. FastAPI microservices on GKE Autopilot serve predictions from Vertex AI-trained models, with per-organization data isolation enforced at database, storage, and API layers. Vertex AI Pipelines orchestrate seasonal retraining from yield monitor records, NDVI satellite time series, and SSURGO soil features, targeting October 1 prediction delivery for the corn marketing window.
target_output.architecture_overview: GCP-native architecture with three FastAPI microservices (InferenceService, ManagementService, PublicAPIService) deployed on GKE Autopilot in us-central1. React 18 SPA served via Cloud CDN with Mapbox GL JS for field polygon rendering. Vertex AI Pipelines orchestrate the four-stage ML workflow: satellite tile ingestion, BigQuery feature engineering, per-organization model retraining, and batch prediction generation. PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL for operational records with organization_id row-level isolation. BigQuery ML datasets partitioned per organization for feature store and training data. GCS buckets partitioned per organization (org-{uuid}-satellite-tiles, org-{uuid}-yield-uploads). Redis 7 on Memorystore for prediction API caching and per-organization API rate limit counters. Vertex AI Model Registry tracks trained model versions with RMSE and R2 metrics for promotion gating.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18 TypeScript, Mapbox GL JS 3.x (field polygon clustering enabled for orgs with 5000+ fields), Recharts for prediction CI timelines, React Query for server state. Backend: Python 3.12 FastAPI (3 services), Vertex AI SDK for Gemini Pro LLM calls and Pipelines orchestration. Data: PostgreSQL 16 Cloud SQL, BigQuery for feature store, GCS for imagery and upload staging, Redis 7 Memorystore. Auth: Auth0 RS256 JWT with organization_id claims; API key table in PostgreSQL with per-org quota. CI/CD: Cloud Build with RMSE validation gate, Artifact Registry, GKE rolling deploys. Observability: Google Cloud Monitoring, Vertex AI Model Monitoring, Cloud Logging structured with field_id and org correlation.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Yield Monitor Ingestion: operators upload raw yield monitor CSV/SHP files via drag-drop dashboard; async validation pipeline normalizes yield values to bu/ac and detects outlier passes. Field Prediction Dashboard: Mapbox GL JS layer renders all fields colored by predicted yield percentile relative to farm average; fields clustered below zoom 12. Prediction Detail View: per-field panel with historical yield chart, current prediction with 80% CI band, top-5 feature importances, and LLM agronomic explanation (with FeatureImportanceChart fallback if degraded). Public API: partner merchandising systems call GET /v1/predictions/{field_id} with API key to retrieve prediction summaries for pre-harvest contract sizing. Model Accuracy Dashboard: agronomists view per-model RMSE and R2 scores across cross-validation folds before approving production promotion.
target_output.system_components: PublicAPIService (Go-equivalent rate limiting middleware with per-org Redis counters, JWT and API key dual auth), InferenceService (sync and async prediction endpoints, Gemini Pro LLM integration with 8s timeout), ManagementService (field CRUD, organization admin, yield monitor upload handling), Vertex AI Pipelines (4-stage DAG: imagery ingest, feature engineering, model train, prediction batch), PostgreSQL 16 Cloud SQL (operational records with org row-level isolation), BigQuery (feature store datasets partitioned per org), GCS (per-org buckets for satellite tiles and yield uploads), Redis 7 Memorystore (prediction cache, rate limit counters keyed by org+key+minute), Auth0 tenant (RS256 JWT issuance), Cloud Build pipeline (unit tests, RMSE gate, deploy).
target_output.workflows: Seasonal Retraining Workflow: Cloud Scheduler triggers the Vertex AI Pipeline DAG on August 1 for corn. Stage 1 downloads new-season Sentinel-2 NDVI tiles from licensed imagery provider into org-scoped GCS buckets. Stage 2 BigQuery SQL jobs compute 10-week NDVI trajectory features per field and join with cached SSURGO soil vectors and NOAA weather station interpolations. Stage 3 trains an XGBoost model per organization on the merged feature table, validated against prior-season holdout with RMSE gate. Stage 4 generates batch predictions for all active fields with confidence intervals derived from quantile regression. Predictions are written to field_predictions table and cache-invalidated in Redis. Pre-Harvest API Export Workflow: Grain merchandiser calls POST /v1/api-keys to provision an API key; key is stored in organization_api_keys with quota_per_minute ceiling. On each GET /v1/predictions/{field_id}, PublicAPIService middleware evaluates INCR on Redis key rate_limit:{organization_id}:{api_key_id}:{unix_minute}; if count exceeds org quota, returns 429 with Retry-After header. Successful requests hit InferenceService which reads from Redis prediction cache (5-min TTL) falling back to PostgreSQL.
target_output.data_model: yield_monitor_records (id UUID, field_id FK, organization_id FK, harvest_year INT, geometry GEOMETRY(MULTIPOLYGON,4326), yield_bu_ac NUMERIC, harvest_date DATE, equipment_id, outlier_flag BOOL, ingested_at). satellite_imagery_tiles (id UUID, field_id FK, organization_id FK, acquisition_date DATE, band_type ENUM ndvi/ndre/red_edge/nir, tile_gcs_path, cloud_cover_pct, source_provider). soil_survey_units (id UUID, field_id FK, ssurgo_mukey VARCHAR, sand_pct, clay_pct, om_pct, aws_100cm_cm3, hydric_flag, cached_at -- prevents re-query of USDA WFS). field_predictions (id UUID, field_id FK, model_version_id FK, organization_id FK, prediction_bu_ac NUMERIC, ci_lower, ci_upper, feature_importance_json JSONB, prediction_date DATE, degraded_explanation BOOL). model_versions (id UUID, organization_id FK, crop_type ENUM corn/soybean, training_harvest_years INT[], rmse_cv NUMERIC, r2_cv NUMERIC, promoted_at, deprecated_at). organization_api_keys (id UUID, organization_id FK, key_hash VARCHAR, quota_per_minute INT, last_rotated_at, revoked_at).
target_output.api_design: InferenceService: GET /v1/predictions/{field_id} (returns prediction with CI, reads Redis cache first), POST /v1/predictions/batch (triggers Vertex AI pipeline for org), GET /v1/explanations/{field_id} (Gemini Pro LLM call with 8s timeout; response: {explanation: string|null, degraded: bool, feature_importance: {feature: string, importance: float}[]} -- returns degraded=true with HTTP 200 on LLM failure). ManagementService: POST /v1/fields (register field with GeoJSON boundary), POST /v1/yield-monitors/upload (multipart upload trigger), GET /v1/models/current (active model version per org). PublicAPIService: all /v1/* endpoints behind X-API-Key header. Rate limiting middleware: Redis INCR on rate_limit:{organization_id}:{api_key_id}:{unix_minute_bucket}, TTL 120s; returns 429 with Retry-After: 60 when org quota exceeded.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Auth0 RS256 JWT with organization_id and role claims. All PostgreSQL queries include WHERE organization_id = :org_id enforced at repository layer. GCS bucket IAM: each org bucket grants the GKE service account read/write only for its own bucket path. CMEK via Cloud KMS for BigQuery datasets. API key hashes stored with SHA-256; plaintext returned only at provisioning time. LLM prompt construction sanitizes all user-controlled strings (field names, organization names) to prevent prompt injection. Satellite imagery license compliance: raw tiles stored in private GCS buckets with no public URL generation; tiles served only to the owning organization. Data deletion workflow: on customer offboarding, automated job purges all PostgreSQL rows, GCS objects, and BigQuery data within 90 days.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GKE Autopilot cluster us-central1 with three namespaces (inference, management, public-api). HorizontalPodAutoscaler on InferenceService and PublicAPIService targeting 60% CPU utilization; scale-out pre-warmed August 15 for harvest season. Vertex AI Pipelines scheduled via Cloud Scheduler with pipeline_run_id logged to PostgreSQL for audit trail. Cloud Build pipeline: unit tests, RMSE holdout validation (fail if RMSE > 15 bu/ac), Docker build, push to Artifact Registry, rolling deploy with readiness probe on /healthz. Three environments: dev (single node pool), staging (multi-AZ, shared Vertex AI project), production (multi-AZ, dedicated Vertex AI project for cost isolation). Rollback: Cloud Build deployment step tags previous image; manual rollback via kubectl rollout undo. Secrets in GCP Secret Manager; rotated 90-day cycle.
target_output.observability: Google Cloud Monitoring dashboards for GKE pod CPU/memory, Cloud SQL connection pool utilization, Redis memory, and Memorystore eviction rate. Vertex AI Model Monitoring: prediction distribution drift alerts when p5 or p95 prediction values shift more than 20% from training baseline within a 7-day window. Custom Cloud Monitoring metrics: pipeline_stage_completion_rate (target 100% by Oct 1), prediction_cache_hit_rate (target >80%), llm_explanation_latency_p99, llm_fallback_rate (alert if >5% of explanation requests degraded). Structured Cloud Logging with field_id, organization_id, and pipeline_run_id correlation on all API and pipeline log entries.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $8,000/month at 5M acres served. GKE Autopilot billed per pod resource request -- scale down non-prediction namespaces outside August-October harvest season. BigQuery on-demand queries for feature engineering estimated at $400/month during peak retraining season; consider switching to BigQuery reservations if monthly spend exceeds $800. GCS satellite tile storage: $0.02/GB/month; lifecycle rule archives tiles older than 3 years to Nearline storage ($0.01/GB). Vertex AI Pipelines compute: $1,200/month during August-October retraining cycles. Redis Memorystore M2 instance handles 50K prediction cache entries with TTL eviction.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): yield monitor upload and validation pipeline, SSURGO soil feature extraction with BigQuery cache, single-season XGBoost model for corn, prediction dashboard with Mapbox field visualization and CI bands, Auth0 multi-tenant auth, Public API v1 with per-org rate limiting (ISS-001 fix applied from day 1). Phase 2 (6 weeks): LLM explanation endpoint with degraded fallback (ISS-002 fix), model accuracy dashboard, API key management UI, Vertex AI Model Monitoring integration, Cloud Build RMSE promotion gate. Phase 3 (8 weeks): soybean model, intra-season prediction update cadence (biweekly as imagery accumulates July-September), satellite imagery provider sourcing optimization to hit $0.02/acre cost constraint, CME futures API integration for hedge recommendation prototype.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 strict typing with mypy in CI. pytest coverage gate at 85% for all FastAPI route handlers and Vertex AI pipeline stage functions. Integration tests use a Testcontainers PostgreSQL instance with seeded org and field fixtures. React components tested with React Testing Library; Mapbox GL JS mocked in Jest. PR requires 2 approvals for changes touching organization_id isolation logic. All BigQuery SQL transformations versioned in dbt project alongside Python services. Model training code in separate Python package with version-pinned scikit-learn and XGBoost dependencies.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Satellite imagery cloud cover during August growing season delays NDVI feature computation, causing retraining pipeline to miss October 1 deadline. Mitigation: fallback to Sentinel-1 SAR imagery (cloud-penetrating) for fields where optical cloud cover exceeds 30% in the critical August-September window. Risk: XGBoost per-organization models produce poor predictions for new farms with fewer than 3 seasons of yield monitor history. Mitigation: fall back to county-level ensemble model for organizations with insufficient training data, flagged in the prediction response with low_confidence=true. Tradeoff: BigQuery ML datasets per organization vs. shared dataset with org partitioning -- chosen per-org isolation for regulatory simplicity despite higher BigQuery slot contention. Tradeoff: Vertex AI Pipelines over Airflow -- simpler GCP integration but less flexible for custom operator development if pipeline complexity grows.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed GCS per-org bucket partitioning over shared bucket with path ACLs for cleaner IAM. Confirmed XGBoost over neural network for initial model given interpretability requirement (feature importances for LLM explanation). Confirmed Redis Memorystore over Firestore for prediction cache given sub-100ms latency requirement. October 1 deadline confirmed as hard SLA for corn; soybean predictions deferred to Phase 3.

metadata.notes: Round 3 yield prediction AI system; fixes per-org rate limiting Redis schema and LLM fallback path from prior rounds.

profile.domain: Food Safety Audit Digital Tool for Fresh Produce Packing Houses

project_goal.value: Replace paper-based FSMA food safety audit checklists in fresh produce packing houses with a structured digital data collection tool that creates a queryable audit trail for compliance reporting
project_goal.rationale: Paper records make it impossible to aggregate compliance trends or produce fast reports during regulatory inspections

target_users.value: Packing house food safety auditors, line supervisors, and quality managers at fresh produce facilities
target_users.rationale: Auditors conduct inspections on tablet devices; supervisors respond to corrective action tickets

project_class.rationale: Pilot deployment in a single packing house to validate digital workflow before production rollout

capabilities.rationale: Tablet UI, Django backend, SQLite data store, and monthly summary analytics are the required capability set

complexity_level.rationale: Structured checklist data model with corrective action workflow has moderate relational complexity

risk_level.rationale: Audit records are used in regulatory inspections; data loss or corruption could affect compliance standing

data_sensitivity.rationale: Internal operational records; not subject to external privacy regulations but confidential from competitors

external_exposure.rationale: Deployed on local network within the packing house; no external internet access required

access_model.value: Django built-in auth with role-based permissions: auditor (create/edit findings), supervisor (resolve corrective actions), manager (view reports, export)
access_model.rationale: Simple role hierarchy mapped to Django permission groups without external auth provider

feature_scope.value: Digital audit checklists by packing line and inspection type, nonconformity flagging with corrective action ticket generation, photo attachment capture, monthly food safety summary PDF reports, audit history search and filter
feature_scope.rationale: These features replicate and improve on the paper workflow

mvp_scope.value: Structured checklist completion on tablet with nonconformity flagging, corrective action ticket assignment to supervisors, monthly PDF summary report generation
mvp_scope.rationale: MVP validates the core audit workflow before building advanced analytics

security_baseline.value: HTTPS on internal network (self-signed cert), Django session auth with CSRF protection, database file encrypted at OS level, daily SQLite backup to network share
security_baseline.rationale: On-premise deployment with no external exposure; OS-level encryption sufficient for internal data

frontend_stack.value: React 18 with TypeScript, optimized for tablet touch interaction, offline-capable checklist form with service worker caching
frontend_stack.rationale: React enables complex conditional checklist logic; service worker enables tablet offline use during cold-room inspections

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Django 5.0, Django REST Framework for tablet API
backend_stack.rationale: Django admin accelerates prototype development; DRF provides typed API for React frontend

data_platform.value: SQLite with WAL mode for concurrent tablet writes, stored on an internal Linux server
data_platform.rationale: SQLite with WAL handles the low concurrency of a single packing house; no managed DB overhead for a prototype

hosting_target.value: On-premise Linux server in packing house network; served over local Wi-Fi to auditor tablets
hosting_target.rationale: Packing house has no cloud connectivity requirement; on-premise avoids internet dependency

privacy_retention_policy.value: Audit records retained for 2 years per FSMA produce safety rule inspection readiness requirements; corrective action photos retained 1 year; records purged on annual retention review
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: FSMA produce safety rule requires records available for FDA inspection for minimum 2 years

future_scope.value: Multi-facility deployment with centralized reporting server, integration with GFSI audit scheme APIs (SQF, GlobalG.A.P.), mobile barcode scanning for packing line identification
future_scope.rationale: Production rollout would expand to all facilities in the network

constraints.value: Must run on existing packing house Linux server (Intel NUC, 16GB RAM); tablet devices are Android 12 on Wi-Fi; no cloud services; development budget is a university research grant
constraints.rationale: On-premise hardware constraint and zero cloud spend budget for a research prototype

observability_baseline.value: Sentry on-premise for error tracking; Django admin for operational monitoring; daily backup status email to facility manager
observability_baseline.rationale: Lightweight observability appropriate for a single-facility prototype

execution_preference.value: University research sprint model: 4-week development cycles with weekly review by packing house food safety manager
execution_preference.rationale: Research prototype timeline driven by academic semester and grant deliverables

project.goal: Digital food safety audit checklist system for FSMA compliance
