project.target_users: Packing house auditors, supervisors, quality managers

frontend.form_handling: React Hook Form for conditional checklist fields
frontend.offline: Service worker with Workbox for checklist form caching

backend.api: REST endpoints consumed by React tablet UI

security.auth: Django built-in session auth with CSRF
security.transport: HTTPS with self-signed certificate on local network
security.db_security: SQLite file encrypted at OS level via dm-crypt

data.backup: Daily cron rsync to network-attached storage

devops.hosting: On-premise Linux server, systemd service, Nginx reverse proxy

open_questions.tablet_photo_upload: Confirm max photo file size for corrective action attachments
open_questions.checklist_versioning: Determine if in-flight checklists must be re-versioned when templates are updated

reasoner_reviews.product: The digital audit trail use case is well-scoped for a prototype. The most critical workflow is the corrective action lifecycle -- nonconformity flagging through resolution -- because this is what FSMA inspectors will ask to see. Checklist template versioning is an open question that needs resolution before development starts: FSMA regulations evolve and old inspection records must be queryable against the template version used at time of inspection.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: SQLite with WAL mode is appropriate for the prototype given the low concurrency (5-10 simultaneous tablet connections at most). Django admin significantly reduces prototype development time for checklist template management. The service worker offline caching strategy must handle the case where an auditor in the cold room completes a checklist and syncs when they return to Wi-Fi -- the React form state needs IndexedDB persistence, not just memory, to survive tablet screen lock.
reasoner_reviews.security: The self-signed certificate approach is acceptable for a prototype on a closed network. Django CSRF protection is critical for the tablet web app. The SQLite file should be on a dm-crypt encrypted partition with the key managed by the facility IT, not stored on the same disk. Django admin must be restricted by IP to the server's localhost only -- auditors should never have direct Django admin access.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Intel NUC 16GB RAM is sufficient for SQLite + Django + Nginx serving 10 tablet connections. The main constraint is disk I/O during concurrent WAL writes from multiple tablets. If audits pile up during a busy inspection day with 10 concurrent tablets all writing simultaneously, SQLite WAL writer lock contention could cause mobile app delays. Monitor SQLite busy_timeout setting.

backend.service_design: Django app with four modules: checklists (template CRUD, version management), inspections (audit session lifecycle, finding capture), corrective_actions (ticket assignment, status transitions), reports (PDF generation via ReportLab). Django REST Framework ViewSets expose tablet API.
backend.api_patterns: DRF with TokenAuthentication for tablets (avoids cookie-based session issues on Android WebView). Endpoints: /api/checklists/ (template list), /api/inspections/ (audit session CRUD), /api/findings/ (nonconformity record), /api/corrective-actions/ (ticket management), /api/reports/monthly/ (trigger PDF generation).
backend.failure_handling: SQLite busy_timeout set to 5000ms to handle WAL lock contention during concurrent tablet writes. Django atomic transactions for finding + corrective action creation. Sentry captures uncaught exceptions with user and packing line context.

frontend.app_structure: React SPA served by Nginx with two main views: InspectionConsole (tablet-optimized checklist form) and ReviewDashboard (manager/supervisor list views and report downloads).
frontend.pages_and_flows: InspectionConsole: auditor selects packing line and inspection type, completes conditional checklist sections, marks nonconformities with required photo and comment, submits. Offline: Workbox caches form assets; IndexedDB stores in-progress checklist state for recovery after tablet sleep. ReviewDashboard: supervisor views open corrective action tickets, marks resolved with notes. Manager views monthly summary and triggers PDF export.
frontend.accessibility_notes: Checklist form buttons minimum 48px touch targets for tablet glove use in cold-room conditions. High-contrast color scheme for marking nonconformities under packing house lighting.

security.auth_design: Django DRF TokenAuthentication. Tokens issued per device at facility setup; device tokens stored in Django admin. No self-service login -- accounts provisioned by facility IT.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Django signal handlers log all finding creation, corrective action status changes, and report exports to an audit_trail table with user, timestamp, and record ID. Audit trail is read-only via DB constraint (no UPDATE or DELETE on audit_trail table).

data.entities: audit_checklists (id, name, version INT, inspection_type ENUM sanitation/pest_control/equipment/water_quality, created_at, deprecated_at), checklist_sections (id, checklist_id FK, title, order_seq), inspection_findings (id, inspection_session_id FK, section_id FK, response ENUM pass/fail/na, nonconformity_detail, photo_path, severity ENUM critical/major/minor, flagged_at), corrective_actions (id, finding_id FK, assigned_to_id FK, due_date, status ENUM open/in_progress/resolved/verified, resolution_notes, resolved_at), sanitation_records (id, packing_line_id FK, inspection_session_id FK, submitted_by_id FK, submitted_at, checklist_version), packing_line_assignments (id, packing_line VARCHAR, auditor_id FK, active_since)
data.storage_design: SQLite with WAL mode on dm-crypt encrypted partition. Photos stored as files in /var/audit-photos/ on same server. Daily rsync backup to NAS. SQLite VACUUM scheduled weekly to prevent fragmentation.
data.retention_and_deletion: Annual Python management command reviews inspection_session.submitted_at and deletes records older than 2 years. Photo files deleted in same batch. Deletion logged to audit_trail before execution.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a lightweight on-premise FSMA audit tool for a single packing house. Key constraint is the Intel NUC hardware -- must keep the stack minimal. SQLite WAL is appropriate for low tablet concurrency. The most important design decision is the offline checklist strategy: IndexedDB persistence on the tablet to survive screen lock during cold-room inspections. Django admin accelerates checklist template management without custom UI. Checklist template versioning must be immutable (deprecated flag, never delete) so old inspection records remain queryable against the template version used.
target_output.title: Packing House Food Safety Audit Prototype Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: An on-premise research prototype replacing paper FSMA food safety audit checklists in a fresh produce packing house. React 18 tablet web app with offline IndexedDB checklist state persistence communicates with a Django 5.0 REST API on a local Linux server. SQLite with WAL mode stores structured inspection findings, corrective action tickets, and monthly summary reports. Designed for FDA inspection readiness with 2-year audit trail retention and immutable checklist template versioning.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single-server on-premise architecture: Nginx serves the React 18 SPA and proxies tablet API requests to Django 5.0 with DRF running under Gunicorn on a 16GB Intel NUC. SQLite with WAL journal mode handles concurrent writes from up to 10 Android tablets on the packing house Wi-Fi network. Service worker caching via Workbox enables offline checklist form completion in cold rooms where Wi-Fi drops. Checklist submissions queue in IndexedDB and sync on reconnection. Django admin provides checklist template management and device token provisioning. ReportLab generates monthly food safety summary PDFs directly in the Django process. Sentry (self-hosted) captures uncaught exceptions with packing line and user context.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18 TypeScript, React Hook Form for conditional checklist fields, Workbox service worker for offline asset caching, IndexedDB (via idb library) for in-progress checklist state persistence. Backend: Python 3.12, Django 5.0, Django REST Framework with TokenAuthentication, ReportLab for PDF generation. Database: SQLite with WAL mode, dm-crypt encrypted partition. Infrastructure: Intel NUC 16GB on-premise, Nginx reverse proxy, Gunicorn WSGI, systemd process management. Monitoring: Sentry self-hosted, daily cron backup status email.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Checklist Template Management: facility manager creates/versions inspection checklists (sanitation, pest control, equipment, water quality) via Django admin; templates are immutable once used -- new versions replace deprecated ones. Inspection Session: auditor selects packing line and inspection type, completes conditional section fields on tablet, marks pass/fail/N.A. per item. Nonconformity Flagging: failed items require severity classification (critical/major/minor), text detail, and photo attachment; submission triggers corrective action ticket. Corrective Action Lifecycle: supervisor receives ticket, updates status (open/in_progress/resolved), supervisor manager verifies resolution. Monthly Summary Report: Django management command or UI trigger generates ReportLab PDF summarizing findings by packing line, nonconformity rate by inspection type, and open corrective action count for management and FDA readiness review.
target_output.system_components: Nginx (TLS termination with self-signed cert, static SPA serving, API proxy to Gunicorn), Gunicorn + Django 5.0 (four app modules: checklists, inspections, corrective_actions, reports), SQLite WAL (on dm-crypt encrypted /data partition), /var/audit-photos/ file storage (same server, backed up to NAS), Sentry self-hosted (exception tracking), systemd service units (Gunicorn, Sentry), NAS cron rsync backup (daily 02:00), Workbox service worker (tablet offline checklist asset cache).
target_output.workflows: Packing Line Inspection Workflow: auditor opens tablet browser, authenticates with device token, selects 'Sanitation Inspection - Line 3', completes 28-item checklist with conditional sub-questions triggered by fail responses, attaches photos for two critical nonconformities, submits. Django creates inspection_session, inspection_findings records, and two corrective_action tickets assigned to the sanitation supervisor. Supervisor Notification and Resolution: supervisor opens ReviewDashboard, views open corrective action tickets for their line, updates status to in_progress with notes, re-submits after fix as resolved. Quality manager verifies and marks verified. Corrective action lifecycle logged to audit_trail at each status transition. Monthly FDA Readiness Report: on the first of each month, Django management command (or manager UI trigger) queries the prior month's findings grouped by packing_line and inspection_type, computes nonconformity rates per line, and calls ReportLab to generate a PDF with summary tables and a corrective action closure rate metric. PDF stored in /var/audit-reports/ and emailed to facility manager.
target_output.data_model: audit_checklists (id, name, version INT, inspection_type ENUM sanitation/pest_control/equipment/water_quality, created_by_id FK, created_at, deprecated_at -- deprecated never deleted). checklist_sections (id, checklist_id FK, title, order_seq, conditional_trigger_item_id FK nullable). checklist_items (id, section_id FK, question_text, response_type ENUM pass_fail/multiple_choice/numeric, required_on_fail ENUM photo/comment/both/none). inspection_sessions (id, packing_line_id FK, checklist_id FK, checklist_version INT, submitted_by_id FK, started_at, submitted_at, status ENUM in_progress/submitted). inspection_findings (id, session_id FK, item_id FK, response ENUM pass/fail/na, nonconformity_detail TEXT, photo_path VARCHAR, severity ENUM critical/major/minor, flagged_at). corrective_actions (id, finding_id FK, assigned_to_id FK, due_date DATE, status ENUM open/in_progress/resolved/verified, resolution_notes TEXT, resolved_at). audit_trail (id, user_id FK, action VARCHAR, record_type VARCHAR, record_id INT, detail_json JSONB, timestamp -- no UPDATE/DELETE constraint). packing_line_assignments (id, line_name VARCHAR, auditor_id FK, active_from DATE).
target_output.api_design: DRF endpoints under /api/v1/: GET /checklists/ (active templates for current inspection type), POST /inspections/ (start session), PATCH /inspections/{id}/ (save partial progress), POST /inspections/{id}/submit/ (finalize), POST /findings/ (record individual item response with photo multipart), GET /corrective-actions/?assignee=me (supervisor ticket queue), PATCH /corrective-actions/{id}/ (status update), GET /reports/monthly/?year=2026&month=03 (trigger or fetch PDF). Auth: DRF TokenAuthentication header (Authorization: Token {device_token}). Max photo upload size: 5MB enforced at Nginx client_max_body_size.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Django CSRF middleware enabled for all non-API routes. DRF TokenAuthentication for tablet API -- tokens provisioned per device by facility IT via Django admin, never user self-service. Django admin restricted to 127.0.0.1 via Nginx allow directive. SQLite file on dm-crypt LUKS partition with key stored in facility IT password manager. Audit trail table protected with PostgreSQL-equivalent SQLite trigger rejecting UPDATE and DELETE operations. FSMA record retention: 2-year retention enforced by annual Django management command with pre-deletion audit log entry. Sentry DSN stored in systemd environment file, not in Django settings.py.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Single Intel NUC server. Systemd units: gunicorn.service (Django app, 4 workers), nginx.service, sentry.service. Nginx config: SSL with self-signed cert, proxy_pass to Gunicorn Unix socket, client_max_body_size 5m. Deployment: git pull on server, pip install -r requirements.txt, python manage.py migrate, systemctl restart gunicorn. GitHub Actions runs test suite on PR (pytest with SQLite test DB) -- no automated deploy for prototype. Daily 02:00 cron: rsync /data/audit.db and /var/audit-photos/ to NAS; email facility manager with transfer summary. SQLite VACUUM runs Sunday 03:00.
target_output.observability: Sentry self-hosted captures Django unhandled exceptions with request URL, user ID, packing line context. Django management command weekly_health_check.py reports: SQLite file size, WAL file size (alert if >50MB indicating checkpoint backlog), last backup timestamp from rsync log, count of open corrective actions older than 7 days. Output emailed to facility manager every Monday. No external monitoring services given zero cloud budget.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero recurring cloud cost. Server hardware: existing Intel NUC. NAS backup: existing facility NAS. Development cost: university research grant covers 2 developer-months. SQLite scaling ceiling: handles current facility load (10 tablets, 50 inspections/week) comfortably. Scaling beyond 3 concurrent facilities would require migration to PostgreSQL -- documented as production migration prerequisite.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (4 weeks): checklist template data model and Django admin, inspection session API, tablet checklist form with React Hook Form conditional fields, service worker offline caching, DRF TokenAuthentication. Phase 2 (3 weeks): nonconformity flagging with photo upload, corrective action ticket creation and supervisor queue, audit trail logging. Phase 3 (3 weeks): monthly PDF summary report via ReportLab, manager report dashboard, data retention management command, FSMA inspection readiness review with facility food safety manager.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python black and isort formatters in GitHub Actions CI. pytest-django with factory_boy fixtures for all Django views and API endpoints; target 80% coverage. React components tested with React Testing Library -- tablet touch interactions mocked. SQLite WAL behavior tested with concurrent write stress test in pytest (5 threads simulating simultaneous checklist submissions). PR merged only after passing CI and review by one senior developer on the university research team.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: SQLite WAL contention during mass inspection days (seasonal audits with 10 tablets active simultaneously). Mitigation: set busy_timeout=5000ms and monitor WAL file size; if contention is observed, implement a write queue in Django with asyncio to serialize concurrent writes. Risk: Android tablet service worker behaving inconsistently across Android WebView versions. Mitigation: test offline sync flow on all tablet models in use at facility before deployment. Tradeoff: SQLite over PostgreSQL -- chosen for zero infrastructure overhead in a single-server prototype; documented migration path for production. Tradeoff: ReportLab PDF generation in Django process vs. async task -- synchronous is acceptable for prototype since monthly reports are low-frequency, but production would use Celery.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed immutable checklist template versioning: templates deprecated (deprecated_at timestamp set) but never deleted; inspection_sessions record checklist_version for historical queryability. Confirmed IndexedDB for offline tablet state over service worker cache storage -- IndexedDB survives tablet screen lock; cache storage does not. Confirmed DRF TokenAuthentication per device over session cookies -- Android WebView cookie handling is unreliable on the packing house Wi-Fi network.

metadata.notes: On-premise food safety audit prototype for FSMA compliance; SQLite/Django on local server for packing house.

profile.domain: Farm Field Boundary and Prescription Data API Service

project_goal.value: Expose farm field boundary polygons and variable-rate prescription maps to authorized precision agriculture equipment terminals and third-party farm software tools via a partner-facing REST API, migrated from a monolithic farm management system
project_goal.rationale: Equipment terminals need a stable API to fetch field boundaries and prescriptions without coupling to the monolith

target_users.value: Precision agriculture equipment OEM terminals (planters, sprayers, combines) and authorized third-party farm software tools accessing field data on behalf of farm operator accounts
target_users.rationale: Equipment OEMs are the primary integration partners; farm software tools are secondary consumers

project_class.rationale: Standalone REST API with no browser UI; all consumers are machines or partner integrations

capabilities.rationale: Core capability set for a partner-facing data API with OAuth 2.0 consent flows

complexity_level.rationale: GeoJSON field boundary queries and OAuth 2.0 consent model add complexity above a simple CRUD API

risk_level.rationale: Farm field boundaries have commercial value; unauthorized access exposes farm layout and operational plans

data_sensitivity.rationale: Farm field data is proprietary to the operator; partner access is contractually gated

external_exposure.rationale: API is exposed to authorized OEM equipment partners and farm software vendors, not public consumers

access_model.value: OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow for farm operator consent delegation to equipment terminals; API key auth for server-to-server integrations with farm software tools; scope-based access control per data type (fields:read, prescriptions:read, prescriptions:write)
access_model.rationale: Equipment terminals require operator consent delegation; server-to-server partners use simpler API key auth

feature_scope.value: Field boundary GeoJSON serving, prescription map retrieval and publication, operator consent management, data-sharing audit log, API key management for partner integrations, webhook notifications when prescriptions are updated
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set required to support all active OEM integration partners

mvp_scope.value: OAuth 2.0 consent flow, field boundary endpoint, prescription download endpoint, consent record management, basic API usage audit log
mvp_scope.rationale: Covers the two highest-demand equipment terminal use cases (field boundary fetch and prescription download)

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 for all API traffic, OAuth 2.0 with RS256 JWT, API key hashing with SHA-256, request signing option for high-trust OEM partners, consent records immutable once granted
security_baseline.rationale: Partner-facing API requires strong auth and audit trail for farm data access

frontend_stack.value: None -- API service only
frontend_stack.rationale: No browser UI; field boundary visualization is handled by consuming equipment terminal or farm software

backend_stack.value: Node.js 20 with Fastify, PostGIS for spatial queries via node-postgres
backend_stack.rationale: Fastify schema validation with JSON Schema aligns with GeoJSON field boundary response shapes; node-postgres with PostGIS for spatial query support

data_platform.value: PostGIS (PostgreSQL 16 extension) for field boundary geometry storage and spatial queries; Redis 7 for OAuth token cache and rate limit counters
data_platform.rationale: PostGIS ST_AsGeoJSON and ST_Intersects enable efficient field boundary queries; Redis for token validation hot path

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in us-east-1, ALB with WAF, Route 53 custom domain for API
hosting_target.rationale: Managed containers on Fargate match the monolith migration pattern; existing AWS account in us-east-1

privacy_retention_policy.value: API access logs retained 90 days; consent records retained for life of operator account plus 3 years; field boundary versions retained 5 years for equipment terminal replay
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Audit log retention for dispute resolution; consent record retention per AgGateway data ownership principles

future_scope.value: Webhook push for prescription updates to subscribed equipment terminals, machine telemetry ingestion endpoint, ISOXML export format alongside GeoJSON
future_scope.rationale: OEM partners have requested push notifications to avoid polling; ISOXML is the ISO standard format for equipment terminals

constraints.value: Must maintain API compatibility with existing monolith consumer format (GeoJSON Feature Collections for field boundaries); migration must be transparent to OEM partners already integrated with the monolith endpoint
constraints.rationale: Zero-downtime migration requires exact API response compatibility to avoid breaking existing OEM integrations

observability_baseline.value: AWS CloudWatch for ECS metrics and API access logs, custom CloudWatch metrics for consent grant rate and API error rate per partner, PagerDuty for 5xx error rate alerts
observability_baseline.rationale: Partner-facing API requires per-partner observability to detect integration issues quickly

execution_preference.value: Sprint-based with API contract review gate before each new OEM partner onboarding; OpenAPI spec reviewed by OEM partner engineering teams
execution_preference.rationale: Partner API changes require coordination; spec-first development avoids breaking OEM integrations

backend.framework: Fastify with JSON Schema request/response validation
backend.auth: OAuth 2.0 authorization server (custom) with RS256 JWT

security.oauth: Authorization code flow with PKCE for equipment terminal consent
security.api_key: SHA-256 hashed API keys for server-to-server partners

data.cache: Redis 7 for OAuth token cache and rate limit counters

devops.environments: dev, staging, production

constraints.compatibility: GeoJSON Feature Collection response format matching existing monolith
constraints.migration: Zero-downtime parallel deployment before monolith endpoint deprecation

open_questions.webhook_priority: Confirm if webhook delivery is in MVP or post-MVP

reasoner_reviews.product: The migration framing is the defining constraint -- zero-downtime transparency to existing OEM partners means GeoJSON response shapes must be byte-compatible with the monolith. The consent delegation model for equipment terminals needs a clear UX for farm operators (even though this is an API service, the consent flow requires a minimal HTML grant page). Partner onboarding documentation is critical: equipment OEM engineers will integrate against the OpenAPI spec before writing code.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: PostGIS ST_AsGeoJSON is the correct choice for field boundary serialization -- it handles projection and coordinate precision natively. The OAuth 2.0 authorization server should be built minimal (custom JWT issuance) rather than adding Keycloak or Auth0, since the only grant type needed is authorization code with PKCE and client_credentials. Redis token validation on the hot path avoids a database round-trip on every API call. Fastify JSON Schema validation against the GeoJSON Feature Collection schema enforces response compatibility.
reasoner_reviews.security: Partner-facing API must enforce field data access at the database query layer using operator_account_id and data_sharing_consents checks -- never relying solely on the OAuth scope claims in the JWT. A compromised JWT should not grant access beyond what the consent record explicitly permits. Request signing via HMAC-SHA256 for high-trust OEM partners provides an additional layer of origin verification beyond bearer tokens.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The monolith compatibility constraint means field boundary GeoJSON responses must include the same property keys (field_id, field_name, acreage, crop_type, geometry) in the same Feature Collection wrapper. Prescription map responses must maintain the existing JSON schema with rate_units, product_name, zones array. Any deviation will break OEM terminal display logic without a firmware update cycle.

backend.service_design: Single Fastify application with three route domains: /auth (OAuth 2.0 endpoints), /v1/fields (boundary queries), /v1/prescriptions (map CRUD and download). Shared middleware stack: TLS termination at ALB, JWT verification, consent scope check, rate limit enforcement.
backend.api_patterns: RESTful with OpenAPI 3.1 spec as source of truth. GeoJSON Feature Collection response for field boundary endpoints. Prescription maps returned as JSON with zones array (compatible with monolith schema). OAuth 2.0 endpoints follow RFC 6749: /auth/authorize, /auth/token, /auth/revoke.
backend.scaling_notes: PostGIS spatial queries use GiST index on geometry column. Redis Cluster for token cache. ECS Fargate auto-scaling on ALB request count metric. Read replica for field boundary queries during planting season peak.
backend.backend_risks: OAuth 2.0 custom implementation risk: incorrect PKCE verification could allow authorization code interception. Mitigation: use well-tested node-oauth2-server library with custom adapter for PostgreSQL token storage.

security.auth_design: OAuth 2.0 authorization server with authorization code + PKCE flow for equipment terminals. Client credentials flow for server-to-server farm software integrations. RS256 JWT with scope claims (fields:read, prescriptions:read, prescriptions:write) and operator_account_id embedded.
security.authorization_model: Consent table check on every request: SELECT FROM data_sharing_consents WHERE operator_account_id = :op AND grantee_client_id = :client AND scope_granted CONTAINS :requested_scope. Denies even if JWT is valid but consent was revoked.
security.secrets_management: AWS Secrets Manager for PostgreSQL credentials and RS256 private key. API key plaintext returned once at provisioning; SHA-256 hash stored in DB. 90-day rotation reminder for API keys.
security.security_risks: Consent replay attack: revoked consent records must be hard-marked as revoked_at in DB; OAuth token revocation endpoint must also invalidate Redis cached tokens immediately on revocation.

data.entities: field_boundaries (id UUID, operator_account_id FK, field_name, acreage NUMERIC, crop_type, geometry GEOMETRY(MULTIPOLYGON,4326), created_at, version INT), prescription_maps (id UUID, field_id FK, operator_account_id FK, product_name, rate_units, zones_json JSONB, published_at, superseded_at), data_sharing_consents (id UUID, operator_account_id FK, grantee_client_id FK, scope_granted TEXT[], granted_at, revoked_at, revocation_reason), api_access_grants (id UUID, client_id FK, key_hash VARCHAR, quota_per_minute INT, last_rotated_at, revoked_at), api_audit_log (id BIGSERIAL, client_id, operator_account_id, endpoint VARCHAR, method VARCHAR, status_code INT, latency_ms INT, timestamp)
data.storage_design: PostGIS RDS PostgreSQL 16 with GiST index on field_boundaries.geometry. Redis 7 for OAuth JWT cache (TTL matching JWT exp claim) and per-client rate limit counters. S3 for prescription map ISOXML export files (future scope).

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate cluster in us-east-1 with two services: api-service (Fastify app, 2-8 tasks), oauth-service (OAuth 2.0 endpoints, 2 tasks). ALB routes /auth/* to oauth-service, /v1/* to api-service. WAF rules block SQL injection patterns and rate-limit per source IP.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: lint, jest unit tests (90% coverage gate), OpenAPI spec validation against response fixtures, Docker build, push ECR, ECS rolling deploy. Staging environment runs OEM partner integration smoke tests against real terminal emulators before production promotion.
devops.devops_risks: ECS rolling deploy with zero-downtime requires ALB connection draining set to 30s. The monolith must remain live in parallel until all OEM partners have verified the migrated endpoint, requiring a parallel deployment window of at least 4 weeks.

target_output.thinking_summary: Migrating a farm field boundary and prescription API from a monolith to a standalone Fastify service. The governing constraint is GeoJSON response compatibility: OEM equipment terminal firmware expects exact Feature Collection shapes. OAuth 2.0 with PKCE for consent delegation is required for equipment terminals; server-to-server partners use API key auth. PostGIS ST_AsGeoJSON handles field boundary serialization with projection control. Consent checks at DB layer (not just JWT scope) prevent revoked-consent data leakage.
target_output.title: Farm Field Boundary and Prescription Data API Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A partner-facing REST API service exposing field boundary polygons and variable-rate prescription maps to authorized precision agriculture equipment terminals (planters, sprayers, combines) and farm software integrations. Built with Node.js 20 and Fastify on ECS Fargate, with PostGIS for spatial field boundary queries. OAuth 2.0 authorization code with PKCE handles farm operator consent delegation to equipment terminals; API key auth covers server-to-server software partners. GeoJSON Feature Collection responses maintain byte-level compatibility with the migrated monolith endpoint to avoid disrupting existing OEM integrations.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single-region AWS deployment with two ECS Fargate services behind an ALB with WAF: api-service (Fastify, /v1/* routes) and oauth-service (OAuth 2.0 endpoints, /auth/* routes). ALB path-based routing directs requests to the appropriate service. PostGIS on RDS PostgreSQL 16 stores field boundary polygons with GiST spatial index; ST_AsGeoJSON serializes boundaries to GeoJSON in the database layer to avoid application-side geometry conversion. Redis 7 caches validated JWT tokens to eliminate DB round-trips on the auth hot path and tracks per-client rate limit counters. AWS WAF blocks SQL injection patterns and rate-limits per source IP at the edge. GitHub Actions CI enforces OpenAPI spec validation against response fixtures before any deploy, guaranteeing monolith response format compatibility.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Node.js 20, Fastify 4.x with JSON Schema validation, node-postgres with PostGIS extension functions (ST_AsGeoJSON, ST_Intersects), node-oauth2-server with PostgreSQL adapter for token storage. Database: PostGIS RDS PostgreSQL 16, GiST index on geometry column, read replica for boundary query load. Cache: Redis 7 for JWT validation cache and per-client rate limit counters. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate (api-service 2-8 tasks, oauth-service 2 tasks), ALB with WAF, ACM TLS cert, Route 53 custom API domain. CI/CD: GitHub Actions, ECR, ECS rolling deploy with 30s connection draining. Observability: AWS CloudWatch, custom metrics per partner, PagerDuty alerts.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Field Boundary API: equipment terminals fetch GeoJSON Feature Collection of all fields for an authorized operator; ST_Intersects query supports bbox filter for terminal map viewport sync. Prescription Map API: equipment terminals download current prescription map for a field; agronomists publish new prescription versions from farm management system, triggering version supersession. OAuth 2.0 Consent Flow: operator grants or revokes data access to a named equipment terminal or farm software client; minimal HTML consent grant page hosted by oauth-service. API Key Management: farm software partners provision server-to-server API keys with scope selection; keys expire on 90-day rotation cycle. Data-Sharing Audit Log: all field data accesses logged with client_id, operator_account_id, and scope; queryable by operator to review what data each terminal accessed.
target_output.system_components: api-service (Fastify routes for /v1/fields, /v1/prescriptions, /v1/consents, /v1/audit-log), oauth-service (RFC 6749 endpoints /auth/authorize, /auth/token, /auth/revoke, minimal HTML consent grant page), PostGIS RDS (field boundaries, prescription maps, consent records, audit log), Redis 7 (JWT validation cache keyed by jti claim, per-client rate limit counters keyed by client_id:minute_bucket), ALB (path routing, TLS termination, health checks), WAF (SQL injection rules, per-IP rate limiting), Secrets Manager (DB credentials, RS256 private key).
target_output.workflows: Equipment Terminal Field Sync Workflow: Planter terminal powers on in the field, sends OAuth authorization code with PKCE to oauth-service to exchange for a JWT with fields:read scope and operator_account_id. Terminal calls GET /v1/fields?bbox=-95.2,41.5,-95.0,41.7 -- api-service verifies JWT, checks data_sharing_consents table for active fields:read grant, executes PostGIS query SELECT ST_AsGeoJSON(boundary)... WHERE operator_account_id = :op AND ST_Intersects(geometry, ST_MakeEnvelope(:bbox)), returns GeoJSON Feature Collection matching monolith schema. Prescription Publish and Download Workflow: Agronomist publishes a new variable-rate nitrogen prescription for a field via the farm management system which calls POST /v1/prescriptions with zones_json payload; api-service supersedes the prior version (sets superseded_at), creates new prescription_maps record. Equipment terminal calls GET /v1/prescriptions/{field_id}/current -- receives JSON with product_name, rate_units, zones array in monolith-compatible schema. Webhook notification queued (future scope).
target_output.data_model: field_boundaries (id UUID PK, operator_account_id UUID FK, field_name VARCHAR, acreage NUMERIC, crop_type VARCHAR, geometry GEOMETRY(MULTIPOLYGON,4326) -- GiST indexed, created_at, version INT). prescription_maps (id UUID PK, field_id UUID FK, operator_account_id UUID FK, product_name VARCHAR, rate_units VARCHAR, zones_json JSONB, published_at, superseded_at -- null if current). data_sharing_consents (id UUID PK, operator_account_id UUID FK, grantee_client_id UUID FK, scope_granted TEXT[], granted_at, revoked_at, revocation_reason -- immutable once granted; revocation sets revoked_at). api_access_grants (id UUID PK, client_id UUID FK, key_hash VARCHAR, scope_granted TEXT[], quota_per_minute INT, last_rotated_at, revoked_at). api_audit_log (id BIGSERIAL, client_id UUID, operator_account_id UUID, endpoint VARCHAR, method VARCHAR, status_code INT, latency_ms INT, timestamp -- append-only, 90-day retention).
target_output.api_design: All endpoints under /v1/ with OpenAPI 3.1 spec as contract. GET /v1/fields (params: operator_id, bbox optional) -> GeoJSON FeatureCollection with properties: field_id, field_name, acreage, crop_type, geometry. GET /v1/fields/{field_id} -> GeoJSON Feature. GET /v1/prescriptions/{field_id}/current -> {id, field_id, product_name, rate_units, zones: [{zone_id, rate_value, geometry}]}. POST /v1/prescriptions (publish new version, sets superseded_at on prior). GET /v1/consents (operator lists active consents). DELETE /v1/consents/{id} (operator revokes; triggers Redis JWT invalidation). GET /v1/audit-log?client_id=&from=&to= (operator data access history). Auth: /auth/authorize (PKCE), /auth/token (code exchange), /auth/revoke. Rate limiting: 429 with Retry-After header when per-client Redis counter exceeds quota_per_minute.
target_output.security_and_compliance: OAuth 2.0 authorization code with PKCE enforced for all equipment terminal clients; PKCE code_verifier validated server-side to prevent authorization code interception. RS256 JWT with 1-hour expiry; jti claim used as Redis cache key for fast revocation propagation. data_sharing_consents DB check on every request -- valid JWT alone is insufficient; active consent record required. API key SHA-256 hash stored in api_access_grants; plaintext returned once at provisioning. AWS WAF managed rules: AWSManagedRulesSQLiRuleSet, AWSManagedRulesCommonRuleSet. HMAC-SHA256 request signing available for high-trust OEM partners who register a signing key. AgGateway data ownership principle: operators can view the full audit log of which clients accessed their data and revoke any consent at any time.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate cluster us-east-1. api-service: 2-8 tasks on ALB request count auto-scaling (target 1000 req/task/min). oauth-service: 2 tasks (low traffic, no auto-scaling). ALB connection draining 30s for zero-downtime rolling deploys. Blue-green option available for breaking API changes. Migration: api-service deployed in parallel with monolith for 4-week OEM partner verification window; ALB weighted routing shifts traffic 10%->50%->100% after each partner verifies. Secrets Manager rotation: RS256 private key 90-day automated rotation with 2-key overlap period. GitHub Actions: lint, jest (90% gate), OpenAPI contract tests via Spectral, Docker build, ECR push, ECS deploy.
target_output.observability: CloudWatch custom metrics per partner: requests_per_minute, error_rate_4xx, error_rate_5xx, consent_grant_count, consent_revocation_count. API access log structured with client_id, operator_account_id, endpoint, latency_ms shipped to CloudWatch Logs Insights for per-partner SLA reporting. PagerDuty escalation: 5xx error rate >1% for any partner client, Redis JWT cache hit rate <90% (indicating cache miss storm), PostGIS query p99 latency >500ms. Runbook for consent revocation storms (mass revocation events should not exceed cache invalidation capacity).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ~$400/month at 500K API calls/day across all OEM partners. ECS Fargate: 2 baseline tasks + auto-scale on request count. RDS PostgreSQL t4g.medium with 1 read replica for field boundary queries during planting season (March-May peak). Redis cache.t4g.small for JWT token validation. S3 not used in MVP. Cost reduction: Redis JWT cache reduces RDS query load; read replica handles boundary query load during peak planting season.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (6 weeks): OAuth 2.0 authorization server with PKCE, field boundary GET endpoint (GeoJSON Feature Collection, PostGIS ST_AsGeoJSON), prescription map GET/POST endpoints (monolith-compatible schema), data_sharing_consents enforcement, API audit log, ECS Fargate deployment with ALB. Phase 2 (4 weeks): API key management for server-to-server partners, consent revocation with Redis JWT invalidation, per-partner CloudWatch metrics, operator audit log UI (HTML endpoint). Phase 3 (4 weeks): webhook push for prescription updates, ISOXML prescription export endpoint, OEM partner test suite for regression testing during monolith decommission.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode. Fastify route schemas defined as JSON Schema and compiled into TypeScript types via json-schema-to-ts. Jest unit tests for OAuth PKCE flow, consent check middleware, and PostGIS query builders -- 90% coverage gate. OpenAPI spec validated in CI with Spectral against GeoJSON Feature Collection schema to catch monolith compatibility regressions before any deploy. PostGIS queries reviewed for ST_Intersects bbox performance against GiST index using EXPLAIN ANALYZE in staging. All breaking API changes require OEM partner approval before merging to main.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: monolith GeoJSON response shape has undocumented property fields used by specific OEM firmware versions. Mitigation: capture live monolith responses in staging using a traffic mirror before migration starts; generate a canonical GeoJSON fixture suite from real monolith output for CI contract tests. Risk: consent revocation must propagate to Redis JWT cache within seconds; if Redis is unavailable during revocation, stale JWTs remain valid. Mitigation: if Redis is unavailable, fallback to DB consent check on every request until Redis recovers. Tradeoff: custom OAuth 2.0 server vs. Auth0/Cognito -- chosen custom for OEM partner-specific PKCE requirements and existing monolith token schema compatibility. Tradeoff: PostGIS ST_AsGeoJSON in DB layer vs. application-layer GeoJSON serialization -- DB-layer serialization avoids importing geometry libraries to Node.js and maintains projection control.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed webhook delivery is post-MVP (Phase 3). Confirmed OAuth 2.0 authorization code with PKCE only -- no implicit flow for equipment terminals. Confirmed GeoJSON Feature Collection as canonical field boundary response format matching monolith. Confirmed 90-day API key rotation policy with 2-key overlap for zero-downtime rotation.

metadata.notes: Farm field boundary and prescription REST API migrated from monolith; partner-facing OAuth 2.0 + PostGIS on ECS Fargate.

profile.domain: Mobile Crop Scouting App for Crop Consultants

project_goal.value: Enable crop consultants to record GPS-tagged pest and weed pressure observations with photo capture during field scouting visits, and automatically generate structured PDF scouting reports for grower clients
project_goal.rationale: Current workflow uses paper notes and manual report writing; mobile app eliminates transcription and speeds report delivery to growers

target_users.value: Independent crop consultants and agronomists who scout multiple grower client fields during the growing season, typically working alone in fields with variable cellular coverage
target_users.rationale: Primary users are solo field workers; report recipients are grower clients who receive PDF reports by email

project_class.rationale: Core user experience is field-based mobile data collection; backend serves the mobile app and report generation

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, analytics, integrations
capabilities.rationale: React Native mobile frontend, FastAPI backend, PostgreSQL data store, Cognito auth, PDF report generation, AWS SES email delivery

complexity_level.rationale: GPS photo capture, offline observation queuing, and PDF report generation add moderate complexity above a basic CRUD app

risk_level.rationale: Scouting data has commercial value but no regulatory sensitivity; primary risk is data loss during offline operation

data_sensitivity.rationale: Pest pressure observations and grower field data are proprietary but not subject to external privacy regulations

external_exposure.rationale: App and API require Cognito authentication; grower clients receive reports by email but do not access the app directly

access_model.value: Cognito user pools with consultant and admin roles; each consultant account scoped to their own scouting events and grower client roster; admin role for billing and account management
access_model.rationale: Consultants must not see each other's client field data; admin manages subscription billing

feature_scope.value: GPS-tagged pest and weed observation recording with photo attachment, economic threshold rating per zone, field zone map view, scouting report PDF generation, automated email delivery to grower contacts, grower client roster management, observation history and trend charts by pest species and field zone
feature_scope.rationale: Complete feature set for a scouting consultant's workflow

mvp_scope.value: Pest and weed observation recording with GPS tag and photo, field zone assignment, scouting report PDF generation and email delivery to grower, grower client roster management
mvp_scope.rationale: MVP covers the end-to-end scouting workflow from observation capture to report delivery

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 in transit, Cognito JWT with RS256, S3 pre-signed URLs for photo access (15-minute expiry), consultant data scoped by account ID at API layer
security_baseline.rationale: Scouting photos and observations must not be accessible across consultant accounts

frontend_stack.value: React Native with Expo, EAS Updates for over-the-air schema change deployments without App Store review
frontend_stack.rationale: Expo EAS Updates enables field data schema changes (new pest species, revised threshold ratings) to deploy OTA without a 2-week App Store review cycle

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI, WeasyPrint for PDF report generation
backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI for async photo upload handling; WeasyPrint renders Jinja2 HTML templates to PDF for scouting reports

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS for structured observations; S3 for photo storage
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL for relational scouting data; S3 pre-signed URLs for secure mobile photo delivery without proxying binaries through the API

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in us-east-1
hosting_target.rationale: Managed containers consistent with team existing AWS infrastructure

privacy_retention_policy.value: Scouting observations and photos retained for 5 growing seasons (5 years); grower contact information retained while account is active plus 1 year after cancellation
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Consultants reference historical scouting data for multi-year pest pressure trend analysis

future_scope.value: Weed seed bank mapping, integration with agronomic recommendation platforms (e.g., Climate FieldView), real-time economic threshold push notifications during scouting visit
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap features contingent on MVP adoption

constraints.value: EAS Updates OTA deployment for field data schema changes required to avoid App Store 2-week review delays; offline observation capture required for fields without cellular coverage
constraints.rationale: OTA deploys and offline capability are non-negotiable for the field use case

observability_baseline.value: Sentry for React Native crash reporting and FastAPI error tracking; CloudWatch for ECS and RDS metrics
observability_baseline.rationale: Sentry provides mobile crash reporting with device and OS version context; CloudWatch for backend infrastructure

execution_preference.value: Feature-flag-gated deployments; EAS channels for gradual rollout to beta testers before production release
execution_preference.rationale: EAS channels enable controlled rollout to beta consultant testers before full release

project.goal: GPS-tagged pest scouting app with PDF report generation and grower email delivery
project.target_users: Independent crop consultants

frontend.ota_updates: EAS Updates for OTA schema deployments
frontend.offline: AsyncStorage and offline observation queue with sync on reconnect
frontend.navigation: React Navigation with tab and stack navigators

backend.email: AWS SES for scouting report delivery

security.auth: Cognito user pools with consultant role

data.photos: S3 bucket with private ACL, pre-signed URL access

devops.environments: dev, staging, production with EAS channels

constraints.ota: EAS Updates required for schema changes

open_questions.threshold_source: Confirm if economic thresholds are hardcoded or fetched from API for OTA updates

reasoner_reviews.product: The OTA update requirement via EAS Updates is the most important architectural constraint -- it means all field data schema changes (new pest species added to the observation form, revised economic thresholds) must be deployable without an App Store review. This requires that observation form structure (pest species list, weed categories, threshold values) is fetched from the API at app startup, not hardcoded in the bundle. The scouting report PDF template should also be server-rendered, not client-rendered, to allow report format changes without an OTA deploy.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Offline observation queue in AsyncStorage is the critical reliability feature. The queue must persist observations through app force-quit and device restart, not just screen lock. React Native AsyncStorage survives app restarts but not uninstall -- consultants should be warned if they have unsynced observations before uninstalling. Photo uploads must handle slow cellular upload speeds during field sync; multipart upload with S3 pre-signed URLs avoids proxying photo bytes through the FastAPI backend. WeasyPrint PDF generation is CPU-intensive; run it in a background ECS task triggered by report request to avoid blocking the API response.
reasoner_reviews.security: S3 pre-signed URLs for photo access must have a 15-minute expiry to prevent indefinite access after a consultant account is deactivated. The PDF report is generated server-side and should be stored in S3 (not returned inline) with a pre-signed URL sent to the grower email -- this avoids regenerating large PDFs on every email click. consultant_account_id must be enforced at the database query layer for all scouting_events, pest_observations, and grower_contacts rows.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: EAS Updates channel strategy: production channel for released app, beta channel for consultant testers, development channel for CI. OTA updates that change observation form schemas must include a migration flag that prompts consultants with the old app version to update before starting new scouting events to avoid form schema mismatch in queued offline observations.

backend.service_design: Single FastAPI app with four domain modules: scouting (observation endpoints), growers (client roster CRUD), reports (PDF generation and email delivery), auth (Cognito JWT validation middleware). S3 operations handled via boto3 with pre-signed URL generation.
backend.api_patterns: POST /observations (create GPS-tagged pest observation, returns S3 pre-signed upload URL for photo), GET /scouting-events/{id}/observations (list observations for a scouting visit), POST /reports/generate (trigger async WeasyPrint PDF generation, returns job_id), GET /reports/{id}/download-url (returns S3 pre-signed URL for completed PDF), GET /app-config (pest species list, economic thresholds, weed categories -- fetched at app startup for OTA-updateable content).
backend.background_jobs: PDF generation task runs in a separate ECS task (Fargate task definition) triggered by SQS message from POST /reports/generate. SES delivery triggered after PDF is written to S3.
backend.failure_handling: SQS dead-letter queue for failed PDF generation tasks. Sentry captures WeasyPrint rendering errors with template name and data payload. S3 pre-signed URL generation failures return 503 with Retry-After header.

frontend.app_structure: React Native Expo app with three tab navigators: Scout (new scouting event, active observations), Clients (grower roster, contact management), Reports (report history, status, email re-send).
frontend.pages_and_flows: Start Scouting Visit: consultant selects grower client and field zone, app fetches current GPS coordinates, opens observation form pre-populated with app-config pest species and threshold values. Record Observation: consultant taps pest species from fetched list, rates pressure level (0-4 economic threshold scale), takes photo (React Native ImagePicker), submits. If offline: queued to AsyncStorage sync queue with pending status. Complete Visit and Generate Report: consultant taps 'Generate Report', app calls POST /reports/generate with scouting_event_id, displays job status, notifies when PDF ready.
frontend.state_management: Zustand for app-config (pest species, thresholds) fetched at startup. React Query for scouting events and grower data. AsyncStorage for offline observation sync queue.
frontend.frontend_risks: EAS Updates OTA deploy that modifies the observation form schema while a consultant has unsynced offline observations in AsyncStorage could cause schema mismatch on sync. Mitigation: include schema_version in queued observations; API validates and rejects mismatched schema with error prompting user to review pending observations.

security.auth_design: Cognito user pool with consultant and admin groups. JWT validated by FastAPI middleware using python-jose and Cognito JWKS endpoint. consultant_account_id extracted from JWT sub claim and applied to all DB queries.
security.authorization_model: Row-level: all scouting_events, pest_observations, grower_contacts filtered by consultant_account_id = :cognito_sub. S3 photos stored under consultants/{account_id}/photos/ -- pre-signed URLs generated only after account_id ownership confirmed.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Structured CloudWatch logs for all report generation and email delivery events with consultant_account_id, scouting_event_id, and grower_contact_id for traceability.

data.entities: scouting_events (id UUID, consultant_account_id FK, grower_contact_id FK, field_zone_id FK, started_at, completed_at, status ENUM in_progress/completed), pest_observations (id UUID, scouting_event_id FK, consultant_account_id FK, pest_species_code VARCHAR, pressure_level INT 0-4, economic_threshold_met BOOL, gps_lat NUMERIC, gps_lon NUMERIC, photo_s3_key VARCHAR, notes TEXT, recorded_at), weed_pressure_ratings (id UUID, scouting_event_id FK, field_zone_id FK, weed_species_code VARCHAR, density_rating ENUM low/medium/high/severe, recorded_at), field_zones (id UUID, grower_contact_id FK, consultant_account_id FK, zone_name, acreage, crop_type, field_boundary_geojson JSONB), grower_contacts (id UUID, consultant_account_id FK, grower_name, email, phone, active BOOL), scouting_reports (id UUID, scouting_event_id FK, consultant_account_id FK, pdf_s3_key, generation_status ENUM queued/generating/complete/failed, generated_at, delivered_to_email)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 RDS (structured observations and grower data). S3 bucket private ACL for photos under consultants/{account_id}/photos/{observation_id}.jpg and reports under consultants/{account_id}/reports/{report_id}.pdf.
data.analytics_events: scouting_event_completed, observation_recorded, report_generated, report_delivered, offline_sync_completed, ota_update_applied

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a field-first mobile scouting app with two non-negotiable constraints: offline observation capture with AsyncStorage persistence through app restarts, and EAS Updates OTA deployment for pest schema changes without App Store review. PDF generation must be server-side (WeasyPrint in a background ECS task via SQS) to allow report format changes without OTA deploys. S3 pre-signed URLs for photo uploads avoid proxying multi-megabyte images through the FastAPI backend over slow rural cellular connections. App-config endpoint serves pest species lists and economic thresholds so form content updates deploy server-side.
target_output.title: Mobile Crop Scouting App Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A React Native Expo mobile app for independent crop consultants that enables GPS-tagged pest and weed pressure recording during field scouting visits, with automatic PDF scouting report generation and AWS SES email delivery to grower clients. EAS Updates OTA deployment enables field data schema changes (pest species lists, economic thresholds) without App Store review cycles. Offline observation capture with AsyncStorage queue persistence handles no-coverage field conditions. Backend is Python 3.12 FastAPI on ECS Fargate with PostgreSQL 16 for observation storage and S3 for photo and PDF storage.
target_output.architecture_overview: AWS-based architecture with a React Native Expo app communicating over TLS to a FastAPI backend on ECS Fargate. Cognito user pool handles consultant authentication with JWT-scoped account isolation. Pest observations with GPS coordinates and photos are captured on the mobile app; when offline, observations queue in AsyncStorage and sync to the API on reconnection. Photo uploads use S3 pre-signed URLs generated by the API to bypass the backend and upload directly from the mobile device to S3. PDF scouting reports are generated server-side by a background ECS task (WeasyPrint + Jinja2) triggered via SQS after a report request; completed PDFs are stored in S3 and delivered to grower contacts via AWS SES. An app-config endpoint serves OTA-updateable content (pest species, weed categories, economic thresholds) fetched at app startup, so form schema updates deploy without EAS bundle pushes.
target_output.technology_stack: Mobile: React Native with Expo SDK 51, React Navigation (tab + stack), React Query for server state, Zustand for app-config state, AsyncStorage for offline queue, EAS Updates (update channels: production/beta/development). Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI with async handlers, WeasyPrint + Jinja2 for PDF rendering, python-jose for Cognito JWT validation, boto3 for S3 pre-signed URL generation. Data: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS t4g.medium, S3 private bucket for photos and PDFs. Messaging: SQS for PDF generation task queue. Auth: Cognito user pool. Email: AWS SES. CI/CD: GitHub Actions, EAS Build, ECS rolling deploy. Observability: Sentry (mobile + backend), CloudWatch.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Scouting Visit Management: consultant starts a visit linked to a grower client and field zone; the app tracks visit start time and active observation count. Pest Observation Capture: consultant selects pest species from app-config list, rates pressure on 0-4 economic threshold scale, captures GPS coordinates automatically, takes optional photo (uploads directly to S3 via pre-signed URL). Offline Queue: observations created offline stored in AsyncStorage with pending status; sync triggered on connectivity restoration. Weed Pressure Rating: consultant selects weed species and density rating per field zone (same online/offline pattern). App-Config Content: pest species list, weed categories, economic threshold values fetched from GET /app-config at startup and cached in Zustand -- updates server-side without OTA deploy. Report Generation: consultant completes visit and taps 'Generate Report'; API queues WeasyPrint task via SQS; consultant notified in-app when PDF is ready; report emailed to grower's address via SES with S3 pre-signed URL valid 72 hours.
target_output.system_components: FastAPI app (four modules: scouting, growers, reports, auth middleware), SQS queue (PDF generation jobs), PDF worker ECS task (WeasyPrint + Jinja2, separate task definition from API), PostgreSQL 16 RDS (observations, grower contacts, scouting events, reports), S3 private bucket (photos under consultants/{id}/photos/, PDFs under consultants/{id}/reports/), Cognito user pool (JWT auth), SES (grower report email delivery), ECS Fargate cluster (API service 2-4 tasks, PDF worker service 1-3 tasks), CloudWatch + Sentry.
target_output.workflows: Field Observation Workflow (Online): Consultant opens Scout tab, taps 'New Visit', selects grower 'Hollingsworth Farms' and field zone 'North 40', app starts GPS tracking. Taps 'Add Observation', selects 'Western Corn Rootworm' from Zustand app-config list, rates pressure level 3 (above economic threshold), photos the damaged root ball. App calls POST /observations with GPS coordinates, pest_species_code, pressure_level=3, economic_threshold_met=true; API creates pest_observations record, returns S3 pre-signed upload URL. Mobile uploads 2.3MB photo directly to S3 via PUT to pre-signed URL. Observation appears on visit summary with threshold alert badge. Field Observation Workflow (Offline): Consultant enters no-signal zone; app detects offline state. Records observation with photo saved to device camera roll. Observation stored in AsyncStorage with {status: pending, payload: {...}, photo_local_uri}. On connectivity restore, Expo NetInfo event triggers sync loop: for each pending observation, calls POST /observations (with S3 upload URL returned), uploads photo, marks observation synced in AsyncStorage queue. Scouting Report Delivery Workflow: Consultant completes visit ('Complete Visit' button), calls POST /reports/generate with scouting_event_id. API validates event belongs to consultant_account_id, creates scouting_reports record (status: queued), sends SQS message. PDF worker picks up SQS message, queries all observations and weed ratings for the event, renders Jinja2 template to PDF via WeasyPrint, writes PDF to S3 path, updates scouting_reports status to complete. SES sends email to grower_contact.email with PDF S3 pre-signed URL (72h expiry) and plain-text observation summary.
target_output.data_model: scouting_events (id UUID PK, consultant_account_id UUID, grower_contact_id UUID FK, field_zone_id UUID FK, started_at, completed_at, status ENUM in_progress/completed). pest_observations (id UUID PK, scouting_event_id UUID FK, consultant_account_id UUID, pest_species_code VARCHAR, pressure_level INT CHECK(0-4), economic_threshold_met BOOL, gps_lat NUMERIC(10,7), gps_lon NUMERIC(10,7), photo_s3_key VARCHAR, notes TEXT, recorded_at, sync_source ENUM online/offline_sync). weed_pressure_ratings (id UUID PK, scouting_event_id UUID FK, field_zone_id UUID FK, weed_species_code VARCHAR, density_rating ENUM low/medium/high/severe, recorded_at). field_zones (id UUID PK, grower_contact_id UUID FK, consultant_account_id UUID, zone_name VARCHAR, acreage NUMERIC, crop_type VARCHAR, field_boundary_geojson JSONB). grower_contacts (id UUID PK, consultant_account_id UUID, grower_name VARCHAR, email VARCHAR, phone VARCHAR, active BOOL). scouting_reports (id UUID PK, scouting_event_id UUID FK, consultant_account_id UUID, pdf_s3_key VARCHAR, generation_status ENUM queued/generating/complete/failed, generated_at, delivered_to_email). app_config_versions (id INT PK, pest_species_json JSONB, weed_categories_json JSONB, threshold_values_json JSONB, deployed_at -- GET /app-config returns latest record).
target_output.api_design: POST /scouting-events (start visit), PATCH /scouting-events/{id}/complete (mark completed). POST /observations (body: scouting_event_id, pest_species_code, pressure_level, economic_threshold_met, gps_lat, gps_lon, notes; response: {observation_id, photo_upload_url: string|null}). GET /scouting-events/{id}/observations (list with photo pre-signed URLs). POST /weed-ratings (weed pressure record). POST /reports/generate (body: scouting_event_id; returns {report_id, status: queued}). GET /reports/{id}/status (polling endpoint for PDF worker completion). GET /reports/{id}/download-url (returns {pdf_url, expires_at} once complete). GET /app-config (returns latest pest_species_json, weed_categories_json, threshold_values_json). POST /growers (add grower contact). GET /growers (list with active filter). All endpoints require Cognito JWT Authorization header; consultant_account_id injected from JWT sub claim into all DB queries.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Cognito JWT validated on every request via python-jose JWKS verification. consultant_account_id from JWT sub applied as WHERE predicate on all PostgreSQL queries -- no endpoint allows cross-account data access. S3 photos stored with private ACL; pre-signed URLs generated with 15-minute expiry for observation photos, 72-hour expiry for grower-delivered report PDFs. PDF worker ECS task IAM role grants S3 write to consultants/*/reports/ only. SES configured with SPF and DKIM records for the sending domain to ensure report emails reach grower inboxes. Sentry PII scrubbing configured to exclude grower contact email addresses from error reports.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate cluster us-east-1. API service: 2-4 tasks auto-scaling on CPU 60% target. PDF worker service: 1-3 tasks auto-scaling on SQS queue depth (scale up at 10 queued messages). SQS queue with DLQ (maxReceiveCount=3) for failed PDF generation tasks. GitHub Actions CI: pytest (85% coverage gate), mypy type check, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy. EAS Build: GitHub Actions triggers EAS Build on main merge; EAS Update publishes OTA bundle to production channel after manual QA approval. Three environments: dev (Cognito dev pool, S3 dev bucket), staging (full EAS preview channel test), production (EAS production channel, RDS Multi-AZ).
target_output.observability: Sentry React Native SDK captures mobile crashes with device model, OS version, Expo SDK version, and current screen context. Sentry FastAPI integration captures API errors with consultant_account_id and endpoint context. CloudWatch metrics: SQS queue depth (alert if >50 messages), PDF worker task count, RDS connection count, S3 PUT success rate. Custom CloudWatch metric: offline_sync_observations_count (count of observations synced from offline queue per hour -- monitors field adoption). PagerDuty alert: SQS DLQ message count >0 (PDF generation failures require manual review and re-queue).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ~$350/month at 200 active consultants, each scouting 5 fields/week. ECS Fargate: 2 API tasks + 1 PDF worker baseline. S3 storage: 500GB estimated for 5-year photo retention at average 3MB per observation photo. RDS t4g.medium: handles current load comfortably; upgrade to t4g.large needed at 500+ consultants based on connection count projections. EAS Updates: free tier covers OTA update volume. SES: $0.10/1000 emails -- negligible cost.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (6 weeks): Cognito auth, scouting event and pest observation API, GPS capture and photo S3 upload in React Native, offline AsyncStorage observation queue, app-config endpoint for pest species list, scouting report PDF generation via WeasyPrint + SQS, SES email delivery to grower, EAS Updates OTA setup with production and beta channels. Phase 2 (4 weeks): weed pressure rating capture and report section, economic threshold visual alerts in app, observation history and trend charts (pest pressure by species over rolling 30-day window), EAS Build CI integration. Phase 3 (4 weeks): field zone map view with GPS observation pin overlay, 5-year scouting history analytics, grower self-service report archive link, beta channel gradual rollout workflow for OTA deploys.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode for React Native and backend Python with mypy. pytest-asyncio for FastAPI async endpoint tests; 85% coverage gate. React Native tested with React Testing Library (native); offline queue sync logic tested with mocked NetInfo events and AsyncStorage. EAS Updates OTA deploys require app-config schema version bump and backward-compatibility test with the prior app bundle version before production publish. WeasyPrint PDF template changes reviewed against generated PDF output in CI (screenshot diff against baseline PDF render). PR requires 1 approval; changes to offline sync or S3 pre-signed URL logic require senior review.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: EAS Updates OTA deploy of app-config changes while a consultant has offline observations queued with the old schema version. Mitigation: include app_config_version in each queued observation; API validates and returns 409 Conflict with instructions to update app before syncing if schema version mismatch detected. Risk: WeasyPrint PDF rendering fails for scouting events with 50+ observations (large report). Mitigation: test WeasyPrint with 100-observation reports in staging; if render time exceeds 60 seconds, switch to paginated multi-section Jinja2 template. Tradeoff: server-side PDF rendering vs. client-side (React Native PDF library) -- chosen server-side so report template updates deploy without OTA pushes. Tradeoff: S3 pre-signed URL direct upload vs. API proxy -- direct upload avoids bottlenecking large photo uploads through the API on slow rural cellular, at the cost of more complex client-side upload retry logic.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed economic thresholds are fetched from GET /app-config (server-side) rather than hardcoded in the bundle, enabling OTA updates to threshold values without EAS bundle push. Confirmed S3 direct upload via pre-signed URL over API proxy for photo uploads given rural cellular bandwidth constraints. Confirmed offline queue uses AsyncStorage (not SQLite/WatermelonDB) for simplicity at current data volumes; SQLite migration path documented for Phase 3 if observation volume grows.

metadata.notes: Mobile crop scouting app with EAS OTA updates, offline observation queue, and server-side PDF report generation.

profile.domain: FSMA Food Traceability Web Application for Fresh Produce Supply Chain

project_goal.value: Track fresh produce lots from farm harvest through packinghouse processing and outbound shipments to retail distribution centers, enabling one-step-forward and one-step-back trace within two hours as required by FDA FSMA Section 204 Food Traceability Rule
project_goal.rationale: FSMA Rule 204 mandates two-hour recall trace capability for high-risk produce categories starting January 2026

target_users.value: Packinghouse food safety managers, shipping coordinators, retail distribution center receiving teams, and food safety auditors performing recall investigations
target_users.rationale: Multiple supply chain roles require different views of traceability data

project_class.rationale: Browser-based application for supply chain participants; microservices backend for traceability event isolation

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, integrations, analytics, admin_panel, batch_jobs
capabilities.rationale: Full supply chain traceability requires trading partner integrations, batch reconciliation jobs, and recall investigation tooling

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-party supply chain data with GS1 lot identification standards and two-hour trace SLA across microservices

risk_level.rationale: Recall investigation failures or data corruption could result in FDA enforcement action and public health risk

data_sensitivity.rationale: Supply chain lot movements and trading partner shipment volumes are commercially sensitive and subject to NDA

external_exposure.rationale: Trading partners (growers, distributors, retailers) access the system with their own credentials to submit and query traceability records

access_model.value: Azure AD B2C with organization-scoped tenancy; trading partner organizations managed by admin; roles: food_safety_manager, shipping_coordinator, receiving_team, auditor, platform_admin
access_model.rationale: Multi-tenant partner access requires org-scoped isolation; auditor role needs cross-organization read for recall investigations

feature_scope.value: Harvest lot registration with GS1 GTIN-14 lot codes, packinghouse packing event recording linking farm lots to outbound shipping labels, inbound receiving recording at distribution centers, one-step-forward and one-step-back trace query, recall investigation dashboard, trading partner organization management, batch reconciliation jobs for unmatched shipments
feature_scope.rationale: Complete FSMA Rule 204 Key Data Element capture across the supply chain

mvp_scope.value: Harvest lot registration, packinghouse packing event capture, shipping label creation with GS1 SSCC, distribution center receiving record, one-step-forward trace query, basic recall investigation screen
mvp_scope.rationale: MVP covers the FSMA Rule 204 critical tracking events for fresh produce covered commodities

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3, Azure AD B2C JWT with org-scoped claims, confidential data encrypted at rest in MongoDB, Azure Key Vault for secrets, audit log for all traceability record creation and recall investigation access
security_baseline.rationale: FSMA traceability records are subject to FDA inspection; access must be audited

frontend_stack.value: Vue 3 with TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: Vue 3 Composition API aligns with team expertise; TypeScript for supply chain data model type safety

backend_stack.value: Java 21 with Spring Boot (microservices)
backend_stack.rationale: Spring Boot microservices for traceability event, recall investigation, and partner management services

data_platform.value: MongoDB 7 for traceability events, Redis 7 for trace query cache
data_platform.rationale: MongoDB document model fits variable Key Data Element structures across commodity types; Redis for sub-second trace query responses

hosting_target.value: AKS (Azure Kubernetes Service)
hosting_target.rationale: Kubernetes for microservices orchestration; Azure for AD B2C integration proximity

privacy_retention_policy.value: FSMA Rule 204 requires traceability records retained for minimum 2 years from creation date; supply chain partner NDAs require confidential shipment volume data retained maximum 5 years; records deleted on partner offboarding after retention period
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Regulatory minimum plus contractual maximum retention constraints

future_scope.value: EDI 856 ASN integration with major retail chains, FDA Reportable Food Registry automated submission, blockchain-anchored lot audit trail for premium product lines
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap contingent on FSMA compliance validation

constraints.value: Two-hour trace SLA is a hard regulatory requirement; GS1 GTIN-14 and SSCC lot code standards must be supported; system must handle 500 trading partner organizations at launch
constraints.rationale: FDA enforcement and trading partner volume constraints

observability_baseline.value: Azure Monitor for AKS and MongoDB Atlas metrics, Application Insights for Spring Boot service tracing, PagerDuty for recall investigation SLA breach alerts
observability_baseline.rationale: Recall investigation SLA monitoring is a regulatory imperative

execution_preference.value: Domain-driven microservices design with event sourcing for traceability events; two-week sprints
execution_preference.rationale: Event sourcing provides immutable traceability audit trail required for FSMA compliance

compliance_context.value: FDA FSMA Section 204 Food Traceability Rule compliance required; GS1 standards for lot identification (GTIN-14, SSCC); GFSI traceability scheme requirements for SQF and GlobalG.A.P. certified facilities; trading partner NDA requirements for shipment data confidentiality
compliance_context.rationale: High-risk regulatory profile requires explicit compliance context for all architecture decisions

project.goal: FSMA Rule 204 compliant fresh produce traceability from farm to retail DC
project.target_users: Packinghouse managers, shipping coordinators, DC receiving teams, food safety auditors

security.auth: Azure AD B2C with org-scoped JWT
security.encryption: MongoDB Atlas encryption at rest, Azure Key Vault

data.cache: Redis 7 Azure Cache for sub-second trace queries

devops.environments: dev, staging, production

open_questions.lot_aggregation: Confirm how mixed-origin lots (multiple farm sources packed together) are represented in the data model

reasoner_reviews.product: FSMA Rule 204 January 2026 deadline is a hard external constraint -- this is not a soft roadmap target. The Critical Tracking Events (CTEs) defined in Rule 204 (harvesting, cooling, initial packing, shipping, receiving) must map explicitly to data model entities. The Key Data Elements (KDEs) for each CTE are specified by FDA regulation and must be captured exactly -- missing any KDE invalidates the trace record for regulatory purposes. The two-hour trace SLA must be tested as a performance requirement, not just a UI feature.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: MongoDB document model is a reasonable choice for variable Key Data Element structures across different commodity types, but the trace query path must be carefully designed. A one-step-forward trace starting from a farm lot GTIN must traverse: harvest_lots -> packing_events -> shipping_labels -> receiving_records. This multi-hop graph traversal in MongoDB requires careful indexing strategy -- likely denormalized lot_chain arrays on each document rather than relying on $lookup across collections. Redis cache for the hot trace path is essential for the two-hour SLA.
reasoner_reviews.security: Auditor role requires cross-organization read access for recall investigations, which breaks the standard org-scoped tenancy model. This must be explicitly modeled as a privileged role with audit logging of every cross-org query. Azure AD B2C org-scoped claims alone are insufficient for the auditor case -- the application must implement a separate permission check for auditor recall access. All traceability record creation events must be appended to an immutable audit log collection (no update or delete operations permitted on audit log documents).
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 500 trading partner organizations at launch requires that the partner onboarding workflow is automated -- manual Azure AD B2C tenant configuration per partner will not scale. Trading partner self-registration flow must be part of the MVP. GS1 SSCC generation requires a GS1 Company Prefix assigned to the platform operator -- this must be resolved before development starts.

backend.service_design: Three Spring Boot microservices: TraceabilityService (harvest lot, packing event, shipping, receiving CRUD and trace query), RecallService (recall investigation execution, SLA tracking, scope report generation), PartnerService (organization management, user provisioning, trading partner onboarding). Services communicate via Azure Service Bus for async traceability event propagation.
backend.api_patterns: RESTful with OpenAPI 3.1. POST /v1/lots (harvest lot registration with GS1 GTIN-14), POST /v1/packing-events (link farm lot to outbound SSCC labels), POST /v1/shipments (shipping record with SSCC), POST /v1/receivings (distribution center receiving). GET /v1/trace/forward/{lot_id} (one-step-forward trace), GET /v1/trace/backward/{lot_id} (one-step-back trace). POST /v1/recall-investigations (open investigation from flagged lot).
backend.background_jobs: Batch reconciliation Spring Batch job runs nightly: matches unconfirmed shipments (shipped but no receiving record within 48 hours) and flags for partner follow-up. MongoDB Atlas scheduled trigger deletes traceability records past retention period.
backend.failure_handling: TraceabilityService uses optimistic locking on packing_events to prevent duplicate lot assignments. RecallService monitors trace query duration against two-hour SLA and triggers PagerDuty alert at 90-minute mark.
