frontend.app_structure: Vue 3 SPA with Pinia stores for authentication state, active recall investigations, and trade partner context. Route guards enforce role-based access (auditor route group vs. standard partner routes).
frontend.pages_and_flows: Harvest Lot Registration: packinghouse food safety manager enters harvest date, commodity, growing area, quantity, GS1 GTIN-14 code; form validates GTIN-14 check digit. Packing Event: links one or more incoming farm lot IDs to outbound SSCC labels; supports mixed-origin lots. Trace Query: auditor enters a lot ID or SSCC and sees a visual supply chain graph of one-step-forward (where did this lot go?) or one-step-back (what farm lots contributed to this label?). Recall Investigation Dashboard: food safety manager opens investigation, runs trace, views scope report showing all distribution center shipments containing the flagged lot.
frontend.frontend_risks: Vue 3 supply chain graph visualization for trace results -- rendering a graph with 200+ nodes for a large lot that was repacked multiple times requires a dedicated graph library (e.g., Cytoscape.js) rather than a simple list. Without this, complex trace results will be unreadable.

security.auth_design: Azure AD B2C custom user flows for trading partner self-registration and login. JWT claims include organization_id and role. Auditor role added by platform admin only -- not self-assignable.
security.authorization_model: Standard API requests: WHERE organization_id = :org_id enforced at repository layer. Recall investigation requests: auditor role bypasses org filter but every cross-org query appended to recall_audit_log with auditor_id, queried_org_id, lot_id, timestamp.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: MongoDB append-only traceability_audit_log collection: no update or delete indexes. Application-level enforcement: TraceabilityService uses a custom Mongoose-equivalent Spring Data interceptor that rejects any update/delete operations on audit_log collection documents.
security.security_risks: Mixed-origin packing events (multiple farm lots into one outbound label) create a fan-out trace problem: a recall of one farm lot triggers recall of all co-packed lots on the same SSCC. Auditors must be warned of fan-out scope before executing a forward trace that could unnecessarily expand recall scope.

data.entities: harvest_lots (MongoDB doc: {_id, organization_id, gtin14, commodity, growing_area_id, harvest_date, quantity_units, quantity_value, location_description, created_at, audit_log_id}), packing_events ({_id, organization_id, inbound_lot_ids: [{lot_id, quantity}], outbound_sscc_labels: [sscc], pack_date, facility_id, lot_chain_ids: [all_ancestor_lot_ids] -- denormalized for trace}), shipping_records ({_id, organization_id, sscc_labels: [sscc], ship_date, destination_facility_id, transporter_id, bill_of_lading}), receiving_records ({_id, organization_id, sscc_labels_received: [sscc], receive_date, source_facility_id, rejection_flag, rejection_reason}), recall_investigations ({_id, opened_by_auditor_id, trigger_lot_id, status ENUM open/in_progress/contained, trace_scope_report: {...}, opened_at, contained_at}), traceability_audit_log ({_id, user_id, organization_id, action, collection, document_id, timestamp -- append only, no delete index})
data.storage_design: MongoDB Atlas M30 cluster (Azure East US 2) with replica set. Collections: harvest_lots, packing_events, shipping_records, receiving_records indexed on gtin14, sscc, organization_id, and lot_chain_ids array. Redis 7 Azure Cache: forward/backward trace results cached by lot_id with 30-minute TTL.
data.data_risks: Mixed-origin packing events (fan-out) may cause MongoDB lot_chain_ids array to grow unbounded for commodity lots repacked many times (e.g., bagged salad with 10+ farm origins). Mitigation: cap lot_chain_ids at depth 5; flag lots exceeding depth limit for manual trace review.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing an FSMA Rule 204 traceability system. The regulatory two-hour trace SLA is the overriding constraint -- every architecture decision must be evaluated against its impact on trace query performance. MongoDB document model with denormalized lot_chain_ids arrays on packing events is the key design choice for sub-second trace traversal. The auditor cross-organization access model for recall investigations breaks standard org-scoped tenancy and requires explicit special-casing in authorization logic. Note: this plan contains intentional flaws for training purposes -- the data model section references a PostgreSQL schema instead of MongoDB documents matching the contracted data_platform, and the deployment section omits the AKS topology in favor of generic container language.
target_output.title: FSMA Fresh Produce Traceability Platform Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A partner-facing web application enabling fresh produce supply chain participants to record FDA FSMA Rule 204 Critical Tracking Events from farm harvest through packinghouse packing and outbound shipments to retail distribution centers, supporting two-hour one-step-forward and one-step-back recall trace. Vue 3 TypeScript frontend communicates with Java 21 Spring Boot microservices on AKS. MongoDB 7 Atlas stores traceability events with denormalized lot_chain_ids for sub-second trace graph traversal. Azure AD B2C manages multi-tenant trading partner authentication with org-scoped access isolation.
target_output.architecture_overview: Azure-hosted microservices architecture with three Spring Boot services (TraceabilityService, RecallService, PartnerService) deployed on AKS behind an Azure API Management gateway. Vue 3 SPA served via Azure Static Web Apps with route-based code splitting. Azure AD B2C handles trading partner authentication with org-scoped JWT claims. MongoDB Atlas M30 cluster stores traceability event documents with denormalized lot_chain_ids arrays to avoid cross-collection joins on the two-hour trace path. Redis 7 Azure Cache caches trace results with 30-minute TTL for recall investigation repeat queries. Azure Service Bus propagates traceability events between microservices asynchronously. Azure DevOps CI/CD pipeline deploys to AKS with Helm charts. Application Insights distributed tracing correlates trace queries across Spring Boot services.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Vue 3 TypeScript, Pinia state management, Cytoscape.js for supply chain trace graph visualization, Chart.js for traceability analytics. Backend: Java 21 Spring Boot 3.2, Spring Data MongoDB, Spring Batch for reconciliation jobs, Azure Service Bus client. Data: MongoDB Atlas M30 (Azure East US 2), Redis 7 Azure Cache. Auth: Azure AD B2C with custom user flows. Infrastructure: AKS (3 microservices), Azure API Management, Azure Static Web Apps, Azure Key Vault, Azure Service Bus. CI/CD: Azure DevOps Pipelines, Helm. Observability: Application Insights, Azure Monitor, PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Harvest Lot Registration: packinghouse food safety managers register incoming farm lots with GS1 GTIN-14 code, commodity, growing area, harvest date, and quantity -- captures FSMA Rule 204 Harvesting CTE KDEs. Packing Event Recording: links inbound farm lot IDs to outbound GS1 SSCC shipping labels; supports mixed-origin lots (multiple farm sources into one SSCC); denormalizes lot_chain_ids on packing_event document for trace path. Shipment Creation: records SSCC labels, ship date, destination DC facility, and bill of lading for FSMA Shipping CTE. Distribution Center Receiving: DC receiving teams confirm received SSCCs and flag rejections. One-Step Trace Query: auditors query forward (where did lot X go downstream?) or backward (what farms contributed to SSCC Y?) and see Cytoscape.js supply chain graph. Recall Investigation: food safety manager opens investigation linked to a flagged lot, executes forward trace to generate scope report of all downstream DC shipments within FSMA two-hour SLA.
target_output.system_components: Azure API Management gateway (rate limiting, JWT validation, trading partner routing), TraceabilityService (Spring Boot, harvest lot/packing/shipping/receiving CRUD, trace query execution), RecallService (recall investigation lifecycle, SLA timer, scope report generation), PartnerService (Azure AD B2C integration, trading partner org management, user provisioning), MongoDB Atlas (traceability event collections with lot_chain_ids indexes), Redis 7 Azure Cache (trace result cache), Azure Service Bus (async event propagation between services), Azure Static Web Apps (Vue 3 SPA), Azure Key Vault (MongoDB Atlas connection string, service secrets), Spring Batch reconciliation job (nightly unmatched shipment flagging).
target_output.workflows: Packinghouse Packing Event Workflow: Food safety manager opens Packing Event form, selects inbound farm lot IDs (e.g., lot GTIN 00312345678901 from Hollingsworth Farms, 1200 lbs), assigns to outbound SSCC label (00000312345600000001), records pack date and facility. TraceabilityService creates packing_event MongoDB document with inbound_lot_ids array, outbound_sscc_labels array, and denormalized lot_chain_ids (all ancestor lot IDs including the inbound farm lots and their predecessor lots). Azure Service Bus event published to notify downstream services. Redis trace cache invalidated for all affected lot IDs. Recall Investigation Workflow: FDA field investigator contacts food safety manager reporting a Salmonella cluster linked to romaine lot GTIN 00312345600000023. Manager opens Recall Investigation in RecallService, triggers GET /v1/trace/forward/{lot_id}. RecallService executes MongoDB query on packing_events where lot_chain_ids contains the trigger lot, returns all downstream SSCCs; follows SSCCs into shipping_records and receiving_records. SLA timer starts at investigation open; if trace query returns in under 2 hours, SLA met flag set. Scope report generated listing all DC receiving records. PagerDuty alert fires at 90-minute mark if investigation still open.
target_output.data_model: FLAW: The following schema uses PostgreSQL table definitions instead of MongoDB document structures as specified in the contracted data_platform. harvest_lots table (id UUID PK, organization_id UUID, gtin14 VARCHAR(14), commodity VARCHAR, growing_area_id UUID, harvest_date DATE, quantity_value NUMERIC, quantity_units VARCHAR, created_at). packing_events table (id UUID PK, organization_id UUID, pack_date DATE, facility_id UUID, lot_chain_ids UUID[]). shipping_records table (id UUID PK, organization_id UUID, ship_date DATE, destination_facility_id UUID, bill_of_lading VARCHAR). receiving_records table (id UUID PK, organization_id UUID, receive_date DATE, source_facility_id UUID, rejection_flag BOOL). This SQL schema does not support the GS1 SSCC array relationships or the denormalized lot_chain_ids document structure required for sub-second MongoDB trace queries.
target_output.api_design: TraceabilityService REST endpoints: POST /v1/lots (harvest lot with gtin14, commodity, growing_area, harvest_date, quantity), POST /v1/packing-events (inbound_lot_ids array, outbound_sscc_labels array, pack_date, facility_id), POST /v1/shipments (sscc_labels, ship_date, destination_facility_id, bill_of_lading), POST /v1/receivings (sscc_labels_received, receive_date, source_facility_id). GET /v1/trace/forward/{lot_id} (one-step-forward trace, returns downstream SSCCs and receiving records), GET /v1/trace/backward/{sscc} (one-step-back, returns contributing farm lots). RecallService: POST /v1/recall-investigations (open investigation with trigger_lot_id), GET /v1/recall-investigations/{id}/scope-report. All endpoints: Azure AD B2C JWT required via API Management policy; organization_id from JWT claims applied to all queries.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Azure AD B2C custom user flows: trading partner self-registration (requires admin approval for org creation), standard login with MFA option. JWT organization_id and role claims validated at API Management gateway and re-validated in each Spring Boot service. Auditor role: bypasses org_id filter for recall trace queries; every cross-org query logged to traceability_audit_log MongoDB collection (append-only, no delete index on the collection). Azure Key Vault for MongoDB Atlas connection string and service account credentials. FSMA Rule 204 compliance: all seven Critical Tracking Events (harvesting, cooling, initial packing, first land-based receiver, shipping, receiving, transformation) captured in data model. Traceability records immutable after creation (no update endpoints for submitted records). GS1 GTIN-14 check digit validation enforced server-side on all lot registration requests.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Vague generic description: the system runs in containers on a Kubernetes cluster using standard CI/CD practices. Services are deployed with rolling updates. The database is managed by a cloud provider. Monitoring uses industry-standard tools. This section intentionally omits AKS namespace topology, Helm chart structure, Azure DevOps pipeline stages, and MongoDB Atlas tier/region specifics that the contracted hosting_target (AKS) and data_platform (MongoDB Atlas) require.
target_output.observability: Application Insights distributed tracing configured for all three Spring Boot services with correlation IDs propagated through Azure Service Bus messages. Custom Application Insights metrics: trace_query_duration_ms (alert if p95 > 90 minutes to warn before two-hour SLA breach), recall_investigation_sla_met_rate (target 100%), packing_event_creation_rate (monitors supply chain data ingestion throughput). Azure Monitor alerts: MongoDB Atlas connection pool saturation, AKS node CPU >80%, Redis Azure Cache eviction rate >5%. PagerDuty escalation: recall investigation open for >90 minutes without trace completion.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ~$3,200/month at 500 trading partner organizations with 10,000 traceability events/day. MongoDB Atlas M30 (3-node replica set): $600/month. Redis Azure Cache C2: $180/month. AKS node pool (3 Standard_D4s_v3 nodes): $900/month. Azure Service Bus Standard: $70/month. Azure API Management Developer tier: $250/month (upgrade to Standard at 500+ partners). Estimated scale inflection: at 2,000 trading partners and 50,000 events/day, MongoDB Atlas upgrade to M50 required and AKS node pool expansion to 6 nodes.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): FSMA Rule 204 CTE data model in MongoDB Atlas, harvest lot and packing event APIs, shipping and receiving APIs, GS1 GTIN-14 and SSCC validation, one-step-forward and one-step-back trace query with Redis cache, Azure AD B2C trading partner auth and org management, recall investigation basic flow, two-hour SLA monitoring. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Recall investigation scope report PDF export, Cytoscape.js supply chain graph visualization, mixed-origin packing event fan-out warning, Spring Batch nightly reconciliation job for unmatched shipments, trading partner self-registration workflow. Phase 3 (8 weeks): EDI 856 ASN integration with major retail chains, FDA Reportable Food Registry API integration, GFSI audit report export (SQF/GlobalG.A.P. format), blockchain lot audit trail anchoring prototype.
target_output.development_guidelines: Java 21 strict typing with SpotBugs and Checkstyle in Azure DevOps pipeline. JUnit 5 with Testcontainers MongoDB for Spring Data repository tests; 85% coverage gate on TraceabilityService and RecallService. Integration test suite validates two-hour trace SLA against a MongoDB Atlas test cluster loaded with 100,000 synthetic lot records. Vue 3 components tested with Vitest and Vue Testing Library; Cytoscape.js graph rendering tested with snapshot comparison. GS1 GTIN-14 and SSCC check digit validation logic tested with 50 known-valid and known-invalid code fixtures. Azure DevOps release pipeline requires food safety manager UAT sign-off on recall investigation workflow before each production deployment.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: MongoDB lot_chain_ids array grows unbounded for heavily repacked commodity lots (bagged salad with 15+ farm origins packed and repacked through multiple facilities). Mitigation: cap lot_chain_ids array depth at 5 levels; flag lots exceeding cap with manual_trace_required status in RecallService; document limitation in FDA compliance assessment. Risk: Azure AD B2C trading partner self-registration creates org records that have not been contractually onboarded, leading to unauthorized data access if approval workflow is not enforced. Mitigation: org status ENUM pending_approval/active/suspended; all API requests return 403 for pending_approval organizations until platform admin activates. Tradeoff: MongoDB document model with denormalized lot_chain_ids vs. relational graph database (e.g., Neo4j) -- MongoDB chosen for team familiarity and Atlas managed service simplicity; Neo4j would offer native graph traversal but adds operational complexity for a team with no graph DB experience. Tradeoff: Azure Service Bus async event propagation vs. synchronous inter-service calls -- async chosen for resilience but introduces eventual consistency between services; trace queries that arrive within milliseconds of a packing event creation may return stale results until Service Bus propagation completes.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed mixed-origin packing events (multiple farm lot IDs into one SSCC) are supported via inbound_lot_ids array on packing_event document with denormalized lot_chain_ids. Confirmed GS1 Company Prefix assignment is a pre-launch business requirement (not a technical decision) -- platform operator must obtain GS1 prefix before SSCC generation can go live. Confirmed two-hour trace SLA measured from recall investigation open time, not from FDA notification receipt. Confirmed trading partner self-registration requires platform admin approval before org becomes active.

metadata.notes: Intentionally flawed: data_model section uses PostgreSQL table syntax instead of MongoDB document structure matching contracted data_platform; deployment_and_operations section uses generic language omitting required AKS and Helm topology details.

project_goal.value: Data pipeline ingesting pesticide application records from multiple farm management platforms, standardizing to EPA reporting schemas, and generating state-mandated pesticide use reports on a quarterly basis for hundreds of enrolled farms
project_goal.rationale: Regulatory reporting obligation requiring standardized data from heterogeneous farm software sources

target_users.value: Compliance officers and agricultural consultants managing quarterly pesticide use report submissions to state departments of agriculture on behalf of enrolled farm operations
target_users.rationale: Internal compliance team of 3-5 operators; not end-user-facing

project_class.rationale: Batch ETL pipeline orchestrated by Apache Airflow with no user-facing frontend; all interaction via Airflow UI and generated reports

capabilities.rationale: ETL batch jobs, farm management platform API integrations, PostgreSQL analytics, and EPA schema report generation

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-source heterogeneous API integrations, EPA product code validation, applicator license verification, and multi-state report format variations add significant complexity

risk_level.rationale: Incorrect or late pesticide use reports result in regulatory violations and fines for enrolled farms; data accuracy is critical

data_sensitivity.rationale: Farm pesticide application records contain commercially sensitive crop protection strategy data for hundreds of enrolled operations

external_exposure.rationale: Pipeline runs entirely within AWS MWAA environment; state agency submission uses outbound SFTP only, no inbound internet exposure

access_model.value: AWS IAM roles for Airflow DAG task execution; Airflow RBAC for compliance officer DAG monitoring; farm platform API credentials in AWS Secrets Manager
access_model.rationale: Least-privilege IAM task roles prevent cross-pipeline data access; Airflow RBAC limits DAG visibility to authorized compliance officers

feature_scope.value: Farm management platform API connectors (AgriWebb, Climate FieldView, Granular), EPA product code validation against EPA pesticide registry, applicator license number verification, compliance error flagging for manual review, state-specific report format generation, SFTP submission to state agencies, submission receipt archival per farm
feature_scope.rationale: Full quarterly reporting pipeline from data ingestion through submission and receipt archival

mvp_scope.value: AgriWebb connector, EPA product code validation, single-state report format (California DPR), SFTP submission, submission receipt archival in S3; Climate FieldView and Granular connectors in Phase 2
mvp_scope.rationale: California DPR has most strict reporting requirements; proving the pipeline against strictest format validates the architecture

security_baseline.value: Farm platform API credentials in AWS Secrets Manager with 90-day rotation; S3 submission archives encrypted with SSE-KMS; PostgreSQL encrypted at rest; Airflow connections encrypted; state agency SFTP with host key verification
security_baseline.rationale: Confidential farm data requires encryption at rest and in transit; credential rotation limits exposure from compromise

frontend_stack.value: None; Airflow UI for DAG monitoring and manual trigger; no custom frontend
frontend_stack.rationale: Data pipeline with no custom UI; compliance officers interact via Airflow MWAA web interface

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Apache Airflow 2.9 on AWS MWAA; custom DAG operators for each farm platform connector
backend_stack.rationale: Airflow is the standard orchestration platform for this scale of batch ETL; MWAA eliminates infrastructure management

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on AWS RDS for staging and validated record storage; S3 for raw API response archives and submission receipt storage
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL handles relational pesticide record validation and reporting; S3 for raw data lineage and receipt archival

hosting_target.value: AWS MWAA (Managed Workflows for Apache Airflow) in us-west-2; RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ; S3 buckets in us-west-2
hosting_target.rationale: California DPR regulatory jurisdiction; us-west-2 data residency for California state agency compliance

privacy_retention_policy.value: Pesticide application records retained 5 years per EPA FIFRA record-keeping requirements; raw API response archives retained 2 years for audit lineage; submission receipts retained 7 years per California DPR requirements
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: EPA FIFRA and California DPR specify minimum record retention periods for pesticide use data

future_scope.value: Climate FieldView and Granular connectors, additional state report formats (Oregon, Washington), machine learning model for anomaly detection in application rate outliers, self-service farm enrollment portal
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 expands connector and state coverage; Phase 3 adds ML-assisted quality control

constraints.value: California DPR submission deadline is 90 days after quarter end; pipeline must complete in under 48 hours for 500 enrolled farms; farm platform API rate limits must be respected; no pesticide data may leave AWS us-west-2
constraints.rationale: Regulatory deadline and data residency requirements are non-negotiable; API rate limits prevent partner credential revocation

observability_baseline.value: AWS CloudWatch for MWAA DAG run metrics; custom CloudWatch metrics for validation error rates per farm and per source platform; SNS alerts for DAG failures and farms with >10% validation error rate
observability_baseline.rationale: Compliance officers need visibility into per-farm validation error rates to prioritize manual review workload

execution_preference.value: Quarterly scheduled DAG runs triggered 7 days after quarter-end to allow farm platforms to finalize records; manual re-run capability per farm for error corrections
execution_preference.rationale: 7-day buffer after quarter-end allows farm operators to submit late applications before pipeline runs

compliance_context.value: EPA FIFRA pesticide use reporting requirements; California DPR Pesticide Use Reporting (PUR) mandate; state-specific applicator license verification requirements; 5-year EPA and 7-year California DPR record retention requirements
compliance_context.rationale: Multi-regulatory compliance requirement spanning federal EPA FIFRA and California DPR state mandate

project.goal: Quarterly pesticide use report pipeline for enrolled farms across multiple farm management platforms
project.target_users: Internal compliance officers submitting to California DPR
project.timeline: 12-week to first California DPR quarterly submission

backend.connectors: AgriWebb REST API connector in MVP; Climate FieldView and Granular in Phase 2
backend.validation: EPA product code lookup against local validation DB, applicator license check via state agency API

data.archive: S3 us-west-2 with SSE-KMS for raw API responses and submission receipts
data.validation_db: EPA pesticide product registry loaded weekly from EPA CDX API

devops.ci: GitHub Actions for DAG unit tests and S3 deployment
devops.monitoring: CloudWatch DAG metrics and custom validation error rate metrics

constraints.data_residency: All data must remain in AWS us-west-2

reasoner_reviews.product: Round 2 addressed the EPA product code validation gap and added the applicator license verification step. The remaining issue from Round 2 audit is the submission receipt archival mechanism: the plan describes writing receipts to PostgreSQL but the contract specifies S3 archival. This contract alignment issue must be resolved in Round 3.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The Round 2 DAG structure with TaskGroups per farm is the right pattern for parallelizing the 500-farm extraction without overwhelming the AgriWebb API. The missing piece is the per-farm Airflow Variable for rate limit tracking — the current plan uses a shared counter which creates a race condition when multiple farm extraction tasks run concurrently. Round 3 should address this with per-farm extraction backoff.
reasoner_reviews.security: Round 2 added Secrets Manager rotation for farm platform credentials. The remaining security gap is the SFTP host key verification for California DPR — the plan mentions SFTP submission but does not specify how the DPR server host key is pinned to prevent MITM during submission.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 48-hour throughput constraint for 500 farms requires that the AgriWebb connector honor a maximum of 10 concurrent farm extraction tasks to stay within AgriWebb's documented API rate limits of 100 requests/minute across all concurrent calls.

backend.service_design: Airflow DAG 'pesticide_quarterly_report' with four TaskGroups: (1) extract_all_farms — one ExtractAgriWebbOperator task per enrolled farm using Airflow's dynamic task mapping over farm_ids, max_active_tis_per_dag=10 to respect AgriWebb rate limits; (2) validate_all_farms — ValidatePesticideRecordsOperator per farm checking EPA product codes and applicator licenses; (3) generate_reports — GenerateCaliforniaDPRReportOperator per farm producing PUR-format XML; (4) submit_and_archive — SubmitToCaliforniaDPROperator per farm with SFTP upload and S3 receipt archival.
backend.background_jobs: Weekly EPA CDX product registry sync DAG: downloads EPA pesticide product database XML, parses to PostgreSQL epa_products table (epa_reg_number PK, product_name, active_ingredients[], registrant, cancelled_date). Quarterly applicator license validation: queries California DCA API for each unique applicator license number in the quarter's applications.
backend.failure_handling: Farm-level task failures do not block other farms: Airflow task-level retries (3 retries, 30-min backoff) per farm. Failed farms logged to PostgreSQL farm_run_status table with error_category ENUM (api_error/validation_error/sftp_error). Manual re-run: DAG accepts farm_id parameter for single-farm re-execution. SFTP submission failures trigger SNS alert to compliance officer with farm_name and error detail.

security.auth_design: AWS IAM task execution roles: extract_role (Secrets Manager GetSecretValue for farm API credentials only), validate_role (RDS read/write for staging tables, read for EPA validation DB), submit_role (S3 PutObject for receipts, Secrets Manager GetSecretValue for SFTP credentials). Airflow connections encrypted with Fernet key stored in AWS Secrets Manager.
security.secrets_management: AgriWebb API credentials per farm stored as individual Secrets Manager secrets named agriWebb/farm/{farm_id}/api_key. Rotation Lambda rotates on 90-day schedule via Secrets Manager rotation. California DPR SFTP credentials in Secrets Manager sftp/california_dpr with host key fingerprint stored alongside credentials for verification.
security.security_risks: Risk of submitting a farm's data to the wrong state agency SFTP if farm-to-state mapping table has incorrect entries. Mitigate with per-submission dry-run mode that validates SFTP target and farm mapping before transmitting data.

data.entities: pesticide_applications (id UUID PK, farm_enrollment_id UUID FK, source_platform ENUM agriWebb/fieldview/granular, application_date DATE, field_id STRING, epa_reg_number STRING FK, product_name STRING, active_ingredient STRING, application_rate_per_acre FLOAT, total_quantity FLOAT, unit ENUM oz/lb/gal/fl_oz, applicator_license_number STRING, crop_type STRING, target_pest STRING, application_method STRING, created_at TIMESTAMP, validation_status ENUM pending/valid/error, validation_errors JSONB), farm_enrollments (id UUID PK, farm_name STRING, state ENUM CA/OR/WA, agriWebb_farm_id STRING, operator_contact STRING, enrolled_at DATE), epa_products (epa_reg_number STRING PK, product_name STRING, active_ingredients JSONB, registrant STRING, cancelled_date DATE nullable, last_synced_at TIMESTAMP), applicator_licenses (license_number STRING PK, state STRING, holder_name STRING, license_type STRING, expiry_date DATE, verified_at TIMESTAMP), submission_records (id UUID PK, farm_enrollment_id UUID FK, reporting_period STRING YYYY-QN, state STRING, submitted_at TIMESTAMP, sftp_transfer_id STRING, receipt_s3_key STRING, submission_status ENUM submitted/acknowledged/rejected).
data.schema_notes: pesticide_applications partitioned by quarter (YYYY-QN) for efficient per-quarter pipeline scans. validation_errors stored as JSONB array of {field, error_code, detail} for flexible per-field error reporting without schema changes as validation rules evolve.
data.retention_and_deletion: pesticide_applications: 5-year retention in PostgreSQL, then archive to S3 Parquet via COPY TO. submission_records and receipt_s3_keys: 7-year retention per California DPR. RDS automated backups: 35-day retention. S3 submission archives: lifecycle rule transitions to Glacier after 2 years, permanent retention thereafter.

devops.deployment_topology: AWS MWAA environment (Airflow 2.9, mw1.medium workers, min 1 max 5 workers for burst during quarterly run). RDS PostgreSQL 16 db.r6g.large Multi-AZ in same VPC. S3 buckets: pesticide-pipeline-raw-{env} (raw API response archives) and pesticide-pipeline-submissions-{env} (submission receipts and reports), both SSE-KMS. MWAA VPC with no public subnets; all outbound via NAT Gateway (AgriWebb API, EPA CDX API, California DPR SFTP).
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: (1) pytest on DAG unit tests with mocked AgriWebb API responses, (2) airflow dag-validation for syntax checks, (3) aws s3 sync dags/ to MWAA S3 DAG bucket on merge to main. Quarterly run triggered by Airflow scheduled interval (0 8 8 1,4,7,10 *) — 7 days after quarter-end at 08:00 PT.
devops.observability_stack: CloudWatch custom metrics namespace PesticidePipeline: farm_extraction_success_count, farm_validation_error_count, sftp_submission_success_count, pipeline_total_duration_minutes. CloudWatch dashboard with per-quarter trend charts. SNS topic pesticide-pipeline-alerts subscribed by compliance officer email; alerts triggered on: any DAG failure, farms with validation_error_count >10, total pipeline duration >36 hours (approaching 48h constraint).
devops.devops_risks: MWAA environment upgrade requires DAG compatibility testing; pin Airflow provider package versions in requirements.txt to prevent provider-API breakage on MWAA version updates.

ISS-001.title: Submission receipts written to PostgreSQL instead of S3 per contract
ISS-001.detail: Round 2 plan described writing submission receipts to the submission_records table in PostgreSQL. The contract data_platform specifies S3 for submission receipt storage. PostgreSQL should store only metadata (s3_key reference), not the receipt file content.

history.note: Not yet identified in Round 1
Receipt storage still in PostgreSQL in Round 2 plan

ISS-002.title: SFTP host key verification not specified for California DPR submission
ISS-002.detail: Round 2 plan mentions SFTP submission to California DPR but does not specify how the DPR server host key is verified to prevent MITM during report submission.

history.note: Identified in Round 1
Still not addressed in Round 2

ISS-003.title: Shared AgriWebb rate limit counter race condition
ISS-003.detail: Round 2 used a shared Airflow Variable for AgriWebb rate limit tracking across concurrent farm tasks. Addressed in Round 3 by using max_active_tis_per_dag=10 enforcement at the DAG level instead of a shared counter.

history.note: Identified in Round 2 review
Fixed with max_active_tis_per_dag task concurrency limit

previous_plan.title: Pesticide Compliance Reporting Pipeline v2
previous_plan.executive_summary: Python/Airflow MWAA pipeline ingesting AgriWebb pesticide application records quarterly, validating EPA product codes and applicator licenses, generating California DPR PUR-format XML reports, and submitting via SFTP.
previous_plan.data_model: pesticide_applications with EPA product code and applicator license fields. submission_records with receipt XML content stored in PostgreSQL (ISSUE: should be S3). epa_products table synced weekly from EPA CDX.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Secrets Manager for farm API credentials and SFTP credentials. IAM task execution roles. SFTP submission to California DPR (host key verification not specified — ISSUE: no host key pinning).
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: MWAA quarterly DAG with dynamic task mapping over farm_ids, max_active_tis_per_dag=10 for AgriWebb rate limit compliance. ISS-003 resolved in Round 2.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 revision addressing ISS-001 (submission receipts in PostgreSQL instead of S3) and ISS-002 (SFTP host key verification absent). Moving receipt XML bytes to S3 with only s3_key metadata stored in PostgreSQL. Adding paramiko known_hosts verification using DPR host key fingerprint from Secrets Manager. Also noting ISS-003 was resolved in Round 2 via max_active_tis_per_dag task concurrency. The compliance_context field is required because risk_level=high and data_sensitivity=confidential.

fix_report.action_taken: Updated SubmitToCaliforniaDPROperator to write DPR submission receipt XML bytes to S3 key pesticide-pipeline-submissions-prod/receipts/{farm_id}/{quarter}/receipt_{timestamp}.xml using boto3 S3 PutObject with SSE-KMS. PostgreSQL submission_records.receipt_s3_key stores the S3 object key reference only, not file content. Updated data_model and deployment_and_operations to reflect this split.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Submission receipt storage now matches contract data_platform specification; S3 SSE-KMS encryption provides receipt integrity and retention lifecycle management

fix_report.action_taken: Added California DPR SFTP host key fingerprint storage in Secrets Manager (sftp/california_dpr.host_key_sha256) and paramiko known_hosts verification in SubmitToCaliforniaDPROperator using RejectPolicy. Fingerprint updated via a separate maintenance DAG that verifies and stores the current DPR host key annually. Documented in security_and_compliance and system_components sections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: SFTP MITM risk eliminated; paramiko RejectPolicy raises exception and halts submission if server key does not match stored fingerprint, triggering SNS alert before any data is transmitted

target_output.title: Pesticide Compliance Reporting Pipeline
target_output.executive_summary: Python 3.12 Airflow 2.9 data pipeline on AWS MWAA orchestrating quarterly pesticide use report submissions for 500 enrolled California farms. AgriWebb connector extracts application records via dynamic task mapping (max 10 concurrent farm tasks for API rate compliance), validates EPA product registration codes against weekly-synced EPA CDX registry and applicator licenses against California DCA API, generates California DPR PUR-format XML reports, and submits via paramiko SFTP with host key verification. Submission receipts archived to S3 SSE-KMS with 7-year retention; PostgreSQL stores only s3_key metadata reference. Round 3 resolves all three tracked issues.
target_output.architecture_overview: Apache Airflow 2.9 DAG on AWS MWAA orchestrating a four-stage ETL pipeline. Stage 1 (Extract): dynamic task mapping generates one ExtractAgriWebbOperator task per enrolled farm, pulling application records for the quarter via AgriWebb REST API with per-task retry and max_active_tis_per_dag=10 for rate limit compliance. Stage 2 (Validate): ValidatePesticideRecordsOperator per farm checks each application's EPA registration number against the epa_products PostgreSQL table (populated weekly from EPA CDX API sync DAG) and verifies applicator license numbers against California DCA API. Validation errors written to pesticide_applications.validation_errors JSONB for manual review queue. Stage 3 (Generate): GenerateCaliforniaDPRReportOperator builds PUR-format XML per farm from validated application records. Stage 4 (Submit and Archive): SubmitToCaliforniaDPROperator connects to DPR SFTP via paramiko with host key verification from Secrets Manager fingerprint, uploads XML report, receives receipt, writes receipt bytes to S3 SSE-KMS, stores s3_key in submission_records. All data remains in AWS us-west-2.
target_output.technology_stack: Orchestration: Apache Airflow 2.9 on AWS MWAA mw1.medium workers (min 1, max 5). Pipeline: Python 3.12, boto3, paramiko 3.x for SFTP with host key verification, requests for AgriWebb and EPA CDX API calls, lxml for DPR PUR-format XML generation. Database: PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ db.r6g.large. Storage: S3 us-west-2 with SSE-KMS (raw API archives and submission receipts). Secrets: AWS Secrets Manager with 90-day rotation Lambda for farm API credentials. Monitoring: CloudWatch custom metrics and SNS alerts. CI/CD: GitHub Actions DAG deployment to MWAA S3 bucket.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Extract Stage: per-farm AgriWebb API connector pulling application_events for the quarter window (start_date, end_date parameters), writing raw API responses to S3 raw archive bucket, staging normalized records to pesticide_applications PostgreSQL table with validation_status=pending. Validate Stage: EPA product code lookup (epa_reg_number match against epa_products table; flag cancelled registrations), applicator license check (query California DCA license API for each unique license_number in the quarter's records), write validation_errors JSONB per application, set validation_status to valid or error. Manual Review Queue: compliance officer queries PostgreSQL for farms with any validation_status=error applications and resolves before submission deadline. Generate Stage: per-farm California DPR PUR XML generation using validated application records, lxml building PUR schema elements. Submit Stage: paramiko SFTP to California DPR server with SHA-256 host key verification, receipt bytes written to S3, s3_key stored in submission_records. Submission Receipt Archival: S3 lifecycle rule transitions receipts to Glacier after 2 years with 7-year minimum retention.
target_output.system_components: ExtractAgriWebbOperator (Python Airflow operator): authenticates with Secrets Manager credentials, paginates AgriWebb /applications endpoint with quarter date range, writes raw JSON responses to S3 pesticide-pipeline-raw-prod/agriWebb/{farm_id}/{quarter}/, upserts normalized records to pesticide_applications staging table. ValidatePesticideRecordsOperator: queries epa_products for each unique epa_reg_number in farm's staged records, calls California DCA API for license validation, updates validation_errors JSONB and validation_status per record. GenerateCaliforniaDPRReportOperator: fetches valid records for farm/quarter, builds PUR XML document using lxml with DPR schema namespace, writes draft to S3 pesticide-pipeline-submissions-prod/reports/{farm_id}/{quarter}/report.xml. SubmitToCaliforniaDPROperator: retrieves SFTP credentials and host key SHA-256 fingerprint from Secrets Manager sftp/california_dpr, initializes paramiko SSHClient with RejectPolicy and populates known_hosts with stored fingerprint, opens SFTP connection, uploads report XML, reads receipt bytes from server response, writes receipt to S3 pesticide-pipeline-submissions-prod/receipts/{farm_id}/{quarter}/receipt_{iso_timestamp}.xml via boto3 PutObject with SSE-KMS, stores S3 key in submission_records. EPA CDX Sync DAG: weekly DAG downloading EPA pesticide product database XML from EPA CDX, parsing with lxml, upsert to epa_products table.
target_output.workflows: Quarterly Pipeline Execution Workflow: Airflow scheduled trigger fires 7 days after quarter-end (08:00 PT on the 8th of January, April, July, October). extract_all_farms TaskGroup maps over enrolled farm_ids from farm_enrollments table; max 10 concurrent extraction tasks honor AgriWebb 100 req/min limit. Each extract task writes raw archive to S3 and stages records to pesticide_applications. validate_all_farms TaskGroup runs per-farm after extraction completes; flags applications with unknown EPA registration numbers (cross-referenced against weekly-updated epa_products) or expired/invalid applicator license numbers (verified against California DCA API). Farms with >10% validation error rate trigger SNS alert to compliance officer for manual review. generate_reports TaskGroup runs only for farms with zero validation errors (or manual override flag set by compliance officer). submit_and_archive TaskGroup runs SubmitToCaliforniaDPROperator per farm sequentially (max 1 concurrent SFTP connection per DPR server policy): verifies host key, uploads PUR XML, archives receipt to S3, records submission metadata in PostgreSQL. Manual Re-run Workflow: Compliance officer corrects validation errors in pesticide_applications, sets manual_override_flag=true for farm, triggers DAG with conf={farm_id: UUID, quarter: '2025-Q4'} for single-farm re-run starting at generate_reports stage.
target_output.data_model: pesticide_applications: (id UUID PK, farm_enrollment_id UUID FK, source_platform ENUM agriWebb/fieldview/granular, application_date DATE, field_id VARCHAR, epa_reg_number VARCHAR FK epa_products, product_name VARCHAR, active_ingredient VARCHAR, application_rate_per_acre NUMERIC, total_quantity NUMERIC, unit ENUM oz/lb/gal/fl_oz, applicator_license_number VARCHAR FK applicator_licenses, crop_type VARCHAR, target_pest VARCHAR, application_method VARCHAR, validation_status ENUM pending/valid/error, validation_errors JSONB, created_at TIMESTAMP, quarter CHAR(7)) PARTITION BY quarter. farm_enrollments: (id UUID PK, farm_name VARCHAR, state ENUM CA/OR/WA, agriWebb_farm_id VARCHAR, operator_contact VARCHAR, enrolled_at DATE). epa_products: (epa_reg_number VARCHAR PK, product_name VARCHAR, active_ingredients JSONB, registrant VARCHAR, cancelled_date DATE, last_synced_at TIMESTAMP). applicator_licenses: (license_number VARCHAR PK, state VARCHAR, holder_name VARCHAR, license_type VARCHAR, expiry_date DATE, verified_at TIMESTAMP). submission_records: (id UUID PK, farm_enrollment_id UUID FK, reporting_period CHAR(7), state ENUM, submitted_at TIMESTAMP, sftp_transfer_filename VARCHAR, receipt_s3_key VARCHAR, submission_status ENUM submitted/acknowledged/rejected). S3 key convention: receipts/{farm_id}/{quarter}/receipt_{iso_timestamp}.xml with SSE-KMS encryption and 7-year lifecycle.
target_output.api_design: No public-facing API. Internal Airflow DAG trigger interface: POST MWAA /api/v1/dags/pesticide_quarterly_report/dagRuns with JSON conf {farm_id, quarter} for single-farm re-run. External integrations (outbound only): AgriWebb REST API GET /v2/applications?farm_id=&start_date=&end_date= (paginated, OAuth2 client credentials from Secrets Manager). EPA CDX API GET /api/list/ListOfPestProductWithRegistrant (XML download weekly). California DCA License Verification API GET /api/pesticide-applicator/license?license_number= (one request per unique license per quarter). California DPR SFTP: paramiko sftp.put() with host key verification; DPR provides SFTP receipt as a response file in the upload directory.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Farm API credentials (AgriWebb OAuth2 client ID and secret per farm) stored in Secrets Manager as agriWebb/farm/{farm_id}/credentials with 90-day rotation Lambda. California DPR SFTP credentials stored as sftp/california_dpr.username, sftp/california_dpr.password, sftp/california_dpr.host_key_sha256. SubmitToCaliforniaDPROperator: paramiko SSHClient.set_missing_host_key_policy(RejectPolicy), then manually adds the stored SHA-256 fingerprint to known_hosts via client.get_host_keys().add() before connect(). If server presents a different fingerprint, paramiko raises SSHException, halts the task, and SNS alert fires before any data is transmitted. S3 submission archives: SSE-KMS with customer-managed KMS key rotated annually; S3 Object Lock COMPLIANCE mode (7-year retention) on submission receipts bucket. PostgreSQL RDS: encryption at rest with AWS-managed key; VPC-only access; no public endpoint. EPA FIFRA compliance: pesticide_applications retained 5 years, submission_records and receipt S3 objects retained 7 years per California DPR. All pipeline processing in AWS us-west-2 with no cross-region data transfer.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: MWAA environment: Airflow 2.9, mw1.medium worker class, min 1 max 5 workers. DAG deployment: GitHub Actions merges to main trigger aws s3 sync dags/ to MWAA S3 bucket; MWAA auto-picks up new DAG versions within 30 seconds. Quarterly run schedule: cron 0 8 8 1,4,7,10 (08:00 PT on 8th of Jan/Apr/Jul/Oct). EPA CDX sync: cron 0 6 * * 1 (weekly Monday 06:00 PT). CloudWatch custom metric alarms: pipeline_total_duration_minutes >2160 (36 hours, warning before 48-hour constraint), farm_validation_error_count per-farm >10 (SNS alert to compliance officer). S3 lifecycle rules: submission receipts bucket — transition to Glacier Deep Archive after 2 years (7-year total retention per California DPR), raw API archive bucket — expire after 2 years. RDS automated snapshot: 35-day retention with point-in-time recovery. Secrets Manager rotation: 90-day schedule with rotation Lambda testing new credentials before marking old as deprecated.
target_output.observability: CloudWatch custom metrics namespace PesticidePipeline (emitted by Airflow operators via boto3 put_metric_data): farm_extraction_success_count (per quarter), farm_validation_error_count (per farm per quarter — key metric for compliance officer workload), sftp_submission_success_count, sftp_submission_failure_count, pipeline_total_duration_minutes, epa_cdx_sync_products_updated (weekly). CloudWatch dashboard: pipeline execution timeline, per-quarter farm validation error rate trend, submission success rate by state. SNS topic pesticide-pipeline-alerts (email subscription for compliance officers): DAG failure events, farm_validation_error_count >10, pipeline_total_duration_minutes >2160, SFTP host key mismatch exception (critical — potential MITM, compliance officer must investigate before any resubmission).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $620/month: MWAA mw1.medium $320 (prorated to quarterly burst; min-workers=1 idles cheaply between quarters), RDS db.r6g.large Multi-AZ $190, S3 storage and requests $35, Secrets Manager $25, CloudWatch and SNS $15, NAT Gateway $35 (outbound API calls). Scaling: adding Climate FieldView and Granular connectors in Phase 2 adds two new TaskGroup stages and new connector operator classes without architectural changes; MWAA max_workers=5 is sufficient for 1,500 farms before needing mw1.large upgrade.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (12 weeks, complete): AgriWebb connector, EPA product code validation against CDX registry, applicator license validation via California DCA API, California DPR PUR XML generation, paramiko SFTP submission with host key verification, S3 SSE-KMS receipt archival, Airflow MWAA deployment. All three tracked issues (ISS-001, ISS-002, ISS-003) resolved by Round 3. Phase 2 (8 weeks): Climate FieldView connector (different API schema requiring separate operator), Granular connector, Oregon DPR and Washington WSU report formats and SFTP endpoints, second state's applicator license API. Phase 3 (6 weeks): ML anomaly detection for application rate outliers (unusually high rate/acre flagging suspicious records before submission), self-service farm enrollment portal for compliance officers to add new farms without code changes.
target_output.development_guidelines: Airflow operators implemented as Python classes inheriting BaseOperator with execute(context) method; no business logic in DAG definition files. All external API calls wrapped in retry decorators (tenacity.retry with exponential backoff) separate from Airflow task-level retries. paramiko SFTP connections opened and closed within a context manager in each task execution; no persistent SFTP connections across tasks. PostgreSQL queries parameterized (psycopg2 execute with %s placeholders); no string interpolation. validation_errors JSONB schema versioned: each error entry includes schema_version field to enable future migrations. pytest fixtures provide mocked AgriWebb API responses and EPA CDX XML for deterministic CI testing without external API calls. All boto3 S3 PutObject calls include explicit SSEKMSKeyId parameter to prevent accidental unencrypted writes.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: California DPR updates PUR XML schema without notice, breaking the lxml report generator. Mitigation: lxml schema validation against a pinned DPR XSD file as a pre-submission check; generate_reports task fails loudly on schema validation error before SFTP transmission. Risk: AgriWebb API deprecates the /v2/applications endpoint, breaking extraction for all enrolled farms. Mitigation: connector class implements versioned API client with fallback to /v3 when detected; monitoring alert on HTTP 410 responses. Tradeoff: sequential SFTP submissions (max 1 concurrent per DPR server policy) means 500 farms take approximately 8-10 hours in the submit stage at 60-90 seconds per farm; acceptable within 48-hour total pipeline constraint. Risk: MWAA min-workers=1 cold start adds 3-5 minutes to quarterly pipeline initiation; acceptable given 48-hour total budget.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Submission receipt archival confirmed as S3 SSE-KMS with receipt_s3_key metadata in PostgreSQL (ISS-001 resolved). California DPR SFTP host key verification confirmed via paramiko RejectPolicy with SHA-256 fingerprint from Secrets Manager (ISS-002 resolved). AgriWebb concurrent extraction limit confirmed as max_active_tis_per_dag=10 corresponding to AgriWebb documented 100 req/min rate limit at approximately 10 requests per farm per extraction (ISS-003 resolved in Round 2).

metadata.notes: Round 3 pesticide reporting pipeline; ISS-001 (S3 receipt archival) and ISS-002 (SFTP host key pinning) resolved; ISS-003 was resolved in Round 2; moderate quality due to VARCHAR usage in data_model where ENUMs would be stronger.

project_goal.value: Crop insurance management platform for agricultural lenders and insurance agents tracking policy enrollments, premium calculations, and indemnity claim workflows for thousands of farm policy holders during spring enrollment surges
project_goal.rationale: Replaces fragmented spreadsheet and email-based workflows for USDA RMA-approved insurance providers

target_users.value: Licensed crop insurance agents, agricultural lenders, loss adjusters, and farm policy holders
target_users.rationale: Four distinct user roles with separate workflow needs and data access requirements

project_class.rationale: Requires rich browser UI for agents and a robust backend handling premium calculations, batch processing, and payment workflows

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, analytics, payments, admin_panel, batch_jobs
capabilities.rationale: Insurance workflows span agent portals, payment processing, nightly batch reconciliation, and compliance reporting

complexity_level.rationale: Thousands of concurrent policy holders during compressed spring enrollment window requires horizontal scaling and queue-based batch processing

risk_level.rationale: Handles financial transaction data, premium payments, and indemnity disbursements under USDA RMA oversight

data_sensitivity.rationale: Policy premium amounts, indemnity claims, farm unit valuations, and payment records are financial PII

external_exposure.rationale: All portal access requires authenticated agent or lender login; no public data exposure

access_model.value: RBAC with agent, lender, adjuster, admin, and policyholder roles enforced via Azure AD B2C claims
access_model.rationale: Each role has distinct data scope: agents see their book of business, adjusters see assigned claims, policyholders see own policies only

feature_scope.value: Policy enrollment wizard, actuarial rate table fetch from USDA RMA API, premium calculation engine, indemnity claim intake and routing, loss adjuster inspection recording, batch nightly reconciliation, admin compliance reporting
feature_scope.rationale: End-to-end insurance lifecycle from enrollment through claim settlement

mvp_scope.value: Policy enrollment with USDA RMA rate fetch, premium calculation and producer approval flow, basic claim intake with adjuster assignment
mvp_scope.rationale: Core enrollment and claim initiation workflows must be live before spring signup window opens

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 in transit, AES-256 encryption at rest, MFA for all agent and admin roles, Azure Key Vault for secrets, WAF on ingress
security_baseline.rationale: SOC 2 Type II and USDA RMA AIP standards require encryption and MFA at minimum

frontend_stack.value: Next.js 14 with TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: SSR for initial load performance; TypeScript end-to-end with backend; first-class Azure deployment support

backend_stack.value: Java 21 with Spring Boot
backend_stack.rationale: Actuarial calculation engine requires precise decimal arithmetic; Spring Boot ecosystem for batch processing and transaction management

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server, Redis 7 for session and rate caching
data_platform.rationale: Relational integrity for policy-claim relationships; Redis for enrollment surge caching

hosting_target.value: AKS (Azure Kubernetes Service) in eastus2 region
hosting_target.rationale: Kubernetes provides horizontal pod autoscaling for enrollment surge; Azure-native for AD B2C integration

privacy_retention_policy.value: Policy and claim records retained 7 years per USDA RMA AIP recordkeeping requirements; audit logs retained 5 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Federal crop insurance program record retention mandates exceed standard commercial requirements

future_scope.value: Automated loss adjustment decision support, integration with satellite yield estimation services, mobile app for field adjuster inspections
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap items pending MVP validation

constraints.value: Spring enrollment surge window is 6 weeks; system must handle 10x normal load; Azure-only deployment per enterprise agreement
constraints.rationale: Enrollment surge is predictable and seasonal; Azure EA drives cloud platform choice

observability_baseline.value: Azure Monitor with Application Insights APM, structured JSON logging, PagerDuty on-call for critical enrollment period
observability_baseline.rationale: Azure-native monitoring aligns with AKS and Azure DB telemetry; PagerDuty for after-hours surge incidents

execution_preference.value: Sprint-based delivery with 2-week iterations; enrollment MVP hardened before spring window
execution_preference.rationale: Fixed spring deadline drives MVP scope discipline

compliance_context.value: USDA RMA Approved Insurance Provider (AIP) standards, SOC 2 Type II for financial data handling, state insurance department regulations for licensed agents
compliance_context.rationale: Operating as an AIP mandates USDA RMA compliance; SOC 2 required by enterprise lender clients

project.goal: Manage crop insurance policy lifecycle from enrollment through claim settlement
project.target_users: Agents, lenders, adjusters, policyholders
project.timeline: MVP before spring enrollment window (12 weeks)

frontend.state: React Query for server state, Zustand for enrollment wizard

backend.framework: Spring Boot 3.2 with Spring Batch, Spring Security

security.encryption: AES-256 at rest via Azure Disk Encryption, TLS 1.3 in transit
security.vault: Azure Key Vault for API keys and DB credentials

data.batch: Spring Batch with PostgreSQL job repository

devops.environments: dev, staging, production

reasoner_reviews.product: The enrollment wizard and USDA RMA rate fetch are the critical path for MVP. Spring enrollment has a hard deadline -- any feature that does not directly support enrollment submission and premium approval should be deferred to post-launch. The claim intake flow is secondary but must be in place to avoid reverting to email.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Spring Boot with Spring Batch is a sound choice for nightly reconciliation jobs during active enrollment. The actuarial rate engine must use BigDecimal arithmetic throughout -- floating-point rounding errors in premium calculations are a compliance failure. AKS HPA targeting CPU 60% with pre-scaled baseline during the known surge window is preferable to reactive scaling alone.
reasoner_reviews.security: Azure AD B2C RBAC claims must be validated at the Spring Security layer, not just the API gateway. Agent license numbers must be stored and verified -- an unlicensed agent submitting policies is a regulatory violation. All premium and indemnity amounts must be immutably logged to an append-only audit table.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 6-week enrollment surge is the dominant architectural constraint. Pre-warm AKS node pools 48 hours before enrollment opens. PostgreSQL connection pooling via PgBouncer is required to prevent connection exhaustion under surge load.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The USDA RMA API rate table fetch is an external dependency that can fail during peak enrollment. A local cache of actuarial rate tables refreshed nightly is essential -- enrollment cannot block on a live RMA API call.

backend.service_design: Spring Boot monolith with internal module boundaries: enrollment, premiums, claims, adjusters, batch, and audit modules. Each module has its own service and repository layer with a shared domain model for policy entities.
backend.api_patterns: RESTful with OpenAPI 3.1 at /api/v1. Versioned endpoints for RMA integration. Spring Batch REST API for job monitoring. BigDecimal throughout premium calculation service.
backend.business_modules: EnrollmentModule: farm unit submission, coverage election, RMA rate table lookup. PremiumModule: calculation engine with BigDecimal, producer approval workflow. ClaimModule: loss event intake, adjuster assignment, inspection recording, settlement computation.
backend.background_jobs: Spring Batch nightly jobs: RMA rate table refresh from USDA API, premium reconciliation, claim settlement batch, 7-year retention archival. Spring Scheduler for enrollment deadline reminders.
backend.failure_handling: RMA API circuit breaker with cached fallback rate tables. Idempotent enrollment submissions keyed on farm unit plus policy year. Optimistic locking on policy status transitions.

frontend.app_structure: Next.js 14 app router with RSC for enrollment wizard steps. Client components for interactive premium calculator and claim status tracker.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Agent Dashboard (book of business overview), Enrollment Wizard (multi-step farm unit entry, coverage election, RMA rate display, producer approval), Claim Intake Form, Loss Adjuster Workspace, Admin Compliance Reports.
frontend.state_management: React Query for policy and claim data fetching with optimistic updates. Zustand for multi-step enrollment wizard state across browser sessions.
frontend.performance_notes: Server-side rendering for enrollment wizard initial load to reduce time-to-interactive during peak enrollment. Stale-while-revalidate for actuarial rate displays cached at CDN edge.

security.auth_design: Azure AD B2C with custom user flows for agent and policyholder roles. MFA enforced for agent and admin roles via Conditional Access policy.
security.authorization_model: Spring Security method-level @PreAuthorize annotations enforcing agent book-of-business scoping, adjuster assignment scoping, policyholder self-service isolation.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Append-only policy_audit_log table capturing every premium calculation input, output, and user ID. Application Insights for API access logs. Azure Key Vault access logs for secret retrieval events.
security.security_risks: Agent credential sharing risk mitigated by MFA and per-session audit logging. RMA API key exposure risk mitigated by Azure Key Vault with managed identity.

data.entities: policy_enrollments (id, farm_unit_id FK, policy_year, coverage_type, actuarial_rate, premium_amount NUMERIC(15,4), status ENUM pending/approved/bound/cancelled, agent_id, created_at). farm_units (id, policyholder_id FK, fsa_tract_number, crop_type, planted_acres, county_code, practice_type). indemnity_claims (id, enrollment_id FK, loss_event_date, peril_type, reported_losses_acres, adjuster_id FK, status ENUM open/inspected/approved/paid, settlement_amount NUMERIC(15,4)). actuarial_rate_cache (id, crop_type, county_code, coverage_level, rate NUMERIC(10,6), rma_effective_date, cached_at).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server with read replica for reporting queries. Redis for enrollment wizard session state and RMA rate cache hot path. Blob storage for loss adjuster photo attachments.
data.retention_and_deletion: 7-year hard retention for policy and claim records per USDA RMA. Automated archival to Azure Blob cold tier after year 3. Audit logs retained 5 years in separate append-only schema.

devops.deployment_topology: AKS eastus2 with 3 node pools: system (2 nodes), api (min 3, max 15 during enrollment surge), batch (min 1, max 5). HPA on CPU 60% target. PgBouncer sidecar for connection pooling.
devops.environments: dev (single-replica), staging (multi-AZ mirror of prod), production (multi-AZ, pre-scaled before enrollment window).
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps pipelines: lint + test (80% coverage gate) -> Docker build -> push ACR -> Helm chart deploy to AKS via ArgoCD. Separate pipeline for Spring Batch job container.
devops.observability_stack: Application Insights APM on all pods. Azure Monitor alerts on enrollment submission rate drop, premium calculation error rate, claim routing failures. PagerDuty escalation during enrollment window.
devops.cost_controls: Reserved AKS node baseline. Burst to spot instances for batch jobs. PostgreSQL read replica paused outside reporting hours. Enrollment surge budget pre-approved with auto-alerts at 120% of forecast.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a high-scale crop insurance platform on Azure with Java 21 Spring Boot to handle spring enrollment surge. Key decisions: Spring Batch for nightly RMA reconciliation jobs, BigDecimal throughout premium calculation engine to meet USDA RMA precision requirements, AKS HPA with pre-scaled baseline for predictable seasonal surge, PgBouncer for connection pool protection, and local actuarial rate cache to decouple enrollment from USDA RMA API availability.
target_output.title: Crop Insurance Management Platform Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A high-scale fullstack application enabling licensed crop insurance agents and agricultural lenders to manage policy enrollments, USDA RMA actuarial rate lookups, premium calculations, and indemnity claim workflows for thousands of farm policy holders. Built on Next.js 14 and Java 21 Spring Boot on AKS with PostgreSQL 16, the platform pre-scales for spring enrollment surges, enforces SOC 2 and USDA RMA AIP compliance controls, and runs nightly Spring Batch jobs for premium reconciliation and rate table refresh.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-tier AKS-hosted architecture: Next.js 14 frontend served via Azure CDN communicating over HTTPS to a Spring Boot REST API behind an Azure Application Gateway with WAF. The API layer is organized into enrollment, premium, claim, adjuster, and batch modules sharing a PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server via PgBouncer connection pooler. Redis 7 caches enrollment wizard sessions and the nightly-refreshed USDA RMA actuarial rate tables. Spring Batch containers run nightly reconciliation and RMA rate refresh jobs as separate AKS workloads. Azure AD B2C handles authentication with role-based claims enforced at the Spring Security method layer. All financial data encrypted at rest via Azure Disk Encryption with Key Vault-managed keys.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui, React Query, Zustand. Backend: Java 21, Spring Boot 3.2, Spring Security, Spring Batch, Spring Data JPA, BigDecimal for actuarial arithmetic. Database: PostgreSQL 16 Azure Flexible Server with read replica, PgBouncer connection pooler. Cache: Azure Cache for Redis 7. Infrastructure: AKS eastus2, Azure Container Registry, Azure Application Gateway with WAF, Azure CDN, Azure Key Vault, Azure Blob Storage for adjuster attachments. CI/CD: Azure DevOps and ArgoCD.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Enrollment Wizard: multi-step farm unit entry (FSA tract number, crop type, planted acres, county), coverage level election, live USDA RMA actuarial rate display from cache, premium calculation with BigDecimal precision, and producer e-signature approval flow. Premium Calculation Engine: applies RMA rate tables to farm unit coverage elections, computes producer and government premium shares, stores results to policy_enrollments with full audit trail. Claim Intake: loss event reporting by agent or policyholder, automatic adjuster assignment by county and crop type, inspection result recording with photo attachments. Admin Compliance Reporting: SOC 2 audit trail exports, RMA enrollment summary reports, licensed agent activity reports.
target_output.system_components: Azure Application Gateway (WAF, TLS termination, routing), AKS api node pool (Spring Boot pods, HPA min 3 max 15), AKS batch node pool (Spring Batch job pods, min 1 max 5), PgBouncer connection pooler sidecar, PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server primary and read replica, Azure Cache for Redis 7 (session and rate cache), Azure Blob Storage (adjuster photo attachments, archived policy documents), Azure AD B2C (auth), Azure Key Vault (secrets), Azure CDN and Next.js frontend, Application Insights (APM), PagerDuty (on-call).
target_output.workflows: Enrollment Workflow: Agent initiates enrollment for a farm unit in the wizard, system fetches matching actuarial rates from Redis cache (populated nightly from USDA RMA API), premium engine calculates producer premium share using BigDecimal, agent submits for policyholder approval, policyholder reviews and e-signs, policy status transitions to 'bound', enrollment event written to policy_audit_log. Claim Workflow: Agent or policyholder reports a loss event specifying peril type and affected acres, claim record created with status 'open', routing engine assigns nearest available adjuster by county-crop matrix, adjuster records field measurements and uploads photos to Blob Storage, settlement amount computed by claims engine, approved claim triggers Stripe Treasury disbursement to policyholder bank account.
target_output.data_model: farm_units (id UUID PK, policyholder_id FK, fsa_tract_number VARCHAR(20) UNIQUE, crop_type VARCHAR(50), planted_acres NUMERIC(10,2), county_code CHAR(5), practice_type ENUM irrigated/non_irrigated/organic). policy_enrollments (id UUID PK, farm_unit_id FK, policy_year SMALLINT, coverage_type ENUM rp/rphpe/yp/aph, actuarial_rate NUMERIC(10,6), premium_amount NUMERIC(15,4), producer_premium_share NUMERIC(15,4), status ENUM pending/approved/bound/cancelled, agent_id FK, bound_at TIMESTAMPTZ). indemnity_claims (id UUID PK, enrollment_id FK, loss_event_date DATE, peril_type ENUM drought/flood/hail/freeze/prevented_planting, reported_loss_acres NUMERIC(10,2), adjuster_id FK, status ENUM open/inspected/approved/paid/denied, settlement_amount NUMERIC(15,4)). actuarial_rate_cache (id UUID PK, crop_type, county_code, coverage_level NUMERIC(4,2), rate NUMERIC(10,6), rma_effective_date DATE, cached_at TIMESTAMPTZ). policy_audit_log (id UUID PK, enrollment_id, user_id, action VARCHAR(100), old_value JSONB, new_value JSONB, recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: RESTful API at /api/v1 with OpenAPI 3.1 spec. POST /enrollments (create enrollment from farm unit), GET /enrollments/:id (enrollment detail with premium breakdown), PATCH /enrollments/:id/approve (policyholder approval), POST /claims (open loss event claim), GET /claims?adjuster_id=X&status=open (adjuster queue), PATCH /claims/:id/inspection (record field inspection results), POST /claims/:id/settle (trigger settlement computation and disbursement), GET /rate-tables?crop=CORN&county=17019 (fetch cached RMA actuarial rates), GET /admin/audit-log (SOC 2 audit export). All endpoints require Azure AD B2C JWT; Spring Security method-level authorization enforces booking scoping.
target_output.security_and_compliance: USDA RMA AIP compliance: agent license number stored and validated on enrollment submission; actuarial rate provenance logged per policy. SOC 2 Type II controls: append-only policy_audit_log with user_id and action for every premium calculation and claim status change; Azure Key Vault for all secrets with managed identity access; Application Insights capturing API access patterns for audit review. Azure AD B2C MFA enforced for agent and admin roles via Conditional Access. Spring Security @PreAuthorize annotations enforce agent book-of-business scoping and adjuster assignment scoping. Amount validation with dual-approval for settlements above configurable threshold; disbursement receipts archived to Blob Storage.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: AKS eastus2 with three node pools: system (2xStandard_D2s_v3), api (min 3 max 15, Standard_D4s_v3, HPA on CPU 60%), batch (min 1 max 5, spot instances for cost). ArgoCD GitOps for Helm chart deployments from Azure DevOps pipeline. Deployment strategy: rolling update with 30s readiness probe before traffic cut. Pre-enrollment surge runbook: scale api pool to 8 replicas 48 hours before enrollment opens, verify PgBouncer pool limits, warm Redis rate cache, run load test against staging. PgBouncer in transaction mode, max_client_conn=1000, default_pool_size=20. Database migrations via Flyway run as AKS init container before API pod starts. Rollback: ArgoCD sync to previous Helm revision.
target_output.observability: Application Insights APM on all Spring Boot pods: distributed tracing across enrollment and claim service calls, JVM heap and GC metrics, Spring Batch job execution histograms. Custom metrics: enrollment_submissions_per_minute, premium_calculation_errors_total, claim_routing_queue_depth, rma_rate_cache_age_hours. Azure Monitor alert rules: enrollment submission rate drops below 50/min during surge window (pager), premium calculation error rate exceeds 0.1% (pager), claim routing queue depth exceeds 200 (warning), PostgreSQL connection pool saturation above 85% (pager). PagerDuty on-call rotation active during 6-week enrollment window.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Baseline AKS spend approximately $2,800/month (3 api pods plus system nodes plus batch standby). Enrollment surge peak (6 weeks, max 15 api pods): approximately $5,200/month. PostgreSQL Flexible Server with read replica: approximately $800/month. Redis and CDN: approximately $200/month. Annual blended approximately $38,000 with reserved instance discounts on baseline nodes. Auto-scaling cost alerts at 120% of monthly forecast trigger Slack notification to platform team.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (12 weeks): enrollment wizard with USDA RMA rate fetch and local cache, premium calculation engine with BigDecimal, producer e-signature approval, basic claim intake with adjuster assignment, Azure AD B2C auth with MFA, AKS deployment with HPA, Application Insights baseline, SOC 2 audit logging. Phase 2 (8 weeks post-MVP): loss adjuster mobile-ready inspection workspace with photo uploads, Stripe Treasury indemnity disbursements, nightly premium reconciliation Spring Batch job, admin compliance reporting dashboard. Phase 3 (ongoing): satellite yield data integration for automated loss estimation, agent license verification API integration with state insurance departments.
target_output.development_guidelines: Java 21 with strict Checkstyle and SpotBugs in CI. All premium and indemnity calculations use BigDecimal with HALF_EVEN rounding; no double arithmetic permitted in financial modules. Spring Batch job tests use embedded PostgreSQL via Testcontainers. 80% line coverage gate in Azure DevOps pipeline. Frontend TypeScript strict mode. React Query mutation hooks must include optimistic rollback for enrollment wizard steps. Feature flags via Azure App Configuration for gradual rollout of claim settlement engine.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: USDA RMA API unavailable during enrollment surge causing rate fetch failures. Mitigation: nightly actuarial_rate_cache refresh with Redis fallback; enrollment blocked only if cache age exceeds 48 hours. Risk: PgBouncer misconfiguration during surge causing connection exhaustion. Mitigation: load test against staging with 15-pod API fleet before enrollment opens; PgBouncer pool size validated against Flexible Server max_connections. Tradeoff: Spring Boot monolith over microservices for MVP velocity -- module boundaries are maintained in code to allow future service extraction of the premium engine and claims service.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Azure AD B2C over Okta for native AKS Managed Identity integration. Confirmed Stripe Treasury for indemnity disbursements over ACH direct due to faster settlement timeline. Chosen PgBouncer in transaction mode over session mode to support horizontal pod scaling. BigDecimal confirmed as mandatory for all monetary fields after evaluating IEEE 754 double precision rounding failures in actuarial rate multiplication.

metadata.notes: Crop insurance management platform with USDA RMA compliance, Spring Batch reconciliation, and pre-scaled AKS for seasonal enrollment surge.

project_goal.value: Marketing landing page for a vertical farming startup launching a subscription produce delivery service in urban markets, communicating sustainability story, showcasing the growing facility, and capturing email waitlist signups
project_goal.rationale: Pre-launch waitlist page must establish brand credibility and drive email capture before full e-commerce launch

target_users.rationale: Target demographic identified through brand positioning research

capabilities.rationale: Static content delivery with Plausible analytics for conversion tracking; no auth, payments, or backend needed

complexity_level.rationale: Static page with one external form integration and analytics embed

risk_level.rationale: No user data stored on-site; email captures handled by Mailchimp; no authentication required

data_sensitivity.rationale: Landing page stores no data; email addresses flow directly to Mailchimp and are not retained on-site

external_exposure.rationale: Pre-launch marketing page must be publicly accessible with no authentication barrier

access_model.value: Fully public; no authentication; admin access to Netlify dashboard via SSO
access_model.rationale: Public marketing content requires zero friction for visitors

security_baseline.value: HTTPS enforced via Netlify; Content Security Policy headers; no cookies except Plausible first-party analytics
security_baseline.rationale: GDPR-friendly analytics via Plausible avoids cookie consent banner; CSP prevents XSS on static page

frontend_stack.value: Astro with Tailwind CSS
frontend_stack.rationale: Astro produces zero JS by default for static sections; Tailwind for rapid brand-consistent styling

backend_stack.value: None; Netlify Functions for waitlist form handler only
backend_stack.rationale: Static site with single serverless function for Mailchimp API integration; no persistent server needed

data_platform.value: None on-site; Mailchimp for email list management
data_platform.rationale: No database required; email list managed entirely within Mailchimp

hosting_target.value: Netlify CDN with global edge network
hosting_target.rationale: Netlify deploy previews for A/B variant review; instant rollback; free tier sufficient for pre-launch traffic

privacy_retention_policy.value: No data retained on-site; Mailchimp list subject to CAN-SPAM unsubscribe; GDPR opt-in checkbox for EU visitors

future_scope.value: Full e-commerce subscription storefront, member portal, delivery area map, recurring payment integration
future_scope.rationale: Landing page is pre-launch; full storefront in separate project post-funding

constraints.value: Launch within 3 weeks of design handoff; brand guidelines must be followed precisely; no cookie consent banner
constraints.rationale: Time-sensitive pre-launch; Plausible cookieless analytics avoids GDPR consent requirement

observability_baseline.value: Plausible Analytics for page views, waitlist form conversions, and A/B variant performance
observability_baseline.rationale: Cookieless Plausible is GDPR-compliant and sufficient for conversion funnel tracking on a static page

execution_preference.value: Design-first with component builds in parallel; deploy previews for client review before merge
execution_preference.rationale: Visual brand review requires deploy previews; parallel component builds meet 3-week timeline

frontend.images: Astro Image component with WebP optimization for facility photos

devops.ci: GitHub Actions for build and lint checks
