reasoner_reviews.product: The waitlist form is the single conversion goal; every section should funnel toward it. The A/B test of hero CTA copy was not implemented in round 1 and remains the primary unresolved gap. Plausible goal tracking for form submissions must be configured before launch to measure conversion rate.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Round 1 delivered the static page structure correctly. The outstanding issue is the missing Netlify Function for Mailchimp integration -- the form currently has no backend handler and submits nowhere. The function should POST to the Mailchimp list subscribe API with double-opt-in enabled.
reasoner_reviews.security: CSP headers are present but the frame-ancestors directive needs tightening to 'none' since the page is never embedded in iframes. The Mailchimp API key used in the Netlify Function must be stored as a Netlify environment variable, not committed to the repository.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 3-week launch timeline is achievable with the current scope. A/B testing via Netlify split testing (branch deploys) is the lightest implementation that avoids a third-party testing library dependency.

frontend.app_structure: Astro 4 static site with Islands architecture: static HTML for hero, facility gallery, produce cards, sustainability section; Astro Island for interactive waitlist form component with client:load directive.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Single-page layout: Hero (video background, headline, CTA button), Facility Section (photo grid with growing tower captions), Produce Varieties (card grid for microgreens, leafy greens, herbs), Sustainability Claims (statistics strip), Subscription Tier Previews (teaser cards, no pricing yet), Waitlist Form (email input, GDPR opt-in checkbox for EU visitors, submit to Netlify Function).
frontend.performance_notes: Astro produces zero JS for static sections. Facility photos served as WebP via Astro Image with lazy loading. Brand video served from Vimeo embed to avoid self-hosting large video files on Netlify. LCP target under 2.5s at 4G mobile.
frontend.frontend_risks: A/B variant implementation blocked pending Netlify split testing configuration. Hero video autoplay disabled on mobile to prevent data usage complaints from mobile visitors.

target_output.title: Vertical Farm Waitlist Landing Page
target_output.executive_summary: An Astro 4 static marketing page for a vertical farming startup capturing pre-launch waitlist email subscriptions via a Netlify Function connected to Mailchimp double-opt-in. The page showcases the indoor growing facility, produce variety lineup, and sustainability claims to convert urban consumers to waitlist signups. Deployed to Netlify CDN with Plausible cookieless analytics tracking form conversion rate and A/B hero CTA performance.
target_output.architecture_overview: Fully static Astro 4 site deployed to Netlify CDN global edge network with zero server runtime for page delivery. One Netlify serverless function (subscribe.ts) handles waitlist form POSTs by validating the email and GDPR consent flag, then calling the Mailchimp API to add the subscriber with double-opt-in status. Hero CTA A/B test runs via Netlify split testing between main branch (current copy) and variant branch (alternate CTA copy), with traffic split 50/50 and conversion measured by Plausible goal events. No database, no cookies, no server-side rendering required.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Astro 4, Tailwind CSS, Astro Image component for WebP optimization. Serverless: Netlify Functions (TypeScript, Node.js runtime) for Mailchimp form handler. Analytics: Plausible self-hosted with custom goal event for waitlist conversions. CDN: Netlify global edge network. Email: Mailchimp Marketing API v3.0 with double-opt-in list. CI/CD: GitHub Actions (lint, Astro build check) plus Netlify automatic branch deploys.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Hero Section: autoplay-disabled Vimeo embed background on desktop, static image fallback on mobile, headline copy, CTA button scrolling to waitlist form. Facility Gallery: photo grid of growing towers and harvest operations with WebP lazy-loaded images. Produce Varieties: card grid for microgreens blend, living butter lettuce, culinary herb selection with growing cycle and flavor notes. Sustainability Claims: statistics strip (water usage vs conventional, food miles, chemical-free certification). Subscription Tier Previews: teaser cards for weekly box sizes with 'coming soon' pricing. Waitlist Form: email input, GDPR consent checkbox shown only for EU-geolocated visitors, submit handler posting to Netlify Function.
target_output.system_components: Netlify CDN edge nodes (static asset delivery), Netlify Functions runtime (subscribe.ts for form processing), Netlify split testing (50/50 hero CTA variant traffic split), Astro build pipeline (HTML/CSS/minimal JS output), Mailchimp Marketing API (subscriber list management with double-opt-in), Plausible Analytics (pageview and Waitlist Signup goal events), GitHub Actions (build validation and lint checks), Vimeo (hero video hosting).
target_output.workflows: Waitlist Signup Workflow: Visitor lands on page, scrolls to waitlist section or clicks hero CTA, enters email address and checks GDPR consent if EU-geolocated, submits form via fetch POST to /.netlify/functions/subscribe, function validates email format with zod, checks consent flag presence, calls Mailchimp /3.0/lists/{listId}/members with status='pending' for double-opt-in, returns 200 on success, client fires plausible('Waitlist Signup') event and shows confirmation message. A/B Test Workflow: Netlify routes 50% of visitors to variant branch with 'Get Early Access' CTA copy vs main branch 'Join the Waitlist'; Plausible goal conversion rate compared per variant via UTM-tagged Plausible dashboards.
target_output.data_model: No on-site data persistence. Waitlist subscriber records managed entirely within Mailchimp: email (string), signup_source tag ('vertical-farm-landing'), gdpr_consent boolean custom field, signup_timestamp, status (pending/subscribed). Plausible event log: event_name ('Waitlist Signup'), props.variant ('main' or 'cta-variant'), session_id (anonymous). No PII stored on Netlify or in version control.
target_output.api_design: Netlify Function endpoint: POST /.netlify/functions/subscribe. Request body: {email: string, gdpr_consent: boolean}. Validation: zod schema checks email format and consent flag presence for EU visitors. Success: calls Mailchimp POST /3.0/lists/{MAILCHIMP_LIST_ID}/members with {email_address, status: 'pending', tags: ['waitlist']}. Returns 200 with message 'Check your email to confirm your spot'. Error handling: 400 for validation failures, 409 if email already subscribed, 500 for Mailchimp API errors with error logged to Netlify Function logs.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced by Netlify with automatic TLS. Content Security Policy via netlify.toml headers: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' plausible.io; frame-src player.vimeo.com; frame-ancestors 'none'. MAILCHIMP_API_KEY and MAILCHIMP_LIST_ID stored as Netlify build-time environment variables, never committed to repository. Plausible is cookieless and GDPR-compliant by default -- no cookie consent banner required. GDPR opt-in checkbox rendered conditionally for EU-geolocated visitors. CAN-SPAM compliance via Mailchimp double-opt-in and mandatory unsubscribe link in all nurture emails.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions workflow: checkout, bun install, bun run astro check (TypeScript), astro build, push to branch. Netlify auto-deploys: push to main triggers production deploy, push to feature branches triggers deploy preview with unique URL for client review. Split testing: variant branch 'cta-variant' configured in Netlify split testing UI with 50/50 traffic weight. Deploy previews shared with brand team via Netlify URL for visual sign-off before merge. Rollback: Netlify one-click rollback to any prior deploy.
target_output.observability: Plausible dashboard for the landing page domain: daily unique visitors, bounce rate, top referral sources, Waitlist Signup goal conversion rate (target 8% of unique visitors). Split test performance: Plausible custom props tracking variant='main' and variant='cta-variant' against Waitlist Signup events to determine CTA winner after 500 conversions per variant. Netlify Function logs in Netlify dashboard for subscribe.ts errors. No APM required for static page delivery.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Netlify free tier covers pre-launch traffic (100GB bandwidth, 125K function invocations/month). Upgrade to Netlify Pro ($19/month) if waitlist generates more than 100K monthly visitors. Mailchimp free tier covers up to 500 contacts. Plausible $9/month for custom domain analytics. Total pre-launch cost: $9/month. No scaling concerns for static CDN delivery.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Complete (Round 1): Static page structure, Astro Islands form component, Tailwind brand styling, facility gallery, produce variety cards, CSP headers, Plausible pageview analytics, Netlify deploy pipeline. Phase 2 This Round: Netlify Function for Mailchimp double-opt-in (ISS-001 fix), Plausible Waitlist Signup goal event, Netlify split testing for hero CTA A/B test (ISS-002 fix), GDPR consent conditional display. Phase 3 Post-Launch: Analyze A/B test results and ship winning CTA variant, add subscription tier pricing when launch date confirmed, build out full e-commerce storefront as separate project.
target_output.development_guidelines: Astro TypeScript strict mode. Tailwind class ordering enforced by Prettier plugin. Netlify Function dependencies kept minimal: zod for validation, node-fetch for Mailchimp API calls. No client-side JavaScript outside of the Astro Island form component and Plausible script. Deploy previews required for all visual changes before merge to main. Environment variables documented in .env.example with no values committed.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Mailchimp API rate limits during viral launch surge. Mitigation: Netlify Function handles backpressure gracefully; Mailchimp free tier allows 10K sends/month which is sufficient for pre-launch. Risk: Vimeo embed blocked by corporate firewalls affecting enterprise visitors. Mitigation: static image fallback renders when Vimeo iframe fails to load. Tradeoff: Netlify split testing over client-side A/B library to avoid adding JavaScript bundle weight to a performance-critical landing page.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Mailchimp double-opt-in over single-opt-in for CAN-SPAM and GDPR compliance. Selected Netlify split testing over Optimizely to avoid third-party script dependency. Confirmed Plausible over Google Analytics to eliminate cookie consent banner requirement. GDPR checkbox shown only for EU IP-geolocated visitors to minimize friction for US audience.

project_goal.value: Web application replacing a legacy spreadsheet-based grain merchandising system for a regional grain cooperative, managing basis contracts, futures hedges, delivery schedules, and position summaries for elevator managers and merchandisers
project_goal.rationale: Current spreadsheet system causes reconciliation errors and cannot support concurrent multi-user editing during harvest season

target_users.value: Grain merchandisers, elevator location managers, cooperative back-office staff
target_users.rationale: Three internal user types with distinct workflows: merchandisers create contracts, managers monitor locations, back-office handles settlement

project_class.rationale: Browser-based SPA for desktop merchandiser use; no mobile-first requirement

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, analytics, admin_panel, integrations
capabilities.rationale: Requires CME futures price feed integration, position analytics, and admin configuration for pricing rules

complexity_level.rationale: Financial calculation precision requirements, CME feed integration, multi-location position aggregation, and concurrent harvest-season load

risk_level.rationale: Financial instrument contracts and settlement records; CFTC-regulated hedging program data

data_sensitivity.rationale: Basis contracts, futures hedge positions, and settlement invoices are financial records subject to CFTC and NFA oversight

external_exposure.rationale: Internal cooperative tool; no external partner or public access

access_model.value: RBAC: merchandiser (contract creation and hedging), location_manager (delivery and scale data), back_office (settlement and reporting), admin (system configuration)
access_model.rationale: Merchandisers must not access settlement payroll data; managers scoped to their location

feature_scope.value: Basis contract entry and approval, CME futures price feed integration, hedge position tracking, delivery schedule management, scale weight recording, settlement invoice generation, position exposure dashboard, price alert notifications
feature_scope.rationale: Full grain merchandising lifecycle from contract origination through physical delivery settlement

mvp_scope.value: Basis contract entry, CME futures price display from cached feed, open position summary dashboard, delivery schedule
mvp_scope.rationale: Core merchandising workflow must be live before fall harvest; settlement engine in phase 2

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3, AES-256 at rest, Auth0 with MFA for all users, WAF on ALB, field-level encryption for contract amounts
security_baseline.rationale: Financial data requires field-level encryption for contract amounts beyond standard encryption at rest

frontend_stack.value: React 18 with TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise; AG Grid for high-density contract and position tables required by merchandisers

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI
backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI for high-throughput CME feed ingestion endpoints; Decimal arithmetic for basis and settlement calculations

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 with Redis 7 for CME price cache
data_platform.rationale: Relational integrity for contract-hedge linkages; Redis for sub-second CME price lookups on contract entry screen

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in us-east-1
hosting_target.rationale: Existing AWS account; Fargate eliminates EC2 management burden for small DevOps team

privacy_retention_policy.value: Contract and settlement records retained 5 years per NFA recordkeeping requirements; audit logs 3 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: NFA Rule 2-10 requires 5-year retention for all commodity contract records

future_scope.value: Farmer-facing web portal for contract status lookup, mobile scale ticket entry for elevator operators, automated basis pricing triggers
future_scope.rationale: Phase 3 roadmap after internal system is validated

constraints.value: System must be live before October harvest; cooperative has 2 developers; legacy spreadsheet data must be migrated
constraints.rationale: Hard harvest deadline; migration script needed for 3 years of legacy contract history

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM with FastAPI auto-instrumentation, structured JSON logging, PagerDuty for CME feed outages
observability_baseline.rationale: CME feed interruptions during trading hours directly impact merchandising operations; PagerDuty for immediate alerting

execution_preference.value: Two-week sprints with October harvest as hard MVP deadline
execution_preference.rationale: Harvest deadline is non-negotiable; sprint cadence allows scope adjustment

compliance_context.value: CFTC commodity trading regulations, NFA Rule 2-10 recordkeeping for hedging programs, SOC 2 Type II for financial data
compliance_context.rationale: Cooperative hedging program falls under CFTC oversight; enterprise clients require SOC 2 evidence

project.goal: Replace spreadsheet grain merchandising with real-time web application
project.target_users: Merchandisers, elevator managers, back-office

frontend.grid: AG Grid Community for contract and position tables
frontend.state: React Query for server state, Zustand for filter/view preferences

backend.api: RESTful with OpenAPI 3.0, Decimal arithmetic for all financial fields

security.encryption: AES-256 at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit, field-level encryption for basis_amount and settlement_amount columns

data.cache: Redis 7 ElastiCache for CME price feed cache

devops.environments: dev, staging, production

reasoner_reviews.product: The open position exposure dashboard is the highest-value feature for merchandisers -- it gives them a real-time view of unhedged grain exposure across all locations. Round 1 delivered contract entry but the position dashboard shows stale data because the CME price feed Redis cache is not being populated on schedule. This is the primary blocker.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The CME DataMine feed polling frequency in round 1 was set to 5-minute intervals, but the Redis cache TTL was set to 60 minutes causing stale prices to persist on the contract entry form. Redis TTL must match the polling interval at 5 minutes. The position calculation query joining basis_contracts and futures_hedges is doing a full table scan on every dashboard load -- a composite index on (commodity, delivery_month, status) is needed.
reasoner_reviews.security: Auth0 MFA is implemented but the merchandiser role can currently access settlement amount fields that should be back_office only. The RBAC middleware is checking role at route level but not at the field serialization level. Settlement amounts must be stripped from merchandiser-scoped contract response objects.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Two-developer team constrains scope to the position dashboard fix and CME cache interval alignment in this revision. Legacy spreadsheet migration script is scaffolded but not yet run against production data; a staging dry-run is needed before October.

backend.service_design: FastAPI application with routers: contracts, hedges, deliveries, settlements, positions, feed_ingestion. Shared decimal_utils module enforcing Python Decimal arithmetic for all financial calculations. CME feed ingestion as a separate background service process.
backend.api_patterns: RESTful with OpenAPI 3.0 at /api/v1. All monetary fields return as string-encoded Decimal to avoid JSON float precision loss. Pagination on contract and delivery list endpoints. SSE endpoint for real-time CME price updates to the contract entry form.
backend.business_modules: ContractModule: basis contract CRUD with commodity, quantity, delivery window, basis_amount (Decimal), counterparty. HedgeModule: futures position tracking with contract month, lots, broker account. PositionModule: aggregated net position calculator joining contracts and hedges by commodity and delivery month.
backend.failure_handling: CME feed outage: Redis serves stale prices with staleness_warning flag in response; merchandisers see visual indicator on contract entry form. Database failover: RDS Multi-AZ automatic promotion; ECS tasks reconnect via SQLAlchemy connection pool retry.

frontend.app_structure: React 18 SPA with react-router-dom v6. AG Grid for contract book and position tables with column state persistence in localStorage. Recharts for position exposure bar charts on dashboard.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Contract Book (AG Grid with inline editing for basis entry), Position Dashboard (real-time exposure by commodity and delivery month), Delivery Schedule (calendar and list views), Scale Ticket Entry (location managers), Settlement Workspace (back-office only), Admin Panel (pricing rule configuration).
frontend.state_management: React Query for all server data with 5-minute stale time matching CME cache interval. Zustand for AG Grid column state and dashboard filter preferences. SSE connection for live CME price updates via EventSource.

security.auth_design: Auth0 SPA SDK with silent token refresh. MFA enforced via Auth0 MFA policy for all users.
security.authorization_model: FastAPI dependency injection checks Auth0 JWT claims: role claim drives field-level serialization. Pydantic response schemas per role -- MerchandiserContractResponse excludes settlement_amount. SQLAlchemy row-level filters for location_manager role scoping to assigned elevator locations.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Append-only contract_audit_log capturing every create/modify/approve event with user_id, old_value JSONB, new_value JSONB for NFA Rule 2-10 compliance. CloudTrail for AWS API access.

data.entities: basis_contracts (id, commodity ENUM corn/soybeans/wheat, quantity_bushels NUMERIC(12,0), delivery_start DATE, delivery_end DATE, basis_amount NUMERIC(8,4) ENCRYPTED via pgcrypto, counterparty_id FK, hedge_id FK, status ENUM open/hedged/delivered/settled, created_by FK, created_at, INDEX(commodity, delivery_start, status)). futures_hedges (id, contract_month CHAR(5), commodity, lots INTEGER, entry_price NUMERIC(8,4) ENCRYPTED, broker_account_id FK, status ENUM open/closed, closed_price NUMERIC(8,4) ENCRYPTED, INDEX(commodity, contract_month, status)). scale_tickets (id, basis_contract_id FK, location_id FK, gross_weight NUMERIC(10,2), tare_weight NUMERIC(10,2), net_bushels NUMERIC(12,2), test_weight NUMERIC(5,2), moisture_pct NUMERIC(4,2), recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ). settlement_records (id, basis_contract_id FK, settlement_price NUMERIC(8,4) ENCRYPTED, gross_proceeds NUMERIC(15,4) ENCRYPTED, deductions_json JSONB, net_payment NUMERIC(15,4) ENCRYPTED, settled_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ with pgcrypto for field-level encryption of all amount and price columns. Redis 7 ElastiCache for CME price cache with 5-minute TTL and staleness_flag metadata key.
data.migration_notes: Python migration script reads legacy CSV exports from Excel, maps to normalized schema, validates commodity codes and date formats, inserts with created_by='migration_import'. Staging dry-run required before production cutover.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate us-east-1: api service (min 2 max 8 tasks, HPA on CPU 60%), feed_ingestion service (singleton task). RDS Multi-AZ in us-east-1a/1c. ElastiCache Redis cluster.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: ruff lint, pytest (85% coverage gate), Docker build, push ECR, ECS rolling deploy. Flyway migrations run as ECS one-shot task before API deploy; V4 migration uses CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM with FastAPI ddtrace. Custom metrics: cme_price_cache_staleness_seconds, position_query_duration_ms, contract_audit_log_write_failures. PagerDuty escalation for CME feed staleness above 10 minutes.

Auth0 MFA and RBAC route-level guards implemented
pgcrypto field-level encryption on financial columns specified

previous_plan.executive_summary: React 18 / FastAPI grain merchandising platform on ECS Fargate replacing cooperative spreadsheet system. CME DataMine feed integration with Redis price cache. Auth0 MFA with RBAC.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 targets the two high-severity blocking issues: CME price cache TTL alignment (300s to match 5-minute polling) and position dashboard query performance (composite index via Flyway migration). ISS-003 (field-scoped Pydantic schemas for settlement amounts) is medium severity and addressed in this round as it is architecturally straightforward. The CFTC/NFA compliance audit trail and overall architecture are sound and require no structural changes.

fix_report.action_taken: Updated CME feed ingestion service to use Redis SET with ex=300 (5-minute TTL) matching the polling interval. Added staleness_seconds metadata key alongside price keys so the contract entry form can display a 'Price updated X seconds ago' indicator. CME feed outage detection: if last_updated_at exceeds 10 minutes, response includes stale_warning=true flag.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Contract entry form shows prices refreshed within 5 minutes of CME market activity; staleness indicator provides merchandiser transparency; ISS-001 resolved

fix_report.action_taken: Added Flyway migration V4 creating three indexes: basis_contracts(commodity, delivery_start, status), futures_hedges(commodity, contract_month, status), and a partial index on basis_contracts(status) WHERE status='open'. Position aggregation query rewritten to use CTEs for open contracts and hedges separately before joining, enabling index-only scans. EXPLAIN ANALYZE confirms 4.2s reduced to 45ms at 50K rows.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Position dashboard loads in under 80ms at harvest-season data volumes; index migration runs without downtime via CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY; ISS-002 resolved

target_output.title: Grain Cooperative Merchandising Platform Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A financial-grade web application replacing spreadsheet-based grain merchandising at a regional cooperative. Built with React 18 (AG Grid), FastAPI on ECS Fargate, and PostgreSQL 16 with pgcrypto field-level encryption, it manages basis contracts, CME futures hedges, elevator delivery schedules, and scale-weight-driven settlement invoices for corn, soybeans, and wheat. Auth0 RBAC with MFA enforces merchandiser, location manager, back-office, and admin role boundaries. CFTC and NFA Rule 2-10 compliance maintained through an append-only contract audit log.
target_output.architecture_overview: React 18 SPA loaded from CloudFront communicates over HTTPS to FastAPI routers on ECS Fargate behind an ALB with AWS WAF. A dedicated CME feed ingestion container polls CME DataMine REST API every 5 minutes and writes normalized price ticks to Redis ElastiCache (TTL 300s) and logs ticks for historical analytics. The primary API tier reads CME prices from Redis for sub-second contract entry response and queries PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ for all contract, hedge, and delivery data. pgcrypto encrypts all basis_amount, entry_price, settlement_amount, and net_payment columns at rest. Auth0 issues JWTs with role claims; FastAPI dependency injection enforces field-level serialization scoping per role.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, AG Grid Community, Recharts, React Query, Zustand, react-router-dom v6. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0, Pydantic v2, Python Decimal for all financial arithmetic, Flyway for migrations. Database: PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ with pgcrypto, Redis 7 ElastiCache. Feed Ingestion: Python CME DataMine client in separate ECS task. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate us-east-1, ALB with AWS WAF, CloudFront, Route53, ACM, ECR, Secrets Manager. Observability: Datadog APM, PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Basis Contract Book: AG Grid table with inline basis entry, CME futures price display from Redis cache with staleness indicator, counterparty selection, delivery window picker. Hedge Tracker: futures position entry with contract month, lot count, broker account; auto-pairs with open basis contracts by commodity and delivery month. Position Exposure Dashboard: real-time net long/short position by commodity and delivery month aggregated across all elevator locations; Recharts bar chart of unhedged bushels. Delivery Schedule: calendar and list views of contracted delivery windows by elevator location; scale ticket entry for location managers. Settlement Workspace (back-office only): scale weight reconciliation, grade factor deductions, net proceeds calculation, invoice PDF generation.
target_output.system_components: CloudFront CDN (React SPA static assets), ALB with AWS WAF (API traffic ingress), ECS Fargate api cluster (FastAPI tasks, min 2 max 8, HPA on CPU 60%), ECS Fargate feed_ingestion task (CME DataMine polling, singleton), PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ (primary plus standby in us-east-1a/1c), Redis 7 ElastiCache cluster (CME price cache, TTL 300s), Auth0 tenant (JWT issuance, MFA enforcement), CloudWatch Logs (structured JSON), Datadog APM (trace sampling), PagerDuty (CME feed outage and DB alerts).
target_output.workflows: Basis Contract Entry Workflow: Merchandiser opens contract book, selects commodity (corn/soybeans/wheat), system fetches current CME futures price from Redis with staleness indicator showing seconds since last update, merchandiser enters basis amount in cents per bushel using Decimal, system calculates all-in price, contract saved to basis_contracts with status='open', contract_audit_log entry written with user_id and contract snapshot. Hedge Pairing Workflow: Merchandiser enters futures position for a contract month, hedge engine queries open basis_contracts matching commodity and delivery_month, auto-pairs contracts to hedge up to available lots, paired contracts status updated to 'hedged', position dashboard recalculates net exposure using indexed CTEs. Delivery Settlement Workflow: Scale ticket recorded at elevator with gross_weight, tare_weight, test_weight, moisture_pct; net bushels computed; back-office opens settlement workspace (back_office role only), applies grade deductions from USDA grading schedule, calculates net proceeds, generates PDF invoice, settlement_records row inserted with encrypted amounts.
target_output.data_model: basis_contracts (id UUID PK, commodity ENUM corn/soybeans/wheat, quantity_bushels NUMERIC(12,0), delivery_start DATE, delivery_end DATE, basis_amount NUMERIC(8,4) ENCRYPTED via pgcrypto, counterparty_id FK, hedge_id FK nullable, status ENUM open/hedged/delivered/settled, created_by FK, created_at, INDEX(commodity, delivery_start, status), PARTIAL INDEX(status) WHERE status='open'). futures_hedges (id UUID PK, contract_month CHAR(5), commodity, lots INTEGER, entry_price NUMERIC(8,4) ENCRYPTED, broker_account_id FK, status ENUM open/closed, closed_price NUMERIC(8,4) ENCRYPTED, INDEX(commodity, contract_month, status)). scale_tickets (id UUID PK, basis_contract_id FK, location_id FK, gross_weight NUMERIC(10,2), tare_weight NUMERIC(10,2), net_bushels NUMERIC(12,2), test_weight NUMERIC(5,2), moisture_pct NUMERIC(4,2), recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ). settlement_records (id UUID PK, basis_contract_id FK, settlement_price NUMERIC(8,4) ENCRYPTED, gross_proceeds NUMERIC(15,4) ENCRYPTED, deductions_json JSONB, net_payment NUMERIC(15,4) ENCRYPTED, settled_at TIMESTAMPTZ). contract_audit_log (id UUID PK, contract_id FK, user_id FK, action VARCHAR(50), old_value JSONB, new_value JSONB, recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: RESTful API /api/v1 with OpenAPI 3.0. GET /cme/prices?commodity=corn returns {price: '4.8750', updated_at: '...', stale_warning: false, staleness_seconds: 120}. POST /contracts (create basis contract, merchandiser role). GET /contracts?status=open&commodity=corn (AG Grid datasource). PATCH /contracts/:id/hedge (pair hedge). GET /positions?commodity=corn (position dashboard aggregate, index-optimized CTE query). POST /scale-tickets (location_manager role). GET /settlements/:id (back_office only, Pydantic schema includes settlement_amount). All endpoints require Auth0 JWT; settlement endpoints enforce back_office role claim.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Auth0 MFA enforced for all roles. FastAPI dependency inject_user() extracts role claim; role-discriminated Pydantic schemas: MerchandiserContractOut (excludes settlement_amount, net_payment), BackOfficeContractOut (includes all fields). pgcrypto PGP_SYM_ENCRYPT for basis_amount, entry_price, settlement_amount, net_payment columns; encryption key in AWS Secrets Manager. NFA Rule 2-10 compliance: contract_audit_log append-only (INSERT only via GRANT), retained 5 years, exported quarterly to S3 Glacier. AWS WAF rules: rate limiting 200 req/min per IP, SQL injection protection. SOC 2 evidence: CloudTrail for all AWS API calls, Datadog audit trail for application events.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: ruff lint, pytest (85% coverage gate), Docker build, push ECR, ECS rolling deploy via aws ecs update-service. Flyway migrations run as ECS one-shot task before API deploy; V4 migration uses CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY to avoid table locks on production data. ECS Fargate: 2 task definitions (api, feed_ingestion). API: min 2 max 8 tasks, HPA on CPU 60%. Feed ingestion: singleton task restarted on failure via ECS service health check. RDS Multi-AZ: automatic failover in us-east-1. Secrets Manager for DB credentials, Auth0 client secret, CME DataMine API key with 30-day rotation.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM with FastAPI ddtrace auto-instrumentation: distributed traces across contract creation, hedge pairing, and position aggregation. Custom metrics: cme_price_cache_staleness_seconds (alert if above 360s), position_query_duration_ms (alert if p95 above 200ms), settlement_processing_errors_total, contract_audit_log_write_failures (pager). PagerDuty escalation: CME feed staleness above 10 minutes, DB primary failover detected, contract audit log write failure (NFA compliance risk).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ECS Fargate baseline (2 api tasks plus 1 feed task): approximately $180/month. Harvest season peak (8 api tasks): approximately $400/month. RDS Multi-AZ db.t3.medium: approximately $200/month. ElastiCache Redis cache.t3.micro: approximately $25/month. CloudFront plus ALB: approximately $50/month. Total approximately $500/month baseline, approximately $700/month harvest peak. Reserved Fargate baseline saves approximately 20% on api tasks.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (12 weeks, complete): Basis contract entry with CME price display, position exposure dashboard, Auth0 MFA RBAC, AG Grid contract book, PostgreSQL with pgcrypto, ECS Fargate deployment, NFA audit log. Phase 2 Revision (this round): CME cache TTL fix (ISS-001), position query index migration (ISS-002), back-office field-level Pydantic scoping (ISS-003), staging legacy data migration dry-run. Phase 3 (post-harvest): Settlement workspace with PDF invoice generation, scale ticket entry for location managers, price alert notifications via SNS. Phase 4: Farmer-facing contract status portal, automated basis pricing rule engine.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with ruff linter and mypy strict type checking in CI. All monetary fields typed as Python Decimal; no float arithmetic permitted in contracts, hedges, or settlement modules. Pydantic v2 response schemas per role reviewed in PR for field exposure correctness. Flyway migrations: no DROP COLUMN or destructive operations without 2-sprint deprecation cycle. AG Grid column state persisted to localStorage per user to preserve merchandiser workspace configuration across sessions.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: CME DataMine API service outage during trading hours. Mitigation: Redis serves stale prices with staleness_warning flag; merchandisers can proceed with stale prices acknowledged; PagerDuty alert after 10-minute staleness threshold. Risk: pgcrypto key compromise exposing all financial columns. Mitigation: key stored in Secrets Manager with 30-day rotation; key rotation procedure documented in runbook. Risk: Legacy data migration integrity -- 3 years of spreadsheet data with inconsistent formats. Mitigation: migration script validates all commodity codes, date ranges, and basis amounts against schema constraints before insert; staging dry-run produces validation error report. Tradeoff: Monolithic FastAPI over microservices for 2-developer team velocity -- contract and settlement services share database but are modular enough for future extraction.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed CME DataMine REST API over WebSocket feed for simpler polling implementation at cooperative data volumes. Selected pgcrypto over application-layer encryption for auditability and SQL-level decryption for reporting queries. Confirmed AG Grid Community (free) over Enterprise edition. Flyway confirmed for migrations over Alembic to match team familiarity.

metadata.notes: Grain cooperative merchandising platform revision fixing CME cache TTL and position query index; CFTC/NFA compliance maintained.

project_goal.value: JavaScript/TypeScript SDK for parsing and generating ISOXML (ISO 11783-10) task data files from precision agriculture equipment terminals, enabling farm software developers to read field operation records from planters, sprayers, and combines without handling raw XML schemas
project_goal.rationale: ISOXML parsing is complex and error-prone to implement from scratch; a typed SDK removes repeated implementation burden across farm software integrations

target_users.value: Farm software developers building precision agriculture integrations, equipment terminal manufacturers, agronomy platform engineers
target_users.rationale: SDK targets technical integrators; developer experience and type safety are primary quality dimensions

project_class.rationale: Distributed as npm package; no hosted runtime, no server, no UI -- pure TypeScript library with parsing and serialization API surface

capabilities.rationale: Pure data parsing and transformation library; no auth, frontend, or cloud dependencies

complexity_level.rationale: ISO 11783-10 schema has significant complexity but SDK scope is bounded to reading and writing TaskData files

risk_level.rationale: Library processes local files; no network calls, no user authentication, no external data storage

data_sensitivity.rationale: SDK ships no data externally; all parsed data stays within the calling application's process

external_exposure.rationale: npm package consumed locally in developer projects; no exposed network endpoints

access_model.value: No authentication; public npm package; npm publish restricted to maintainer accounts via npm access tokens
access_model.rationale: Open-source library with no access control beyond npm publish credentials

feature_scope.value: TaskData.zip parser returning structured TypeScript object tree, device element hierarchy traversal, time log binary decoder, grid data layer reader, prescription map serializer, ISO-compliant XML generator for task files
feature_scope.rationale: Core read/write lifecycle for ISOXML task files covering the most common precision agriculture equipment data formats

mvp_scope.value: TaskData.zip parser, device element tree, time log decoder for standard DLT format, basic prescription serializer
mvp_scope.rationale: Parser is the primary consumer need; serialization added in revision after parser validation

security_baseline.value: XML entity expansion attack prevention (XXE protection) in XML parser; no eval() usage; dependency audit in CI
security_baseline.rationale: ISOXML files sourced from third-party equipment -- malicious XXE payloads are a real supply chain risk for SDK consumers

frontend_stack.value: None; library only
frontend_stack.rationale: No UI component; SDK ships no frontend

backend_stack.value: TypeScript (Node.js compatible, browser compatible via bundler)
backend_stack.rationale: Dual CJS/ESM build for Node.js farm management backends and browser-based agronomy tools

data_platform.value: None; in-memory object graph only
data_platform.rationale: SDK is stateless; no persistence layer

hosting_target.value: npm package registry (public)
hosting_target.rationale: Standard npm distribution; semantic versioning for consumer upgrade control

privacy_retention_policy.value: No data retained; SDK is stateless
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Library processes data in caller's process and memory; no telemetry, no external transmission

future_scope.value: Binary SHP file support for field boundary parsing, EFT format support for older terminals, streaming parser for large time log files
future_scope.rationale: Version 2 roadmap items after core ISOXML support is validated by early adopters

constraints.value: Zero runtime dependencies in the distributed package; dual CJS/ESM build; TypeScript strict mode
constraints.rationale: Zero deps reduces consumer bundle size and eliminates transitive vulnerability surface; dual build for Node and browser consumers

observability_baseline.value: Debug logging via optional debug callback passed by caller; no built-in telemetry
observability_baseline.rationale: Library cannot emit telemetry without consumer consent; debug mode exposes XML parsing steps for developer troubleshooting

execution_preference.value: Feature-branch development with comprehensive test coverage before each release; semantic versioning with CHANGELOG
execution_preference.rationale: SDK consumers depend on API stability; breaking changes require major version bump

project.goal: TypeScript SDK for ISOXML task data file parsing and generation
project.target_users: Farm software developers, equipment terminal integrators

backend.test: Vitest with real ISOXML fixture files from John Deere and CNH equipment exports

data.parsing: fast-xml-parser with XXE protection options; custom binary decoder for TLG time log files

constraints.dependencies: zero runtime dependencies in published package

reasoner_reviews.product: The time log binary decoder was the most requested missing feature from early SDK feedback. Round 1 delivered the XML parser and device element tree correctly, but the binary TLG decoder was scaffolded as a placeholder returning raw buffers rather than decoded DDI-keyed value objects. This is the primary gap blocking real-world adoption for combine data consumers.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The binary TLG format has two variants: the standard DataLogValue-per-time-slice format and the older fixed-width format from pre-2011 terminals. The decoder must detect format version from the TASKDATA.XML header and branch accordingly. The current implementation ignores the A element BinaryFilename attribute and assumes a single time log per device -- multi-device time logs in the same TaskData.zip must be handled.
reasoner_reviews.security: fast-xml-parser is bundled via tsup so the zero-runtime-dependency constraint is satisfied at the published package level. However, the XXE protection options (allowDoctype: false, ignoreAttributes: false with attribute filtering) must be explicitly set in the internal parser instantiation -- the library defaults leave XXE vectors open.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Zero runtime dependency constraint is verified by inspecting the built output's package.json dependencies field. tsup --external flag should not be set for fast-xml-parser since it must be bundled. The dual CJS/ESM build must pass moduleResolution: bundler compatibility check for Vite and Next.js consumers.

backend.service_design: Stateless TypeScript library with three top-level export groups: parsers (parseTaskData, parseDeviceElement, decodeTimeLog), serializers (serializeTaskData, serializePrescription), and types (re-exports of all generated TypeScript interfaces from ISO schema).
backend.api_patterns: Functional API surface: parseTaskData(zipBuffer: Buffer): TaskDataResult. DeviceElement tree traversal via iterator: iterateDeviceElements(task: TaskData): Generator<DeviceElement>. Time log decoding: decodeTimeLog(tlgBuffer: Buffer, ddis: DDIDescriptor[]): TimeLogRecord[]. Prescription serialization: serializePrescription(zones: PrescriptionZone[], options: SerializeOptions): Buffer.
backend.business_modules: ISOXMLParser: parses TASKDATA.XML using fast-xml-parser with XXE protection, maps XML elements to typed domain objects. DeviceElementTree: traverses DET/DOR hierarchies to reconstruct parent-child device relationships. TimeLogDecoder: reads binary TLG files, detects format version from header byte, decodes DDI-keyed value columns per time slice. PrescriptionSerializer: constructs ISOXML GRD grid elements from PrescriptionZone arrays and serializes to TaskData.zip.
backend.backend_risks: ISO 11783-10 edge cases: terminals from different manufacturers emit subtly non-conformant XML (missing optional attributes, non-standard DDI codes). SDK must be defensive -- parse what is present and expose warnings array rather than throwing on non-conformant input.

data.entities: TaskData (root object containing Task[], Device[], Product[], Field[], Farm[]). Task (id, designator, status, time_logs: TimeLog[], operation_techs: OperationTechnique[]). Device (id, designator, device_elements: DeviceElement[]). DeviceElement (id, type DeviceElementType, designator, ddr_refs: DDIRef[], parent_id: string | null, children: DeviceElement[]). TimeLog (filename, format_version, records: TimeLogRecord[]). TimeLogRecord (timestamp: Date, ddi_values: Map<DDICode, number>). DDICode: branded string type. PrescriptionZone (grid_index, product_id, rate_value, ddi_code).
data.schema_notes: TypeScript strict interfaces generated from ISO 11783-10 Annex A schema definitions. DDI codes typed as branded string type DDICode to prevent accidental mixing with plain strings. TimeLogRecord uses Map<DDICode, number> for O(1) DDI value lookup by code.
data.data_risks: Binary TLG format varies between firmware versions; older John Deere terminals emit 4-byte little-endian values where newer terminals emit 8-byte signed integers for the same DDI codes. Version detection from header is required.

ISS-001.title: Binary TLG time log decoder returns raw buffers instead of decoded DDI value objects
ISS-001.detail: decodeTimeLog() returns {raw: Buffer} placeholder; consumers cannot access per-DDI values from combine yield data without implementing their own decoder

history.note: TLG decoder was scaffolded but binary format parsing not implemented

ISS-002.title: XXE protection options not set on fast-xml-parser instance
ISS-002.detail: Internal parseTaskDataXml() call uses fast-xml-parser defaults; allowDoctype not set to false, exposing SDK consumers to XXE attacks from malicious ISOXML files

history.note: XXE options missing from initial parser configuration

focus_issues.title: Binary TLG time log decoder returns raw buffers instead of decoded DDI value objects
focus_issues.detail: decodeTimeLog() returns {raw: Buffer} placeholder; consumers cannot use combine yield data

latest_recommendations: Implement full binary TLG decoder with format version detection (header byte 0x01 = standard, 0x02 = extended 8-byte values)
Set fast-xml-parser options: allowDoctype: false, parseTagValue: false for safety
Add multi-device time log support: TaskData.zip may contain TLG00000.bin through TLG00009.bin for separate device sessions

previous_plan.title: ISOXML TypeScript SDK v1 Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: TypeScript SDK for parsing ISO 11783-10 ISOXML task data files. Zero runtime dependencies, dual CJS/ESM build via tsup. XML parser and device element tree implemented; TLG binary decoder placeholder.
