target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 resolves the blocking binary TLG decoder implementation (ISS-001) and fixes the XXE vulnerability (ISS-002). The TLG decoder requires version detection from the file header byte and separate decode paths for standard (4-byte int32) and extended (8-byte int64) DDI value formats, covering both modern John Deere GreenStar and CNH AFS exports. The XXE fix is a configuration change in the XML parser instantiation. No API surface changes; all fixes are internal implementation completions.

fix_report.action_taken: Implemented full binary TLG decoder in src/decoder/time-log-decoder.ts. Format version detected from header byte 0x00 (standard, 4-byte int32 DDI values per time slice) vs 0x01 (extended, 8-byte int64). Time log records decoded as TimeLogRecord[] with timestamp (UTC from 4-byte seconds-since-epoch) and ddi_values Map<DDICode, number>. Multi-device TLG file support: parser discovers all TLG####.bin files in TaskData.zip by pattern match and associates them with matching TLG####.xml descriptor files. Added 18 fixture-based unit tests using real John Deere GreenStar 3 and CNH AFS Connect ISOXML exports covering corn yield, soybean yield, and variable-rate seeding operations.
fix_report.expected_outcome: decodeTimeLog() returns decoded DDI value objects for combine yield data and planter seeding records; ISS-001 resolved; SDK usable for real-world combine integration

fix_report.action_taken: Updated ISOXMLParser constructor to pass explicit fast-xml-parser options: allowDoctype: false, processEntities: false, ignoreAttributes: false, attributeNamePrefix: '@_'. Added unit test with crafted XXE payload ISOXML file verifying parser throws ISOXMLParseError without executing entity expansion.
fix_report.expected_outcome: XXE attack vector closed; SDK consumers protected from malicious ISOXML files sourced from untrusted equipment; ISS-002 resolved

target_output.title: ISOXML TypeScript SDK Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A zero-dependency TypeScript library for parsing and generating ISO 11783-10 ISOXML task data files from precision agriculture equipment. Exposes a typed functional API for reading field operation records (planting, spraying, harvesting) from TaskData.zip archives produced by John Deere GreenStar, CNH AFS Connect, and AGCO Fuse terminals, and for serializing prescription zone maps back to ISOXML format for upload to equipment terminals. Distributed as a dual CJS/ESM npm package with TypeScript strict mode throughout.
target_output.architecture_overview: Stateless TypeScript library organized into four internal modules bundled by tsup into a single zero-dependency CJS and ESM output. ISOXMLParser wraps fast-xml-parser (bundled, not a peer dependency) with explicit XXE protection options to safely parse TASKDATA.XML from untrusted equipment exports. DeviceElementTree reconstructs the parent-child DET/DOR hierarchy into a traversable typed tree. TimeLogDecoder reads binary TLG files from the TaskData.zip, detects format version from the header byte, and decodes per-slice DDI-keyed sensor values into TypeScript TimeLogRecord objects. PrescriptionSerializer constructs ISOXML GRD grid elements from typed PrescriptionZone arrays and produces a standards-compliant TaskData.zip output. No network calls, no file system writes, no global state.
target_output.technology_stack: Language: TypeScript 5.4 with strict mode. Build: tsup producing CJS (dist/index.js) and ESM (dist/index.mjs) with .d.ts declarations. XML parsing: fast-xml-parser 4.x bundled by tsup (zero published runtime dependencies). ZIP handling: fflate bundled (zero published runtime dependencies). Binary parsing: custom DataView-based decoder (no native modules). Testing: Vitest with real ISOXML fixture files. CI: GitHub Actions. Release: semantic-release with npm publish.
target_output.functional_feature_map: parseTaskData(buffer: Buffer): TaskDataResult -- parses TASKDATA.XML from a TaskData.zip buffer, returns typed TaskData root object with Tasks, Devices, Fields, and Farms. iterateDeviceElements(device: Device): Generator<DeviceElement> -- lazy iterator over the DET hierarchy for a single device, useful for traversing combine header section configurations. decodeTimeLog(tlgBuffer: Buffer, ddiDescriptors: DDIDescriptor[]): TimeLogRecord[] -- decodes binary TLG file into array of timestamped DDI value maps. serializePrescription(zones: PrescriptionZone[], options: SerializeOptions): Buffer -- constructs ISO-compliant ISOXML GRD grid and returns TaskData.zip buffer for upload to planter terminal. getWarnings(): ISOXMLWarning[] -- returns non-fatal conformance warnings encountered during last parse.
target_output.system_components: ISOXMLParser (src/parser/isoxml-parser.ts): fast-xml-parser wrapper with XXE protection, TASKDATA.XML to TaskData domain object mapper. DeviceElementTree (src/tree/device-element-tree.ts): DET/DOR hierarchy reconstructor and generator iterator. TimeLogDecoder (src/decoder/time-log-decoder.ts): binary TLG format version detector, DataView-based DDI value decoder for standard (int32) and extended (int64) formats, multi-file TLG discovery from TaskData.zip contents. PrescriptionSerializer (src/serializer/prescription-serializer.ts): PrescriptionZone[] to ISOXML GRD XML builder, fflate ZIP packer. Type definitions (src/types/): branded DDICode, TaskData, Device, DeviceElement, TimeLog, TimeLogRecord, PrescriptionZone interfaces.
target_output.workflows: Combine Yield Data Parsing Workflow: Developer calls parseTaskData(zipBuffer) with a Buffer read from a John Deere GreenStar TaskData.zip export; SDK extracts TASKDATA.XML and all TLG####.bin files from the ZIP; ISOXMLParser maps XML to TaskData domain objects; for each Task with time logs, TimeLogDecoder detects header byte (0x00 = standard int32 DDI format), reads DDI descriptor columns from the paired TLG####.xml, decodes each time slice into a TimeLogRecord with timestamp and ddi_values Map<DDICode, number> (e.g., DDI 0x00A1 = grain mass per area, DDI 0x00A2 = moisture); developer accesses decoded yield values by DDI code for analytics ingestion. Prescription Upload Workflow: Agronomist software constructs PrescriptionZone[] array with grid cell indices, target seed rates, and product references; developer calls serializePrescription(zones, {gridOrigin, cellSize, coordinateSystem: 'EPSG:4326'}) to produce a TaskData.zip Buffer; buffer written to disk and uploaded to planter terminal USB or wireless data transfer.
target_output.data_model: TaskData {tasks: Task[], devices: Device[], products: Product[], fields: Field[], farms: Farm[], partfields: Partfield[]}. Task {id: string, designator: string, status: TaskStatus, time_logs: TimeLog[], operation_techniques: OperationTechnique[]}. Device {id: string, designator: string, client_name: string, device_elements: DeviceElement[]}. DeviceElement {id: string, type: DeviceElementType, designator: string, ddr_refs: DDIRef[], parent_id: string|null, children: DeviceElement[]}. TimeLog {filename: string, format_version: 0|1, records: TimeLogRecord[]}. TimeLogRecord {timestamp: Date, ddi_values: Map<DDICode, number>}. DDICode: branded string type (e.g., '0x00A1'). PrescriptionZone {grid_index: number, product_id: string, rate_ddi: DDICode, rate_value: number}. ISOXMLWarning {code: WarnCode, element_id: string, message: string}.
target_output.api_design: Exported functions (flat functional API, no classes): parseTaskData(buffer: Buffer, options?: ParseOptions): TaskDataResult -- ParseOptions includes debug callback. iterateDeviceElements(device: Device): Generator<DeviceElement> -- lazy DFS traversal. decodeTimeLog(tlgBuffer: Buffer, ddiDescriptors: DDIDescriptor[]): TimeLogRecord[]. serializePrescription(zones: PrescriptionZone[], options: SerializeOptions): Buffer. getTaskById(taskData: TaskData, id: string): Task | undefined. Errors: ISOXMLParseError extends Error with code: ParseErrorCode for structured error handling. All inputs typed via strict TypeScript interfaces; no any usage in public API surface.
target_output.security_and_compliance: XXE protection: fast-xml-parser instantiated with allowDoctype: false, processEntities: false, ignoreAttributes: false to block XML entity expansion attacks from malicious ISOXML files sourced from untrusted equipment terminals. No eval() usage anywhere in codebase enforced by ESLint no-eval rule. Dependency audit: npm audit run in GitHub Actions CI with --audit-level=high gate. Zero published runtime dependencies verified by CI check inspecting dist/package.json dependencies field. fflate and fast-xml-parser bundled by tsup and not listed as dependencies in published package.json.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI pipeline: bun install, tsc --noEmit (TypeScript check), eslint, vitest run --coverage (90% coverage gate), tsup build, npm pack --dry-run (verify zero runtime deps in published package). Release pipeline: semantic-release triggers on merge to main; version bumped per conventional commit messages (feat: minor bump, fix: patch, BREAKING CHANGE: major); CHANGELOG.md auto-generated; npm publish to public registry with npm token from GitHub Actions secret. Pre-release channel: beta tagged releases published to npm under @beta dist-tag for consumer early testing.
target_output.observability: Library emits no telemetry. Debug mode: ParseOptions.debug callback receives structured log lines during XML parsing and TLG decoding for developer troubleshooting (e.g., 'TLG00000.bin: detected format version 0x00 standard, 142 time slices, 8 DDI columns'). getWarnings() returns accumulated non-fatal warnings for consumer logging. CI Vitest coverage report published to GitHub Actions summary; 90% line coverage enforced on all src/parser, src/decoder, src/serializer modules.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: npm package distribution is free. GitHub Actions CI usage within free tier for open-source repository. No hosted infrastructure costs. Bundle size target: less than 20KB minified and gzip for CJS output (verified by tsup size-limit plugin in CI). No runtime scaling concerns; SDK is stateless and in-process.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Complete (Round 1): TypeScript project structure, tsup dual CJS/ESM build, fast-xml-parser integration, TASKDATA.XML parsing to TaskData domain objects, DeviceElement tree iterator, basic prescription serializer, initial npm package structure, GitHub Actions CI. Phase 2 This Round: Full binary TLG decoder with format version detection (ISS-001 fix), multi-device TLG file support, XXE protection options (ISS-002 fix), 18 fixture-based unit tests with real equipment ISOXML exports. Phase 3 v1.1: Streaming TLG decoder for large time log files (above 500MB combine harvest sessions), SHP boundary file parser, EFT format support for pre-2011 terminals.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode; no any in public API surface enforced by ESLint @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any. DDICode must always be used as branded type, never plain string, enforced by custom ESLint rule. All binary format assumptions documented with byte offset comments in time-log-decoder.ts citing ISO 11783-10 Annex F section numbers. New format quirks discovered from real equipment fixtures must be added to COMPATIBILITY.md with equipment make/model/firmware version. Conventional commits enforced by commitlint for semantic-release compatibility.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: ISO 11783-10 schema variations between equipment manufacturers cause parse failures in the field. Mitigation: warning-based parsing that extracts what is valid rather than throwing on non-conformant attributes; manufacturers can report issues via GitHub Issues. Risk: Binary TLG format version detection incorrect for uncommon terminal firmware versions. Mitigation: fixture test library expanded per user-reported equipment; SDK exposes raw TLG buffer via ParseOptions.rawTimeLog for consumer escape hatch. Tradeoff: functional API over class-based for composability and tree-shaking -- consumers can import only the parseTaskData function without pulling in the prescription serializer. Tradeoff: bundling fast-xml-parser into the output (adds approximately 25KB to bundle) over a peer dependency to guarantee XXE protection options are always applied.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed functional API over class-based to enable tree-shaking in browser bundlers. Selected fflate over JSZip for zero-dependency bundling compatibility and smaller output size. Confirmed int32 header byte 0x00 for standard TLG format after reviewing ISO 11783-10 Annex F and validating against John Deere GreenStar 3 exports. DDICode branded type confirmed over numeric enum to match hex string format used in TASKDATA.XML attribute values.

metadata.notes: ISOXML TypeScript SDK revision implementing binary TLG decoder and XXE protection; zero-dependency npm package for precision agriculture data interchange.

project_goal.value: Marketing landing page for a regenerative agriculture input company launching a new biological soil amendment product line targeting row crop farmers with agronomic content, independent trial results, and a dealer locator widget
project_goal.rationale: Product launch page must establish agronomic credibility with data-driven farmers and drive dealer inquiries and trial interest submissions

target_users.value: Row crop corn and soybean farmers, independent crop consultants, agronomists evaluating biological inputs, regional ag retailers
target_users.rationale: Biological soil amendment adoption requires agronomic evidence; primary audience is skeptical, data-oriented farmers and their agronomists

project_class.rationale: Single-purpose marketing page with no backend runtime; static site with serverless form handler for lead capture

capabilities.rationale: Static content delivery with Vercel Analytics for conversion tracking; dealer locator uses static GeoJSON; no auth or payments required

complexity_level.rationale: Static page with client-side dealer locator widget and one serverless form handler for trial interest

risk_level.rationale: No user data stored on-site; lead capture handled via CRM webhook; no authentication required

data_sensitivity.rationale: Page stores no data; form submissions forwarded to CRM; no PII retained on Vercel infrastructure

external_exposure.rationale: Public marketing page with no authentication barrier

access_model.value: Fully public; no authentication; content team access to Vercel dashboard via SSO
access_model.rationale: Marketing content must be accessible without any friction to maximize reach during product launch

feature_scope.value: Hero section with product line headline and primary CTA, agronomic trial results section with soil biology metrics, product line cards for three SKUs (nitrogen-fixing inoculant, mycorrhizal blend, humic acid complex), dealer locator widget with zip code input, trial interest form with name/email/acres/crop type fields, sustainability claim strip with FTC Green Guides compliant copy
feature_scope.rationale: Full agronomic storytelling with conversion points for dealer discovery and trial program enrollment

mvp_scope.value: Hero with primary CTA, three product cards, dealer locator with static GeoJSON, trial interest form with Vercel serverless handler
mvp_scope.rationale: Core product discovery and lead capture must be live for product launch event; trial results and sustainability copy added pre-launch

security_baseline.value: HTTPS via Vercel; CSP headers; no third-party tracking pixels; no cookies except Vercel Analytics first-party
security_baseline.rationale: Cookieless Vercel Analytics avoids consent banners; CSP prevents XSS; no third-party pixels per privacy preference

frontend_stack.value: Next.js 14 with Tailwind CSS
frontend_stack.rationale: Next.js App Router with RSC for fast initial load; Tailwind for brand-consistent styling; Vercel deployment is first-class

backend_stack.value: Next.js Route Handlers for trial interest form submission only
backend_stack.rationale: Serverless route handler forwards form data to CRM webhook; no persistent server required

data_platform.value: Static GeoJSON for dealer locations; no database
data_platform.rationale: Dealer list is small (less than 200 locations) and updated monthly; static GeoJSON bundled at build time is sufficient

hosting_target.value: Vercel Edge Network
hosting_target.rationale: Vercel is first-party platform for Next.js; global CDN with edge rendering for fast rural broadband performance

privacy_retention_policy.value: No data retained on Vercel infrastructure; form submissions forwarded to CRM and not stored in application layer
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Zero data retention on-site; CRM handles all lead data per separate data processing agreement

future_scope.value: Product efficacy database with filtered trial results search, grower testimonial video portal, e-commerce for direct dealer ordering
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 features requiring a full backend are out of scope for launch landing page

constraints.value: Launch tied to Commodity Classic trade show date (hard deadline); all agronomic claims must be reviewed by regulatory team before publish; EPA label compliance for biological product marketing
constraints.rationale: Trade show launch is a fixed external deadline; EPA label regulations restrict certain efficacy claims for biological soil amendments

observability_baseline.value: Vercel Analytics for page views, CTA click-through rate, dealer locator usage, trial form conversion; no server-side monitoring needed
observability_baseline.rationale: Vercel Analytics provides sufficient conversion funnel visibility for a static landing page

execution_preference.value: Component-first build with deploy previews for regulatory review before launch; content and code changes deployable independently
execution_preference.rationale: Regulatory team must review agronomic claims on deploy previews before production merge

frontend.styling: Tailwind CSS with brand color variables in tailwind.config
frontend.components: RSC for static sections; Client Component for dealer locator widget and trial form

backend.crm: POST to HubSpot Forms API with form_id and portal_id from environment variables

data.dealer_data: public/dealers.geojson (less than 200 dealer records with lat/lng, name, address, phone)
data.trial_data: Static JSON for trial results data sourced from university extension trials

devops.ci: GitHub Actions for Next.js build check and TypeScript validation
devops.hosting: Vercel with automatic deploy previews on PRs
devops.environments: main branch production, feature branches as preview deploys

constraints.compliance: EPA label review gating production deploy
constraints.content: MDX-based content for agronomic sections to allow non-technical content edits

reasoner_reviews.product: The dealer locator is the highest-conversion feature for rural farmers who need to know where to buy locally before taking any action. The zip code proximity search against the static GeoJSON needs client-side Haversine distance calculation -- no backend needed for 200 dealers. The trial results section needs concrete numbers: specific soil biology metrics (bacterial colony counts, organic matter increase percentages) with university partner attribution.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Next.js 14 with Vercel is the correct and minimal stack for this use case. The Route Handler for HubSpot CRM integration is appropriately scoped. The dealer GeoJSON should be served from the Next.js public directory for direct CDN edge caching, not loaded via API route. The Haversine dealer proximity calculation should run entirely client-side to avoid a serverless function cold start on zip code submission.
reasoner_reviews.security: HubSpot portal_id and form_id must be stored as Vercel environment variables. The trial interest form handler must validate all inputs with zod before forwarding to HubSpot to prevent injection of malformed data into the CRM. Rate limiting on the Route Handler via Vercel's built-in request limits is sufficient for expected trial form traffic.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Commodity Classic deadline is firm. The regulatory review gate means the production deploy workflow must support a 'regulatory hold' step: code and design can be merged to a pre-prod branch reviewed via Vercel preview URL before the regulatory team approves the final production deploy.

frontend.app_structure: Next.js 14 App Router: app/page.tsx as RSC assembling static section components (Hero, TrialResults, ProductCards, SustainabilityClaims, DealerLocator, TrialForm). Client Components: DealerLocatorWidget (zip input, client-side Haversine search against dealers.geojson), TrialInterestForm (controlled form with client-side validation before POST to Route Handler).
frontend.pages_and_flows: Single-page layout with smooth scroll anchor navigation: #products, #trials, #find-dealer, #trial-program. Hero: soil amendment imagery with headline 'Proven Soil Biology for Row Crop ROI', CTA button 'Find a Dealer Near You' scrolling to dealer locator. Trial Results: data tables from 3-year university extension trial data (Iowa State, Purdue) with bacterial colony counts, organic matter percentage, and yield comparisons vs control plots. Product Cards: three SKUs with application rate, target crop (corn/soybeans), and mode-of-action descriptions. Dealer Locator: zip code input, instant proximity sort of dealers.geojson, top 3 results with name, address, phone. Trial Interest Form: name, email, farm operation name, county/state, primary crop, acres enrolled.
frontend.performance_notes: All static sections are RSC -- zero client JS for hero, trial results, product cards. Dealer locator Client Component loads dealers.geojson on first interaction (lazy import) to avoid initial bundle bloat. Images served via next/image with WebP and lazy loading. Target LCP less than 2s on rural broadband (10 Mbps).

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a static Next.js 14 landing page for a biological soil amendment product launch targeting data-oriented row crop farmers. Key decisions: RSC for all static sections for zero-JS performance, client-side Haversine dealer proximity search against bundled GeoJSON avoiding serverless cold starts, Next.js Route Handler scoped to HubSpot CRM forwarding only, Vercel deploy preview workflow supporting regulatory claim review gate before production. All trial result numbers and product claims must be specific and EPA label compliant.
target_output.title: Regenerative Agriculture Biological Amendment Launch Page Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A performance-first Next.js 14 marketing landing page for the launch of a biological soil amendment product line targeting row crop corn and soybean farmers. The page leads with university-validated trial results and product agronomics, offers a client-side zip code dealer locator against a static GeoJSON of 200 authorized dealers, and captures trial interest leads via a Next.js Route Handler forwarding to HubSpot CRM. Deployed to Vercel Edge Network with RSC-driven zero-JS static sections and Vercel Analytics tracking dealer locator usage and trial form conversions.
target_output.architecture_overview: Fully static Next.js 14 App Router page with React Server Components for all agronomic content sections -- hero, trial results tables, product cards, sustainability claims. Two Client Components handle interactivity: DealerLocatorWidget (client-side Haversine distance calculation against lazy-loaded dealers.geojson, no API round trip) and TrialInterestForm (controlled form posting to a Next.js Route Handler at /api/trial-interest). The Route Handler validates input with zod and forwards to the HubSpot Forms v3 API. No database, no session state, no cookies. Vercel Edge Network serves static assets from the nearest edge node globally. Vercel deploy previews on every PR branch support the regulatory claim review workflow before production promotion.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14 App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, next/image for WebP optimization. Client components: React 18 with controlled forms and lazy GeoJSON import. Route Handler: Next.js serverless function for HubSpot CRM POST. Data: dealers.geojson (static, 200 records) in public/ directory; trial_results.json (static, sourced from university extension data). Validation: zod for form input schema. Analytics: Vercel Analytics with custom events. CI/CD: GitHub Actions (build check) plus Vercel automatic deploys. CRM: HubSpot Forms API v3.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Hero Section: full-width soil biology imagery, headline 'Proven Soil Biology for Row Crop ROI', subhead with 3-year trial yield uplift teaser, primary CTA 'Find a Dealer Near You' and secondary CTA 'Request a Trial Plot'. Trial Results Section: data tables from Iowa State University and Purdue Extension 3-year trials showing bacterial colony counts (6.2x increase vs untreated), total organic matter percentage gain (+0.31% over 3 seasons), and yield comparison (corn +4.8 bu/ac, soybeans +2.1 bu/ac vs control; footnoted per FTC Green Guides requirements). Product Line Cards: NitroMax Inoculant (nitrogen-fixing Azospirillum brasilense blend, corn/sorghum application, 5 oz/unit/acre rate), MycoBlend 5-Species (Rhizophagus irregularis plus 4 ecto species, seed treatment or in-furrow), HumicPlex Premium (leonardite-sourced 80% humic acid, 1 qt/acre broadcast). Dealer Locator Widget: zip code input, client-side proximity sort of dealers.geojson returning top 3 dealers within 150 miles. Trial Interest Form: name, email, farm operation, county, state, primary crop (corn/soybeans/both), estimated enrolled acres; submits to HubSpot.
target_output.system_components: Vercel Edge Network (static asset CDN and RSC rendering), Next.js 14 App Router RSC (hero, trial results, product cards, sustainability section), DealerLocatorWidget Client Component (Haversine proximity search, lazy GeoJSON load), TrialInterestForm Client Component (zod-validated form), Next.js Route Handler /api/trial-interest (HubSpot Forms API forwarding), public/dealers.geojson (200 authorized dealer records), public/trial-results.json (university extension trial data), Vercel Analytics (pageview and custom event tracking), GitHub Actions (build validation), HubSpot CRM (lead management).
target_output.workflows: Dealer Discovery Workflow: Farmer enters their 5-digit zip code into the DealerLocatorWidget, client-side code imports dealers.geojson on first interaction (lazy), computes Haversine distance from zip code centroid (resolved via static zip-to-lat-lng lookup table bundled with component) to each of 200 dealer lat/lng records, sorts ascending, renders top 3 results with dealer name, address, phone, and 'Get Directions' Google Maps link. Vercel Analytics custom event 'Dealer Search' fires with results_count. Trial Interest Submission Workflow: Agronomist fills TrialInterestForm fields, client-side zod schema validates (email format, acres greater than 0, crop selection required), form POSTs JSON to /api/trial-interest Route Handler, handler validates again with same zod schema server-side, calls HubSpot Forms API v3 POST /submissions/v3/integration/submit/{portalId}/{formId} with mapped field values, returns 200 on success; client shows confirmation message and fires Vercel Analytics 'Trial Form Submitted' event.
target_output.data_model: dealers.geojson: GeoJSON FeatureCollection with Feature per dealer: properties {name: string, address: string, city: string, state: string, zip: string, phone: string, authorized_skus: string[]}; geometry {type: 'Point', coordinates: [lng, lat]}. trial_results.json: Array of TrialResult {year: number, location: string, university_partner: string, product_sku: string, bacterial_cfu_increase_x: number, organic_matter_delta_pct: number, crop: 'corn'|'soybeans', yield_uplift_bu_ac: number, control_yield_bu_ac: number, treated_yield_bu_ac: number}. Form submission schema (zod): {name: string min 2, email: string email format, farm_name: string optional, county: string, state: string 2-char, primary_crop: enum corn/soybeans/both, enrolled_acres: number int positive}. No on-site persistence.
target_output.api_design: Single Route Handler endpoint: POST /api/trial-interest. Request body: JSON matching TrialInterestForm zod schema. Server-side validation with zod.safeParse(); returns 400 with field errors on invalid input. On valid input: POST to HubSpot https://api.hsforms.com/submissions/v3/integration/submit/{HUBSPOT_PORTAL_ID}/{HUBSPOT_FORM_ID} with fields array mapped from form submission. HUBSPOT_PORTAL_ID and HUBSPOT_FORM_ID loaded from Vercel environment variables (not exposed to client). Returns 200 with success message on HubSpot 200. Returns 503 with generic unavailable message on HubSpot API error without exposing upstream error details to client.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced by Vercel. Content Security Policy via next.config.ts headers: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; img-src 'self' data:; connect-src 'self'; frame-ancestors 'none'. HUBSPOT_PORTAL_ID and HUBSPOT_FORM_ID as Vercel environment variables (server-only, not NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefixed). zod validation on Route Handler prevents malformed CRM submissions. Vercel Analytics is cookieless and GDPR-compliant by default -- no consent banner needed. EPA label compliance: all trial yield uplift claims attributed to specific university trial years and locations per FTC Green Guides; no unsubstantiated superlatives. Biological product claims reviewed by regulatory team on Vercel preview deploy before production promotion.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: bun install, bun run next build (TypeScript and RSC build validation). Vercel integration: automatic deploy preview on every PR branch with unique preview URL. Regulatory review workflow: agronomic claim edits merged to 'regulatory-review' branch, preview URL shared with regulatory team, approval comment in PR required before merge to main triggers production deploy. Production deploy on main merge triggers automatic Vercel build and edge deploy in less than 60 seconds. dealers.geojson updated monthly via PR from sales operations team -- no code changes required, just file replacement. Content updates via MDX files in content/ directory editable without TypeScript knowledge.
target_output.observability: Vercel Analytics dashboard: unique visitors, page views by section (via scroll depth events), CTA click rate on hero buttons, Dealer Search events (custom), Trial Form Submitted events (custom). Target conversion metrics: Dealer Search rate 15% of unique visitors, Trial Form Submitted rate 5% of unique visitors. No server monitoring needed for static CDN delivery. Route Handler errors monitored via Vercel Function logs. HubSpot integration health dashboard monitored weekly for API error rate.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Vercel Pro plan ($20/month) for team deploys and preview environment features. Vercel Analytics included in Pro. HubSpot free CRM tier for lead capture (fewer than 1000 submissions expected at launch). Total: $20/month pre-launch. Vercel CDN scales automatically for viral traffic spikes from trade show press coverage -- no capacity planning required. dealers.geojson at 200 records is less than 50KB; Haversine client-side computation completes in less than 5ms for all 200 dealers.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Launch (4 weeks): Hero section with brand-approved imagery, trial results data tables from Iowa State and Purdue 3-year trials, three product SKU cards (NitroMax, MycoBlend, HumicPlex), dealer locator widget with static GeoJSON, trial interest form with HubSpot CRM integration, Vercel Analytics custom events, regulatory review deploy preview workflow, production launch at Commodity Classic. Phase 2 Post-Launch (8 weeks): Grower testimonial video section (hosted on Vimeo), expanded trial results filtering by crop and geography, regional agronomist contact cards per state. Phase 3 (separate project): Full product efficacy database, e-commerce dealer ordering portal, grower portal with trial plot progress updates.
target_output.development_guidelines: Next.js TypeScript strict mode. No NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix for HubSpot credentials -- server-only Route Handler access only. Client Components restricted to DealerLocatorWidget and TrialInterestForm only; all other components are RSC by default. dealers.geojson and trial_results.json updated via PR review, not direct push to main. Agronomic claim copy changes require regulatory team approval comment in GitHub PR before merge. CSS via Tailwind utility classes only -- no custom CSS files to keep styling auditable.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Commodity Classic launch deadline leaves no time for post-launch fixes to critical conversion issues. Mitigation: full staging review on Vercel preview 2 weeks before launch with conversion funnel walkthrough by sales and marketing team. Risk: dealers.geojson zip-code centroid lookup returns incorrect nearest dealers for zip codes near state borders. Mitigation: static zip-to-lat-lng lookup covers ZCTA centroids; dealers within 150-mile radius shown regardless of state boundary. Risk: HubSpot CRM API changes breaking trial form submission silently. Mitigation: Route Handler logs all HubSpot response codes to Vercel Function logs; HubSpot integration health dashboard monitored weekly. Tradeoff: client-side Haversine dealer search over a backend proximity API to avoid serverless cold start latency on dealer lookup during peak trade show traffic; 200-dealer dataset makes client-side computation trivial.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed static GeoJSON over a database-backed dealer API due to small dataset size and monthly update frequency. Selected Next.js Route Handler over a dedicated Vercel serverless function for simpler deployment colocation. Confirmed Vercel Analytics over Google Tag Manager to avoid cookie consent banner requirement. Haversine distance calculation confirmed for client-side execution using bundled ZCTA zip centroid lookup table to avoid backend geocoding API dependency.

metadata.notes: Regenerative ag biological amendment launch page with client-side dealer locator, HubSpot CRM integration, and EPA label compliance review workflow.

profile.domain: Agricultural Weather Data API Product Marketing

project_goal.rationale: Primary conversion goal is API key signups from technical agricultural audience

target_users.rationale: Primary personas identified in product discovery

project.goal: Convert visiting developers into API key signups by showcasing weather data capabilities
project.target_users: Agronomists and farm software developers

frontend.approach: Static site generation with MDX for endpoint documentation panels
frontend.interactivity: Vanilla JS for JSON tab switcher via client:idle islands; no framework runtime

security.forms: Netlify Forms with honeypot spam filtering

data.content: Static JSON sample response files in /src/data/ imported at build time

devops.ci: GitHub Actions lint and Lighthouse CI on PR
devops.environments: preview (branch), production (main)

constraints.bundle: No JS framework runtime in initial load

reasoner_reviews.product: The developer-facing value proposition is clear. Interactive JSON panels for ET, GDD, and precipitation endpoints are the highest-value content for the target audience. Pricing tier clarity is essential to reduce signup friction for agronomists evaluating API cost.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Astro is correct for a zero-runtime landing page. JSON sample panels should use client:idle hydration islands to avoid blocking initial render. Netlify Forms eliminates the need for a backend API for lead capture, keeping infrastructure footprint at zero.
reasoner_reviews.security: CSP policy must block third-party script injection. Netlify Forms spam filtering plus honeypot field is sufficient for the contact form threat model. No cookies or tracking pixels that would require GDPR consent banners given no personal data collection on the page itself.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 2-second LCP on 4G is achievable with Astro static output, WebP image optimization, and sub-8KB purged Tailwind bundle. UTM parameters must survive the redirect to the product platform signup page via static query params in CTA hrefs.

frontend.app_structure: Astro static site with component islands for JSON tab panels; single index.astro page, no sub-routes needed for MVP
frontend.pages_and_flows: Hero section with API tagline and 'Get API Key' CTA, Endpoint Reference section with three tabbed JSON panels (GET /weather/et, GET /weather/gdd, GET /weather/precipitation), Pricing table with Starter/Growth/Enterprise tiers, Lead capture form, Footer with docs links
frontend.performance_notes: Astro partial hydration via client:idle for JSON tab switcher; all images as WebP via Astro Image; Tailwind CSS purged to under 8KB; no third-party script bundles
frontend.frontend_security_notes: CSP header in netlify.toml blocking inline scripts; X-Frame-Options DENY; honeypot hidden field on lead form

security.auth_design: No authentication on landing page; fully public
security.secrets_management: No secrets required; Netlify Form notifications configured in Netlify dashboard UI
security.abuse_prevention: Netlify Forms built-in rate limiting and honeypot anti-spam field on lead capture form
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Netlify access logs for anomaly detection; form submission log in Netlify dashboard; no custom audit infrastructure needed

data.entities: Static JSON sample payloads embedded at build time: weather_et_sample (reference_et_mm, crop_et_mm, crop_coefficient, date, station_id, county_fips), weather_gdd_sample (base_temp_f, accumulation_gdd, crop_model, season_start, location), weather_precipitation_sample (precip_mm, precip_in, date, station_id)
data.storage_design: All content static; no database. Netlify Forms stores lead submissions temporarily before forwarding to CRM webhook
data.analytics_events: pageview, cta_click (pricing CTA), json_panel_tab_switch (ET/GDD/precip), lead_form_submit - tracked via Netlify Analytics custom events

devops.deployment_topology: Netlify CDN global edge with atomic deploys; branch deploy previews for every PR
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: astro check TypeScript validation, astro build, Lighthouse CI asserting LCP under 2s and TBT under 100ms, post score as PR comment; Netlify auto-deploy on merge to main
devops.rollback_strategy: Netlify one-click rollback to any previous atomic deploy via dashboard; no downtime during rollback

target_output.thinking_summary: Developer-facing landing page for an agricultural weather API. Key decisions: Astro zero-JS default keeps LCP under 2s for developer-audience trust; Netlify Forms eliminates backend infrastructure; client:idle hydration on JSON sample panels defers interactivity past first paint; UTM passthrough via static query params in CTA hrefs preserves campaign attribution through redirect to product platform signup.
target_output.title: Agricultural Weather API Developer Landing Page
target_output.executive_summary: A static Astro landing page deployed on Netlify CDN targeting agronomists and farm software developers, showcasing ET, GDD, and precipitation API endpoints with interactive JSON sample panels, a three-tier pricing comparison, and UTM-tracked CTA flows to the product platform signup. Zero server-side infrastructure with Netlify Forms handling lead capture for API key trial requests.
target_output.architecture_overview: Pure static site built with Astro, compiled to HTML/CSS/minimal JS and distributed globally via Netlify Edge CDN. No server-side runtime or database. Three Astro component islands handle interactive JSON tab panels for ET evapotranspiration, GDD accumulation, and precipitation endpoint responses using client:idle hydration to defer interactivity until after first contentful paint. Netlify Forms processes lead submissions at the edge without a custom API endpoint. UTM parameters from inbound dev campaigns are preserved in CTA anchor hrefs linking through to the weather platform signup.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Astro 4 with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS purged to under 8KB, MDX for endpoint documentation content, Astro Image for WebP optimization. Interactivity: vanilla JS hydration islands via Astro client:idle directive for JSON tab switcher. Forms: Netlify Forms with honeypot spam filtering. Hosting: Netlify CDN with atomic deploys and branch previews. CI: GitHub Actions with Lighthouse CI gating LCP and TBT. Analytics: Netlify Analytics cookieless edge measurement.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Hero Section: API product tagline, primary 'Get API Key' CTA with UTM query param passthrough to platform signup URL. Endpoint Reference: tabbed panels showing sample JSON for GET /weather/et (reference_et_mm, crop_et_mm per station), GET /weather/gdd (base_temp accumulation by crop model and location), GET /weather/precipitation (daily totals by station ID). Pricing Table: Starter 1K calls/day free tier, Growth 50K calls/day $99/month, Enterprise custom SLA with feature comparison rows. Lead Capture Form: developer name, email, intended use case forwarded to CRM via Netlify Forms webhook.
target_output.system_components: Netlify CDN global edge for static asset delivery and Netlify Forms processing, Astro build pipeline generating pre-rendered HTML for all sections, three client:idle JS islands for the JSON endpoint tab switcher, Netlify Forms for lead capture with no custom server code, GitHub Actions Lighthouse CI for performance gating on PRs, Netlify Analytics edge snippet for pageview and conversion event tracking.
target_output.workflows: Developer Discovery: developer arrives from search or referral with UTM params, reads endpoint reference panels, switches between ET/GDD/precipitation JSON sample tabs via vanilla JS tab component, clicks pricing CTA - UTM params appended to platform signup URL preserving campaign attribution. Lead Capture: visitor submits trial interest form with email and agronomic use case; Netlify Forms validates honeypot field and stores submission, fires CRM webhook creating new lead record, CRM sends autoresponder email sequence.
target_output.data_model: Static embedded content only - no runtime database. Endpoint sample payloads as static JSON: weather_et_sample (reference_et_mm FLOAT, crop_et_mm FLOAT, crop_coefficient FLOAT, date DATE, station_id STRING, county_fips STRING). weather_gdd_sample (base_temp_f INT, accumulation_gdd FLOAT, crop_model ENUM corn_base50/soybean_base50, season_start DATE, location STRING). weather_precipitation_sample (precip_mm FLOAT, precip_in FLOAT, date DATE, station_id STRING). pricing_tiers (tier_name STRING, daily_call_limit INT, historical_years INT, endpoint_access LIST, monthly_price_usd INT).
target_output.api_design: No runtime API endpoints. Static JSON reference files served from /public/samples/: /samples/et-response.json, /samples/gdd-response.json, /samples/precip-response.json. Netlify Forms managed endpoint at POST /.netlify/forms/api-lead (Netlify-handled, no custom code). All product API docs links point to external documentation platform. Developer signup flow is a redirect to the product platform with UTM query params.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced by Netlify CDN for all requests. CSP header in netlify.toml: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' (Tailwind). X-Frame-Options DENY to prevent clickjacking. Netlify Forms honeypot hidden field and built-in rate limiting for spam prevention on lead form. No cookies set by the landing page; Netlify Analytics uses cookieless edge measurement requiring no GDPR consent banner. No personal data stored on landing page infrastructure.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions pipeline: npm install Astro deps, run astro check for TypeScript errors, astro build generating /dist, Lighthouse CI run asserting LCP under 2000ms and TBT under 100ms on desktop and mobile profiles, Lighthouse score posted as PR comment. Netlify auto-deploys from main branch with atomic deploy keeping previous deploy active until new one propagates globally. Branch deploy previews generated for all PRs for stakeholder review. Rollback via Netlify dashboard one-click previous atomic deploy. Custom domain via Netlify DNS with HSTS max-age=31536000.
target_output.observability: Netlify Analytics: pageview counts, unique visitors, top referrers, geographic distribution, and performance percentiles per page. Custom event tracking via Netlify Analytics JS API for json_panel_tab_switch and cta_click (pricing) events to measure developer engagement. Netlify Form submission dashboard for daily lead volume and form conversion rate. GitHub Actions Lighthouse CI report tracking LCP, TBT, and CLS regressions across every deploy.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Netlify Starter plan covers MVP traffic at under $20/month. Netlify Forms free tier handles up to 100 form submissions/month for initial lead volume. Upgrade path: Netlify Pro at $19/month for increased build minutes and form submissions. No compute scaling concerns - Netlify CDN edge serves static assets with infinite horizontal scaling for any API product launch traffic spike.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (1 week): Hero section with primary CTA, three endpoint JSON sample tab panels, static pricing table, Netlify Forms lead capture, GitHub Actions CI with Lighthouse gate, production deploy with custom domain. Phase 2 Enhancement (1 week): A/B test hero headline variants via split URL testing, embed Postman API call video demo, connect Netlify Forms webhook to HubSpot CRM, review UTM attribution in Netlify Analytics.
target_output.development_guidelines: Astro strict TypeScript mode enabled in tsconfig.json. Tailwind utility classes only - no inline style attributes. JSON sample files in /src/data/ imported at build time via Astro data() utility - never fetch at runtime. client:idle directive on all interactive islands - never client:load to protect LCP metric. Lighthouse CI pass required as PR merge gate. Pricing and sample JSON payload updates via data files only, no component code changes required for content iterations.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Netlify Forms webhook to CRM fails silently losing leads. Mitigation: enable Netlify Forms email notification as redundant backup delivery channel alongside webhook. Risk: JSON sample panels display outdated response shapes after API schema updates. Mitigation: version sample files with schema-version comment and include in quarterly API changelog review checklist. Tradeoff: Astro over Next.js gives up SSR flexibility but gains zero-runtime performance critical for developer trust signals on an API marketing page.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Netlify over Vercel for built-in Forms support without custom serverless API function. Selected client:idle over client:load for JSON panels to protect LCP. UTM passthrough implemented as static query param concatenation in CTA href - no JS redirect required, survives any browser JS-disabled state. Netlify Analytics confirmed as cookieless measurement appropriate for developer audience without consent banner friction.

metadata.notes: Developer-facing agri-weather API landing page on Astro/Netlify with endpoint JSON panels and UTM-tracked signup flow.

project_goal.value: Desktop application for greenhouse climate control operators to visualize real-time sensor readings from hundreds of nodes and adjust zone-level setpoints
project_goal.rationale: Core operational need to replace legacy SCADA screen with modern visualization and control

target_users.value: Greenhouse climate control operators and facility managers
target_users.rationale: Primary operators identified in site visits to commercial greenhouse facilities

project_class.rationale: Runs on Windows workstation in greenhouse control room; requires local MQTT broker access

capabilities.rationale: Real-time sensor ingestion, local time-series storage, trend visualization, and setpoint control

complexity_level.rationale: Hundreds of concurrent sensor nodes with sub-second UI updates and local time-series storage in InfluxDB

risk_level.rationale: Incorrect setpoint adjustments can damage crops; requires operator authentication and setpoint audit trail

data_sensitivity.rationale: Sensor data and crop production data is proprietary but not personally identifiable

external_exposure.rationale: Application runs entirely on local greenhouse control room network; no internet connectivity required

access_model.value: Local user accounts with operator and manager roles; manager role required to modify setpoints
access_model.rationale: Prevent unauthorized setpoint changes that could cause crop damage

feature_scope.value: Real-time sensor dashboard per zone, setpoint adjustment UI with manager-role confirmation, threshold alert panel, 90-day historical trend charts, InfluxDB data export
feature_scope.rationale: Covers full operational monitoring and control workflow

mvp_scope.value: Real-time temperature, humidity, CO2, and VPD readings per zone; threshold alerts; setpoint adjustment for manager role; 30-day trend charts
mvp_scope.rationale: Covers core climate monitoring workflow for initial deployment

security_baseline.value: Local user accounts with bcrypt-hashed passwords, role-based setpoint write enforcement in Rust broker, all setpoint changes logged to SQLite audit table
security_baseline.rationale: Prevent unauthorized crop-damaging setpoint changes with full audit trail

frontend_stack.value: Electron with React 18 and Recharts
frontend_stack.rationale: Electron enables local system access; Recharts for high-frequency sensor time-series rendering

backend_stack.value: Rust with Tokio async runtime as local sensor data broker
backend_stack.rationale: Rust provides low-latency MQTT subscription and IPC throughput needed for hundreds of concurrent sensor nodes

data_platform.value: InfluxDB for time-series sensor data; SQLite for user accounts and audit log
data_platform.rationale: InfluxDB native time-series storage with efficient range queries; SQLite for relational audit exports

hosting_target.value: Windows workstation in greenhouse control room, delivered as NSIS installer
hosting_target.rationale: Self-contained local deployment with no cloud dependency

privacy_retention_policy.value: Sensor data retained locally for 90 days in InfluxDB raw; 365 days as 5-minute downsamples; automated purge of raw data older than retention window
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Disk capacity constraint on control room workstation

future_scope.value: Cloud backup of sensor history, multi-facility remote monitoring dashboard, predictive climate model integration
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 enterprise features for multi-greenhouse operators

constraints.value: Must run on Windows 10 workstation with 16GB RAM; no internet connection in control room; installer must complete in under 5 minutes
constraints.rationale: Existing hardware and network constraints in greenhouse facility

observability_baseline.value: Local Grafana dashboard for InfluxDB metrics; application error logs to local file; Windows Event Log integration for crash reporting
observability_baseline.rationale: Local-only observability stack consistent with no-internet constraint

execution_preference.value: Iterative delivery with on-site operator testing after each sprint
execution_preference.rationale: Operator feedback essential for UI usability during high-pressure climate events

project.goal: Replace legacy SCADA screen with real-time climate dashboard and setpoint control
project.target_users: Greenhouse climate control operators

frontend.state: Zustand for sensor ring-buffer state cache

backend.broker: MQTT subscriber connecting to greenhouse MQTT broker
backend.ipc: contextBridge IPC between Rust broker and Electron renderer

security.auth: Local bcrypt user accounts with session tokens in keytar
security.authorization: Manager role required for setpoint writes enforced in Rust broker

data.local: SQLite for users and audit log

devops.environments: dev (macOS with mock MQTT), production (Windows control room)

reasoner_reviews.product: Zone-level visualization is the core operator need. The alert panel must surface alarms without requiring operators to scan all zones manually. Setpoint confirmation dialog is a critical safety gate - operators under pressure can misclick without it.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Rust broker via IPC to Electron renderer is correct for sub-second latency with hundreds of sensor nodes. React state should not hold raw sensor streams - Zustand with a ring buffer per sensor node prevents memory growth. InfluxDB downsampling rules must be configured to prevent disk exhaustion within the 90-day retention window.
reasoner_reviews.security: Setpoint write actions are safety-critical. All setpoint changes must be logged with operator ID, previous value, new value, zone ID, and timestamp before dispatching to the MQTT broker. Role check must happen in the Rust broker process, not only in the React UI, to prevent IPC bypass.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Air-gapped Windows workstation constraint eliminates cloud dependencies cleanly. Electron Builder NSIS installer must bundle InfluxDB and the Rust broker as co-packaged services started by Electron main process on app launch.

frontend.app_structure: Electron main process spawns Rust broker child process and opens React renderer; renderer communicates via contextBridge IPC API
frontend.pages_and_flows: Zone Dashboard (grid of sensor cards per zone showing live temp/humidity/CO2/VPD), Alert Panel (priority-sorted threshold breaches with acknowledge), Setpoint Editor (zone selector, value slider, manager-role confirmation dialog), Historical Trends (90-day Recharts line charts per parameter), User Management (manager-only)
frontend.state_management: Zustand store with per-sensor-node ring buffer (last 60 readings) for Recharts streaming; alert queue with acknowledged/active status
frontend.performance_notes: Recharts components use React.memo and windowed rendering; sensor updates batched from IPC at 250ms intervals to avoid UI jank

backend.service_design: Rust Tokio async service: MQTT client, payload parser, InfluxDB write client, IPC publisher, setpoint command handler with role validation
backend.api_patterns: IPC message types: SENSOR_UPDATE (node_id, zone_id, parameter, value, timestamp), ALERT_TRIGGER (alert_id, zone_id, threshold_value, current_value), SETPOINT_WRITE (command_id, zone_id, parameter, new_value, session_token)
backend.failure_handling: MQTT reconnect with exponential backoff on broker disconnect; IPC buffer flush on renderer reconnect; InfluxDB write retry queue for transient failures
backend.scaling_notes: Rust async tasks handle up to 500 concurrent MQTT sensor topics; InfluxDB batch write at 1-second intervals to reduce write amplification

security.auth_design: SQLite user table with bcrypt-hashed passwords (cost 12); session tokens as signed JWTs stored in OS keytar; auto-logout after 30-minute inactivity
security.authorization_model: Operator role: read-only sensor dashboard and alerts. Manager role: setpoint write access. Role check enforced in Rust broker IPC handler - SETPOINT_WRITE validated against session token role claim before any MQTT publish.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: SQLite setpoint_audit: id, operator_id, zone_id, parameter, previous_value, new_value, confirmed_at, dispatched_at. Exported as CSV on demand for facility safety reviews.
security.security_risks: IPC message spoofing if renderer context is compromised; mitigated by role check in Rust broker process rather than renderer

data.entities: sensor_nodes (node_id, zone_id, type ENUM temp/humidity/co2/vpd, calibration_offset, last_seen_at), greenhouse_zones (zone_id, zone_name, crop_type, area_sqm), setpoint_configurations (zone_id, parameter, target_value, alert_low, alert_high, updated_by, updated_at), climate_readings in InfluxDB (measurement sensor_data, tags node_id/zone_id/parameter, field value FLOAT)
data.storage_design: InfluxDB local with 90-day retention policy and continuous query downsampling raw to 5-minute means retained 365 days. SQLite for users, setpoint config, and audit log.
data.retention_and_deletion: InfluxDB retention policy raw_90d drops chunks after 90 days. 5-minute downsamples retained 365 days. SQLite audit log retained indefinitely per safety record requirements.

devops.deployment_topology: NSIS installer bundles Rust broker binary, InfluxDB Windows binary, and local Grafana into single package; Electron main starts services on app launch
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: cargo test for Rust broker, Jest for React components, electron-builder cross-compile for Windows on ubuntu runner, artifact upload of .exe installer
devops.rollback_strategy: Electron auto-updater can rollback to previous installer version; NSIS uninstaller included for manual rollback
devops.devops_risks: Windows Defender may flag unsigned Electron installer; mitigated by code signing certificate in CI pipeline

ISS-001.title: Rust broker role check missing - setpoint bypass via direct IPC
ISS-001.detail: Previous plan showed role authorization only in React UI confirmation dialog. A malicious or buggy renderer could dispatch SETPOINT_WRITE IPC messages without manager-role validation in the broker, allowing unauthorized setpoint changes that could damage crops.

history.note: Identified in security review of IPC architecture

ISS-002.title: InfluxDB retention policy not configured - disk exhaustion risk
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 plan did not specify InfluxDB retention policy or continuous query downsampling. At 500 sensor nodes reporting every 5 seconds, raw data volume exceeds 4GB per month; without retention policy the 90-day window fills the control room disk.

history.note: Identified in data review of storage sizing

focus_issues.title: Rust broker role check missing - setpoint bypass via direct IPC
focus_issues.detail: Role authorization must be enforced in Rust broker IPC handler, not only in React UI confirmation dialog.

focus_issues.title: InfluxDB retention policy not configured - disk exhaustion risk
focus_issues.detail: Round 1 plan omitted InfluxDB retention policy and continuous query downsampling configuration.

latest_recommendations: Move setpoint role check into Rust broker IPC handler, not React UI confirmation dialog
Add explicit InfluxDB retention policy with continuous query downsampling spec to data section

convergence_state.reason: Two unresolved issues from round 1 blocking pass

previous_audits.summary: Solid architecture for a local desktop application but two security and data concerns block pass. Core Rust/InfluxDB/Electron stack is appropriate for the use case.
strengths: Rust Tokio broker correctly handles high-frequency MQTT ingestion
IPC ring buffer design prevents memory growth in React renderer
concerns: Role check only in React UI is insufficient for safety-critical setpoint authorization
No InfluxDB retention policy specified leaving disk exhaustion risk unaddressed
blocking_issues: Setpoint role authorization must be enforced in Rust broker process
InfluxDB disk exhaustion without retention policy at 500-node ingestion rate
recommendations: Add role check in Rust IPC handler for SETPOINT_WRITE messages validating session token before any MQTT publish
Specify InfluxDB retention policy: 90d raw plus continuous query to 5-min downsamples retained 365d

issue_updates.title: Rust broker role check missing
issue_updates.detail: Security section described role check only in React confirmation dialog, not in broker process

issue_updates.title: InfluxDB retention policy not configured
issue_updates.detail: Data model mentioned InfluxDB but specified no retention window or downsampling rules

previous_plan.title: Greenhouse Climate Dashboard v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Desktop Electron/Rust application for real-time greenhouse sensor monitoring with InfluxDB time-series storage. Rust broker handles MQTT ingestion for hundreds of sensor nodes.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Electron main process hosts Rust broker child and React renderer. Sensor data flows from greenhouse MQTT broker through Rust subscriber to InfluxDB and via IPC to React dashboard.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Local user accounts with bcrypt passwords. Manager role required for setpoint changes enforced via React UI confirmation dialog. Audit log in SQLite.
previous_plan.data_model: InfluxDB for sensor_data measurements. SQLite for users and audit_log. No retention policy specified.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision for greenhouse climate desktop app. ISS-001 requires moving setpoint role check from React UI into Rust broker IPC handler - the broker must validate the session token role claim before dispatching any SETPOINT_WRITE to the MQTT broker. ISS-002 requires specifying InfluxDB retention policy (90d raw, 365d 5-min continuous query downsamples) to prevent disk exhaustion at 500-node ingestion rates. Both fixes are targeted additions to security and data sections.

fix_report.action_taken: Moved setpoint role authorization from React UI confirmation dialog to Rust IPC handler. Broker validates session token role claim (manager required) before processing SETPOINT_WRITE and rejects with SETPOINT_ACK status rejected_auth if operator-role token presented.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Setpoint changes cannot be dispatched to MQTT broker without a valid manager-role session token even if the React renderer is compromised or bypassed.

fix_report.action_taken: Added explicit InfluxDB retention policy (90d raw sensor_data) and continuous query downsampling (1-second readings aggregated to 5-minute means in sensor_data_5m retained 365d) to prevent disk exhaustion at 500-node 5-second reporting interval.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Disk usage stabilizes under 10GB for raw data within 90-day window; downsampled data supports 365-day trend charts without raw volume growth.

target_output.title: Greenhouse Climate Control Desktop Application
target_output.executive_summary: A Windows desktop application built with Electron/React 18 and a Rust Tokio sensor broker for real-time climate monitoring of commercial greenhouse zones. Ingests temperature, humidity, CO2, and VPD readings from hundreds of MQTT sensor nodes, renders live dashboards with threshold alerting, and enables manager-authenticated setpoint adjustments with role check enforced in the Rust broker process. InfluxDB stores 90 days of raw sensor history and 1-year downsampled trend data locally on the control room workstation.
target_output.architecture_overview: Electron main process spawns a Rust Tokio child process as the local sensor data broker. The Rust broker subscribes to the greenhouse MQTT broker over local LAN, normalizes sensor payloads, writes batched measurements to InfluxDB, and pushes real-time updates to the Electron renderer via contextBridge IPC. The React renderer maintains a Zustand ring-buffer store per sensor node for Recharts streaming. Setpoint commands from the React UI flow through IPC to the Rust broker where manager-role session token validation is enforced before any MQTT publish to zone controller topics. All setpoint changes are written to SQLite audit log before dispatch.
target_output.technology_stack: Desktop shell: Electron 31 with contextBridge IPC. Frontend: React 18, Recharts for time-series streaming, Zustand for sensor ring-buffer state. Sensor broker: Rust 1.78 with Tokio, rumqttc MQTT client, influxdb2 write client. Local storage: InfluxDB 2.7 with retention policies and continuous queries; SQLite via rusqlite for users/setpoints/audit. Packaging: Electron Builder NSIS Windows installer bundling InfluxDB and Rust broker binaries.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Zone Dashboard: grid of sensor cards showing live temperature (C), humidity (%), CO2 (ppm), and VPD (kPa) per greenhouse zone with color-coded threshold status. Alert Panel: priority-sorted list of active threshold breaches with zone, parameter, current value, and acknowledge button. Setpoint Editor: zone dropdown, parameter selection, value slider with bounds, manager-role confirmation dialog, IPC dispatch with audit write. Historical Trends: Recharts 90-day line charts for any zone/parameter sourced from InfluxDB range queries against 5-min downsampled data. User Management: manager-only screen for adding and deactivating operator accounts.
target_output.system_components: Electron main process (app lifecycle, IPC bridge, child process management), Rust Tokio sensor broker (MQTT subscriber, IPC publisher, setpoint command handler with role validation, InfluxDB batch writer), React renderer (Zustand ring-buffer store, Recharts dashboard, alert queue, setpoint editor), InfluxDB 2.7 local instance (sensor_data measurement with 90d retention, sensor_data_5m with 365d retention via continuous query), SQLite database (users, setpoint_configurations, setpoint_audit), local Grafana instance (optional trend analysis for facility managers).
target_output.workflows: Sensor Ingestion: Rust broker receives MQTT message on greenhouse/{zone_id}/{node_id}/{parameter}, parses JSON payload, writes to InfluxDB batch buffer, emits SENSOR_UPDATE IPC event; Zustand updates ring buffer; React sensor card re-renders at 250ms batched interval. Setpoint Change: manager opens Setpoint Editor, selects zone and parameter, inputs new value, confirmation dialog shows previous and new values; on confirm, IPC SETPOINT_WRITE dispatched to Rust broker with session_token; broker decodes token, asserts role == manager (rejects with rejected_auth if operator), writes setpoint_audit row to SQLite, publishes MQTT command to greenhouse/setpoints/{zone_id}, emits SETPOINT_ACK to renderer. Threshold Alert: broker evaluates each reading against setpoint_configurations alert bounds; on breach emits ALERT_TRIGGER IPC; React alert panel inserts alert into Zustand queue; operator acknowledges via UI.
target_output.data_model: InfluxDB measurement sensor_data: tags (node_id, zone_id, parameter ENUM temp/humidity/co2/vpd), field (value FLOAT), timestamp NANOSECOND. Retention policy raw_90d: drops raw chunks after 90 days. Continuous query: SELECT mean(value) INTO sensor_data_5m FROM sensor_data GROUP BY time(5m), * - retained 365 days. Estimated raw volume at 500 nodes x 5s: 3.5GB per 30 days, contained within 90d window at under 10GB. SQLite tables: sensor_nodes (node_id PK, zone_id, parameter, calibration_offset REAL, last_seen_at INT), greenhouse_zones (zone_id PK, zone_name, crop_type, area_sqm REAL), setpoint_configurations (zone_id, parameter, target_value REAL, alert_low REAL, alert_high REAL, updated_by, updated_at INT), setpoint_audit (id PK, operator_id, zone_id, parameter, previous_value REAL, new_value REAL, confirmed_at INT, dispatched_at INT), users (id PK, username UNIQUE, password_hash, role ENUM operator/manager, active INT).
target_output.api_design: No external HTTP API - all communication is local IPC. Rust broker IPC messages: SENSOR_UPDATE {node_id, zone_id, parameter, value: f64, timestamp_ns: u64}. ALERT_TRIGGER {alert_id, zone_id, parameter, threshold_type: low/high, threshold_value: f64, current_value: f64}. SETPOINT_WRITE {command_id, zone_id, parameter, new_value: f64, session_token: String} - broker validates token role before processing. SETPOINT_ACK {command_id, status: accepted/rejected_auth/rejected_invalid, actor_id}. InfluxDB Flux queries from Electron main process for historical trend data at user request.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Local user accounts in SQLite with bcrypt-hashed passwords (cost factor 12). Session tokens as signed JWTs with role claim stored in OS keytar; auto-logout after 30-minute inactivity. Role enforcement: SETPOINT_WRITE IPC messages validated in Rust broker by decoding session JWT and asserting role == manager before any MQTT publish or SQLite audit write; SETPOINT_ACK with rejected_auth returned if operator-role token presented. React confirmation dialog is UX safety layer only, not the authorization boundary. Setpoint audit log written to SQLite setpoint_audit before MQTT publish in same Rust transaction. Air-gapped local network - no outbound internet connections.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Electron Builder NSIS installer packages Rust broker binary, InfluxDB 2.7 Windows binary, and optional Grafana into single installer. Electron main starts InfluxDB and Rust broker as child processes on app launch with IPC health check before showing UI. GitHub Actions CI: cargo test, Jest, electron-builder Windows cross-compile on ubuntu runner, artifact upload of signed .exe installer. Dev environment: macOS with mock MQTT broker (EMQX Docker) replaying recorded sensor payloads. Production: Windows 10 workstation, 500GB SSD for InfluxDB storage headroom. Code signing certificate to prevent Windows Defender prompts.
target_output.observability: Local Grafana connected to InfluxDB for historical sensor trend analysis by facility managers. Rust broker structured JSON logs to AppData/greenhouse-monitor/broker.log with 100MB rotation and 7-file retention. Electron renderer errors written to AppData error log. Windows Event Log for app start/stop/crash. Key operational indicators: broker MQTT connection status, IPC throughput, InfluxDB write lag, active alert count per zone.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: One-time development cost; no recurring cloud infrastructure. InfluxDB and Rust broker run on existing Windows workstation. At 500 sensor nodes at 5-second intervals, 90-day raw retention uses approximately 10GB; 365-day 5-minute downsamples add approximately 200MB. Workstation SSD recommendation: 500GB minimum for comfortable headroom. Future multi-facility path: add InfluxDB Cloud sync layer without changing local architecture.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (4 weeks): Rust broker MQTT subscriber with IPC, React zone dashboard with Recharts streaming, InfluxDB integration with 90d retention policy and continuous query downsampling, SQLite auth, manager-role setpoint editor with broker-enforced role check and audit log, NSIS installer. Phase 2 (4 weeks): threshold alert panel with acknowledge workflow, 90-day historical trend chart screen, local Grafana configuration, operator user management screen. Phase 3 (4 weeks): multi-facility architecture design, InfluxDB Cloud sync prototype, Electron auto-updater for future schema-independent OTA updates.
target_output.development_guidelines: Rust: clippy clean with deny(warnings); all IPC message handlers must validate session token before any side effect. React: TypeScript strict mode, React.memo on sensor card components. Sensor update batching: IPC events batched at 250ms in Rust broker to prevent renderer frame drops. SQLite writes: setpoint_audit write in same transaction with MQTT publish readiness check - roll back if MQTT publish subsequently fails. Testing: Rust unit tests for IPC message parsing and role validation; React Jest tests for alert queue state transitions.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: InfluxDB process crash causes sensor data gap during active growing event. Mitigation: Rust broker buffers last 60 seconds in memory and replays to InfluxDB on reconnect. Risk: Windows Defender flags unsigned installer. Mitigation: code signing certificate in CI. Tradeoff: bundled InfluxDB in installer over separate service install simplifies non-technical facility staff deployment. Tradeoff: SQLite for audit log over InfluxDB measurement - SQLite chosen for relational CSV export support required for safety reviews.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Rust over Go for broker: ownership model prevents data races in concurrent MQTT handlers without mutex overhead. InfluxDB 2.7 over TimescaleDB: native Flux continuous queries simplify downsampling without PostgreSQL extension management. Setpoint role check confirmed in Rust broker per ISS-001 resolution. InfluxDB 90-day raw retention plus 365-day 5-min downsamples confirmed per ISS-002 resolution.

metadata.notes: Round 2 revision fixing Rust broker role-check bypass (ISS-001) and InfluxDB disk exhaustion risk (ISS-002) for greenhouse climate desktop app.

project_goal.value: Python ML utilities library for agricultural remote sensing providing Sentinel-2 atmospheric correction, SCL cloud masking, NDVI/NDRE/NDWI index calculation, and analysis-ready GeoTIFF export
project_goal.rationale: Addresses repeated boilerplate in agronomic ML pipelines consuming Sentinel-2 imagery

target_users.value: Agricultural data scientists, remote sensing researchers, and agronomists building crop analysis workflows in Python
target_users.rationale: Primary users identified in internal survey of agtech ML team

project_class.rationale: Distributed as a PyPI package; no hosted service or UI surface

capabilities.rationale: Pure Python processing library; no auth, frontend, or hosted infrastructure

complexity_level.rationale: Geospatial raster processing with external Sen2Cor subprocess wrapper and multiprocessing batch mode

risk_level.rationale: No network exposure; processes local files only; no personal data handled

data_sensitivity.rationale: Library operates on satellite imagery files; no personal or financial data in scope

external_exposure.rationale: Library runs in local Python environment; no server or public API surface

access_model.value: Public PyPI package, no authentication required
access_model.rationale: Open library distribution with no access controls

feature_scope.value: Sentinel-2 SAFE scene loader, Sen2Cor atmospheric correction wrapper with TOA fallback, SCL cloud mask generator, NDVI/NDRE/NDWI index calculators, field boundary crop utility, analysis-ready COG GeoTIFF exporter, multiprocessing batch pipeline
feature_scope.rationale: Full pipeline from raw scene to analysis-ready raster outputs

mvp_scope.value: Scene loader, SCL cloud masking, NDVI and NDRE calculation, GeoTIFF export, single-scene CLI entry point
mvp_scope.rationale: Covers the most common single-scene workflow for immediate usefulness

security_baseline.value: No network calls; input path validation to prevent directory traversal; dependency pinning in pyproject.toml; no shell=True in subprocess calls
security_baseline.rationale: Minimal attack surface for a local file-processing library

frontend_stack.value: None - library only; CLI entry point via argparse
frontend_stack.rationale: No UI surface; developer-facing CLI for single-scene workflows

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with rasterio, NumPy, scikit-image
backend_stack.rationale: Standard geospatial Python stack; rasterio wraps GDAL for GeoTIFF I/O via binary wheels

data_platform.value: Local filesystem only - reads/writes GeoTIFF files; no database
data_platform.rationale: Library operates on local file paths; no persistent storage layer needed

hosting_target.value: PyPI distribution via Trusted Publisher GitHub Actions OIDC
hosting_target.rationale: Standard Python package distribution without long-lived API tokens

privacy_retention_policy.value: No data retained by library; all outputs written to user-specified paths; no telemetry or usage reporting
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Data minimization principle; library has no persistent state

future_scope.value: Sentinel-1 SAR support, Planet Labs imagery adapter, cloud-native COG streaming mode for remote GCS/S3 scenes
future_scope.rationale: Expanding to additional sensor types and cloud-native workflows in future releases

constraints.value: Must work without Sen2Cor installed with graceful degradation to TOA reflectance; no system GDAL dependency - rasterio binary wheels only
constraints.rationale: GDAL system dependency causes install failures in many ML environments; Sen2Cor not always available

observability_baseline.value: Python logging via agrirs.logger namespace; verbose flag on CLI; no external monitoring service
observability_baseline.rationale: Library-appropriate observability via standard Python logging

execution_preference.value: Test-driven development with pytest; publish to PyPI on every tagged release via GitHub Actions
execution_preference.rationale: High test coverage essential for scientific accuracy of spectral index calculations

project.goal: Reusable Python library abstracting Sentinel-2 pre-processing for agronomic ML workflows
project.target_users: Agricultural data scientists and remote sensing researchers

security.input_validation: Path traversal prevention on all file inputs

data.io: rasterio for GeoTIFF read/write; no database
data.temp_files: System tempdir with context manager cleanup

devops.ci: GitHub Actions pytest matrix Python 3.10/3.11/3.12, ruff, mypy --strict, coverage >=90%
devops.publish: PyPI Trusted Publisher OIDC on tag push

constraints.gdal: No system GDAL - rasterio binary wheels only
constraints.sen2cor: Optional subprocess with graceful TOA fallback

reasoner_reviews.product: The library fills a real gap in the agtech ML toolchain. The most-used path will be single-scene NDVI calculation - that entry point must have a clean three-line API. Batch multiprocessing mode is a strong differentiator for large-dataset users working with full-season imagery archives.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Module structure should follow pipeline stages: io, correction, masks, indices, export, batch. Each stage independently importable for custom pipeline composition. Sen2Cor subprocess wrapper must handle platform path differences and version detection. Pipeline builder class enables composable workflows for advanced users.
reasoner_reviews.security: Input validation is the primary concern for a file-processing library. All path arguments resolved to absolute paths via Path.resolve() and validated against a base directory in batch mode to prevent traversal. No network calls permitted in library core. Sen2Cor subprocess uses validated path components only - never shell=True.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Rasterio-only GDAL constraint achievable using conda-forge or PyPI binary wheels. Sen2Cor optional detection via shutil.which at wrapper init time; if absent raises Sen2CorNotFoundError with installation instructions unless graceful_fallback=True.

backend.service_design: Python package with five public modules: agrirs.io (scene loading and GeoTIFF export), agrirs.correction (Sen2Cor wrapper and TOA fallback), agrirs.masks (SCL cloud mask generation), agrirs.indices (NDVI, NDRE, NDWI calculators), agrirs.batch (multiprocessing pipeline runner). CLI entry via agrirs.__main__ using argparse.
backend.api_patterns: Functional API: load_scene(path) -> Scene, apply_correction(scene) -> Scene, generate_cloud_mask(scene) -> np.ndarray, calculate_index(scene, index='ndvi') -> IndexResult, export_geotiff(result, scene, output_path). Pipeline builder: Pipeline([correction, cloud_mask, ndvi]).run(scene_path, output_dir).
