backend.failure_handling: BandNotAvailableError for missing required bands, CloudCoverageExceededError for scenes above configurable threshold, Sen2CorNotFoundError if binary absent and fallback disabled, InvalidPathError for traversal attempts in batch mode.
backend.scaling_notes: Batch mode uses multiprocessing.Pool with worker count defaulting to os.cpu_count() - 1; stateless worker functions receive scene path and output directory; no shared mutable state between workers.

data.entities: Scene dataclass: bands (dict[str, DatasetReader] keyed by band ID e.g. B04/B08/B8A), crs (pyproj.CRS), transform (affine.Affine), acquisition_date (date), cloud_coverage_pct (float), granule_id (str). IndexResult: array (np.ndarray float32), index_name (ndvi/ndre/ndwi), no_data_mask (np.ndarray bool), scene_id (str). BatchResult: processed (list[str]), failed (list[tuple[str, Exception]]), elapsed_seconds (float).
data.storage_design: No persistent storage. Temporary files in system tempdir within context manager with finally cleanup. Sen2Cor intermediate files in temp dir cleaned on exit. Final outputs to user-specified output_dir.
data.data_risks: Large Sentinel-2 scenes up to 800MB per granule in batch mode can exhaust disk if temp cleanup fails on exception; mitigated by context manager with finally block and explicit temp directory cleanup.
data.migration_notes: Semantic versioning; breaking API changes gated behind major version bumps; deprecation warnings added one minor version before removal.

devops.deployment_topology: PyPI package distribution via GitHub Actions Trusted Publisher OIDC; no hosted infrastructure
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: matrix test on Python 3.10/3.11/3.12 on ubuntu-latest, ruff linting, mypy --strict, pytest with 90% coverage gate, Codecov badge reporting. Release workflow: tag push triggers PyPI publish via pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish OIDC.
devops.observability_stack: Python logging via agrirs.logger; DEBUG shows per-band read timing and correction step durations; INFO shows scene summary and index result stats; WARNING for high cloud coverage scenes over 60%. No external monitoring.
devops.devops_risks: Sen2Cor subprocess wrapper breaks on new Sen2Cor CLI versions that change flag signatures; mitigated by version detection and documented supported version range in README.

target_output.thinking_summary: Python library for Sentinel-2 pre-processing targeted at agronomic ML workflows. Key decisions: functional API organized around pipeline stages enables composable custom workflows; Sen2Cor subprocess wrapper with graceful TOA fallback handles the optional dependency constraint; multiprocessing batch mode uses stateless worker functions to avoid shared-state bugs across large scene batches; 90% pytest coverage enforced in CI ensures scientific correctness of NDVI/NDRE spectral index formulas.
target_output.title: Agricultural Remote Sensing Utilities Library (agrirs)
target_output.executive_summary: A Python library on PyPI providing a composable pipeline for Sentinel-2 satellite imagery pre-processing in agricultural ML workflows. Exposes functional modules for scene loading, Sen2Cor atmospheric correction with TOA fallback, SCL cloud masking, NDVI/NDRE/NDWI index calculation, and Cloud Optimized GeoTIFF export. Batch mode uses Python multiprocessing for large-dataset throughput. No system GDAL dependency - rasterio binary wheels only.
target_output.architecture_overview: Package organized into five public modules following the remote sensing processing chain: agrirs.io handles Sentinel-2 SAFE directory loading and COG GeoTIFF output via rasterio, agrirs.correction wraps Sen2Cor subprocess with automatic TOA reflectance fallback when Sen2Cor is absent, agrirs.masks generates cloud and shadow masks from the SCL Scene Classification Layer band with morphological dilation, agrirs.indices computes NDVI (NIR-Red/NIR+Red using B08 and B04), NDRE (NIR-RedEdge/NIR+RedEdge using B08 and B05), and NDWI (Green-NIR/Green+NIR using B03 and B08) as float32 NumPy arrays, and agrirs.batch provides a multiprocessing pool runner for directory-level batch processing. A Pipeline builder composes correction, masking, and index steps into a single callable.
target_output.technology_stack: Runtime: Python 3.12. Core dependencies: rasterio 1.3 (GeoTIFF I/O via binary wheel, no system GDAL required), NumPy 1.26 (array math for spectral index formulas), scikit-image 0.22 (morphological dilation for SCL cloud mask buffering), pyproj 3.6 (CRS validation). Optional: Sen2Cor 2.11 via subprocess. Dev: pytest 8, coverage.py, ruff, mypy, hypothesis for property-based index formula tests. Distribution: PyPI via Trusted Publisher OIDC GitHub Actions.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Scene Loader: parse Sentinel-2 SAFE directory, open per-band GeoTIFF files as rasterio DatasetReaders, extract acquisition metadata from MTD_MSIL2A.xml. Atmospheric Correction: wrap Sen2Cor CLI with version detection; raise Sen2CorNotFoundError with install guidance if absent; scale to TOA reflectance if graceful_fallback=True. Cloud Masking: read SCL band, classify ESA class codes into cloud/shadow/clear, apply morphological dilation by configurable buffer_pixels. Index Calculator: NDVI using B08 and B04, NDRE using B08 and B05, NDWI using B03 and B08; float32 arrays with NaN for no-data pixels. COG Export: write index arrays with original CRS and Affine transform, nodata=-9999, Cloud Optimized GeoTIFF profile. Batch Pipeline: Pool(workers) processing scene list with BatchResult error collection.
target_output.system_components: agrirs.io.SceneLoader (SAFE directory parser, band file resolver, metadata XML reader), agrirs.correction.Sen2CorWrapper (subprocess manager, version check, TOA fallback), agrirs.masks.CloudMaskGenerator (SCL band reader, ESA class code mapper, morphological dilator using scikit-image), agrirs.indices.IndexCalculator (NDVI/NDRE/NDWI formula implementations with band availability validation), agrirs.export.GeoTIFFExporter (rasterio write with COG profile and CRS preservation), agrirs.batch.BatchPipeline (multiprocessing Pool manager, error collector), agrirs.__main__ CLI (argparse single-scene and batch modes).
target_output.workflows: Single-Scene NDVI Pipeline: researcher loads scene = load_scene('/data/S2A_MSIL2A_20240601.SAFE'), generates mask = generate_cloud_mask(scene, buffer_pixels=3), computes ndvi = calculate_index(scene, 'ndvi', mask=mask), exports export_geotiff(ndvi, scene, '/output/ndvi_20240601.tif') - three lines covering the full pre-processing chain. Batch Processing: researcher creates Pipeline([Sen2CorCorrection(fallback=True), CloudMask(buffer_pixels=3), NDVIIndex()]) and calls results = pipeline.run_batch(scene_dir='/data/scenes/', output_dir='/output/', workers=4); BatchResult.failed reports scenes with exception tracebacks for debugging.
target_output.data_model: Scene dataclass: bands (dict[str, rasterio.DatasetReader] keyed by band ID B04/B08/B8A/etc), crs (pyproj.CRS), transform (affine.Affine), acquisition_date (datetime.date), cloud_coverage_pct (float from metadata XML), granule_id (str), processing_level (ENUM L1C/L2A). IndexResult: array (np.ndarray float32 shape HxW), index_name (ENUM ndvi/ndre/ndwi), no_data_mask (np.ndarray bool True where masked), scene_id (str), computed_at (datetime). BatchResult: processed (list[str] output paths), failed (list[tuple[str, Exception]]), elapsed_seconds (float), worker_count (int).
target_output.api_design: Public functional API: load_scene(safe_path: Path | str, bands: list[str] | None = None) -> Scene. generate_cloud_mask(scene: Scene, classes: list[int] = SCL_CLOUD_CLASSES, buffer_pixels: int = 0) -> np.ndarray. calculate_index(scene: Scene, index: Literal['ndvi','ndre','ndwi'], mask: np.ndarray | None = None) -> IndexResult. export_geotiff(result: IndexResult, scene: Scene, output_path: Path, cog: bool = True) -> Path. Pipeline(steps).run(scene_path, output_dir) -> IndexResult. Pipeline.run_batch(scene_dir, output_dir, workers) -> BatchResult. CLI: python -m agrirs process --scene /path.SAFE --index ndvi --output /out/ [--no-correction] [--workers N].
target_output.security_and_compliance: Input path validation: all path arguments resolved to absolute paths via Path.resolve(); batch mode validates resolved scene paths are within scene_dir to prevent traversal attacks. No network calls in library core. Sen2Cor subprocess arguments from validated path components only - never shell=True. Dependency pinning in pyproject.toml with upper bounds; Dependabot for security updates. No telemetry. ESA Copernicus data licensing: documentation notes users must comply with Copernicus data license; library does not enforce or circumvent access controls.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: PyPI distribution via GitHub Actions Trusted Publisher OIDC - no long-lived tokens. Release workflow: tag push triggers full test matrix, python -m build wheel and sdist, pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish upload. Test matrix: Python 3.10, 3.11, 3.12 on ubuntu-latest. Coverage gate: 90% line coverage in CI with Codecov report. Development install: pip install -e '.[dev]'.
target_output.observability: Python logging via logging.getLogger('agrirs'). DEBUG: per-band open timing ms, Sen2Cor subprocess stdout, worker assignments. INFO: acquisition date, cloud coverage pct, index result min/max/mean, output file path and size. WARNING: cloud_coverage_pct over 60%. ERROR: Sen2CorNotFoundError, BandNotAvailableError, InvalidPathError with full traceback. Library never calls logging.basicConfig() - users configure their own handler.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero hosting cost - PyPI distribution is free. Single-scene NDVI pipeline on 100m resolution 60km x 60km Sentinel-2 tile completes in under 10 seconds on a 4-core workstation. Batch mode at 4 workers processes approximately 20 scenes per hour with Sen2Cor correction. Memory footprint approximately 2GB RAM per worker for a full 10m resolution granule.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (4 weeks): Scene loader for L2A SAFE, SCL cloud mask generator, NDVI and NDRE calculators, COG GeoTIFF exporter, CLI single-scene entry point, pytest suite with 90% coverage, PyPI alpha release. Phase 2 (2 weeks): Sen2Cor subprocess wrapper with TOA fallback, NDWI calculator, multiprocessing batch pipeline, Pipeline builder class, hypothesis property-based tests for index formula bounds [-1, 1]. Phase 3 (2 weeks): field boundary crop utility using shapely, type stubs for mypy consumers, conda-forge feedstock, MkDocs documentation with agronomic workflow examples.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 type hints required on all public functions; mypy --strict must pass. Ruff formatting and linting in CI. No mutable default arguments. All temp files in context managers with finally cleanup. Public API documented with NumPy docstring format including Parameters, Returns, Raises, and Examples. No external HTTP calls in library core. Index formula implementations must have hypothesis property-based tests asserting output range [-1, 1] for valid band inputs.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Sen2Cor CLI changes in new versions break subprocess wrapper. Mitigation: version detection at init, documented supported version range, integration test against Sen2Cor 2.11 in optional CI matrix. Risk: rasterio binary wheel unavailable for new Python releases. Mitigation: pin tested Python version range in pyproject.toml classifiers. Tradeoff: rasterio-only over GDAL Python bindings sacrifices vector operation access but eliminates the primary install failure mode in ML environments. Tradeoff: multiprocessing over asyncio for batch mode - satellite image processing is CPU-bound, making multiprocessing correct over async I/O concurrency.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed rasterio binary wheels over GDAL Python bindings per engineering constraint. Functional API over class-based chosen for simpler single-function imports in Jupyter notebooks. Sen2Cor fallback to TOA confirmed as default graceful behavior. Batch worker default set to os.cpu_count() - 1 leaving one core for OS responsiveness.

metadata.notes: Agricultural remote sensing Python library for Sentinel-2 processing with NDVI/NDRE/NDWI and multiprocessing batch mode.

profile.domain: AI-Assisted Weed Identification and Herbicide Recommendation

project_goal.value: AI-assisted weed species identification and herbicide recommendation system using in-field photos to classify weed species and suggest EPA-registered herbicide programs filtered by crop type, growth stage, and local resistance history
project_goal.rationale: Core value: reduce herbicide misapplication and resistance development via data-driven species-specific recommendations

target_users.value: Row crop farmers, crop consultants, and agronomists managing weed pressure in corn, soybean, and wheat fields
target_users.rationale: Primary personas identified in market research

project_class.rationale: Core value delivered by ML classification model with LLM-assisted recommendation generation and RAG retrieval

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, ai_llm, data, analytics, integrations
capabilities.rationale: Mobile UI, classification API, LLM recommendation generation, species and resistance data, usage analytics, herbicide label database integration

complexity_level.rationale: ML classification pipeline, LLM RAG integration, resistance database lookups, EPA label compliance filtering, offline-capable mobile app

risk_level.rationale: Incorrect herbicide recommendations could cause crop damage or regulatory violations; requires EPA label compliance constraint and recommendation disclaimer

data_sensitivity.rationale: Farm field locations and scouting data are proprietary; no personal health or financial data

external_exposure.rationale: Farmer and consultant accounts required; no unauthenticated access to classification API

access_model.value: Account-based RBAC with farmer and agronomist roles; agronomists manage multiple client farms
access_model.rationale: Agronomists see all managed client fields; farmers see only their own data

feature_scope.value: In-field weed photo capture and classification, top-3 species identification with confidence scores, herbicide program recommendations filtered by EPA label and resistance history, field scouting event logging, regional resistance trend analytics
feature_scope.rationale: Complete weed management workflow from identification to actionable recommendation

mvp_scope.value: Photo capture, ML species classification, herbicide recommendation with EPA label filter and resistance exclusion, scouting event logging, farmer and agronomist accounts
mvp_scope.rationale: Core identification-to-recommendation loop is sufficient for market validation

security_baseline.value: Cognito authentication, HTTPS only, image uploads via S3 pre-signed URLs, herbicide recommendations include mandatory EPA label disclaimer with logged acceptance timestamp
security_baseline.rationale: Protect field data and establish regulatory safe harbor for recommendation liability

frontend_stack.value: React Native with Expo
frontend_stack.rationale: Cross-platform mobile iOS/Android with expo-camera and Expo EAS for OTA updates

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI
backend_stack.rationale: ML inference pipeline naturally in Python; FastAPI provides async handling and OpenAPI docs

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 with Redis 7
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL for species/resistance/herbicide relational data; Redis for inference result caching by image hash

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in us-east-1
hosting_target.rationale: Managed containers for ML inference service with GPU-optional task definition

privacy_retention_policy.value: Field scouting photos retained 2 years in S3; classification results and recommendations retained 5 years; users can delete their own scouting events
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Agronomic record retention expectations; user data deletion rights

future_scope.value: Drone imagery batch classification, prescription herbicide rate map generation, integration with farm management software APIs
future_scope.rationale: Expansion from point identification to field-level prescription generation

constraints.value: Herbicide recommendations must only include EPA-registered products for identified crop; LLM must query herbicide label database - not generate recommendations from parametric knowledge alone
constraints.rationale: EPA FIFRA compliance - recommendations outside label use are illegal

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM with ML model confidence distribution metrics, classification latency tracking, recommendation post-validation failure rate
observability_baseline.rationale: Track model drift and recommendation quality including LLM hallucination rate over time

execution_preference.value: Agile sprints with agronomist advisory review of recommendation quality before production release
execution_preference.rationale: Domain expert sign-off on recommendation logic critical before farmer-facing release

llm_integration.value: GPT-4o via OpenAI API for herbicide recommendation narrative; retrieval-augmented with herbicide label database; post-generation validator cross-checks all product names against retrieved set
llm_integration.rationale: LLM provides natural-language context while RAG constraint ensures EPA label compliance; validator catches hallucinations

project.goal: AI-powered weed identification and EPA-compliant herbicide recommendation for row crop farmers
project.target_users: Farmers and crop consultants

frontend.state: React Query for server state, Zustand for local observation queue
frontend.ota: Expo EAS Updates for OTA schema changes

backend.ml: PyTorch CNN classifier fine-tuned on labeled weed image dataset
backend.llm: OpenAI GPT-4o with RAG retrieval from herbicide label database

security.uploads: S3 pre-signed URL for image upload
security.compliance: EPA FIFRA label constraint on all herbicide recommendations

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 for species, resistance, herbicide, and scouting data
data.cache: Redis 7 for inference result cache keyed by image hash
data.storage: S3 for field photos with per-user prefix

devops.hosting: ECS Fargate with GPU-optional task definition
devops.environments: dev, staging, production

constraints.herbicide_data: All recommendations from database only, no LLM parametric knowledge
constraints.epa: Only registered products for identified crop type

reasoner_reviews.product: The identification-to-recommendation loop is the core value proposition. Confidence score display is important - farmers need to know when to distrust a low-confidence identification before applying herbicide. The resistance history filter is a genuine differentiator versus generic herbicide lookup tools.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: PyTorch CNN inference on ECS Fargate with Redis caching for identical image hashes is correct for this query volume. The RAG constraint on LLM recommendations is architecturally critical - the retrieval step against herbicide label database must be auditable and logged per recommendation event. GPT-4o should receive structured context objects not raw images to keep token costs bounded.
reasoner_reviews.security: EPA FIFRA compliance is not optional. Every herbicide recommendation event must log: identified species, crop type, growth stage, retrieved herbicide product IDs, LLM prompt hash, LLM response text, post-validation result, and disclaimer acceptance timestamp. Pre-signed S3 URLs prevent large image files from transiting the API service.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: LLM must receive retrieved herbicide label records as structured context. Post-generation validator must parse every product name in LLM response and reject the response if any name not found in the retrieved product set, retrying once with a stricter prompt before falling back to structured recommendation without narrative.

frontend.app_structure: React Native Expo app with three stacks: Identify (camera + results), Scouting (field event log), Insights (resistance trend charts)
frontend.pages_and_flows: Camera Screen (expo-camera viewfinder, crop type selector, capture button), Classification Result (top-3 species cards with confidence bars, 'Get Recommendations' CTA), Herbicide Recommendation (RAG-sourced product cards grouped by HRAC mode of action, LLM narrative, EPA label link, disclaimer accept), Scouting Event Log (GPS-tagged observation history), Resistance Trends (county-level charts per chemistry group)
frontend.state_management: Zustand local observation queue for offline-captured photos; React Query for classification and recommendation fetches; pending observations sync on connectivity restore
frontend.frontend_security_notes: Image upload via pre-signed S3 URL - app requests URL from API, uploads directly to S3. Disclaimer accept event logged server-side before recommendation text displayed to user.

backend.service_design: FastAPI with three domain modules: inference (CNN classification pipeline), recommendations (RAG retrieval plus LLM generation with post-validation), scouting (event CRUD and analytics)
backend.llm_integration_design: GPT-4o called with structured prompt: identified weed species and confidence, crop type and BBCH growth stage, retrieved herbicide_products (trade_name, active_ingredients, mode_of_action_group HRAC, registered_crops, label_url), local resistance_records excluding resistant HRAC groups. Post-generation validator parses product names from LLM response and cross-checks against retrieved set; rejects and retries once on hallucination detection; falls back to structured product list without narrative after second failure.
backend.api_patterns: POST /v1/classify (S3 key, crop_type, growth_stage_bbch) -> ClassificationResult with top-3 species confidence. POST /v1/recommend (classification_result_id, field_id, county_fips) -> HerbicideRecommendation with RAG products and LLM narrative. POST /v1/recommend/{id}/accept records disclaimer_accepted_at. POST /v1/scouting-events. GET /v1/analytics/resistance-trends.
backend.failure_handling: Inference timeout over 5s returns cached result or prompts photo retake. LLM API failure falls back to structured product list without narrative, labeled as basic recommendation. Image hash cache hit returns stored ClassificationResult without GPU inference.

security.auth_design: Cognito user pools with email/password; MFA enforced for agronomist accounts managing client farm data
security.authorization_model: RBAC: farmer restricted to own field data via user_id filter; agronomist accesses client fields only with field_sharing_consent record. S3 photos in per-user prefix with bucket policy denying cross-user access.
security.privacy_controls: Field GPS coordinates stored with farmer account isolation. User-initiated scouting event deletion cascades to S3 photo removal.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: recommendation_audit table: recommendation_id, classification_result_id, retrieved_product_ids array, llm_prompt_hash, llm_response_text, post_validation_passed, retry_count, disclaimer_accepted_at, user_id, created_at.

data.entities: weed_species (species_id, common_name, scientific_name, family, typical_crops), herbicide_products (product_id, trade_name, active_ingredients, mode_of_action_group HRAC, registered_crops, epa_reg_number, label_url, efficacy_rating), resistance_records (species_id, county_fips, crop_type, moa_group HRAC, confirmed_resistant, source, recorded_year), field_scouting_events (event_id, field_id, user_id, gps_lat, gps_lon, crop_type, growth_stage_bbch, photo_s3_key, classification_result_id, created_at), classification_results (result_id, image_hash, top1_species_id, top1_confidence, top2_species_id, top2_confidence, top3_species_id, top3_confidence, model_version, inferred_at)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 for all relational data. Redis 7 for classification result cache (image_hash -> result_id, 24h TTL). S3 for field photos with SSE-S3 per-user prefix.
data.analytics_events: classification_requested, classification_returned with confidence bucket, recommendation_generated, recommendation_accepted (disclaimer), herbicide_product_clicked (label link), resistance_filter_applied

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate inference service (2 vCPU 8GB RAM; GPU task g4dn.xlarge optional), recommendation service (1 vCPU 4GB), PostgreSQL RDS multi-AZ, ElastiCache Redis
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: ruff, mypy, pytest 85% coverage, Docker build ECR push, ECS rolling deploy. Expo EAS Build for iOS/Android on tag. Expo EAS Update for OTA JS bundle.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM: ml.classification.confidence histogram, ml.inference.latency_ms, recommendation.rag_retrieval_ms, recommendation.llm_latency_ms, recommendation.post_validation_fail_rate. PagerDuty: post_validation_fail_rate over 5% in 10 minutes, p95 inference latency over 8s.
devops.devops_risks: OpenAI API unavailability during peak spring scouting season; mitigated by rule-based fallback recommendation from herbicide_products table without LLM narrative

target_output.thinking_summary: AI system for weed identification with two architectural constraints driving key decisions: ML classification output must drive RAG herbicide lookup (not LLM parametric memory), and a post-generation validator must catch hallucinated product names before they reach farmers. PyTorch CNN on ECS Fargate with Redis image-hash caching handles inference. GPT-4o receives structured context objects, not raw images, keeping token costs bounded. The recommendation_audit table with logged disclaimer_accepted_at creates the regulatory safe harbor for EPA FIFRA compliance.
target_output.title: AI Weed Identification and Herbicide Recommendation System
target_output.executive_summary: A mobile-first AI system for row crop farmers and agronomists that classifies in-field weed photos using a fine-tuned PyTorch CNN and generates EPA-compliant herbicide program recommendations via GPT-4o retrieval-augmented against a curated herbicide label database. React Native/Expo mobile app with FastAPI backend on AWS ECS Fargate and PostgreSQL 16. All recommendations constrained to database-retrieved products with post-generation hallucination validation, mandatory EPA disclaimer acceptance logging, and a full recommendation audit trail.
target_output.architecture_overview: React Native Expo mobile app captures field photos and submits to a FastAPI inference service on ECS Fargate. The inference module runs a PyTorch CNN classifier returning top-3 weed species with confidence scores cached in Redis by image hash. The recommendation module performs RAG retrieval: herbicide_products and resistance_records are queried by identified species, crop type, and county to build a structured context document passed to GPT-4o with a system prompt restricting recommendations to retrieved products only. A post-generation validator checks every product name in the LLM response against the retrieved set and retries once on hallucination detection. All recommendation events logged to recommendation_audit before disclaimer shown to user.
target_output.technology_stack: Mobile: React Native 0.74 with Expo SDK 51, expo-camera, Expo EAS Build and Updates. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, PyTorch 2.3 CNN inference, OpenAI Python SDK GPT-4o. Database: PostgreSQL 16 (species/herbicide/resistance/scouting), Redis 7 (inference cache). Storage: AWS S3. Hosting: ECS Fargate with GPU-optional task definition. Auth: AWS Cognito. Monitoring: Datadog APM. CI/CD: GitHub Actions plus Expo EAS.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Photo Classification: farmer captures canopy-level weed photo, app uploads to S3 via pre-signed URL, sends S3 key to POST /v1/classify; CNN returns top-3 weed species (e.g., waterhemp 87%, Palmer amaranth 9%, lambsquarters 4%) with confidence bars. Herbicide Recommendation: user selects primary species, system queries resistance_records for county, retrieves registered herbicide products excluding resistant HRAC groups, constructs GPT-4o prompt with retrieved products, post-validates output, displays product cards grouped by HRAC mode of action with label links and LLM narrative. Scouting Event Log: GPS-tagged observation history per field. Resistance Trends: county-level charts of resistance confirmation rates per HRAC group.
target_output.system_components: Expo mobile app (camera capture, Zustand offline queue, Cognito auth), FastAPI inference service (CNN endpoint, RAG recommendation endpoint, scouting CRUD), PyTorch CNN model container on ECS Fargate, Redis inference cache (image_hash 24h TTL), PostgreSQL 16 (weed_species, herbicide_products, resistance_records, scouting_events, classification_results, recommendation_audit), S3 (per-user prefix SSE-S3 photos), OpenAI GPT-4o API (RAG-constrained recommendation generation), Datadog APM (confidence distribution, post-validation fail rate).
target_output.workflows: Weed Classification: farmer opens Camera Screen, selects crop type and BBCH growth stage, captures photo; app requests S3 pre-signed PUT URL, uploads directly to S3, submits POST /v1/classify with s3_key; inference service checks Redis cache by image_hash, on miss loads from S3 and runs PyTorch CNN forward pass, stores ClassificationResult in PostgreSQL and caches in Redis, returns top-3 species. Herbicide Recommendation: user taps 'Get Recommendations'; POST /v1/recommend triggers RAG: query herbicide_products WHERE crop_type = corn AND species_id = waterhemp ORDER BY efficacy_rating LIMIT 20, join resistance_records to exclude resistant HRAC groups; GPT-4o called with structured JSON context; post-generation validator parses product names and checks against retrieved set; disclaimer modal shown; on acceptance recommendation_audit row written with disclaimer_accepted_at.
target_output.data_model: weed_species (species_id UUID PK, common_name, scientific_name, family, typical_crops TEXT[]). herbicide_products (product_id UUID PK, trade_name, active_ingredients TEXT[], mode_of_action_group VARCHAR(10) HRAC, registered_crops TEXT[], epa_reg_number, label_url, efficacy_rating FLOAT). resistance_records (id UUID PK, species_id FK, county_fips CHAR(5), crop_type, moa_group VARCHAR(10), confirmed_resistant BOOL, source, recorded_year INT). field_scouting_events (event_id UUID PK, field_id UUID, user_id UUID FK, gps_lat FLOAT, gps_lon FLOAT, crop_type, growth_stage_bbch VARCHAR(10), photo_s3_key, classification_result_id FK, notes TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). classification_results (result_id UUID PK, image_hash SHA256, top1_species_id FK, top1_confidence FLOAT, top2_species_id FK, top2_confidence FLOAT, top3_species_id FK, top3_confidence FLOAT, model_version VARCHAR, inferred_at TIMESTAMPTZ). recommendation_audit (recommendation_id UUID PK, classification_result_id FK, retrieved_product_ids UUID[], llm_prompt_hash SHA256, llm_response_text TEXT, post_validation_passed BOOL, retry_count INT, disclaimer_accepted_at TIMESTAMPTZ, user_id FK, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: POST /v1/classify: body {s3_key, crop_type ENUM corn/soybean/wheat, growth_stage_bbch}. Returns {result_id, top3: [{species_id, common_name, confidence, thumbnail_url}], cached BOOL}. POST /v1/recommend: body {classification_result_id, field_id, county_fips}. Returns {recommendation_id, products: [{trade_name, moa_group, epa_reg_number, label_url, efficacy_rating}], narrative TEXT, resistance_excluded_moa_groups TEXT[], disclaimer_text, requires_acceptance BOOL}. POST /v1/recommend/{id}/accept: records disclaimer_accepted_at. POST /v1/scouting-events. GET /v1/analytics/resistance-trends?county_fips={}&crop_type={}&year_from={}.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Cognito user pools with email/password; MFA enforced for agronomist accounts. Pre-signed S3 upload URLs - images never transit FastAPI. RBAC: farmer accounts restricted to own field data; agronomist access to client fields requires field_sharing_consent record. EPA FIFRA compliance: system prompt instructs GPT-4o to recommend only products in retrieved set; post-generation validator parses all product names in LLM response against retrieved product list and rejects with retry if hallucinated names detected; after second failure falls back to structured product list without narrative. disclaimer_accepted_at logged to recommendation_audit before recommendation text displayed; this creates the regulatory audit trail for EPA FIFRA safe harbor.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions: ruff, mypy, pytest 85% coverage, Docker build ECR push, ECS rolling deploy. ECS Fargate: inference service 2 vCPU 8GB RAM standard, GPU task g4dn.xlarge for high-volume batches. Expo EAS Build for iOS/Android on tag. Expo EAS Update for OTA JS bundle updates avoiding App Store re-review. Environments: dev (mock CNN fixture species), staging (full pipeline separate S3 and Cognito), production multi-AZ us-east-1. Secrets: AWS Secrets Manager for OpenAI API key with 90-day rotation reminder.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM on FastAPI: ml.classification.confidence histogram (p50/p95), ml.inference.latency_ms, recommendation.rag_retrieval_ms, recommendation.llm_latency_ms, recommendation.post_validation_fail_rate. PagerDuty: post_validation_fail_rate over 5% in 10 minutes (LLM hallucination spike), classification error rate over 2%, p95 inference latency over 8s. Recommendation disclaimer acceptance rate and product click-through tracked as Datadog custom events.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ECS Fargate inference at 500 classifications per day: approximately $150/month. GPT-4o at $0.015 per recommendation (200 input + 300 output tokens): approximately $75/month at 5000 recommendations. S3 photo storage at 10GB/month: approximately $20/month. PostgreSQL RDS db.t4g.medium: approximately $60/month. Redis ElastiCache cache.t4g.micro: $25/month. Total estimated $330/month at MVP volume. Scaling: ECS auto-scaling on CPU 70%; Redis caches 90% of repeat classifications during peak season.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): PyTorch CNN inference endpoint, top-3 species classification, herbicide RAG retrieval from label database, GPT-4o recommendation with post-validation, React Native camera and classification screen, Cognito auth, scouting event logging, recommendation audit table, ECS Fargate deploy. Phase 2 (4 weeks): resistance trend analytics, county resistance map, agronomist role with client management, offline observation queue sync, Expo EAS OTA. Phase 3 (4 weeks): drone batch classification endpoint, farm management software API integration, model retraining pipeline.
target_output.development_guidelines: All herbicide recommendation paths must query herbicide_products table - no hardcoded product names. LLM system prompt in version-controlled prompt file. Post-generation validator runs on every LLM response without exception. pytest fixtures required for mock CNN inference and mock OpenAI responses in all recommendation tests. FastAPI dependency injection for Cognito JWT validation on every protected endpoint. All GPS stored as FLOAT columns; no PostGIS geometry in MVP to avoid extension management complexity.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: CNN misidentifies species leading to off-label herbicide recommendation. Mitigation: confidence score shown prominently; recommendations below 70% confidence show 'Consult your agronomist' additional disclaimer; advisory board validates quality before production. Risk: OpenAI API outage during spring scouting season. Mitigation: rule-based fallback from herbicide_products table labeled as basic recommendation. Risk: resistance records incomplete for many counties. Mitigation: show data provenance (source and recorded_year) on resistance filter explanation. Tradeoff: GPT-4o with RAG over fine-tuned model - deployable immediately and auditable; labeled recommendation training data does not yet exist.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed GPT-4o with RAG over dedicated recommendation model due to absence of labeled training data. Resistance records stored by county FIPS for MVP simplicity over GPS polygon. Pre-signed S3 upload confirmed over API proxying for multi-megapixel field images. Post-generation validator retry limit set to 1; on second failure falls back to structured product list without narrative.

metadata.notes: AI weed identification system with PyTorch CNN and GPT-4o RAG herbicide recommendations constrained to EPA-registered label database.

profile.domain: Growing Degree Day Automation and Phenological Alert System

project_goal.value: Automation tool ingesting daily weather observations from on-farm sensors and third-party networks, computing GDD accumulations per crop model and location, and pushing phenological threshold alerts to farmers via SMS and email
project_goal.rationale: Automates manual GDD tracking done in spreadsheets, reducing late alert risk during critical crop growth stages

target_users.value: Farmers enrolled in GDD alert subscriptions and agronomists managing crop phenology monitoring programs
target_users.rationale: Direct alert recipients and program managers identified in requirements

project_class.rationale: Scheduled batch jobs with alerting; no persistent user-facing UI or frontend

capabilities.rationale: Weather ingestion integrations, nightly batch GDD computation, alert delivery, and GDD accumulation analytics API

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-source weather ingestion, per-crop GDD model library, TimescaleDB time-series management, and reliable alert delivery at scale

risk_level.rationale: Internal-only service; no payment data; delayed alert is an agronomic inconvenience, not a safety hazard

data_sensitivity.rationale: Farm subscription and weather data is proprietary; email/phone used only for alert delivery

external_exposure.rationale: No public-facing endpoints; all weather adapters and alert delivery are outbound-only integrations

access_model.value: Internal service accounts for ingestion jobs; admin CLI with JWT auth for subscription management
access_model.rationale: No end-user login surface needed for MVP automation tool

feature_scope.value: Davis Instruments and Weather Underground ingestion adapters, corn base-50 and soybean base-50 GDD models, configurable phenological threshold alerts, Twilio SMS and SES email delivery, cumulative GDD analytics, delivery receipt tracking
feature_scope.rationale: Full pipeline from raw weather observation to farmer alert

mvp_scope.value: Davis Instruments and Weather Underground adapters, corn and soybean base-50 GDD models, configurable thresholds, SMS and email delivery, 90-day cumulative GDD history
mvp_scope.rationale: Covers the two most common row crop GDD alert use cases for market validation

security_baseline.value: Custom JWT for admin CLI, Kubernetes secrets for all API credentials, Twilio X-Twilio-Signature validation on delivery receipt webhook
security_baseline.rationale: Internal service baseline appropriate for risk level; webhook validation prevents spoofed delivery confirmations

frontend_stack.value: None - automation tool with admin CLI only
frontend_stack.rationale: No browser or mobile UI; management via admin CLI commands

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Celery
backend_stack.rationale: Celery provides mature distributed task scheduling for nightly batch jobs with retry visibility

data_platform.value: TimescaleDB for weather time-series; Redis 7 for Celery broker and result backend
data_platform.rationale: TimescaleDB hypertables provide efficient time-range queries for GDD accumulation; Redis handles Celery queuing

hosting_target.value: Bare-metal Kubernetes on Hetzner dedicated servers
hosting_target.rationale: Cost-optimized for batch workloads; self-managed to avoid cloud egress costs for weather data volumes

privacy_retention_policy.value: Daily observations retained 5 years; GDD accumulations indefinitely; alert delivery logs 2 years; subscriber contact data deleted within 30 days of unsubscription
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Agronomic data retention and subscriber privacy obligations

future_scope.value: ASOS automated surface observing station adapter, wheat base-32 GDD model, predictive GDD forecast integration with NOAA temperature outlooks
future_scope.rationale: Expanding station network coverage and crop model library in phase 5

constraints.value: Nightly batch must complete within 3-hour window 2am-5am; Celery worker must process 10000 station-days per run; no cloud dependencies - Hetzner only
constraints.rationale: Operational window constraint and self-hosted mandate

observability_baseline.value: Grafana and Prometheus for batch metrics, alert delivery success/failure rates, GDD throughput; Alertmanager for PagerDuty escalation on batch failures
observability_baseline.rationale: Self-hosted observability stack matching Hetzner-only constraint

execution_preference.value: GitLab CI for all pipeline stages; deploy via Helm chart to Hetzner Kubernetes
execution_preference.rationale: Self-hosted GitLab consistent with no-cloud mandate

project.goal: Automated GDD accumulation and phenological threshold alerting for enrolled farms
project.target_users: Farmers and agronomists

backend.ingestion: Davis WeatherLink API poller and Weather Underground API poller

security.secrets: Kubernetes secrets for Twilio, SES, weather API keys

data.cache: Redis 7 for Celery broker and GDD result cache

devops.deploy: Helm chart to bare-metal Kubernetes on Hetzner

reasoner_reviews.product: After three rounds, the core GDD pipeline is solid. The remaining gap is alert deduplication - round 3 lacked a mechanism to prevent duplicate threshold alerts when a farm re-crosses a threshold due to late-arrival data corrections. This is the primary focus for round 4.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The alert deduplication problem requires a threshold_crossing_log table with UNIQUE(subscription_id, threshold_gdd, season_year). The check_thresholds task uses INSERT INTO threshold_crossing_log ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING and checks inserted row count - if 0, alert already fired this season. Database constraint is the authority, not application-level cache.
reasoner_reviews.security: Twilio X-Twilio-Signature validation must be enforced on the delivery receipt webhook using RequestValidator.validate(). The Twilio auth token must be loaded from Kubernetes secret. Missing in round 3 despite being mentioned.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 10000 station-days in 3-hour window is achievable at 16 Celery workers on Hetzner AX102 at current GDD computation benchmarks. The constraint is met with existing architecture.

backend.service_design: Celery app with four task modules: ingest (weather observation per adapter), compute (GDD accumulation per subscription), alert_check (threshold evaluation with deduplication), deliver (SMS and email dispatch)
backend.background_jobs: Nightly Celery beat schedule: 02:00 ingest_all_stations chord, 02:45 compute_all_subscriptions after chord, 03:15 check_all_thresholds, 03:30 deliver_pending_alerts. Retry: 3 retries with exponential backoff on ingestion failures.
backend.api_patterns: Admin CLI: gdd admin subscriptions list/add/deactivate, gdd admin run-batch --date, gdd admin replay-alerts. Internal task signatures: ingest_davis_station.s(station_id, date), compute_gdd_accumulation.s(subscription_id, date), check_thresholds.s(subscription_id), deliver_alert.s(alert_id).
backend.failure_handling: Dead letter queue in Redis for failed deliver_alert tasks; Prometheus counter for failed_alert_deliveries; Alertmanager page if over 10 failures in 30 minutes.

security.auth_design: Admin CLI generates short-lived JWTs (HS256, 1h expiry) from Kubernetes secret GDD_ADMIN_JWT_SECRET
security.secrets_management: Kubernetes secrets for TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID, TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN, AWS_SES_SMTP_PASSWORD, DAVIS_API_KEY, WUNDERGROUND_API_KEY mounted as env vars in Celery worker pod
security.abuse_prevention: Twilio X-Twilio-Signature validation on inbound delivery receipt webhook using twilio.request_validator.RequestValidator(TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN).validate(url, post_params, signature); returns 403 on failure. SES bounce/complaint handler unsubscribes addresses automatically.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: alert_delivery_log: alert_id, channel ENUM sms/email, recipient_masked, provider_message_id (Twilio SID or SES message ID), status ENUM sent/delivered/failed, dispatched_at, updated_at.

data.entities: weather_stations (station_id, source ENUM davis/wunderground, lat, lon, active, last_observation_at), daily_observations hypertable (station_id, obs_date, temp_max_f, temp_min_f, precip_in, data_quality ENUM raw/qc_passed/qc_failed, ingested_at), farm_subscriptions (subscription_id, farm_name, station_id FK, crop_type ENUM corn/soybean/wheat, season_start DATE, contact_sms, contact_email, active), gdd_accumulations hypertable (subscription_id, obs_date, daily_gdd FLOAT, cumulative_gdd FLOAT, base_temp INT, computed_at), alert_thresholds (threshold_id, subscription_id FK, gdd_value INT, label TEXT, active), threshold_crossing_log (id UUID PK, subscription_id FK, threshold_gdd INT, season_year INT, first_crossed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, alert_id UUID, UNIQUE(subscription_id, threshold_gdd, season_year)), alert_delivery_log (alert_id, subscription_id FK, threshold_id FK, channel, recipient_masked, provider_message_id, status, dispatched_at, updated_at)
data.storage_design: TimescaleDB hypertables for daily_observations and gdd_accumulations partitioned monthly. Continuous aggregate weekly_gdd_summary. Retention: 5-year policy on daily_observations via TimescaleDB data retention job.
data.consistency_notes: GDD recomputation on late-arrival corrections: recompute_gdd task triggered when qc_failed observation corrected to qc_passed; cumulative GDD recalculated forward from correction date. Threshold crossing NOT re-evaluated for already-fired alerts per threshold_crossing_log UNIQUE constraint.
data.retention_and_deletion: TimescaleDB retention policy: daily_observations drop_chunks after 5 years. gdd_accumulations retained indefinitely. alert_delivery_log compressed after 90 days. Subscriber contact_sms and contact_email nulled on deactivation after 30-day grace period.

devops.deployment_topology: Hetzner AX102 (24 vCPU, 128GB RAM) k3s Kubernetes: celery-workers (4 pods x 4 workers = 16 total), celery-beat, TimescaleDB StatefulSet, Redis StatefulSet, Grafana, Prometheus
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI: pytest, coverage >=85%, ruff, mypy. Docker build push to Gitea container registry. Helm chart deploy kubectl diff and apply on merge to main. Staging namespace with isolated TimescaleDB schema.
devops.rollback_strategy: helm rollback gdd-alerts <revision>. TimescaleDB point-in-time recovery via daily pg_basebackup to Hetzner Storage Box.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus scrapes Celery flower metrics. Grafana dashboards: nightly_batch_completion_time, station_ingestion_success_rate, alert_delivery_success_rate, gdd_computation_throughput. ISS-001.title: Alert deduplication missing - duplicate alerts on late-arrival data corrections
ISS-001.detail: When a late-arriving corrected observation updates cumulative GDD past a threshold previously uncrossed due to missing data, duplicate alerts fire for thresholds already crossed earlier in the season.

history.note: Identified in product review
Partial mitigation discussed but not implemented
threshold_crossing_log table proposed but UNIQUE constraint not specified

ISS-002.title: Twilio delivery receipt webhook lacks X-Twilio-Signature validation
ISS-002.detail: Delivery receipt endpoint accepts Twilio webhook POSTs without validating X-Twilio-Signature, allowing spoofed confirmations to mark alerts as delivered when not.

history.note: Security review flagged missing webhook validation
Mentioned in security section but implementation spec absent

ISS-003.title: GDD recomputation on data correction not specified
ISS-003.detail: When qc_failed observation corrected, cumulative GDD must be recomputed forward. Round 3 added recompute_gdd Celery task.

history.note: recompute_gdd task added to round 3 plan

previous_plan.title: GDD Alert Automation Tool v3
previous_plan.executive_summary: Python/Celery automation tool on Hetzner Kubernetes computing GDD accumulations from multi-source weather data and dispatching threshold alerts via SMS and email.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Celery beat scheduler triggers nightly ingestion, GDD computation, threshold evaluation, and alert delivery task chains. TimescaleDB stores observations and accumulations as hypertables.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Kubernetes secrets for API keys. Admin CLI with JWT. Twilio webhook mentioned but validation not specified.
previous_plan.data_model: threshold_crossing_log mentioned for deduplication. UNIQUE constraint not specified. Retention and compression policies added.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 4 targeted fix for two persistent issues. ISS-001 resolution: the exact deduplication mechanism is INSERT INTO threshold_crossing_log (subscription_id, threshold_gdd, season_year, first_crossed_at, alert_id) VALUES (...) ON CONFLICT (subscription_id, threshold_gdd, season_year) DO NOTHING - if inserted_rows == 0, threshold already fired this season, skip delivery. ISS-002 resolution: Twilio X-Twilio-Signature validation using twilio.request_validator.RequestValidator(TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN).validate(url, post_params, signature) in FastAPI middleware returning 403 on failure. Both are surgical additions to existing plan sections.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified threshold_crossing_log deduplication mechanism: UNIQUE constraint on (subscription_id, threshold_gdd, season_year); check_thresholds Celery task uses INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING and checks inserted row count - if 0, threshold already crossed this season and alert delivery is skipped. Database constraint is the authority regardless of GDD recomputation events.
fix_report.expected_outcome: No duplicate threshold alerts fired across the season regardless of how many GDD recomputation events occur from late-arriving or corrected weather observations.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified Twilio X-Twilio-Signature validation on delivery receipt endpoint using twilio.request_validator.RequestValidator(TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN).validate(full_url, post_params_dict, signature_header). FastAPI middleware returns 403 if validation fails. TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN loaded from Kubernetes secret at startup.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Spoofed delivery receipt POSTs rejected with 403; only authentic Twilio callbacks update alert_delivery_log to delivered status.

target_output.title: GDD Alert Automation Tool
target_output.executive_summary: A Python/Celery automation tool on Hetzner bare-metal Kubernetes that ingests daily weather observations from Davis Instruments and Weather Underground stations, computes corn and soybean GDD accumulations per enrolled farm subscription, and dispatches phenological threshold alerts via Twilio SMS and AWS SES email. Alert deduplication via threshold_crossing_log UNIQUE constraint prevents duplicate notifications on late-arrival data corrections. Twilio delivery receipts validated with X-Twilio-Signature preventing spoofed confirmations.
target_output.architecture_overview: Celery beat scheduler triggers a nightly four-stage pipeline: (1) ingest_all_stations chord dispatches per-station tasks writing daily_observations to TimescaleDB; (2) after chord completion, compute_all_subscriptions updates gdd_accumulations hypertable; (3) check_all_thresholds evaluates cumulative GDD against configured thresholds using INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING deduplication against threshold_crossing_log; (4) deliver_pending_alerts dispatches Twilio SMS and SES email tasks for newly inserted threshold crossings. Redis is Celery broker and result backend. Admin CLI manages subscriptions and manual replays. FastAPI webhook receiver handles Twilio delivery receipts with X-Twilio-Signature validation.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Python 3.12, Celery 5.3, Redis 7 (broker and result backend). Database: TimescaleDB 2.14 (PostgreSQL 16 extension) with hypertables for daily_observations and gdd_accumulations. Alert delivery: Twilio Python SDK (SMS), boto3 SES (email), twilio.request_validator for webhook validation. Weather ingestion: Davis WeatherLink API v2, Weather Underground PWS API. Infrastructure: Hetzner AX102 bare-metal, k3s Kubernetes, Helm. CI: GitLab CI. Monitoring: Grafana, Prometheus, Alertmanager. Admin: Python CLI with argparse.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Weather Ingestion: nightly Davis WeatherLink and Weather Underground API pollers writing daily max/min temperature and precipitation to daily_observations with QC status. GDD Computation: per-subscription Celery task computing Modified GDD (base 50F corn/soybean, base 32F wheat) and updating cumulative season total. Threshold Alert Check: evaluates cumulative GDD against active thresholds; deduplicates via threshold_crossing_log UNIQUE constraint; creates alert record for new crossings only. Alert Delivery: Twilio SMS and SES email tasks with delivery receipt tracking. Delivery Receipt Handler: FastAPI endpoint with X-Twilio-Signature validation updating alert_delivery_log. Admin CLI: subscription management, manual batch run, alert replay.
target_output.system_components: Celery beat pod (nightly schedule), Celery worker pods (16 workers on AX102), TimescaleDB (daily_observations and gdd_accumulations hypertables, weekly_gdd_summary continuous aggregate), Redis 7 (broker and result backend), Davis WeatherLink API adapter, Weather Underground PWS API adapter, Twilio API adapter, AWS SES adapter, FastAPI webhook receiver (delivery receipts with signature validation), Admin CLI, Prometheus plus Grafana plus Alertmanager.
target_output.workflows: Nightly Batch: Celery beat fires at 02:00 - chord of ingest tasks for all active stations writes to daily_observations; on chord completion at 02:45 group of compute_gdd_accumulation tasks per subscription updates gdd_accumulations; at 03:15 group of check_thresholds tasks evaluate cumulative GDD: for each active threshold INSERT INTO threshold_crossing_log (subscription_id, threshold_gdd, season_year, first_crossed_at, alert_id) ON CONFLICT (subscription_id, threshold_gdd, season_year) DO NOTHING - if inserted_rows == 1 enqueue deliver_alert.s(alert_id), if 0 threshold already fired this season skip delivery. Delivery: deliver_alert calls Twilio send_message for SMS, SES send_email for email, writes dispatched_at and provider_message_id to alert_delivery_log. Delivery Receipt: Twilio POSTs to /webhooks/twilio/delivery; FastAPI middleware validates X-Twilio-Signature using RequestValidator(TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN).validate(url, post_params, signature); 403 on failure; on success updates alert_delivery_log status.
target_output.data_model: weather_stations (station_id UUID PK, source ENUM davis/wunderground/asos, lat FLOAT, lon FLOAT, api_key_ref VARCHAR, active BOOL, last_observation_at TIMESTAMPTZ). daily_observations TimescaleDB hypertable (station_id UUID, obs_date DATE, temp_max_f FLOAT, temp_min_f FLOAT, precip_in FLOAT, data_quality ENUM raw/qc_passed/qc_failed, ingested_at TIMESTAMPTZ) - partitioned monthly, 5-year retention. gdd_accumulations TimescaleDB hypertable (subscription_id UUID, obs_date DATE, daily_gdd FLOAT, cumulative_gdd FLOAT, base_temp_f INT, computed_at TIMESTAMPTZ) - partitioned monthly. Continuous aggregate weekly_gdd_summary. farm_subscriptions (subscription_id UUID PK, farm_name, station_id FK, crop_type ENUM corn/soybean/wheat, season_start DATE, contact_sms, contact_email, active BOOL). alert_thresholds (threshold_id UUID PK, subscription_id FK, gdd_value INT, label TEXT e.g. 'Corn V6 stage', active BOOL). threshold_crossing_log (id UUID PK, subscription_id FK, threshold_gdd INT, season_year INT, first_crossed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, alert_id UUID, UNIQUE(subscription_id, threshold_gdd, season_year)). alert_delivery_log (alert_id UUID PK, subscription_id FK, threshold_id FK, channel ENUM sms/email, recipient_masked TEXT, provider_message_id TEXT, status ENUM queued/sent/delivered/failed, dispatched_at TIMESTAMPTZ, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: No public-facing API. Internal Celery task API: ingest_davis_station(station_id, date), ingest_wunderground_station(station_id, date), compute_gdd_accumulation(subscription_id, date), check_thresholds(subscription_id) -> list[alert_id] for newly created crossings, deliver_alert(alert_id). Inbound webhook: POST /webhooks/twilio/delivery (X-Twilio-Signature validated via RequestValidator, updates alert_delivery_log). Admin CLI: gdd admin subscriptions {list, add, deactivate} | gdd admin run-batch --date | gdd admin replay-alerts --subscription-id --date-from --date-to.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Custom JWT (HS256, 1h expiry) for admin CLI from Kubernetes secret GDD_ADMIN_JWT_SECRET. Kubernetes secrets for all external API credentials. Twilio delivery receipt webhook: X-Twilio-Signature validation using twilio.request_validator.RequestValidator(TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN).validate(full_url, post_params_dict, signature_header) in FastAPI middleware - returns 403 on validation failure with warning log including source IP. Alert deduplication: threshold_crossing_log UNIQUE(subscription_id, threshold_gdd, season_year) is the authoritative deduplication mechanism; INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING is the implementation pattern in check_thresholds Celery task - not an application-level lock or Redis cache. Subscriber contact data masked to last-4 digits in all logs; full values stored in PostgreSQL farm_subscriptions.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Hetzner AX102 (24 vCPU, 128GB RAM) k3s single-node Kubernetes. Helm chart deployments: celery-worker (4 replicas, 4 workers, 4 CPU/8GB RAM each), celery-beat (1 replica), timescaledb (StatefulSet 200GB PVC), redis (StatefulSet 10GB PVC). GitLab CI: pytest, ruff, mypy, Docker build to Gitea registry, helm diff and helm upgrade on main merge. Staging: isolated gdd-staging namespace with separate TimescaleDB schema. Rollback: helm rollback gdd-alerts to previous revision. Database backup: daily pg_basebackup to Hetzner Storage Box with restic encryption.
target_output.observability: Prometheus scrapes Celery flower metrics: celery_tasks_total by task_name and state, celery_task_latency_seconds. Custom application Prometheus counters: gdd_station_ingestions_total by source and status, gdd_alert_deliveries_total by channel and status, gdd_deduplication_skips_total (threshold already crossed, no alert sent). Grafana dashboards: nightly_batch_timeline per-stage vs 3-hour budget, ingestion_success_rate by source, alert_delivery_success_rate SMS vs email, cumulative_gdd_by_crop aggregated. Alertmanager: batch_not_completed_by_0500 PagerDuty P1, alert_delivery_failure_rate over 10% in 30 minutes PagerDuty P2.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Hetzner AX102 dedicated: approximately 120 EUR/month. Hetzner Volume 200GB: approximately 10 EUR/month. Hetzner Storage Box backup: approximately 4 EUR/month. Twilio SMS at 0.008 EUR per message: at 5000 alerts/month approximately 40 EUR. AWS SES at $0.10 per 1000 emails: approximately $0.50 at 5000/month. Total approximately 180 EUR/month. At 500 sensor nodes per night 16 Celery workers handle 10000 station-days comfortably within 3-hour window. Scaling path: add second Hetzner node to k3s cluster and increase Celery worker replicas beyond 50000 station-days.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phases 1-3 complete: corn/soybean GDD computation, Celery pipeline, TimescaleDB hypertables, SMS/email delivery, admin CLI, Grafana monitoring. Phase 4 (this round): alert deduplication via threshold_crossing_log UNIQUE constraint and INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING (ISS-001), Twilio X-Twilio-Signature validation in FastAPI middleware (ISS-002), integration test suite for deduplication behavior across season replay. Phase 5 (next): ASOS station adapter, wheat base-32 GDD model, NOAA 6-10 day temperature outlook integration for predictive GDD.
target_output.development_guidelines: All Celery tasks must be idempotent - ingest with same station_id and date must UPSERT on (station_id, obs_date). check_thresholds deduplication must use database UNIQUE constraint as authority - INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING pattern is mandatory, never application-level cache. Twilio webhook handler validates signature before any database writes. All datetimes stored as UTC TIMESTAMPTZ. Admin CLI replay commands do not re-fire already-crossed thresholds because deduplication is handled automatically by the DB constraint.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Davis WeatherLink API rate limit exceeded during ingestion chord. Mitigation: Celery task rate limit at 10 requests per minute per station with exponential backoff; missing observations flagged qc_failed for backfill. Risk: TimescaleDB disk exhaustion near 5-year retention boundary. Mitigation: Alertmanager rule on data volume over 80% capacity; retention job runs daily. Tradeoff: DB UNIQUE constraint deduplication over Redis-based application lock - DB constraint chosen for durability across Celery worker restarts and Redis failures. Tradeoff: single-node k3s over multi-node HA Kubernetes - AX102 sufficient for current 10000 station-days per night; HA adds cost without operational need at current scale.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: ISS-001: INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING confirmed as deduplication mechanism; UNIQUE(subscription_id, threshold_gdd, season_year) constraint is the authority. ISS-002: twilio.request_validator.RequestValidator validated; FastAPI middleware returns 403 on failure. Season boundary for threshold_crossing_log defined as calendar year of season_start date - subscriptions with March season_start use year of that date as season_year.

metadata.notes: Round 4 revision resolving alert deduplication (ISS-001) via threshold_crossing_log UNIQUE constraint and Twilio webhook signature validation (ISS-002).
